table.name: account
table.summary: Used in the accounting section in chart of accounts.  Not related to patient accounts in any way.

column.name: AccountNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: AcctType
column.summary: Enum:AccountType Asset, Liability, Equity,Revenue, Expense

Enumeration.name: AccountType
Enumeration.summary: Used in accounting for chart of accounts.

column.name: BankNumber
column.summary: For asset accounts, this would be the bank account number for deposit slips.

column.name: Inactive
column.summary: Set to true to not normally view this account in the list.

column.name: IsRetainedEarnings
column.summary: This will be set true for exactly one account, and it can't be changed.  On the Balance Sheet report, this special account will also contain the sum of all expenses and income for all previous years.

table.name: accountingautopay
table.summary: In the accounting section, this automates entries into the database when user enters a payment into a patient account.  This table presents the user with a picklist specific to that payment type.  For example, a cash payment would create a picklist of cashboxes for user to put the cash into.

column.name: AccountingAutoPayNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PayType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.

column.name: PickList
column.summary: FK to account.AccountNum.  AccountNums separated by commas.  No spaces.

table.name: activeinstance
table.summary: ActiveInstances are used to track OD sessions.

column.name: ActiveInstanceNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: ComputerNum
column.summary: FK to Computers.ComputerNum

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to Userod.UserNum

column.name: ProcessId
column.summary: Windows Process ID of the Open Dental instance

column.name: DateTimeLastActive
column.summary: Last datetime that was activity was recorded

column.name: DateTRecorded
column.summary: The time at which we recorded DateTimeLastActive. This is not a TimeStamp column because we need to update it even if nothing else in the row changed.

column.name: ConnectionType
column.summary: Enum:ConnectionTypes Used to distinguish the connection type.

EnumValue._: 0 - Direct
EnumValue.name: Direct

EnumValue._: 1 - MiddleTier
EnumValue.name: MiddleTier

EnumValue._: 2 - Thinfinity
EnumValue.name: Thinfinity

EnumValue._: 3 - AppStream
EnumValue.name: AppStream

table.name: adjustment
table.summary: An adjustment in the patient account.  Usually, adjustments are very simple, just being assigned to one patient and provider.  But they can also be attached to a procedure to represent a discount on that procedure.  Attaching adjustments to procedures is not automated, so it is not very common.

column.name: AdjNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: AdjDate
column.summary: The date that the adjustment shows in the patient account.

column.name: AdjAmt
column.summary: Amount of adjustment.  Can be pos or neg.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: AdjType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: AdjNote
column.summary: Note for this adjustment.

column.name: ProcDate
column.summary: Procedure date.  Not when the adjustment was entered.

column.name: ProcNum
column.summary: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum.  Only used if attached to a procedure.  Otherwise, 0.

column.name: DateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.name: StatementNum
column.summary: FK to statement.StatementNum.  Only used when the statement in an invoice.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: TaxTransID
column.summary:
(Deprecated) Holds the Avalara transaction ID associated with this adjustment so that we can track reported adjustments.
            Not editable in the UI.

table.name: alertcategory
table.summary: A grouping of alert types that you can subscribe to. The alert types are stored as AlertCategoryLink rows attached to each category. Users can make their own custom categories by copying existing ones.

column.name: AlertCategoryNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: IsHQCategory
column.summary: False by default, indicates that this is a category that can not be edited or deleted. When a copy of a category is made, the copy changes this value from true to false.

column.name: InternalName
column.summary: Name used by HQ to identify the type of alert category this started as, allows us to associate new alerts.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Name displayed to user when subscribing to alerts categories.

table.name: alertcategorylink
table.summary: Each row is an alert type for an alertcategory. Users can change which types are attached to custom categories.

column.name: AlertCategoryLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: AlertCategoryNum
column.summary: FK to AlertCategory.AlertCategoryNum.

column.name: AlertType
column.summary: Enum:AlertType Identifies what types of alert this row is associated to.

Enumeration.name: AlertType
Enumeration.summary: Enum representing different alert types. Any additional entries to this enum will need to be linked to the AlertCategory table. Do so in ConvertDatabases8. There will be one link to this type's specific category and one link to AlertCategory with InternalName 'OdAllTypes'.

EnumValue._: 0 - Generic. Informational, has no action associated with it
EnumValue.name: Generic

EnumValue._: 1 - Opens the Online Payments Window when clicked
EnumValue.name: OnlinePaymentsPending

EnumValue._: 2 - Only used by Open Dental HQ. The server monitoring incoming voicemails is not working.
EnumValue.name: VoiceMailMonitor

EnumValue._: 3 - Opens the Radiology Order List window when clicked.
EnumValue.name: RadiologyProcedures

EnumValue._: 4 - A patient has clicked "Request Callback" on an e-Confirmation.
EnumValue.name: CallbackRequested

EnumValue._: 5 - Alerts related to the Web Sched New Pat eService.
EnumValue.name: WebSchedNewPat

EnumValue._: 6 - Alerts related to Web Sched New Patient Appointments.
EnumValue.name: WebSchedNewPatApptCreated

EnumValue._: 7 - A number is not able to receive text messages.
EnumValue.name: NumberBarredFromTexting

EnumValue._: 8 - The number of MySQL connections to the server has exceeded half the allowed number of connections.
EnumValue.name: MaxConnectionsMonitor

EnumValue._: 9 - Alerts related to new ASAP appointments via web sched.
EnumValue.name: WebSchedASAPApptCreated

EnumValue._: 10 - Only used by Open Dental HQ. The Asterisk Server is not processing messages or is getting all blank payloads.
EnumValue.name: AsteriskServerMonitor

EnumValue._: 11 - Multiple computers are running eConnector services. There should only ever be one.
EnumValue.name: MultipleEConnectors

EnumValue._: 12 - The eConnector is in a critical state and not currently turned on. There should only ever be one.
EnumValue.name: EConnectorDown

EnumValue._: 13 - The eConnector has an error that is not critical but is worth looking into. There should only ever be one.
EnumValue.name: EConnectorError

EnumValue._: 14 - Alerts related to DoseSpot provider registration.
EnumValue.name: DoseSpotProviderRegistered

EnumValue._: 15 - Alerts related to DoseSpot clinic registration.
EnumValue.name: DoseSpotClinicRegistered

EnumValue._: 16 - An appointment has been created via Web Sched Recall.
EnumValue.name: WebSchedRecallApptCreated

EnumValue._: 17 - Alerts related to turning clinics on or off for eServices.
EnumValue.name: ClinicsChanged

EnumValue._:
18 - Alerts related to turning clinics on or off for eServices. Internal, not displayed to the customer.
            Will be processed by the eConnector and then deleted.
EnumValue.name: ClinicsChangedInternal

EnumValue._: 19 - Multiple computers are running OpenDentalServices. There should only ever be one.
EnumValue.name: MultipleOpenDentalServices

EnumValue._: 20 - OpenDentalService is down.
EnumValue.name: OpenDentalServiceDown

EnumValue._: 21 - Triggered when a new WebMail is received from the patient portal.
EnumValue.name: WebMailReceived

EnumValue._:
22 - Triggered when the consecutive count of failed emails for clinic reaches greater than the value set in 
            EmailAlertMaxConsecutiveFails preference.
EnumValue.name: EconnectorEmailTooManySendFails

EnumValue._: 23 - Alert the user for things like not making a local supplemental backup within the last month.
EnumValue.name: SupplementalBackups

EnumValue._:
24 - Alert the user that the local time on the eConnector does not closely match the time of the database.  Intended to only have
            one instance max.
EnumValue.name: EConnectorMySqlTime

EnumValue._: 25 - Alert the user that there are CareCredit batch errors.
EnumValue.name: CareCreditBatchError

EnumValue._: 26 - Alert the user that there are patients who have texted to indicate they have arrived for their appointment.
EnumValue.name: PatientArrival

EnumValue._: 27 - Alert the user that there are new secure emails that have been downloaded.
EnumValue.name: EmailSecure

EnumValue._: 28 - An appointment has been created via Web Sched Exising Pat
EnumValue.name: WebSchedExistingPatApptCreated

EnumValue._: 29 - Alert the user when they're approaching their Cloud Session Limit (determined by CloudAlertWithinLimit pref)
EnumValue.name: CloudAlertWithinLimit

EnumValue._: 30 - Alert that web forms are ready to be retrieved.
EnumValue.name: WebFormsReady

EnumValue._: 31 - Alert HQ that a push hub client has reached its failure threshold or all push hub clients are failing
EnumValue.name: PushHubDown

EnumValue._: 32 - Alert that user action is required after an update.
EnumValue.name: Update

EnumValue._: 33 - Alert.
EnumValue.name: ReplicationMonitor

EnumValue._: 34 - Alert that no recalls have sent in a period of time.
EnumValue.name: WebSchedRecallsNotSending

EnumValue._: 35 - Alert if an account using 10DLC goes over their daily max limit or if we've neglected to set up our pref correctly
EnumValue.name: TenDlc

EnumValue._:
36 - Alert if a clinic is not signed for eConfirmations but is trying to use the AddToCalendar tag. FKey on this alert type is used to determine
            the type of autocomm that created this alert rather than a FKey to a db row.
EnumValue.name: AddToCalendar

EnumValue._:
37 - Alert that is created when an eClipboard/ODM/ODT device tries to get a tooth chart image, but their eConn server is missing 
            a specific redistributable that allows the creation of the image.
EnumValue.name: EConnectorRedistributableMissing

EnumValue._: 38 - Alert that is created when the SMSQueuer is turned on but no Broadcaster Servers are configured to send out text messages. Requires Engineers to update BroadcasterThreadSettings.
EnumValue.name: SMSThread

EnumValue._: 39 - Alert that is created when a procedure or group note's signature is cleared by another user editing that note.
EnumValue.name: SignatureCleared

EnumValue._: 40 - Alert that is created when an error occurs while uploading an image to Pearl or processing Pearl results.
EnumValue.name: Pearl

EnumValue._: 41 - Alert that is created when an error occurs while uploading an image to BetterDiagnostics or processing BetterDiagnostics results.
EnumValue.name: BetterDiagnostics

EnumValue._: 42 - Alert that is created when running Billing and both email and SMS templates do not contain [MsgToPayURL] replacement tag.
EnumValue.name: MessageToPayTag

EnumValue._: 43 - Alert that is created while a mass email audience is being uploaded.
EnumValue.name: MassEmailUpload

EnumValue._: 44 - Alert that commlogs have been created for a sent mass email.
EnumValue.name: MassEmailReceipt

EnumValue._: 45 - Alert that is created by HQ to send a notification to specific customers.
EnumValue.name: HQNotification

table.name: alertitem
table.summary: Any row in this table will show up in the main menu of Open Dental to get the attention of the user. The user can click on the alert and take an action.  The actions available to the user are also determined in this row.

column.name: AlertItemNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum. Can be 0 or -1. -1 indicates show the alert in all clinics.

column.name: Description

column.name: Type

column.name: Severity
column.summary: Enum:SeverityType The severity will help determine what color this alert should be in the main menu.

Enumeration.name: SeverityType
Enumeration.summary: Represents the urgency of the alert.  Also determines the color for the menu item in the main menu.

EnumValue._: 0 - White
EnumValue.name: Normal

EnumValue._: 1 - Yellow
EnumValue.name: Low

EnumValue._: 2 - Orange
EnumValue.name: Medium

EnumValue._: 3 - Red
EnumValue.name: High

column.name: Actions
column.summary: Enum:ActionType Bitwise flag that represents what actions are available for this alert.

Enumeration.name: ActionType
Enumeration.summary: The possible actions that can be taken on this alert.  Multiple actions can be available for one alert.

column.name: FormToOpen
column.summary: Enum:FormType The form to open when the user clicks "Open Form".

column.name: FKey
column.summary: A FK to a table associated with the AlertType.  0 indicates not in use.

column.name: ItemValue
column.summary: Like description, but more specific. When set use ActionType.ShowItemValue to show this variable within a MsgBoxCopyPaste window.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to Userod.UserNum.  Will only be shown to that specific user.  0 is all users.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: Date this row was added to the database. Not editable by the user

column.name: AlertReadNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: AlertItemNum
column.summary: FK to alertitem.AlertItemNum.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.

table.name: alertsub
table.summary: Subscribes a user and optional clinic to specific alert types.  Users will not get alerts unless they have an entry in this table.

column.name: AlertSubNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum. Can be 0.

column.name: Type
column.summary: Deprecated.

column.name: AlertCategoryNum
column.summary: FK to alertcategory.AlertCategoryNum.

table.name: allergy
table.summary: An allergy attached to a patient and linked to an AllergyDef.

column.name: AllergyNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: AllergyDefNum
column.summary: FK to allergydef.AllergyDefNum

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: Reaction
column.summary: Adverse reaction description.  When importing from eForms, this is where the allergy name goes for "Other" when there's no match.

column.name: StatusIsActive
column.summary: True if still an active allergy.  False helps hide it from the list of active allergies.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: To be used for synch with web server for CertTimelyAccess.

column.name: DateAdverseReaction
column.summary: The historical date that the patient had the adverse reaction to this agent.

column.name: SnomedReaction
column.summary: Snomed code for reaction.  Optional and independent of the Reaction text field.  Not needed for reporting.  Only used for CCD export/import.

table.name: allergydef
table.summary: An allergy definition.  Gets linked to an allergy and patient.  Allergies will not show in CCD messages unless they have a valid Medication (that has an RxNorm) or UniiCode.

column.name: AllergyDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Name of the drug.  User can change this.  If an RxCui is present, the RxNorm string can be pulled from the in-memory table for UI display in addition to the Description.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Because user can't delete.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: The last date and time this row was altered.  Not user editable.

column.name: SnomedType
column.summary: Enum:SnomedAllergy SNOMED Allergy Type Code.  Only used to create CCD in FormSummaryOfCare.

EnumValue._: 0-No SNOMED allergy type code has been assigned.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1-Allergy to substance (disorder), code number 418038007.
EnumValue.name: AllergyToSubstance

EnumValue._: 2-Drug allergy (disorder), code number 416098002.
EnumValue.name: DrugAllergy

EnumValue._: 3-Drug intolerance (disorder), code number 59037007.
EnumValue.name: DrugIntolerance

EnumValue._: 4-Food allergy (disorder), code number 414285001.
EnumValue.name: FoodAllergy

EnumValue._: 5-Food intolerance (disorder), code number 235719002.
EnumValue.name: FoodIntolerance

EnumValue._: 6-Propensity to adverse reactions (disorder), code number 420134006.
EnumValue.name: AdverseReactions

EnumValue._: 7-Propensity to adverse reactions to drug (disorder), code number 419511003
EnumValue.name: AdverseReactionsToDrug

EnumValue._: 8-Propensity to adverse reactions to food (disorder), code number 418471000.
EnumValue.name: AdverseReactionsToFood

EnumValue._: 9-Propensity to adverse reactions to substance (disorder), code number 419199007.
EnumValue.name: AdverseReactionsToSubstance

column.name: MedicationNum
column.summary: FK to medication.MedicationNum.  Optional, only used with CCD messages.

column.name: UniiCode
column.summary: The Unii code for the Allergen.  Optional, but there must be either a MedicationNum or a UniiCode.  Used to create CCD in FormSummaryOfCare, or set during CCD allergy reconcile.

table.name: apikey
table.summary: Used to keep track of Customer's API Key and Developer's name. Just a copy from OD HQ for convenience.

column.name: APIKeyNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: CustApiKey
column.summary: Customer's API key.

column.name: DevName
column.summary: Developer's name, exactly as they entered it in FHIR developer portal.

table.name: apisubscription
table.summary: A subscription by an API client that requests events to be fired for db changes or ui actions. Events are currently sent blindly. In the future, we could support acking for db events, but not very useful for ui events.

column.name: ApiSubscriptionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EndPointUrl
column.summary: This is the URL endpoint to which events will be sent.

column.name: Workstation
column.summary: Name of the workstation that will fire events. Blank if you want all workstations to fire events.

column.name: CustomerKey
column.summary: API Key the subscribing developer gave the customer. There can be multiple 3rd parties products for one database, each with their own key.

column.name: WatchTable
column.summary: Enum: EnumWatchTable, stored as string

column.name: PollingSeconds
column.summary: Frequency of database polling, in seconds.

column.name: UiEventType
column.summary: Enum: EnumApiUiEventType, stored as string.

column.name: DateTimeStart
column.summary: When the subscription started. This gets updated each time db is polled so that it represents the start of the date range for the next polling.

column.name: DateTimeStop
column.summary: When the subscription will expire. MinVal 01-01-0001 if no expiration.

table.name: appointment
table.summary: Appointments can show in the Appointments module, or they can be on the unscheduled list.  An appointment object is also used to store the Planned appointment.  The planned appointment never gets scheduled, but instead gets copied.  Also see histappointment, which keeps a historical record.

column.name: AptNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  The patient that the appointment is for.

column.name: AptStatus
column.summary: Enum:ApptStatus .

Enumeration.name: ApptStatus
Enumeration.summary: Appointment status.

EnumValue._: 0- No appointment should ever have this status.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1- Shows as a regularly scheduled appointment.
EnumValue.name: Scheduled

EnumValue._: 2- Shows greyed out.
EnumValue.name: Complete

EnumValue._: 3- Only shows on unscheduled list.
EnumValue.name: UnschedList

EnumValue._: 4- Deprecated in 17.4.1. Use Appointment.Priority instead.
EnumValue.name: ASAP

EnumValue._: 5- Shows with a big X on it.
EnumValue.name: Broken

EnumValue._: 6- Planned appointment.  Only shows in Chart module. User not allowed to change this status, and it does not display as one of the options.
EnumValue.name: Planned

EnumValue._: 7- Patient "post-it" note on the schedule. Shows light yellow. Shows on day scheduled just like appt, as well as in prog notes, etc.

EnumValue._: 8- Patient "post-it" note completed
EnumValue.name: PtNoteCompleted

column.name: Pattern
column.summary: Time pattern, X for Dr time, / for assist time. Stored in 5 minute increments.  Converted as needed to 10 or 15 minute representations for display.  There's not a hard limit on this.  When dragging, the max is 6.5 hours.  Within the AptEdit window, it can be set to 9 hours.

column.name: Confirmed
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  This field can also be used to show patient arrived, in chair, etc.  The Category column in the definition table is DefCat.ApptConfirmed.

column.name: TimeLocked
column.summary: If true, then the program will not attempt to reset the user's time pattern and length when adding or removing procedures.

column.name: Op
column.summary: FK to operatory.OperatoryNum.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Note.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: ProvHyg
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  Optional.  Only used if a hygienist is assigned to this appt.

column.name: AptDateTime
column.summary: Appointment Date and time.  If you need just the date or time for an SQL query, you can use DATE(AptDateTime) and TIME(AptDateTime) in your query.

column.name: NextAptNum
column.summary: FK to appointment.AptNum.  A better description of this field would be PlannedAptNum.  Only used to show that this apt is derived from specified planned apt. Otherwise, 0.

column.name: UnschedStatus
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  The definition.Category in the definition table is DefCat.RecallUnschedStatus.  Only used if this is an Unsched or Planned appt.

column.name: IsNewPatient
column.summary: This is the first appoinment this patient has had at this office.  Somewhat automated.

column.name: ProcDescript
column.summary: A one line summary of all procedures.  Can be used in various reports, Unscheduled list, and Planned appointment tracker.  Not user editable right now, so it doesn't show on the screen.

column.name: Assistant
column.summary: FK to employee.EmployeeNum.  You can assign an assistant to the appointment.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  0 if no clinic.

column.name: IsHygiene
column.summary: Set true if this is a hygiene appt. This flag is frequently not set even when it is a hygiene appointment because some offices want the dentist color on the appointments.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program updates.  Not user editable.

column.name: DateTimeArrived
column.summary: The date and time that the patient checked in.  Date is largely ignored since it should be the same as the appt.

column.name: DateTimeSeated
column.summary: The date and time that the patient was seated in the chair in the operatory.

column.name: DateTimeDismissed
column.summary: The date and time that the patient got up out of the chair.  Date is largely ignored since it should be the same as the appt.

column.name: InsPlan1
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum for the primary insurance plan at the time the appointment is set complete. May be 0. We can't tell later which subscriber is involved; only the plan.

column.name: InsPlan2
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum for the secoondary insurance plan at the time the appointment is set complete. May be 0. We can't tell later which subscriber is involved; only the plan.

column.name: DateTimeAskedToArrive
column.summary: Date and time patient asked to arrive, or minval if patient not asked to arrive at a different time than appt.

column.name: ProcsColored

column.name: ColorOverride
column.summary:
If set to anything but 0, then this will override the graphic color for the appointment.
            Typically set to the color of the corresponding appointment type (if one is set) or a color manually picked by the user.

column.name: AppointmentTypeNum

table.name: apptreminderrule
table.summary:
This is called eServices Automated Messaging Rules in the UI. These are used to track the automated generation and sending of appointment reminders and confirmations. 
             Users are allowed to define up to two reminders and one confirmation (per clinic.) These can be sent out any number of Days, Hours, and/or 
             Minutes before a scheduled appointment.
             PRACTICE - Appointment Reminder Rules will be saved and edited with clinicNum=0. This denotes the "Defaults" when using clinics, 
             but for a practice the defaults become the practice rules.CLINICS - When using clinics, each clinic has a bool "IsConfirmEnabled" that determines if a particular clinic has automated reminders/confirmations
            enabled. If not, no reminders will be sent out for the clinic. If enabled, and no rules are defined for the clinic, then the clinic will attempt to use the
            defaults that have been defined with clinicNum==0. If a clinic is enabled and has at least one AppointmentReminderRule defined, then NO defaults will be
            used for that clinic.REMINDERS - reminders are sent out using the ApptComm system implemented by DG. These used to be stored as preferences for the practice only,
            now users are allowed to define them on a per clinic basis. Reminders should be considered one way communications and should not be desingned with a
            customer response in mind.CONFIRMATIONS - confirmations are sent using the new automated-confirmation system implemented by RM (proper) and SO (web backend). Confirmations
            are intended to allow end patients to respond to OpenDental via text or email and automatically confirm, or set to a desired status, the appointments
            on the schedule.

column.name: ApptReminderRuleNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TypeCur
column.summary: Enum:ApptReminderType

EnumValue._: -1 - Used to define an Undefined ApptReminderType.
EnumValue.name: Undefined

EnumValue._: 0 - Used to define the rules for when reminders should be sent out.
EnumValue.name: Reminder

EnumValue._: 1 - Defines rules for when confirmations should be sent out.
EnumValue.name: ConfirmationFutureDay

EnumValue._: 2 - DEPRECATED. As of 17.4, all reminders have a status of Reminder.
EnumValue.name: ReminderFutureDay

EnumValue._: 3 - Send emails to patients with their credentials to the Patient Portal.
EnumValue.name: PatientPortalInvite

EnumValue._: 4 - Defines rules for when Schedule Verify ("Thank You"s) should be sent out.
EnumValue.name: ScheduleThankYou

EnumValue._: 5 - Defines rules for when Arrival instructions should be sent out.
EnumValue.name: Arrival

EnumValue._: 6 - Birthday. Defines rule for sending out automated birthday emails.
EnumValue.name: Birthday

EnumValue._:
7 - General Message. Defines rules for sending out automated messages after an appointment is set complete.
            TSPrior will always be negative (occurs in future) for GeneralMessage. Allowing GM before appointment is prohibited since it would simply be duplicating eReminder behavior.
EnumValue.name: GeneralMessage

EnumValue._: 8 - WebSchedRecall. (Note, not yet used in db, but could be if we ever want clinic specific templates) Defines rules for sending out automated messages for Recalls.
EnumValue.name: WebSchedRecall

EnumValue._: 9 - NewPatientThankYou, Thank you for New Patient which is able to send with a new patient web form URL.
EnumValue.name: NewPatientThankYou

EnumValue._: 10 - PaymentPortal Msg-To-Pay, used to send msg-to-pay messages to patients. Currently not an AutoComm feature but doing this now so functionality can be easier added in the future.
EnumValue.name: PayPortalMsgToPay

column.name: TSPrior
column.summary:
Positive value indicates time BEFORE appointment that this rule should be sent.
            Negative value indicates time AFTER appointment that this rule should be sent.
            GeneralMessage: will always be negative (occurs in future). Allowing GM before appointment is prohibited since it would simply be duplicating eReminder behavior.

column.summary: Comma Delimited List of comm types. Enum values of ApptComm.CommType. 0=pref,1=sms,2=email; Like the deprecated pref "ApptReminderSendOrder"

EnumValue._: 1 - Html. Basic html email which uses the master template supplied by OD. Template includes header, styles, and the opening body tag. The user only needs to provide the body itself, which can inclcude tags that get automatically replaced.
EnumValue.name: Html

EnumValue._: 2 - More advanced html that does not include the master template. User must provide everything.

2 - EConnector has sent this to HQ and will remain in this status until it is either terminated or receives a response from the 
            patient.

EnumValue._: 3 - The patient viewed the portal and took no action.

EnumValue._: 4 - The patient viewed the portal but the slot was no longer available.

EnumValue._: 5 - The patient accepted the appointment and the appointment was successfully moved.

EnumValue._: 6 - The patient accepted the appointment but the appointment was not successfully moved.

EnumValue._: 11 - The patient declined and requested that we do not continue contacting them for this appointment.
EnumValue.name: DeclinedStopComm

table.name: benefit
table.summary:
Corresponds to the benefit table in the database which replaces the old covpat table.  A benefit is usually a percentage, deductible, 
            limitation, max, or similar. Each row represents a single benefit.  A benefit can have a value in EITHER PlanNum OR PatPlanNum.  If it is for a 
            PlanNum, the most common, then the benefit is attached to an insurance plan.  If it is for a PatPlanNum, then it overrides the plan benefit, 
            usually a percentage, for a single patient.  Benefits we can't handle yet include posterior composites, COB duplication, amounts used, in/out 
            of plan network, authorization required, missing tooth exclusion, and any date related limitations like waiting periods.Here are examples of typical usage which parallel X12 usage.Example fields shown in this order:CovCat, ProcCode(- indicates blank), BenefitType, Percent, MonetaryAmt, TimePeriod, QuantityQualifier, Quantity, CoverageLevelAnnual Max Indiv $1000: None/General,-,Limitations,-1,1000,CalendarYear,None,0,IndividualRestorative 80%: Restorative,-,CoInsurance,80,-1,CalendarYear,None,0,None$50 deductible: None/General,-,Deductible,-1,50,CalendarYear,None,0,IndividualDeductible waived on preventive: Preventive,-,Deductible,-1,0,CalendarYear,None,0,Individual1 pano every 5 years: None,D0330,Limitations,-1,-1,Years?,Years,5,None2 exams per year: Preventive(or Diagnostic),-,Limitations,-1,-1,BenefitYear,NumberOfServices,2,NoneFluoride limit 18yo: None, D1204, Limitations, -1, -1, CalendarYear/None, AgeLimit, 18,None (might require a second identical entry for D1205)4BW every 6 months: None, D0274, Limitations, -1, -1, None, Months, 6,None.The text above might be difficult to read.  We are trying to improve the white spacing.

column.name: BenefitNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.  Most benefits should be attached using PlanNum.  The exception would be if each patient has a different percentage.  If PlanNum is used, then PatPlanNum should be 0.

column.name: PatPlanNum
column.summary: FK to patplan.PatPlanNum.  It is rare to attach benefits this way.  Usually only used to override percentages for patients.   In this case, PlanNum should be 0.

column.name: CovCatNum
column.summary: FK to covcat.CovCatNum.  Corresponds to X12 EB03- Service Type code.  Situational, so it can be 0.  Will probably be 0 for general deductible and annual max.  There are very specific categories covered by X12. Users should set their InsCovCats to the defaults we provide.

column.name: BenefitType
column.summary: Enum:InsBenefitType Corresponds to X12 EB01. Examples: 0=ActiveCoverage, 1=CoInsurance, 2=Deductible, 3=CoPayment, 4=Exclusions, 5=Limitations. ActiveCoverage doesn't really provide meaningful information.

Enumeration.name: InsBenefitType
Enumeration.summary: Used in the benefit table.  Corresponds to X12 EB01.

EnumValue._: 0- Informational only. Not usually used.  Would only be used if you are just indicating that the patient is covered, but without any specifics.
EnumValue.name: ActiveCoverage

EnumValue._: 1- Used for percentages to indicate portion that insurance will cover.  When interpreting electronic benefit information, this is the opposite percentage, the percentage that the patient will pay after deductible.
EnumValue.name: CoInsurance

EnumValue._: 2- The deductible amount.  Might be two entries if, for instance, deductible is waived on preventive.
EnumValue.name: Deductible

EnumValue._: 3- Informational only. A dollar amount.
EnumValue.name: CoPayment

EnumValue._: 4- Services that are simply not covered at all.
EnumValue.name: Exclusions

EnumValue._: 5- Covers a variety of limitations, including Max, frequency, fee reductions, etc.
EnumValue.name: Limitations

EnumValue._: 6- Sets a period of time after the effective date where a benefit will not be used.
EnumValue.name: WaitingPeriod

column.name: Percent
column.summary: Only used if BenefitType=CoInsurance.  Valid values are 0 to 100.  -1 indicates empty, which is almost always true if not CoInsurance.  The percentage that insurance will pay on the procedure.  Note that benefits coming from carriers are usually backwards, indicating the percentage that the patient is responsible for.

column.name: MonetaryAmt
column.summary: Used for CoPayment, Limitations, and Deductible.  -1 indicates empty

column.name: TimePeriod
column.summary: Enum:BenefitTimePeriod Corresponds to X12 EB06, Time Period Qualifier.  Examples: 0=None,1=ServiceYear,2=CalendarYear,3=Lifetime,4=Years. Might add Visit and Remaining.

Enumeration.name: BenefitTimePeriod
Enumeration.summary: Used in the benefit table.  Corresponds to X12 EB06.

EnumValue._: 0- A timeperiod is frequenly not needed.  For example, percentages.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1- The renewal month is not Jan.  In this case, we need to know the effective date so that we know which month the benefits start over in.
EnumValue.name: ServiceYear

EnumValue._: 2- Renewal month is Jan.
EnumValue.name: CalendarYear

EnumValue._: 3- Usually used for ortho max.
EnumValue.name: Lifetime

EnumValue._: 4- Wouldn't be used alone.  Years would again be specified in the quantity field along with a number.
EnumValue.name: Years

EnumValue._: 5- # in last 12 months.  Does not care about when benefit year begins. Looks at previous 12 months.
EnumValue.name: NumberInLast12Months

column.name: QuantityQualifier
column.summary: Enum:BenefitQuantity Corresponds to X12 EB09. Not used very much. Examples: 0=None,1=NumberOfServices,2=AgeLimit,3=Visits,4=Years,5=Months

Enumeration.name: BenefitQuantity
Enumeration.summary: Used in the benefit table in conjunction with an integer quantity.

EnumValue._: 0- This is used a lot. Most benefits do not need any sort of quantity.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1- For example, two exams per year
EnumValue.name: NumberOfServices

EnumValue._: 2- For example, 18 when fluoride only covered to 18 y.o.
EnumValue.name: AgeLimit

EnumValue._: 3- For example, copay per 1 visit.
EnumValue.name: Visits

EnumValue._: 4- For example, pano every 5 years.
EnumValue.name: Years

EnumValue._: 5- For example, BWs every 6 months.
EnumValue.name: Months

column.name: Quantity
column.summary: Corresponds to X12 EB10. Qualify the quantity using QuantityQualifier.

column.name: CodeNum
column.summary: FK to procedurecode.CodeNum.  Typical uses include fluoride, sealants, etc.  If a specific code is used here, then the CovCat should be None.

column.name: CoverageLevel
column.summary: Enum:BenefitCoverageLevel Corresponds to X12 EB02.  None, Individual, or Family.  Individual and Family are commonly used for deductibles and maximums.  None is commonly used for percentages and copays.

Enumeration.name: BenefitCoverageLevel
Enumeration.summary: Used in the benefit table.

EnumValue._: 0- Since this is a situational X12 field, we can also have none.  Typical for percentages and copayments.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1- The default for deductibles and maximums.
EnumValue.name: Individual

EnumValue._: 2- For example, family deductible or family maximum.
EnumValue.name: Family

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed.

column.name: CodeGroupNum

column.name: TreatArea
column.summary: Enum:TreatmentArea . Only for frequency limitations, ignored for all other benefits. Enforced by the UI. Example 3 fillings per year [mouth]. Tests: 140 through 147. 0 means default.

EnumValue._: 0-goes on claims as blank.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 3-goes on claims as 00.
EnumValue.name: Mouth

Enum:NoSendElectType 0 - send electronically, 1 - don't send electronically, 2 - don't send non-primary (secondary,tertiary, etc.)

EnumValue.name: SendElect

EnumValue._: 1 - Do not send electronically for this carrier.

column.name: IsCDA
column.summary: Canada: True if a CDAnet carrier.  This has significant implications:  1. It can be filtered for in the list of carriers.  2. An ElectID is required.  3. The ElectID can never be used by another carrier.  4. If the carrier is attached to any etrans, then the ElectID cannot be changed (and, of course, the carrier cannot be deleted or combined).

column.name: CDAnetVersion

1=No Encryption, 2=CDAnet standard #1, 3=CDAnet standard #2.  Field A10.

Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program

Tax ID Number.  Not user editable.  No UI for this field.
            Used in when importing insurance plans from 834s to uniquely identify carriers.

column.name: ApptTextBackColor

column.name: IsCoinsuranceInverted
column.summary:
False by default.  Determines if the carrier supplied EB08 field of 271 transactions should be inverted for coinsurance percentages.
            When true carriers sent us insurance percentage so we do not need to invert it, it is already inverted for us.

column.name: TrustedEtransFlags
column.summary: Bit flags.  None (0) by default.  Stores trusted user selected X12 transaction types related to this carrier.

column.name: CobInsPaidBehaviorOverride
column.summary: Enum:EclaimCobInsPaidBehavior .  When sending X12 5010 eclaims, if not set to Default, then this setting overrides the ClaimCobInsPaidBehavior preference.

EnumValue._: Use the global preference value instead of the carrier override.
EnumValue.name: Default

EnumValue._: Only send COB eclaim data claim totals.
EnumValue.name: ClaimLevel

EnumValue._: Only send COD eclaim data respective procedure amounts.
EnumValue.name: ProcedureLevel

EnumValue._: Send COB eclaim data claim totals and respective procedure amounts.
EnumValue.name: Both

column.name: EraAutomationOverride
column.summary: Enum:EraAutomationMode UseGlobal (0) by default. Determines the level of ERA processing automation for this carrier. This will override the EraAutomationBehavior preference when not set to UseGlobal.

Enumeration.name: EraAutomationMode
Enumeration.summary: The level of automation for ERA processing. This setting can be overriden for individual Carriers.

EnumValue._: 0 - Never used for the EraAutomationBehavior preference. Only used for Carrier.EraAutomationOverride to indicate that the carrier uses the EraAutomationBehavior preference instead of an override.
EnumValue.name: UseGlobal

EnumValue._: 1 - ERAs are manually processed.
EnumValue.name: ReviewAll

EnumValue._: 2 - Allows ERAs to be processed with a single button click.
EnumValue.name: SemiAutomatic

EnumValue._: 3 - When ERAs are imported, they are fully processed without any input from a user.
EnumValue.name: FullyAutomatic

column.name: OrthoInsPayConsolidate
column.summary: Enum:EnumOrthoInsPayConsolidate Global (0) by default. Determines how this carrier requires payments made to ortho claims made by the Auto Ortho Tool. This will override the OrthoInsPayConsolidated preference when not set to Global.

EnumValue._: Uses the preference value of OrthoInsPayConsolidated.
EnumValue.name: Global

EnumValue._: Overrides the preference value of OrthoInsPayConsolidated and blocks users from entering payments on claims created by the Auto Ortho Tool.
EnumValue.name: ForceConsolidateOn

EnumValue._: Overrides the preference value of OrthoInsPayConsolidated and allows users to enter payments on claims created by the Auto Ortho Tool.
EnumValue.name: ForceConsolidateOff

table.name: centralconnection
table.summary: Used by the Central Manager.  Stores the information needed to establish a connection to a remote database.

column.name: CentralConnectionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ServerName
column.summary: If direct db connection.  Can be ip address.

column.name: DatabaseName
column.summary: If direct db connection.

column.name: MySqlUser
column.summary: If direct db connection.

column.name: MySqlPassword
column.summary: If direct db connection.  Symmetrically encrypted.

column.name: ServiceURI
column.summary: If connecting to the web service. Can be on VPN, or can be over https.

column.name: OdUser
column.summary: Deprecated.  If connecting to the web service.

column.name: OdPassword
column.summary: Deprecated.  If connecting to the web service.  Symmetrically encrypted.

column.name: Note

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: 0-based.

column.name: WebServiceIsEcw
column.summary: If set to true, the password hash is calculated differently.

column.name: ConnectionStatus
column.summary: Contains the most recent information about this connection.  OK if no problems, version information if version mismatch, nothing for not checked, and OFFLINE if previously couldn't connect.

column.name: HasClinicBreakdownReports
column.summary: If set to True, display clinic breakdown in reports, else only show practice totals.

table.name: cert

column.name: WikiPageLink

column.name: ItemOrder

column.name: IsHidden

column.name: CertCategoryNum

column.name: EmployeeNum

column.name: Note
column.summary: Rarely, a very short note is required.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. The user who made this entry.  Usually some sort of supervisor.

table.name: claim
table.summary: The claim table holds information about individual claims.  Each row represents one claim.

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Must always match claimProc.PatNum

column.name: DateService
column.summary: Usually the same date as the procedures, but it can be changed if you wish.

column.name: DateSent
column.summary: Usually the date it was created.  It might be sent a few days later if you don't send your e-claims every day.

column.name: ClaimStatus
column.summary: Single char: U,H,W,P,S,R, or I.  U=Unsent, H=Hold until pri received, W=Waiting in queue, S=Sent, R=Received, I=Hold for In Process.  A(adj) is no longer used.  P(prob sent) is no longer used.

column.name: DateReceived
column.summary: Date the claim was received.

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.  Every claim is attached to one plan.

column.name: ProvTreat
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  Treating provider for dental claims.  For institutional claims, this is called the attending provider.

column.name: ClaimFee
column.summary: Total fee of claim.

column.name: InsPayEst
column.summary: Amount insurance is estimated to pay on this claim.

column.name: InsPayAmt
column.summary: Amount insurance actually paid.

column.name: DedApplied
column.summary: Deductible applied to this claim.

column.name: PreAuthString
column.summary: The predetermination of benefits number received from ins.  In X12, REF G3.

column.name: IsProsthesis
column.summary: Single char for No, Initial, or Replacement.

column.name: PriorDate
column.summary: Date prior prosthesis was placed.  Note that this is only for paper claims.  E-claims have a date field on each individual procedure.

column.name: ReasonUnderPaid
column.summary: Note for patient for why insurance didn't pay as expected.

column.name: ClaimNote
column.summary: Note to be sent to insurance. Max 400 char.  E-claims also have notes on each procedure.

column.name: ClaimType
column.summary: "P"=primary, "S"=secondary, "PreAuth"=preauth, "Other"=other, "Cap"=capitation.  Not allowed to be blank. Might need to add "Med"=medical claim.

column.name: ProvBill
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  Billing provider.  Assignment can be automated from the setup section.

column.name: ReferringProv
column.summary: FK to referral.ReferralNum.

column.name: RefNumString
column.summary: Referral number for this claim.

column.name: PlaceService
column.summary: Enum:PlaceOfService .

EnumValue._: 0. Code 11
EnumValue.name: Office

EnumValue._: 1. Code 12
EnumValue.name: PatientsHome

EnumValue._: 2. Code 21
EnumValue.name: InpatHospital

EnumValue._: 3. Code 22
EnumValue.name: OutpatHospital

EnumValue._: 4. Code 31
EnumValue.name: SkilledNursFac

EnumValue._: 5. Code 33.  In X12, a similar code AdultLivCareFac 35 is mentioned.
EnumValue.name: CustodialCareFacility

EnumValue._: 6. Code 99.  We use 11 for office.
EnumValue.name: OtherLocation

EnumValue._: 7. Code 15
EnumValue.name: MobileUnit

EnumValue._: 8. Code 03
EnumValue.name: School

EnumValue._: 9. Code 26
EnumValue.name: MilitaryTreatFac

EnumValue._: 10. Code 50
EnumValue.name: FederalHealthCenter

EnumValue._: 11. Code 71
EnumValue.name: PublicHealthClinic

EnumValue._: 12. Code 72
EnumValue.name: RuralHealthClinic

EnumValue._: 13. Code 23
EnumValue.name: EmergencyRoomHospital

EnumValue._: 14. Code 24
EnumValue.name: AmbulatorySurgicalCenter

EnumValue._: 15. Code 02.
EnumValue.name: TelehealthOutsideHome

EnumValue._: 16. Code 10
EnumValue.name: TelehealthInHome

EnumValue._: 17. Code 27
EnumValue.name: OutreachSiteOrStreet

column.name: AccidentRelated
column.summary: blank or A=Auto, E=Employment, O=Other.

column.name: AccidentDate
column.summary: Date of accident, if applicable.  Canada only.

column.name: AccidentST
column.summary: Accident state.

column.name: EmployRelated
column.summary: Enum:YN .

Enumeration.name: YN
Enumeration.summary: 0=Unknown,1=Yes, or 2=No. UI can be tricky. Some success with a 3x1 listbox, multicolumn, see FormPatientEdit. Radiobuttons are also an option.  You can also use another YN variable to store unknown, and then use a click event on the checkbox to change to Y or N.  You can also use a three state checkbox if you translate properly between the enums.

column.name: IsOrtho
column.summary: True if is ortho.

column.name: OrthoRemainM
column.summary: Remaining months of ortho.  Valid values are 1-36, although we allow greater than or equal to 0.

column.name: OrthoDate
column.summary: Date ortho appliance placed.

column.name: PatRelat
column.summary: Enum:Relat  Relationship to subscriber.  The relationship is copied from InsPlan when the claim is created.  It might need to be changed in both places.

Enumeration.name: Relat
Enumeration.summary: Relationship to subscriber for insurance.

column.name: PlanNum2
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.  Other coverage plan number.  0 if none.  This provides the user with total control over what other coverage shows. This obviously limits the coverage on a single claim to two insurance companies.

column.name: PatRelat2
column.summary: Enum:Relat  The relationship to the subscriber for other coverage on this claim.

Enumeration.name: Relat
Enumeration.summary: Relationship to subscriber for insurance.

column.name: WriteOff
column.summary: Sum of ClaimProc.Writeoff for this claim.

column.name: Radiographs
column.summary: The number of x-rays enclosed.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  0 if no clinic.  Since one claim cannot have procs from multiple clinics, the clinicNum is set when creating the claim and then cannot be changed.  The claim would have to be deleted and recreated.  Otherwise, if changing at the claim level, a feature would have to be added that synched all procs, claimprocs, and probably some other tables.

column.name: ClaimForm
column.summary: FK to claimform.ClaimFormNum.  0 if not assigned to use the claimform for the insplan.

column.name: AttachedImages
column.summary: The number of intraoral images attached.  Not the number of files attached.  This is the value that goes on the 2006 claimform.

column.name: AttachedModels
column.summary: The number of models attached.

column.name: AttachedFlags
column.summary: A comma-delimited set of flag keywords.  Can have one or more of the following: EoB,Note,Perio,Misc,Unsup.  Must also contain one of these: Mail or Elect. Additionally, the Submitted flag is for determining when DentalXChange attachment was submitted.

column.name: AttachmentID
column.summary: Example: NEA#1234567 or dxc123456789.  If present, and if the claim note does not already start with this Id, then it will be prepended to the claim note for both e-claims and mail.  If using e-claims, this same ID will be used for all PWK segements.

column.name: CanadianMaterialsForwarded
column.summary: A08.  Any combination of E(email), C(correspondence), M(models), X(x-rays), and I(images).  So up to 5 char.  Gets converted to a single char A-Z for e-claims.

column.name: CanadianReferralProviderNum
column.summary: B05.  Optional. The 9-digit CDA number of the referring provider, or identifier of referring party up to 10 characters in length.

column.name: CanadianReferralReason
column.summary: B06.  A number 0(none) through 13.

column.name: CanadianIsInitialLower
column.summary: F18.  Y, N, or X(not a lower denture, crown, or bridge).

column.name: CanadianDateInitialLower
column.summary: F19.  Mandatory if F18 is N.

column.name: CanadianMandProsthMaterial
column.summary: F21.  If crown, not required.  If denture or bridge, required if F18 is N.  Single digit number code, 0-6.  We added type 7, which is crown.

column.name: CanadianIsInitialUpper
column.summary: F15.  Y, N, or X(not an upper denture, crown, or bridge).

column.name: CanadianDateInitialUpper
column.summary: F04.  Mandatory if F15 is N.

column.name: CanadianMaxProsthMaterial
column.summary: F20.  If crown, not required.  If denture or bridge, required if F15 is N.  0 indicates empty response.  Single digit number code, 1-6.  We added type 7, which is crown.

column.name: InsSubNum
column.summary: FK to inssub.InsSubNum.

column.name: InsSubNum2
column.summary:
FK to inssub.InsSubNum.  The fk to the 'Other' insurance subscriber.  For a primary claim, this will be the secondary insurance
            subscriber.  For a secondary claim, this will be primary insurance subscriber.

column.name: CanadaTransRefNum
column.summary:
G01 assigned by carrier/network and returned in acks.  Used for claim reversal.  For Claim Acknowledgements, this can sometimes
            be a series of spaces, which means the number is effectively empty.  This happens when the Claim Acknowledgement is forwarded to the carrier
            as part of a batch.

column.name: CanadaEstTreatStartDate
column.summary: F37 Used for predeterminations.

column.name: CanadaInitialPayment
column.summary: F28 Used for predeterminations.

column.name: CanadaPaymentMode
column.summary: F29 Used for predeterminations.

column.name: CanadaTreatDuration
column.summary: F30 Used for predeterminations.

column.name: CanadaNumAnticipatedPayments
column.summary: F31 Used for predeterminations.

column.name: CanadaAnticipatedPayAmount
column.summary: F32 Used for predeterminations.

column.name: PriorAuthorizationNumber
column.summary: This is NOT the predetermination of benefits number.  In X12, this is REF G1.

column.name: SpecialProgramCode
column.summary: Enum:EnumClaimSpecialProgram  This is used to track EPSDT.

Enumeration.name: EnumClaimSpecialProgram
Enumeration.summary: 0=none, 1=EPSDT_1, 2=Handicapped_2, 3=SpecialFederal_3, (no 4), 5=Disability_5, 9=SecondOpinion_9

column.name: UniformBillType
column.summary: A three digit number used on 837I.  Aka Bill Code.  UBO4 4.  Examples: 321,823,131,652.  The third digit is claim frequency code.  If this is used, then our CorrectionType should be 0=original.

column.name: MedType
column.summary: Enum:EnumClaimMedType 0=Dental, 1=Medical, 2=Institutional

column.name: AdmissionTypeCode
column.summary: Used for inst claims. Single digit.  X12 2300 CL101.  UB04 14.  Should only be required for IP, but X12 clearly states required for all.

column.name: AdmissionSourceCode
column.summary: Used for inst claims. Single char.  X12 2300 CL102.  UB04 15.  Should only be required for IP, but X12 clearly states required for all.

column.name: PatientStatusCode
column.summary: Used for inst claims. Two digit.  X12 2300 CL103.  UB04 17.  Should only be required for IP, but X12 clearly states required for all.

column.name: CustomTracking
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. Most users will leave this blank.  Some offices may set up tracking statuses such as 'review', 'hold', 'riskmanage', etc.

column.name: DateResent
column.summary: Used for historical purposes only, not sent electronically. Automatically set when CorrectionType is original and the claim is resent.

column.name: CorrectionType
column.summary: Enum:ClaimCorrectionType X12 CLM05-3. Usually set to original, but can be used to resubmit claims.  Also used in 1500 Medical Claim Form field 22.

EnumValue._: 0 - X12 1. Use for claims that are not ongoing.
EnumValue.name: Original

EnumValue._: 1 - X12 7. Use to entirely replace an original claim. A claim reference number will be required.
EnumValue.name: Replacement

EnumValue._: 2 - X12 8. Use to undo an original claim. A claim reference number will be required.
EnumValue.name: Void

column.name: ClaimIdentifier
column.summary:
X12 CLM01. Semi-unique identifier for the claim within the current database. Defaults to PatNum/ClaimNum, but can be edited by user, and is often modified by the clearinghouse to ensure uniqueness on their end.
            This also set for PreAuth claims.  The ClaimIdentifier for a PreAuth will probably not match the ClaimIdentifier for a regular claim, which makes ERA claim matching more straight forward for both PreAuths and regular claims.

column.name: OrigRefNum
column.summary: X12 2300 REF (F8). Used when resending claims to refer to the original claim. The user must type this value in after reading it from the original claim response report.

column.name: ProvOrderOverride
column.summary:
FK to provider.ProvNum.  Ordering provider override.  Goes hand-in-hand with OrderingReferralNum.  Medical eclaims only.
            Defaults to zero.

column.name: OrthoTotalM
column.summary: Total estimated months of ortho.  Valid values are 1-36, although we allow greater than or equal to 0.

column.name: ShareOfCost
column.summary:
Sum of all amounts paid specifically to this claim by the patient or family.
            Goes out in X12 4010/5010 loop 2300 AMT segment if greater than zero.  Default value is 0, thus will not go out by default unless the user
            enters a value.  This field was added for Denti-Cal certification, but can go out for any clearinghouse.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: OrderingReferralNum
column.summary:
FK to referral.ReferralNum.  Goes hand-in-hand with ProvOrderOverride.  Medical eclaims only.  Defaults to zero.
            If set, and the ProvOrderOverride is not set, then this referral will go out at the ordering provider on medical e-claims.

column.name: DateSentOrig
column.summary: The original date the claim was sent.

column.name: DateIllnessInjuryPreg
column.summary:
Date of Current Illness, Injury, or Pregnancy (LMP).  (LMP = Last Menstrual Period)  For use in 1500 Medical Claim Form box 14.
            Identifies the first date of onset of illness, the actual date of injury, or the LMP for pregnancy.

column.name: DateIllnessInjuryPregQualifier
column.summary: Enum:DateIllnessInjuryPregQualifier 3 digit code used in 1500 Medical Claim Form, 'Qual' box of field 14.  Valid values are 431 or 484.

Enumeration.name: DateIllnessInjuryPregQualifier
Enumeration.summary: Non-standard enum numbering.

EnumValue._: 0 - None
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 431 - Onset of Current Symptoms or Illness
EnumValue.name: OnsetCurSymptoms

EnumValue._: 484 - Last Menstrual Period
EnumValue.name: LastMenstrualPeriod

column.name: DateOther
column.summary: Another date related to the patient's condition or treatment.  For use in 1500 Medical Claim Form box 15.

column.name: DateOtherQualifier
column.summary:
Enum:DateOtherQualifier 3 digit code used in 1500 Medical Claim Form, 'Qual' box of field 15.  Valid values are 090, 091, 304, 439, 444,
            453,454, 455, and 471.

Enumeration.name: DateOtherQualifier
Enumeration.summary: Used for 1500 Medical Claim Form, 'Qual' box portion of fields 14 and 15.  Populate with 3 digit enum value. Non-standard enum numbering.

EnumValue._: 0 - None
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 090 - Report Start
EnumValue.name: ReportStart

EnumValue._: 091 - Report End
EnumValue.name: ReportEnd

EnumValue._: 304 - Latest Visit or Consultation
EnumValue.name: LatestVisitConsult

EnumValue._: 439 - Accident
EnumValue.name: Accident

EnumValue._: 444 - First Visit or Consultation
EnumValue.name: FirstVisitConsult

EnumValue._: 453 - Acute Manifestation of a Chronic Condition
EnumValue.name: ChronicCondManifest

EnumValue._: 454 - Initial Treatment
EnumValue.name: InitialTreatment

EnumValue._: 455 - Last X-ray
EnumValue.name: LastXray

EnumValue._: 471 - Prescription
EnumValue.name: Prescription

column.name: IsOutsideLab
column.summary: Used in 1500 Medical Claim Form field 20.  Place an 'X' the 'Yes' if true and the 'No' if false.

column.name: SecurityHash
column.summary: Holds the salted hash of the following claim fields: ClaimFee, ClaimStatus, InsPayEst, InsPayAmt.

column.name: Narrative
column.summary: A note that pertains to all attachments on the claim. Currently only applies to DentalXChange, but could be expanded. 2000 character limit put in place by DentalXChange.

table.name: claimattach
table.summary: Keeps track of one image file attached to a claim.  Multiple files can be attached to a claim using this method. DentalXChange uses the term "attachment" differently, referring to a group of images.

column.name: ClaimAttachNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: FK to claim.ClaimNum

column.name: DisplayedFileName
column.summary: The name of the file that shows on the claim.  For example: tooth2.jpg.

column.name: ActualFileName
column.summary: The actual file is stored in the A-Z folder in EmailAttachments.  (yes, even though it's not actually an email attachment)  The files are named automatically based on Date/time along with a random number.  This ensures that they will be sequential as well as unique.

column.name: ImageReferenceId
column.summary: This is the image Id that DentalXChange gives back after calling addImage(). Storing this will allow users to delete images they sent to DentalXChange.

table.name: claimcondcodelog
table.summary: There is either one or zero per claim.

column.name: ClaimCondCodeLogNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: FK to claim.ClaimNum.

column.name: Code0

column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 19 on the UB04.

column.name: Code2
