column.name: Code3
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 21 on the UB04.

column.name: Code4
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 22 on the UB04.

column.name: Code5
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 23 on the UB04.

column.name: Code6
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 24 on the UB04.

column.name: Code7
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 25 on the UB04.

column.name: Code8
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 26 on the UB04.

column.name: Code9
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 27 on the UB04.

column.name: Code10
column.summary: Corresponds with condition code 28 on the UB04.

table.name: claimform
table.summary: Stores the information for printing different types of claim forms.  Each claimform has many claimformitems attached to it, one for each field on the claimform.  This table has nothing to do with the actual claims.  It just describes how to print them.

column.name: ClaimFormNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: eg. ADA2002 or CA Medicaid

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: If true, then it will not be displayed in various claim form lists as a choice.

column.name: FontName
column.summary: Valid font name for all text on the form.

column.name: FontSize
column.summary: Font size for all text on the form.

column.name: UniqueID
column.summary: Deprecated as of version 17.2. Internal claimforms have been moved over to XML files in OpenDentBusiness.Properties.Resources.

column.name: PrintImages
column.summary: Set to false to not print images.  This removes the background for printing on premade forms.

column.name: OffsetX
column.summary: Shifts all items by x/100th's of an inch to compensate for printer, typically less than 1/4 inch.

column.name: OffsetY
column.summary: Shifts all items by y/100th's of an inch to compensate for printer, typically less than 1/4 inch.

column.name: Width
column.summary: The width of the claim form.

column.name: Height
column.summary: The height of the claim form.

table.name: claimformitem
table.summary: One item is needed for each field on a claimform.

column.name: ClaimFormItemNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClaimFormNum
column.summary: FK to claimform.ClaimFormNum

column.name: ImageFileName
column.summary: If this item is an image.  Usually only one per claimform.  eg ADA2002.emf.  Otherwise it MUST be left blank, or it will trigger an error that the image cannot be found.

column.name: FieldName
column.summary: Must be one of the hardcoded available fieldnames for claims.

column.name: FormatString
column.summary: For dates, the format string. ie MM/dd/yyyy or M d y among many other possibilities.

column.name: XPos
column.summary: The x position of the item on the claim form.  In pixels. 100 pixels per inch.

column.name: YPos
column.summary: The y position of the item.

column.name: Width
column.summary: Limits the printable area of the item. Set to zero to not limit.

column.name: Height
column.summary: Limits the printable area of the item. Set to zero to not limit.

table.name: claimpayment
table.summary: Each row represents a single check from the insurance company.  The amount may be split between patients using claimprocs.  The amount of the check must always exactly equal the sum of all the claimprocs attached to it.  There might be only one claimproc.

column.name: ClaimPaymentNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: CheckDate
column.summary: Date the check was entered into this system, not the date on the check.

column.name: CheckAmt
column.summary: The amount of the check.

column.name: CheckNum
column.summary: The check number.

column.name: BankBranch
column.summary: Bank and branch.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Note for this check if needed.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  0 if no clinic (unassigned).

column.name: DepositNum
column.summary: FK to deposit.DepositNum.  0 if not attached to any deposits.

column.name: CarrierName
column.summary: Descriptive name of the carrier just for reporting purposes.  We use this because the CarrierNums could conceivably be different for the different claimprocs attached.

column.name: DateIssued
column.summary: Date that the carrier issued the check. Date on the check.

column.name: PayType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  0 if not attached to any definitions

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: PayGroup
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  The payment group for this claim payment.

table.name: claimproc
table.summary: Links procedures to claims.  Also links ins payments to procedures or claims.  Also used for estimating procedures even if no claim yet.  Warning: One proc might be linked twice to a given claim if insurance made two payments.  Many of the important fields are actually optional.  For instance, ProcNum is only required if itemizing ins payment, and ClaimNum is blank if Status=adjustment,cap,or estimate.

column.name: ClaimProcNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ProcNum
column.summary: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum.  Is 0 for payments by total or for Canadian Carrier Issued Procedures.

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: FK to claim.ClaimNum.  Is 0 for estimates, adjustments and capitation (CapClaim and CapComplete).

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Must always match claim.PatNum

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: FeeBilled
column.summary: Fee billed to insurance. Might not be the same as the actual fee.  The fee billed can be different than the actual procedure.  For instance, if you have set the insurance plan to bill insurance using UCR fees, then this field will contain the UCR fee instead of the fee that the patient was charged.

column.name: InsPayEst
column.summary: Actual amount this carrier is expected to pay, after taking everything else into account. Considers annual max, override, percentAmt, copayAmt, deductible, etc. This estimate is computed automatically when sent to ins.

column.name: DedApplied
column.summary: 0 if blank.  Deductible applied to this procedure only. Only for procedures attached to claims.  Otherwise, the DedEst and DedEstOverride are used.

column.name: Status
column.summary: Enum:ClaimProcStatus .  When setting recieved\supplemental must set DateEntry.

Enumeration.name: ClaimProcStatus
Enumeration.summary: Claimproc Status.  The status must generally be the same as the claim, although it is sometimes not strictly enforced.

EnumValue._: 0: For claims that have been created or sent, but have not been received.
EnumValue.name: NotReceived

EnumValue._: 1: For claims that have been received.
EnumValue.name: Received

EnumValue._: 2: For preauthorizations.
EnumValue.name: Preauth

EnumValue._: 3: The only place that this status is used is to make adjustments to benefits from the coverage window.  It is never attached to a claim.
EnumValue.name: Adjustment

EnumValue._: 4:This differs from Received only slightly.  It's for additional payments on procedures already received.  Most fields are blank.
EnumValue.name: Supplemental

EnumValue._: 5: CapClaim is used when you want to send a claim to a capitation insurance company.  These are similar to Supplemental in that there will always be a duplicate claimproc for a procedure. The first claimproc tracks the copay and writeoff, has a status of CapComplete, and is never attached to a claim. The second claimproc has status of CapClaim.
EnumValue.name: CapClaim

EnumValue._: 6: Estimates have replaced the fields that were in the procedure table.  Once a procedure is complete, the claimprocstatus will still be Estimate.  An Estimate can be attached to a claim and status gets changed to NotReceived.
EnumValue.name: Estimate

EnumValue._: 7: For capitation procedures that are complete.  This replaces the old procedurelog.CapCoPay field. This stores the copay and writeoff amounts.  The copay is only there for reference, while it is the writeoff that actually affects the balance. Never attached to a claim. If procedure is TP, then status will be CapEstimate.  Only set to CapComplete if procedure is Complete.
EnumValue.name: CapComplete

EnumValue._: 8: For capitation procedures that are still estimates rather than complete.  When procedure is completed, this can be changed to CapComplete, but never to anything else.
EnumValue.name: CapEstimate

EnumValue._: 9: For InsHist procedures.
EnumValue.name: InsHist

column.name: InsPayAmt
column.summary: Amount insurance actually paid.

column.name: Remarks
column.summary: The remarks that insurance sends in the EOB about procedures.

column.name: ClaimPaymentNum
column.summary: FK to claimpayment.ClaimPaymentNum(the insurance check).

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum

column.name: DateCP
column.summary: This is the date that is used for payment reports and tracks the payment date. Once a payment has been attached, the DateCP will exactly match the date of the ClaimPayment it's attached to. See the note under Ledgers.ComputePayments. This will eventually not be used for aging. The ProcDate will instead be used. See ProcDate.

column.name: WriteOff
column.summary: Amount not covered by ins which is written off.  The writeoff estimate goes in a different column.  This is filled with the WriteOffEst value when a claim is created.

column.name: CodeSent
column.summary: The procedure code that was sent to insurance. This is not necessarily the usual procedure code.  It will already have been trimmed to 5 char if it started with "D", or it could be the alternate code.  Not allowed to be blank if it is procedure.

column.name: AllowedOverride
column.summary: The allowed fee (not the override) is a complex calculation which is performed on the fly in Procedure.ComputeEstimates/ClaimProc.ComputeBaseEst.  It is the amount that the percentage is based on.  If this carrier has a lower UCR than the office, then the allowed fee is where that is handled.  It can be pulled from an allowed fee schedule.  It is also where substitutions for posterior composites are handled.  The AllowedOverride allows the user to override the calculation.  -1 indicates blank.  A new use of this field is for when entering insurance payments.  On the eob, it will tell you what the allowed/UCR fee is.  The user will now be able to enter this information into the AllowedOverride field.  They will simultaneously pass the info to the allowed fee schedule.  AllowedOverride is never changed automatically by the program except to sometimes set it to -1 if NoBillIns.

column.name: Percentage
column.summary: -1 if blank.  Otherwise a number between 0 and 100.  The percentage that insurance pays on this procedure, as determined from insurance categories. Not user editable.

column.name: PercentOverride
column.summary: -1 if blank.  Otherwise a number between 0 and 100.  Can only be changed by user.

column.name: CopayAmt
column.summary: -1 if blank. Calculated automatically. User cannot edit but can use CopayOverride instead.  Opposite of InsEst, because this is the patient portion estimate.  Two different uses: 1. For capitation, this automates calculation of writeoff. 2. For any other insurance, it gets deducted during calculation as shown in the edit window. Neither use directly affects patient balance.

column.name: NoBillIns
column.summary: Set to true to not bill to this insurance plan.

column.name: PaidOtherIns
column.summary: -1 if blank. The amount paid or estimated to be paid by another insurance.  This amount is then subtracted from what the current insurance would pay.  When running the calculation and considering other claimprocs, it will ignore any patPlan with a higher ordinal.  So, always blank for primary claims.  User cannot edit, but can use PaidOtherInsOverride.

column.name: BaseEst
column.summary: Always has a value. Used in TP, etc. The base estimate is the ((fee or allowedOverride)-Copay) x (percentage or percentOverride). Does not include all the extras like ded, annualMax,and paidOtherIns that InsEstTotal holds.  BaseEst cannot be overridden by the user.  Instead, the following fields can be manipulated: allowedOverride, CopayOverride, PercentOverride.

column.name: CopayOverride
column.summary: -1 if blank.  See description of CopayAmt.  This lets the user set a copay that will never be overwritten by automatic calculations.

column.name: ProcDate
column.summary: Date of the procedure.  Displayed in Edit Claim window procedures grid.  Currently only used for tracking annual insurance benefits remaining. Important in Adjustments to benefits.  For total claim payments, MUST be the date of the procedures to correctly figure benefits.  Will eventually transition to use this field to actually calculate aging.  See the note under Ledgers.ComputePayments.

column.name: DateEntry
column.summary: Date that it was changed to status received or supplemental.  It is usually attached to a claimPayment at that point, but not if user forgets.  This is still the date that it becomes important financial data.  Only applies if Received or Supplemental.  Otherwise, the date is disregarded.  User may never edit. Important in audit trail.

column.name: LineNumber
column.summary:
Assigned when claim is created as a way to order the procs showing on a claim.  Indireclty goes out in X12 loop 2400.
            Used in Canadian eclaims (field F07). One based index. Is zero for total payments.

column.name: DedEst
column.summary: -1 if blank.  Not sure why we need to allow -1.  Calculated automatically.  User cannot edit, but can use DedEstOverride instead.

column.name: DedEstOverride
column.summary: -1 if blank.  Overrides the DedEst value.

column.name: InsEstTotal
column.summary: Always has a value.  BaseEst-(DedEst or DedEstOverride)-PaidOtherIns-OverAnnualMax.  User cannot edit, but can instead use InsEstTotalOverride. Recalculated each time TP is viewed, is saved as the value calculated for the last viewed TP. This variable should probably just be moved to memory and removed as a DB field.

column.name: InsEstTotalOverride
column.summary: -1 if blank.  Overrides the InsEstTotal value.

column.name: PaidOtherInsOverride
column.summary: -1 if blank.  Overrides the PaidOtherIns value.

column.name: EstimateNote
column.summary: An automatically generated note that displays information about over max, exclusions, and other limitations for which there are no fields.  Only applies to estimate.  Once it's attached to a claim, similar information can go in the remarks field.

column.name: WriteOffEst
column.summary: -1 if blank.  The estimated writeoff as calculated by OD.  Usually only used for PPOs.

column.name: WriteOffEstOverride
column.summary: -1 if blank.  Overrides WriteOffEst.  Usually only used for PPOs.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  Can be zero.  No user interface for editing.  Forced to always be the same as the procedure, or if no procedure, then the claim.

column.name: InsSubNum
column.summary: FK to inssub.InsSubNum.

column.name: PaymentRow
column.summary: 1-indexed.  Allows user to sort the order of payments on an EOB.  All claimprocs for a payment will have the same PaymentRow value.

column.name: PayPlanNum
column.summary: FK to payplan.PayPlanNum.  0 if not attached to a payplan.

column.name: ClaimPaymentTracking
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  Connected to the ClaimPaymentTracking DefCat.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: DateSuppReceived
column.summary:
The date the claim proc was first set to received or supplemental. If status is set to something other than received/supplemental,
            this field will be set to DateTime.MinValue if DateSuppReceived is today.  If DateSuppReceived is set to a day in the past and the status is 
            changed from received/supplemental to something else, the field will not be cleared or updated.  Db only field used by one customer and this is
            how they requested it.  PatNum #19191

column.name: DateInsFinalized
column.summary: Date of the first claimPayment for this claim.

column.name: IsTransfer
column.summary: Bool, defaults to false. When true, indicates that a claimproc is part of a transfer from a claim.

column.name: ClaimAdjReasonCodes
column.summary: Holds a comma delimited list of Claim Adjustment Reason Codes from an ERA. https://x12.org/codes/claim-adjustment-reason-codes

column.name: IsOverpay
column.summary: Bool, defaults to false. When true, Status is NotReceived and indicates that the claimproc represents an insurance overpayment OR underpayment. Example insurance has partially paid on a claim, this procedure has still been paid 0, so it's an underpayment. The status column in the claim edit window will show PndSup.

column.name: SecurityHash
column.summary: Holds the salted hash of the following claimproc fields: ClaimNum, Status, InsPayEst, InsPayAmt.

table.name: claimsnapshot
table.summary: Stores the original insurance writeoff, fee, and expected insurance payment information on claims.

column.name: ClaimSnapshotNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ProcNum
column.summary: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum

column.name: ClaimType
column.summary: "P"=primary, "S"=secondary, "Other"=other, "Cap"=capitation. Never "PreAuth" as PreAuths will never be in this table

column.name: InsPayEst
column.summary: Expected amount the insurance will pay on the procedure.

column.name: Fee
column.summary: Procedure's ProcFee

column.name: DateTEntry
column.summary: The date/time that the snapshot was created.  Not user editable.

column.name: ClaimProcNum
column.summary: FK to claimproc.ClaimProcNum

column.name: SnapshotTrigger
column.summary: Enum:ClaimSnapshotTrigger Stores the trigger to which this ClaimSnapshot was created.

Enumeration.name: ClaimSnapshotTrigger
Enumeration.summary: The Enumeration value for which Claim Snapshot Trigger that will be stored.

column.name: ClaimTrackingNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: FK to claim.ClaimNum

column.name: TrackingType
column.summary: Enum:ClaimTrackingType Identifies the type of claimtracking row.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to user.UserNum

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is inserted or modified.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Generic column for additional info.

column.name: TrackingDefNum
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum for custom tracking when TrackingType=StatusHistory

column.name: TrackingErrorDefNum
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum for custom tracking errors when TrackingType=StatusHistory

table.name: claimvalcodelog
table.summary: Value codes for institutional 'claims'.  Can have up to 12 per claim.

column.name: ClaimValCodeLogNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: FK to claim.ClaimNum.

column.name: ClaimField
column.summary: Descriptive abbreviation to help place field on form (Ex: "FL55" for field 55).

column.name: ValCode
column.summary: Value Code. 2 char.

column.name: ValAmount
column.summary: Value Code Amount.

column.name: Ordinal
column.summary: Order of Value Code

table.name: clearinghouse
table.summary: Since we can send e-claims to multiple clearinghouses, this table keeps track of each clearinghouse.  Will eventually be used for individual carriers as well if they accept

column.name: ClearinghouseNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Description of this clearinghouse

column.name: ExportPath
column.summary: The path to export the X12 file to. \ is now optional.  Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: Payors
column.summary: A list of all payors which should have claims sent to this clearinghouse. Comma delimited with no spaces.  Not necessary if IsDefault.

column.name: Eformat
column.summary: Enum:ElectronicClaimFormat The format of the file that gets sent electronically.

Enumeration.name: ElectronicClaimFormat
Enumeration.summary: For every type of electronic claim format that Open Dental can create, there will be an item in this enumeration.  All e-claim formats are hard coded due to complexity.

EnumValue._: 0-Not in database, but used in various places in program.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1-The American standard through 12/31/11.
EnumValue.name: x837D_4010

EnumValue._: 2-Proprietary format for Renaissance.
EnumValue.name: Renaissance

EnumValue._: 3-CDAnet format version 4.
EnumValue.name: Canadian

EnumValue._: 4-CSV file adaptable for use in Netherlands.
EnumValue.name: Dutch

EnumValue._: 5-The American standard starting on 1/1/12.
EnumValue.name: x837D_5010_dental

EnumValue._: 6-Either professional or medical.  The distiction is stored at the claim level.
EnumValue.name: x837_5010_med_inst

EnumValue._: 7-A specific Canadian carrier located in Quebec which has their own format.
EnumValue.name: Ramq

column.name: ISA05
column.summary: Sender ID Qualifier. Usually ZZ, sometimes 30. Seven other values are allowed as specified in X12 document, but probably never used.

column.name: SenderTIN
column.summary:
Used in ISA06, GS02, 1000A NM1, and 1000A PER.  If blank, then 810624427 is used to indicate Open Dental.
            Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: ISA07
column.summary: Receiver ID Qualifier.  Usually ZZ, sometimes 30. Seven other values are allowed as specified in X12 document, but probably never used.

column.name: ISA08
column.summary: Receiver ID. Also used in GS03. Provided by clearinghouse. Examples: BCBSGA or 0135WCH00(webMD)

column.name: ISA15
column.summary: "P" for Production or "T" for Test.

column.name: Password
column.summary: Password is usually combined with the login ID for user validation.  Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: ResponsePath
column.summary:
The path that all incoming response files will be saved to. \ is now optional.
            Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: CommBridge
column.summary: Enum:EclaimsCommBridge  One of the included hard-coded communications bridges.  Or none to just create the claim files without uploading.

Enumeration.name: EclaimsCommBridge
Enumeration.summary: Each clearinghouse can have a hard-coded comm bridge which handles all the communications of transfering the claim files to the clearinghouse/carrier.  Does not just include X12, but can include any format at all.

EnumValue._: 0-No comm bridge will be activated. The claim files will be created to the specified path, but they will not be uploaded.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 9 Canadian clearinghouse.
EnumValue.name: ITRANS

EnumValue._: 15 Canadian clearinghouse.
EnumValue.name: Claimstream

EnumValue._: 16 UK clearinghouse.
EnumValue.name: NHS

EnumValue._: 21 Canadian clearinghouse.  Similar to ITRANS except supports certificate and carrier list web fetching.
EnumValue.name: ITRANS2

column.name: ClientProgram
column.summary:
If applicable, this is the name of the client program to launch.  It is even used by the hard-coded comm bridges,
            because the user may have changed the installation directory or exe name.  Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: LastBatchNumber
column.summary: Each clearinghouse increments their batch numbers by one each time a claim file is sent.  User never sees this number.  Maxes out at 999, then loops back to 1.  This field must NOT be cached and must be ignored in the code except where it explicitly retrieves it from the db.  Defaults to 0 for brand new clearinghouses, which causes the first batch to go out as #1.

column.name: ModemPort
column.summary: Was not used.  1,2,3,or 4. The port that the modem is connected to if applicable. Always uses 9600 baud and standard settings. Will crash if port or modem not valid.

column.name: LoginID
column.summary:
A clearinghouse usually has a login ID that is used with the password in order to access the remote server.
            This value is not usualy used within the actual claim.  Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: SenderName
column.summary:
Used in 1000A NM1 and 1000A PER.  But if SenderTIN is blank, then OPEN DENTAL SOFTWARE is used instead.
            Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: SenderTelephone
column.summary:
Used in 1000A PER.  But if SenderTIN is blank, then 8776861248 is used instead.  10 digit phone is required by WebMD and is 
            universally assumed, so for now, this must be either blank or 10 digits.  Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: GS03
column.summary: Usually the same as ISA08, but at least one clearinghouse uses a different number here.

column.name: ISA02
column.summary: Authorization information. Almost always blank. Used for Denti-Cal.

column.name: ISA04
column.summary: Security information. Almost always blank. Used for Denti-Cal.

column.name: ISA16
column.summary: X12 component element separator. Two digit hexadecimal string representing an ASCII character or blank. Usually blank, implying 3A which represents ':'. For Denti-Cal, hexadecimal value 22 must be used, corresponding to '"'.

column.name: SeparatorData
column.summary: X12 data element separator. Two digit hexadecimal string representing an ASCII character or blank. Usually blank, implying 2A which represents '*'. For Denti-Cal, hexadecimal value 1D must be used, corresponding to the "group separator" character which has no visual representation.

column.name: SeparatorSegment
column.summary: X12 segment terminator. Two digit hexadecimal string representing an ASCII character or blank. Usually blank, implying 7E which represents '~'. For Denti-Cal, hexadecimal value 1C must be used, corresponding to the "file separator" character which has no visual representation.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  ClinicNum=0 for HQ.

column.name: HqClearinghouseNum
column.summary:
FK to clearinghouse.ClearingHouseNum.  Never 0.  Points to the HQ copy of this clearinghouse.
            If this copy is the HQ copy, then HqClearinghouseNum=ClearinghouseNum.

column.name: IsEraDownloadAllowed
column.summary: Enum:EraBehaviors EraBehaviors.DownloadAndReceive by default.  This flag is implemented individually within each clearinghouse.  Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

Enumeration.name: EraBehaviors
Enumeration.summary: Sets behavior for importing and receiving ERA benefits and associated claims and claim procedures.

EnumValue._: 0 - Do not download ERAs/EOBs
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Download ERAs/EOBs, but do not mark claims and claim procedures as 'Received'.
EnumValue.name: DownloadDoNotReceive

EnumValue._: 2 - Download ERAs/EOBs, and mark claims and claim procedures as 'Received'.
EnumValue.name: DownloadAndReceive

column.name: IsClaimExportAllowed
column.summary: True by default.  This flag is implemented individually within each clearinghouse.  Can be overridden by clinic-level clearinghouses.

column.name: IsAttachmentSendAllowed
column.summary: Currently only used for DentalXChange's attachment service. This indicates that the user has set up the attachment service and would like to use it in Open Dental.

column.name: LocationID
column.summary: A unique identifier provided by the clearinghouse to identify the practice in their system. This is separate from the issue of clinics, where we already enforce only one clinic per clearinghouse. Example: DentalXChange XConnect API key.

table.name: clinic
table.summary: A clinic is usually a separate physical office location.  If multiple clinics are sharing one database, then this is used.  Patients, Operatories, Claims, and many other types of objects can be assigned to a clinic.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: Primary key.  Used in patient,payment,claimpayment,appointment,procedurelog, etc.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Use Abbr for all user-facing forms.  Description is required and should not be blank.

column.name: Address2
column.summary: Second line of address.

column.name: State
column.summary: 2 char in the US.

column.name: Phone
column.summary: Does not include any punctuation.  Exactly 10 digits or blank in USA and Canada.

column.name: BankNumber
column.summary: The account number for deposits.

column.name: DefaultPlaceService
column.summary: Enum:PlaceOfService Usually 0 unless a mobile clinic for instance.

EnumValue._: 0. Code 11
EnumValue.name: Office

EnumValue._: 1. Code 12
EnumValue.name: PatientsHome

EnumValue._: 2. Code 21
EnumValue.name: InpatHospital

EnumValue._: 3. Code 22
EnumValue.name: OutpatHospital

EnumValue._: 4. Code 31
EnumValue.name: SkilledNursFac

EnumValue._: 5. Code 33.  In X12, a similar code AdultLivCareFac 35 is mentioned.
EnumValue.name: CustodialCareFacility

EnumValue._: 6. Code 99.  We use 11 for office.
EnumValue.name: OtherLocation

EnumValue._: 7. Code 15
EnumValue.name: MobileUnit

EnumValue._: 8. Code 03
EnumValue.name: School

EnumValue._: 9. Code 26
EnumValue.name: MilitaryTreatFac

EnumValue._: 10. Code 50
EnumValue.name: FederalHealthCenter

EnumValue._: 11. Code 71
EnumValue.name: PublicHealthClinic

EnumValue._: 12. Code 72
EnumValue.name: RuralHealthClinic

EnumValue._: 13. Code 23
EnumValue.name: EmergencyRoomHospital

EnumValue._: 14. Code 24
EnumValue.name: AmbulatorySurgicalCenter

EnumValue._: 15. Code 02.
EnumValue.name: TelehealthOutsideHome

EnumValue._: 16. Code 10
EnumValue.name: TelehealthInHome

EnumValue._: 17. Code 27
EnumValue.name: OutreachSiteOrStreet

column.name: InsBillingProv
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  0=Default practice provider, -1=Treating provider.

column.name: Fax
column.summary: Does not include any punctuation.  Exactly 10 digits or empty in USA and Canada.

table.name: clockevent
table.summary: One clock-in / clock-out pair.  Or, if the pair is a break, then it's an out/in pair. With normal clock in/out pairs, we want to know how long the employee was working.  It's the opposite with breaks. We want to know how long they were not working, so the pair is backwards. This means that a normal clock in is left incomplete when the clock out for break is created. And once both are finished, the regular in/out will surround the break. Breaks cannot be viewed easily on the same grid as regular clock events for this reason. And since breaks do not affect pay, they should not clutter the normal grid.

column.name: ClockEventNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EmployeeNum
column.summary: FK to employee.EmployeeNum

column.name: TimeEntered1
column.summary: The actual time that this entry was entered.  Cannot be 01-01-0001.

column.name: TimeDisplayed1
column.summary: The time to display and to use in all calculations.  Cannot be 01-01-0001.

column.name: ClockStatus
column.summary: Enum:TimeClockStatus  Home, Lunch, or Break.  The status really only applies to the clock out.  Except the Break status applies to both out and in.

column.name: TimeEntered2
column.summary: The user can never edit this, but the program has to be able to edit this when user clocks out.  Can be 01-01-0001 if not clocked out yet.

column.name: TimeDisplayed2
column.summary: User can edit. Can be 01-01-0001 if not clocked out yet.

column.name: OTimeHours
column.summary: This is a manual override for OTimeAuto.  Typically -1 hour (-01:00:00) to indicate no override.  When used as override, allowed values are zero or positive.  This is an alternative to using a TimeAdjust row.

column.name: OTimeAuto
column.summary: Automatically calculated OT.  Will be zero if none.

column.name: Adjust
column.summary: This is a manual override of AdjustAuto.  Ignored unless AdjustIsOverridden set to true.  When used as override, it's typically negative, although zero and positive are also allowed.

column.name: AdjustAuto
column.summary: Automatically calculated Adjust.  Will be zero if none.

column.name: AdjustIsOverridden
column.summary: True if AdjustAuto is overridden by Adjust.

column.name: Rate2Hours
column.summary: This is a manual override for Rate2Auto.  Typically -1 hour (-01:00:00) to indicate no override.  When used as override, allowed values are zero or positive.  This is the portion of the hours worked which are at Rate2, so it's not in addition to the hours worked.  Also used to calculate the Rate2 OT.

column.name: Rate2Auto
column.summary: Automatically calculated rate2 pay.  Will be zero if none.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  The clinic the ClockEvent was entered at.

column.name: Rate3Hours
column.summary: This is a manual override for Rate3Auto.  Typically -1 hour (-01:00:00) to indicate no override.  When used as override, allowed values are zero or positive.  This is the portion of the hours worked which are at Rate3, so it's not in addition to the hours worked.  Also used to calculate the Rate3 OT.

column.name: Rate3Auto
column.summary: Automatically calculated Rate3 pay.  Will be zero if none.

column.name: IsWorkingHome
column.summary: True if the Clock Event is made by choosing "Available at Home" when clocking in. Will be false if "Available At Office" is selected instead.

column.name: CloudAddressNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: IpAddress
column.summary: The IP address the user is connecting from.

column.name: UserNumLastConnect
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.

column.name: DateTimeLastConnect
column.summary: DateTime of the last successful login from the address.

table.name: codegroup
table.summary: These groups of procedure codes are used in Benefit Frequencies (and Insurance History?). We can't use CovCats because those spans are frequently far too broad.  We often need specific codes. Cached.

column.name: CodeGroupNum

column.name: ProcCodes

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Zero-based. CodeGroups that don't show in either list (IsHidden=true and ShowInAgeLimit=false) get higher ItemOrders so that then are at the bottom of the setup list.

Enumeration.name: EnumCodeGroupFixed
Enumeration.summary: This enum replaces the FrequencyType enum at bottom of Benefits.cs.  See description in CodeGroup.CodeGroupFixed.

EnumValue._: 6- When used in InsHist window, the quadrant is hard coded for each of the 4 rows.
EnumValue.name: SRP

column.name: IsHidden

column.name: ShowInAgeLimit

column.name: ShowInFrequency

table.name: computer
table.summary: Keeps track of the computers in an office.  The list will eventually become cluttered with the names of old computers that are no longer in service.  The old rows can be safely deleted.  Although the primary key is used in at least one table, this will probably be changed, and the computername will become the primary key.

column.name: ComputerNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: CompName
column.summary: Name of the computer.

column.name: LastHeartBeat
column.summary:
Allows us to tell which computers are running.  All workstations record a heartbeat here at an interval of 3 minutes.  
            So if the heartbeat is fairly fresh, then that's an accurate indicator of whether Open Dental is running on that computer.

table.name: computerpref
table.summary: Enables preference specific to individual computers on a customer network.

column.name: ComputerPrefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ComputerName
column.summary: The human-readable name of the computer on the network (not the IP address).

column.name: GraphicsUseHardware
column.summary: Set to true if the tooth chart is to use a hardware accelerated OpenGL window when available. Set to false to use software rendering when available. Of course, the final pixel format on the customer machine depends on the list of available formats. Best match pixel format is always used. This option only applies if GraphicsSimple is set to false.

column.name: GraphicsSimple
column.summary: Enum:DrawingMode Set to 1 to use the low-quality 2D tooth chart in the chart module. Set to 0 to use a 3D DirectX based tooth chart in the chart module. This option helps the program run even when the local graphics hardware is buggy or unavailable.

column.name: SensorType
column.summary: Indicates the type of Suni sensor connected to the local computer (if any). This can be a value of A, B, C, or D.

column.name: SensorBinned
column.summary: Indicates wether or not the Suni sensor uses binned operation.

column.name: SensorPort
column.summary: Indicates which Suni box port to connect with. There are 2 ports on a box (ports 0 and 1).

column.name: SensorExposure
column.summary: Indicates the exposure level to use when capturing from a Suni sensor. Values can be 1 through 7.

column.name: GraphicsDoubleBuffering
column.summary: Indicates if the user prefers double-buffered 3D tooth-chart (where applicable).

column.name: PreferredPixelFormatNum
column.summary: Indicates the current OpenGL pixel format by number which the user prefers (if using OpenGL).

column.name: AtoZpath
column.summary: The path of the A-Z folder for the specified computer.  Overrides the officewide default.  Used when multiple locations are on a single virtual database and they each want to look to the local data folder for images.

column.name: TaskKeepListHidden
column.summary: If the global setting for showing the Task List is on, this controls if it should be hidden on this specified computer

column.name: TaskDock
column.summary: Dock task bar on bottom (0) or right (1).

column.name: TaskX
column.summary: X pos for right docked task list.

column.name: TaskY
column.summary: Y pos for bottom docked task list.

column.name: DirectXFormat
column.summary:
Holds a semi-colon separated list of enumeration names and values representing a DirectX format. If blank, then
            no format is currently set and the best theoretical format will be chosen at program startup. If this value is set to
            'opengl' then this computer is using OpenGL and a DirectX format will not be picked.

column.name: ScanDocSelectSource
column.summary: Show the select scanner dialog when scanning documents. This can also be set to prevent a couple of issues that can happen after scanning with some Canon scanners. One issue is when the Imaging Module won't load some PDFs. The other issue is when listbox selections won't visually update in other windows like the commlog.

column.name: ScanDocShowOptions
column.summary: Show the scanner options dialog when scanning documents.

column.name: ScanDocDuplex
column.summary: Attempt to scan in duplex mode when scanning multipage documents with an ADF.

column.name: ScanDocGrayscale
column.summary: Scan in gray scale when scanning documents.

column.name: ScanDocResolution
column.summary: Scan at the specified resolution when scanning documents.  Example: 150.

column.name: ScanDocQuality
column.summary: 0-100. Quality of jpeg after compression when scanning documents.  100 indicates full quality.  Opposite of compression.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  The most recent clinic for this computer.  Determines which clinic is used when loading Open Dental.

column.name: ApptViewNum
column.summary: FK to apptview.ApptViewNum.  The most recent appt view num for this computer.  Used when opening with the Appts module in conjunction with ClinicNum if this ApptViewNum is associated to the ClinicNum.

column.name: RecentApptView
column.summary: Deprecated.  The index of the most recent appt view for this computer.  Uses it when opening.  This column cannot be dropped due to older versions using it upon opening (prior to calling the update file copier code) so they will throw a UE if this column is ever dropped.

column.name: PatSelectSearchMode
column.summary:
Enum:SearchMode The search mode that is used when loading the patient select window, and while typing.
            When 0 the patient select window will use the DB wide pref PatientSelectUsesSearchButton.

Enumeration.name: SearchMode
Enumeration.summary: The search mode that is used when loading the patient select window, and while typing

column.name: NoShowDecimal

column.name: ComputerOS

Enumeration.name: PlatformOD

EnumValue._: Only happens when workstation has not ran through convert script yet.

EnumValue._: The operating system is Win32s. Win32s is a layer that runs on 16-bit versions of Windows to provide access to 32-bit applications.
EnumValue.name: Win32S

EnumValue._: The operating system is Windows 95 or Windows 98.
EnumValue.name: Win32Windows

EnumValue._: The operating system is Windows NT or later.
EnumValue.name: Win32NT

EnumValue._: The operating system is Windows CE.
EnumValue.name: WinCE

EnumValue._: The operating system is Unix.
EnumValue.name: Unix

EnumValue._: The operating system is Macintosh.

column.name: HelpButtonXAdjustment
column.summary: Deprecated.

column.name: GraphicsUseDirectX11
column.summary: Enum:YN Unknown, Yes, No.

Enumeration.name: YN
Enumeration.summary: 0=Unknown,1=Yes, or 2=No. UI can be tricky. Some success with a 3x1 listbox, multicolumn, see FormPatientEdit. Radiobuttons are also an option.  You can also use another YN variable to store unknown, and then use a click event on the checkbox to change to Y or N.  You can also use a three state checkbox if you translate properly between the enums.

column.name: Zoom
column.summary: Default 0.  Typically a bit above 100. Example 120. Below 100 is allowed but rare, like 80. In addition to normal monitor scale set in Windows. 0 is treated the same as 100, or no additional zoom.

column.name: VideoRectangle
column.summary: This is x,y,w,h format for the desktop location of the Video window. This is saved each time the Video window closes. If the Video window is maximized, then the form will instead be like this: "100,120,600,800,Max". The rectangle here defines the RestoreBounds when user unmaximizes.

column.name: CreditCardTerminalId
column.summary: Holds the value of the last Terminal used in a transaction. Used for PayConnect2 in FormPayConnect2 to set the default value of the Terminal list loading a new terminal payment.

table.name: confirmationrequest
table.summary: Requests that have been sent via EConnector to HQ. HQ will process and update status as responses become available.

column.name: ConfirmationRequestNum
column.summary: PK. Generated by HQ.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum for the corresponding appointment.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum for the corresponding patient.

column.name: ApptNum
column.summary: Foreign key to the appointment represented by this AutoCommAppt.

column.name: DateTimeConfirmExpire
column.summary: Generated by OD. Typically the time of the appointment. This is the time at which HQ will consider this unconfirmed and auto terminate.

column.name: ShortGUID
column.summary: Generated by HQ. Identifies this AutoCommGuid in future transactions between HQ and OD.

column.name: ConfirmCode
column.summary: Generated by HQ. The code that the patient will text back in order to confirm the appointment. If received then it indicates a positive response.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Generated by OD. Timestamp when row is created.

column.name: DateTimeConfirmTransmit
column.summary: Generated by OD. Timestamp when EConnector sent this confirm request to HQ. Stored in local customer timezone.

column.name: DateTimeRSVP
column.summary: Generated by OD. Timestamp when HQ updates this request to indicate that it has been terminated. RSVPStatusCode will change to its final state at this time. Stored in local customer timezone.

column.name: RSVPStatus
column.summary: Enum:RSVPStatusCodes Generated by OD in some cases and HQ in others. Indicates current status in the lifecycle of this ConfirmationRequest.

EnumValue._: Entered manually by something other than EConnector. EConnector will pickup and send to HQ and change to pendingRsvp.
EnumValue.name: AwaitingTransmit

EnumValue._: EConnector has sent this to HQ and will remain in this status until it is either terminated or receives a response from the patient.
EnumValue.name: PendingRsvp

EnumValue._: Patient responded with an affirmative confirmation.
EnumValue.name: PositiveRsvp

EnumValue._: Patient responded and declined the confirmation.
EnumValue.name: NegativeRsvp

EnumValue._: Patient responded by requesting a callback.
EnumValue.name: Callback

EnumValue._: Patient took no action by the time DateTimeExpired passed and the confirmation was terminated.
EnumValue.name: Expired

EnumValue._: HQ or EConnector was unable to create the confirmation so it was terminated prematurely.
EnumValue.name: Failed

EnumValue._:
7 - The appointment date/time was changed before the patient responded to the original confirmation.
            OD proper will simply delete these ConfirmationRequests. HQ will move them to the terminated table and mark them ApptChanged.
EnumValue.name: ApptChanged

column.name: ResponseDescript
column.summary: Generated by OD in some cases and HQ in others. Any human readable error message generated by either HQ or EConnector. Used for debugging.

column.name: GuidMessageFromMobile
column.summary:
FK to smsfrommobile.GuidMessage. Generated at HQ when the confirmation pending is terminated with confirmation text message.
            Also allows SmsFromMobile to be linked to ConfirmationRequest in OD proper.

column.name: ApptDateTime
column.summary: The Date and time of the original appointment. We need this in case the appointment was moved and needs another reminder sent out.

column.name: TSPrior
column.summary: This was the TSPrior used to send this reminder.

column.name: DoNotResend
column.summary: Indicates whether the user has chosen to not resend the confirmation request when the AptDateTime has changed.

column.name: SendStatus
column.summary: Indicates status of message.

column.name: ApptReminderRuleNum
column.summary: FK to apptreminderrule.ApptReminderRuleNum. Allows us to look up the rules to determine how to send this apptcomm out.

column.name: MessageFk
column.summary: FK to primary key of appropriate table.

column.name: DateTimeSent
column.summary: DateTime the message was sent.

column.summary: Enum:EbenefitCategory  The X12 benefit categories.  Each CovCat can link to one X12 category.  Default is 0 (unlinked).

Enumeration.name: EbenefitCategory
Enumeration.summary: The X12 benefit categories.  Used to link the user-defined CovCats to the corresponding X12 category.

EnumValue._: 0- Default.  Applies to all codes.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1- X12: 30 and 35. All ADA codes except ortho.  D0000-D7999 and D9000-D9999
EnumValue.name: General

EnumValue._: 2- X12: 23. ADA D0000-D0999.  This includes DiagnosticXray.
EnumValue.name: Diagnostic

EnumValue._: 7- X12: 36. Exclusive subcategory of restorative.  D2700-D2799
EnumValue.name: Crowns

EnumValue._: 8- X12: 37. ADA range?
EnumValue.name: Accident

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary:
FK to clinic.ClinicNum. The clinic where this card was added. Each clinic could have a different AuthKey and different
            AuthKeys could generate overlapping tokens.

column.name: ExcludeProcSync
column.summary: Only used at OD HQ.  Excludes credit card from syncing default procedures.  False by default.

column.name: PaySimpleToken
column.summary:
Token for PaySimple.  PaySimple returns a token, when requested by the merchant's system, to be used instead
            of actual credit card number in subsequent transactions.

column.name: ChargeFrequency
column.summary:
Stores how often the credit card gets charged for a recurring charge. The card can either be charged fixed days of the month or
            fixed week days of the month. Some examples of the former are "4th day of the month" or "1st and 16th day of the month". Some examples of
            the latter are "Third Monday of the month" or "Every other Friday of the month". If the first character of this column is a 0, then
            the frequency is fixed day of the month. If the first character is 1, the frequency is fixed week days of the month. The next character
            is a pipe for separation. If fixed day of the month, the remaining characters will be a comma-separated list of days of the month. 
            If fixed week days, the next character will represent type of frequency (Every, EveryOther, First, etc.). Then a pipe follows. The last
            character will be the day of the week (0 for Sunday, 1 for Monday, etc.).

column.name: CanChargeWhenNoBal
column.summary:
Set true to indicate the Credit Card in question can be charged when the Patient account balance is $0, which corresponds directly
            to a preference called "RecurringChargesAllowedWhenPatNoBal" (true by default) which must be turned on via Module>Account>Misc to be 
            available.

column.name: PaymentType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. Payment type override for recurring charges.

column.name: IsRecurringActive
column.summary: True by default.  Set to false to inactivate this specific credit card from the recurring charges (both manual and via the service).

column.name: Nickname
column.summary: Nickname or alias. Currently only used in Web API for PaySimple.

column.name: CardHolderName
column.summary: The card holder name associated with the credit card. Not used by all merchant services.

table.name: disease
table.summary: Each row is one disease that one patient has.  Now called a problem in the UI.  Must have a DiseaseDefNum.

column.name: DiseaseNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: DiseaseDefNum
column.summary: FK to diseasedef.DiseaseDefNum.  The disease description is in that table.

column.name: PatNote
column.summary: Any note about this disease that is specific to this patient. When importing from eForms, this is where the disease name goes for "Other" when there's no match.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: The last date and time this row was altered.  Not user editable.

column.name: ProbStatus
column.summary: Enum:ProblemStatus Active=0, Resolved=1, Inactive=2.

Enumeration.name: ProblemStatus
Enumeration.summary: 0=Active, 1=Resolved, 2=Inactive

column.name: DateStart
column.summary: Date that the disease was diagnosed.  Can be minval if unknown.

column.name: DateStop
column.summary: Date that the disease was set resolved or inactive.  Will be minval if still active.  ProbStatus should be used to determine if it is active or not.

column.name: SnomedProblemType
column.summary:
FK to snomed.SnomedCode.  Used in EHR CCD export/import only.  Must be one of the following SNOMED codes:
            Problem/Concern (55607006 or blank), Finding (404684003), Complaint (409586006), Dignosis (282291009), Condition (64572001), FunctionalLimitation (248536006), Symptom (418799008).

column.name: FunctionStatus
column.summary: Enum:FunctionalStatus  Used to export EHR CCD functional status and/or cognitive status information only.

Enumeration.name: FunctionalStatus
Enumeration.summary: Used in EHR to export patient functional and cognitive statuses on CCD documents.

EnumValue._: 0 - Default value.  If not using EHR, then each diseasedef will use this value.
EnumValue.name: Problem

EnumValue._: 1 - This clinical statement contains details of an evaluation or assessment of a patient’s cognitive status. The evaluation may include assessment of a patient's mood, memory, and ability to make decisions. The statement will include, if present, supporting caregivers, non-medical devices, and the time period for which the evaluation and assessment were performed.
EnumValue.name: CognitiveResult

EnumValue._: 2 - A cognitive status problem observation is a clinical statement that describes a patient's cognitive condition, findings or symptoms. Examples of cognitive problem observations are inability to recall, amnesia, dementia, and aggressive behavior. A cognitive problem observation is a finding or medical condition. This is different from a cognitive result observation, which is a response to a question that provides insight to the patient's cognitive status. It reflects findings that provide information about a medical condition, while a result observation reflects responses to questions in a cognitive test or those that provide information about a person's judgement, comprehension ability, and response speed.
EnumValue.name: CognitiveProblem

EnumValue._: 3 - This clinical statement represents details of an evaluation or assessment of a patient’s functional status. The evaluation may include assessment of a patient's  language, vision, hearing, activities of daily living, behavior, general function, mobility and self-care status. The statement will include, if present, supporting caregivers, non-medical devices, and the time period for which the evaluation and assessment were performed.
EnumValue.name: FunctionalResult

EnumValue._: 4 - A functional status problem observation is a clinical statement that represents a patient’s functional perfomance and ability.
EnumValue.name: FunctionalProblem

table.name: diseasedef

column.name: DateTStamp

column.name: ICD9Code
column.summary: FK to icd9.Icd9Code.  Example: 250.00 for diabetes.  User not allowed to enter any string anymore, must pick one from the Icd9Code table.  Some may exist in the databases without linking to a valid Icd9Code table entry if the ConvertDatabase could not find the user typed string in the list of valid Icd9Codes.

column.name: SnomedCode
column.summary: FK to snomed.SnomedCode.  Example: 230572002 for diabetic neuropathy.  User not allowed to enter any string anymore, must pick from the Snomed table.  Some may exist in the databases without linking to a valid Snomed table entry if the ConvertDatabase could find the user typed string in the list of valid SnomedCodes.

column.name: Icd10Code
column.summary: FK to icd10.Icd10Code.  Example: E10.1 for 'Type 1 diabetes mellitus with ketoacidosis'. User not allowed to enter any string anymore, must pick one from the Icd10Code table.

table.name: displayfield
table.summary: Allows customization of which fields display in various lists and grids.  For now, the only grid is ProgressNotes.  Will also eventually let users set column widths and translate titles.  For now, the selections are the same for all computers.

column.name: DisplayFieldNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: InternalName
column.summary: This is the internal name that OD uses to identify the field within this category. This will be the default description if the user doesn't specify an alternate in Description. For Ortho chart, this column will be "Signature", "Provider", or blank. For SuperFamilyGridCols, if this is a patfield column, then this will be blank.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Order to display in the grid or list. Every entry must have a unique itemorder.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Optional alternate description to display for field. Can be in another language. For Ortho, this is the 'key', since InternalName is blank sometimes. For SuperFamilyGridCols, if this is a patfield column, then this will be the name of the patfield.FieldName.

column.name: ColumnWidth
column.summary: For grid columns, this lets user override the column width.  Especially useful for foreign languages.

column.name: Category
column.summary: Enum:DisplayFieldCategory If category is 0, then this is attached to a ChartView.

EnumValue._: 0- Badly named. This should be called Progress Notes.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 2- Family module.
EnumValue.name: PatientInformation

EnumValue._: 10- Account module patient information
EnumValue.name: AccountPatientInformation

EnumValue._: 17 - A/R Manager Sent Grid
EnumValue.name: ArManagerSentGrid

EnumValue._: 18 - A/R Manager Unsent Grid
EnumValue.name: ArManagerUnsentGrid

EnumValue._: 19 - A/R Manager Excluded Grid
EnumValue.name: ArManagerExcludedGrid

EnumValue._: 20 - Statement Limited Custom SuperFamily
EnumValue.name: LimitedCustomStatement

EnumValue._: 21 - SuperFamily Grid
EnumValue.name: SuperFamilyGridCols

column.name: ChartViewNum
column.summary: FK to chartview.ChartViewNum. 0 if attached to a category.

column.name: PickList
column.summary: Newline delimited string which contains the selectable options in combo box dropdowns.  Specifically for the Ortho chart.

column.name: DescriptionOverride
column.summary: Only used in Ortho and SuperFamilyGridCols (PatField). Ortho chart display fields utilize the InternalName field for Signature and Provider indicators, this field is here to override description. Some users want to use different fields but use the same description for multiple tabs. Example: The display field of WeightWeekly shows as "Weight" and in another tab the field for WeightMonthly can also show as "Weight".

table.name: document
table.summary: Represents a single document in the imaging module.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Description of the document.

column.name: DateCreated
column.summary: Date and time. Can be edited by user, but not forward dated without that permission.

column.name: DocCategory
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. Categories for documents. 0 for ChartLetters.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  The document will be located in the patient folder of this patient.

column.name: FileName
column.summary: The name of the file. Does not include any directory info.

column.name: ImgType
column.summary: Enum:ImageType Document, Radiograph, Photo, File, Attachment.

Enumeration.name: ImageType
Enumeration.summary: The type of image for images module.

EnumValue._: 0- Includes scanned documents and screenshots.
EnumValue.name: Document

EnumValue._: 3- For instance a Word document or a spreadsheet. Not an image.
EnumValue.name: File

EnumValue._: 4- Used for Claim Attachments. Preserves original resolution.
EnumValue.name: Attachment

column.name: IsFlipped
column.summary: True if flipped horizontally. A vertical flip would be stored as a horizontal flip plus a 180 rotation.

column.name: DegreesRotated
column.summary: Any positive or negative, including decimals.

column.name: ToothNumbers
column.summary: An optional list of tooth numbers. In Db, rigorously formatted as American numbers, and separated by commas.  For display, uses hyphens for sequences.  Very likely supports international tooth numbers, but not tested for that.

column.name: Note
column.summary: MediumText, so max length=16M for API upload base64.

column.name: SigIsTopaz
column.summary: True if the signature is in Topaz format rather than OD format.

column.name: Signature
column.summary: The encrypted and bound signature in base64 format.  The signature is bound to the byte sequence of the original image. Signature for ChartLetter is also bound to bytes of Word doc, but it might be a slightly different algorithm than non-ChartLetters.

column.name: CropX
column.summary: Crop rectangle X. May be negative. First, image is rotated as needed around center. Then, clipped to this crop rectangle.  X-Y is center of the crop rectangle relative to center of the image, and where positive is to the upper right of the center of the image.

column.name: CropY
column.summary: Crop rectangle Y. May be negative. First, image is rotated as needed around center. Then, clipped to this crop rectangle. X-Y is center of the crop rectangle relative to center of the image, and where positive is to the upper right of the center of the image.

column.name: CropW
column.summary: Crop rectangle Width in original image pixel scale.  May be zero if no cropping.  May be greater than original image width.

column.name: CropH
column.summary: Crop rectangle Height in original image pixel scale.  May be zero if no cropping.  May be greater than original image height.

column.name: WindowingMin
column.summary: The lower value of the "windowing" (contrast/brightness) for radiographs.  Default is 0.  Max is 255.

column.name: WindowingMax
column.summary: The upper value of the "windowing" (contrast/brightness) for radiographs.  Default is 0(no windowing).  Max is 255. For 12 bit images with a max of 4096, the same max of 255 is used here, but it's just scaled proportionally (x16).

column.name: MountItemNum
column.summary: FK to mountitem.MountItemNum. If set, then this image will only show on a mount, not in the main tree. If set to 0, then no mount item is associated with this document.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: Date/time last altered.

column.name: RawBase64
column.summary: The raw file data encoded as base64.  Only used if there is no AtoZ folder.

column.name: Thumbnail
column.summary: Thumbnail encoded as base64.  Only present if not using AtoZ folder. 100x100 pixels, jpg, takes around 5.5k.

column.name: ExternalGUID
column.summary: The primary key associated to a document hosted on an external source.

column.name: ExternalSource
column.summary: Enum:ExternalSourceType None, Dropbox, XVWeb. The source for the corresponding ExternalGUID.

Enumeration.name: ExternalSourceType
Enumeration.summary: Supported sources that help identify what the corresponding ExternalGUID column should be used for.

EnumValue._: This is a document that is not stored in an external source.  All documents stored by Open Dental will be this type.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: This document can be found in a corresponding Dropbox account.
EnumValue.name: Dropbox

EnumValue._: This document is saved from a download from XVWeb program link.
EnumValue.name: XVWeb

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum. Optional. Used for radiographs and ChartLetters.

column.name: IsCropOld
column.summary: Set to true as part of conversion to 21.4. Set back to false once the crop is converted to the new scheme. It would take too long to do this conversion in the normal script because it involves loading each image to obtain width and height. So this is a lazy conversion.

column.name: OcrResponseData
column.summary: Stores a JSON serialized OcrInsScanResponse object. The type of this object is defined by the OcrCaptureType.

column.name: ImageCaptureType
column.summary: Enum:EnumOcrCaptureType 0=Miscellaneous, 1=PrimaryInsFront, 2=PrimaryInsBack, 3=SecondaryInsFront, 4=SecondaryInsBack. Only used when patient scans their insurance card from eClipboard.

Enumeration.name: EnumOcrCaptureType
Enumeration.summary: The type of image. Miscelllaneuous is for all images without special behavior.

EnumValue._: 0- Catch-All type for imageCaptures without unique behavior
EnumValue.name: Miscellaneous

column.name: PrintHeading
column.summary: Set true by default for radiographs and tooth charts. When set to true, it will print additional heading text including patient name, DOB, and today's date.

column.name: ChartLetterStatus
column.summary: If not 0, this document is a Chart Letter. It will only show in the Chart Module and not in the Imaging Module. The document will be a Word document based on a template. The intent is for it to be used for very complex chart notes. It could then be sent to the referring dentist, for example. DocCategory will be 0 for these which naturally hides them in Imaging module. Like a procnote, a chart letter cannot actually be edited or deleted. All edits are preserved for audit trail and a copy is made for each edit.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. Only used when DocChartLetterStatus > 0. Tracks which user made the change.

column.name: ChartLetterHash
column.summary: Only used for ChartLetter. Every time a Word document is saved, we create a new document row with this hash tied to the byte sequence of that Word document. This proves that it was unaltered. The hash is 16 bytes, but we want a human-readable version. So we convert each byte to a two char hex string. So this field will always be 32 characters without any spaces or punc.

table.name: documentmisc
table.summary: For storing docs/images in database.  This table is for the various miscellaneous documents that are not in the normal patient subfolders.

column.name: DocMiscNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateCreated
column.summary: Date created.

column.name: FileName
column.summary:
The name the file would have if it was not in the database. Does not include any directory info.
            DocumentMisc rows that store the contents of the UpdateFiles folder will set this column to an "item order".
            Due to severe limitations with sending large amounts of data all in one query we are going to store the UpdateFiles over several rows.
            The FileName column will store the order of which the UpdateFiles need to go back into when we try to reconstruct it.

column.name: DocMiscType
column.summary: Enum:DocumentMiscType Corresponds to the same subfolder within AtoZ folder. eg. UpdateFiles

Enumeration.name: DocumentMiscType
Enumeration.summary: More types will be added to correspond to most of the subfolders inside the AtoZ folder.  But no point adding them until we implement.

EnumValue._: 0- There will just be zero or one row of this type.  It will contain a zipped archive.
EnumValue.name: UpdateFiles

EnumValue._:
1- Entries with this doc type hold segments of the UpdateFiles RawBase64 zip contents that will be pieced back together later.
            Storing the entire Update Files contents into one row was exceeding MySQL max_allowed_packet limitations so this new type is required.
            Each row of this type will contain ~1MB of RawBase64 data.
EnumValue.name: UpdateFilesSegment

EnumValue._:
2- Entires of this doc type are segments of OpenDentalShareScreen.exe from our website.
            File names are formatted like version{guid}segment# (ex 12.11.2022.501{45e744a7-f6dd-4e55-9f9f-11d1f746eefe}13).
            An OpenDentalShareScreen.exe is complete when a record is present without a segment# (ex 12.11.2022.501{45e744a7-f6dd-4e55-9f9f-11d1f746eefe}).
EnumValue.name: ShareScreenExeSegment

column.name: RawBase64
column.summary: The raw file data encoded as base64.

column.name: ClinicNum

table.name: eclipboardimagecapture
table.summary:
Linker table between patients and the images they have submitted to the office via eClipboard. Lets office know when a patient last submitted
            a certain image. Is used in conjuction with EClipboardImageCaptureDef table to allow offices to set frequencies for how often patients should 
            submit certain images, similar to sheets.

column.name: EClipboardImageCaptureNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DefNum
column.summary: FK to def.DefNum. Should match a DefNum that that is in the in 'EClipboard Images' defcat. Will be zero when IsSelfPortrait.

column.name: IsSelfPortrait
column.summary:
Using DefNum to identify the self portrait is unreliable as the image category that is used to store self portraits may change. Instead, set this field to
            true for any image capture that is a self portrait. Only for self-portraits tied to the pref 'EClipboardAllowSelfPortraitOnCheckIn', not 'eClipboard Images' defcat.

column.name: DateTimeUpserted
column.summary:
Records the date and time the patient took the image. If patient has submitted this eclipboard image before, then we simply update the
            DateUpserted field and DocNum field. We do not insert an entirely new record.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: FK to document.DocNum. If a document is deleted, need to also delete any record from this table with the same DocNum.

Enumeration.name: EnumOcrCaptureType
Enumeration.summary: The type of image. Miscelllaneuous is for all images without special behavior.

EnumValue._: 0- Catch-All type for imageCaptures without unique behavior

column.name: DefNum
column.summary: FK to def.DefNum. Should match a DefNum that is in the in 'EClipboard Images' defcat.  Will be zero when IsSelfPortrait.
