column.name: OcrCaptureType
column.summary: Enum:EnumOcrCaptureType 0=Miscellaneous, 1=PrimaryInsFront, 2=PrimaryInsBack, 3=SecondaryInsFront, 4=SecondaryInsBack

Enumeration.name: EnumOcrCaptureType
Enumeration.summary: The type of image. Miscelllaneuous is for all images without special behavior.

EnumValue._: 0- Catch-All type for imageCaptures without unique behavior
EnumValue.name: Miscellaneous

column.name: Frequency
column.summary: Enum:EnumEClipFreq 0=Once, 1=EachTime, 2=TimeSpan. The frequency that an image capture will be submitted by patients. ResubmitInterval can only be set if Frequency is TimeSpan.

Enumeration.name: EnumEClipFreq
Enumeration.summary: Specifies the frequency that an EClipboardSheetDef or an EClipboardImageCaptureDef will be submitted by patients.

EnumValue._: 0 - Each patient will submit this form or image capture exactly one time.
EnumValue.name: Once

EnumValue._: 1 - Each patient will submit this form or image capture at every visit.
EnumValue.name: EachTime

EnumValue._: 2 - Each patient will submit this form or image capture based on a specified time span measured in Years and Months.
EnumValue.name: TimeSpan

column.name: ResubmitInterval
column.summary: If Frequency is EnumEClipFreq.TimeSpan, this will indicate the acceptable amount of time (measured in Years and Months) that can pass since the last time the patient has submitted this image capture.

table.name: eclipboardsheetdef
table.summary: Holds settings for eClipboard. Each row is attached to one SheetDef or EFormDef. This table might typically only have 3 to 4 rows in it. There could be more for different clinics. This information helps the software decide whether or not to display a sheet or eForm for a specific patient based on certain criteria. Examples include MinAge, MaxAge, Frequency, and ResubmitInterval. Forms will also end up on the eClipboard if their sheet.ShowInTerminal is non-zero or eForm.Status=ReadyForPatientFill.

column.name: EClipboardSheetDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SheetDefNum
column.summary: FK to SheetDef.SheetDefNum. Can be zero if this row is for an eForm.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  0 if no clinic or if default clinic.

column.name: ResubmitInterval
column.summary: If Frequency is EnumEClipFreq.TimeSpan, this will indicate the acceptable amount of time (measured in Years and Months) that can pass since the last time the patient has filled this form out. Once this has elapsed, if the EClipboardCreateMissingFormsOnCheckIn pref is turned on, this form will automatically be added to the patient forms to fill out when the patient is checked in.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The order in which the patient will be asked to fill out this form.

column.name: PrefillStatus
column.summary: Determines how forms will be shown in eClipboard, blank or prefill.

column.name: MinAge
column.summary: Indicates the minimum age of the patient to be given the form to fill out. If their age is below the minimum limit, they will not be given the form to fill out. A value of -1 means ignore any age requirements.

column.name: MaxAge
column.summary: Indicates the maximum age of the patient to be given the form to fill out. If their age is over or equal to this maximum limit, they will not be given the form to fill out. A value of -1 means ignore any age requirements.

column.name: IgnoreSheetDefNums
column.summary: Comma delimited list of sheetdef nums to ignore. This can only be set if the preFillStatus is set to Once. These sheetDefs are ignored until this sheet is filled out. For example, an office may have a sheet for new patients that is only filled out once. Once the new patient has filled out this sheet, these sheetDefs will no longer be ignored when the patient checks in again. Not used in eForms because we have conditional fields, so a single form could be designed to be used for both new and existing patients.

column.name: PrefillStatusOverride
column.summary: For both Sheets and eForms. Holds the previous revision number that marks what def revision number we expect to have a submission for from the patient. This value can be incremented when changes are made to a linked sheet or eForm def, but it is not always updated. This should hold the last revision number that was updated for this eClipboardSheetDef. If a patient has a form filled out that has a revision ID that matches this or is greater (this field will be 0 by default, or they filled out a more up to date form manually in the office and not through eClipboard), then this form will be filtered out if set to EnumEClipFreq.Once when we determine which forms load for the patient in eClipboard. If this value is higher than the RevID of the last form the patient filled out, we consider this eClipboardSheetDef to be updated and the patient will need to fill out the form again when set to EnumEClipFreq.Once.

column.name: EFormDefNum
column.summary: FK to EFormDef.EFormDefNum. Can be zero if this row is for a sheet.

column.name: Frequency
column.summary: Enum:EnumEClipFreq 0=Once, 1=EachTime, 2=TimeSpan. The frequency that a form will be submitted by patients. ResubmitInterval can only be set if Frequency is TimeSpan.

Enumeration.name: EnumEClipFreq
Enumeration.summary: Specifies the frequency that an EClipboardSheetDef or an EClipboardImageCaptureDef will be submitted by patients.

EnumValue._: 0 - Each patient will submit this form or image capture exactly one time.
EnumValue.name: Once

EnumValue._: 1 - Each patient will submit this form or image capture at every visit.
EnumValue.name: EachTime

EnumValue._: 2 - Each patient will submit this form or image capture based on a specified time span measured in Years and Months.
EnumValue.name: TimeSpan

table.name: eduresource
table.summary: EHR education resource.  Only one of the 3 FK fields will be used at a time (DiseaseDefNum, MedicationNum, or LabResultID).  The other two will be blank.   Displays a clickable URL if the patient meets certain criteria.

column.name: EduResourceNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DiseaseDefNum
column.summary: FK to diseasedef.DiseaseDefNum.  This now also handles ICD9s and Snomeds via the entry in DiseaseDef.

column.name: MedicationNum
column.summary: FK to medication.MedicationNum.

column.name: LabResultID
column.summary: FK to labresult.TestID.

column.name: LabResultName
column.summary: Used for display in the grid.

column.name: LabResultCompare
column.summary: String, example <43. Must start with < or > followed by int.  Only used if FK LabResultID is used.

column.name: SmokingSnoMed
column.summary: FK to ehrmeasureevent.CodeValueResult when ehrmeasureevent.EventType=EhrMeasureEventType.TobaccoUseAssessed (8).

table.name: eform
table.summary: EForms is a way for patients to fill out forms. This is similar to sheets, but optimized for dynamic layout instead of fixed layout. The office sets up templates, EFormDefs, which get copied to EForms. Each EForm is linked to one patient.

column.name: EFormNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FormType
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormType 0=PatientForm, 1=MedicalHistory, 2=Consent. This doesn't actually do anything, and all fields are available for all types, but that might eventually change if more types are added.

Enumeration.name: EnumEFormType
Enumeration.summary: Different types of EForms that can be used.

EnumValue._: 0 - Includes patient information and insurance information.
EnumValue.name: PatientForm

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FKey to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateTimeShown
column.summary: The date and time that show in the UI.

column.name: Description
column.summary: The title of the EForm. Copied from EFormDef.Description.

column.name: DateTEdited
column.summary: The date and time when the EForm was lasted edited. Not editable by the user in the UI.

column.name: MaxWidth
column.summary: Required. Can be any value between 50 and 1000. On wide screens, this limits the width of the form. This is needed on pretty much anything other than a phone. Makes it look consistent across devices and prevents useless white space. Default 450.

column.name: EFormDefNum
column.summary: FKey to eformdef.EFormDefNum. This is only used alongside the eClipboardSheetDef table to determine if the patient has filled out that form yet. It's also used with the RevID to prefill fields that don't use a DbLink by pulling from a previous form. I think this also requires using the ReportableName for those fields.

column.name: Status
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormStatus 0-None, 1-ShowInEClipboard, 2-Filled, 3-Imported. In the None status, the office might be filling in the tooth number on a consent form. ShowInEClipboard status makes it show in eClipboard. Sheets use ShowInTerminal for this purpose. Once the patient has filled it, the status is changed to Filled. Sheets uses IsWebForm for this purpose. If a Filled eForm gets imported, then the status is changed to Imported.

EnumValue._: 0 - If a form is added manually in OD proper and then filled out by the patient in OD proper, it will remain this status unless it gets imported.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Forms with this status will show in eClipboard. Unlike sheets, there is no way to set order.
EnumValue.name: ShowInEClipboard

EnumValue._: 2 - This status gets set from eClipboard after filling.
EnumValue.name: Filled

column.name: RevID
column.summary: Revision ID. Used to determine in conjunction with PrefillStatus for eClipboardSheetDef to determine whether to show a patient a new form or have them update their last filled out form. Must match up with EFormDef RevID to show a previously filled out form. 0-based.

column.name: ShowLabelsBold
column.summary: If true, then this form will show labels at 95% and slightly bold. This looks good, but some users might not want it for certain forms, so it's an option. This applies to text, date, radiobuttons, and sigBox. It does not apply to types label, checkbox, or medicationList.

column.name: SpaceBelowEachField
column.summary: The amount of space below each field. Overrides the global default and can be overridden by field.SpaceBelow. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space.

column.name: SpaceToRightEachField
column.summary: The amount of space to the right of each field. Overrides the global default and can be overridden by field.SpaceToRight. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space.

column.name: SaveImageCategory
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. This gets set when an EForm is created from an EFormDef. 0 for none or else it's set to an image category. User can change it. It only saves if user saves manually.

table.name: eformdef
table.summary: EForms are a way for patients to fill out forms. This is similar to sheets, but optimized for dynamic layout instead of fixed layout. The office sets up templates, EFormDefs, which get copied to EForms. Since this is a template EForm, it does not link to a patient. It can be freely changed without affecting any EForms. We also supply internal EFormDefs, which are hard coded as XML rather than being in any office database.

column.name: EFormDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FormType
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormType 0=PatientForm, 1=MedicalHistory, 2=Consent. This doesn't actually do anything, and all fields are available for all types, but that might eventually change if more types are added.

Enumeration.name: EnumEFormType
Enumeration.summary: Different types of EForms that can be used.

EnumValue._: 0 - Includes patient information and insurance information.
EnumValue.name: PatientForm

column.name: Description
column.summary: The title of the EFormDef. Set by the user.

column.name: DateTCreated
column.summary: The date and time when the EFormDef was created. Not editable by the user in the UI.

column.name: IsInternalHidden
column.summary: Deprecated.

column.name: MaxWidth
column.summary: Required. Can be any value between 50 and 1000. On wide screens, this limits the width of the form. This is needed on pretty much anything other than a phone. Makes it look consistent across devices and prevents useless white space. Default 450.

column.name: RevID
column.summary: Revision ID. Gets updated any time an eForm field is added or deleted from an eFormDef (this includes any time a translation is changed). Used to determine in conjunction with PrefillStatus for eClipboardSheetDef to determine whether to show a patient a new form or have them update their last filled out form. Must match up with EForm RevID to show a previously filled out form. 0-based.

column.name: ShowLabelsBold
column.summary: If true, then this form will show labels at 95% and slightly bold. This looks good, but some users might not want it for certain forms, so it's an option. This applies to text, date, radiobuttons, and sigBox. It does not apply to types label, checkbox, or medicationList.

column.name: SpaceBelowEachField
column.summary: The amount of space below each field. Overrides the global default and can be overridden by field.SpaceBelow. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space.

column.name: SpaceToRightEachField
column.summary: The amount of space to the right of each field. Overrides the global default and can be overridden by field.SpaceToRight. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space.

column.name: SaveImageCategory
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. There is a global setting to save forms to the image category which has ItemVal set to "U". We completely ignore that and it will only work for sheets. If this is 0, it will not save to images. Copied to EForm child.

table.name: eformfield
table.summary:
Individual fields for EForm. Each field generally includes a label and a value. Links to a EForm by FKey to eform.EFormNum.
            NOTE: If any new fields get added to this class and EFormFieldDef, make sure to add them to the methods EFormFields.FromDef and EFormFields.ToDef

column.name: EFormFieldNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EFormNum
column.summary: FKey to eform.EFormNum

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FKey to patient.PatNum to let us quickly grab all for a patient, and then loop later.

column.name: FieldType
column.summary: Enum:EFormFieldType 0-TextField, 1-Label, 2-DateField, etc.

column.name: DbLink
column.summary: If this field is importable, then this links to a db field. The list of available fields for each type is in EFormFieldsAvailable. Users can pick from that list. Identical list as in Sheets. It's string-based instead of enum, just like Sheets, because it's too complex to use an enum, even for our reduced number of items. None is always represented in UI as "None" and in db as empty string. All DbLinks are available on all form types to give users more flexibility. Checkboxes can have DBLinks that look like "allergy:..." or "problem:..."

column.name: ValueLabel
column.summary:
Used differently for different types:
            TextField, DateField, CheckBox: The label next to or above the textbox, or checkbox.RadioButtons: This label next to or above the group of radiobuttons. Labels on each radiobutton are in PickListVis.Label: This label is the only thing that shows. A label is always a WPF FlowDocument, which is an XML format. This allows extensive rich text formatting, like bold, color, paragraph formatting, etc. This format can be used directly in OD proper, but it will need to be converted for some other programming languages using external tools. BUT, prior to that, it must be run through a method that adjusts all the font sizes. FlowDocuments only support absolute font sizes instead of relative font sizes. We use 11.5 as the base font size and all other fonts are considered to be relative to this base. So if a font size of 13.8 is present in the FlowDocument, that does not mean to use 13.8; it instead means to use 120%. If your chosen base font size on a mobile device is 16, then the conversion method needs to convert the 13.8 to 19.2 prior to using the FlowDocument.PageBreak: Not used.SigBox: Optional label above sig box.MedicationList: This holds an EFormMedListLayout object, serialized as json, including the Title, column headers, column widths, etc.PatientList: This holds an EFormPatListLayout object, serialized as json, including Label.

column.name: ValueString
column.summary:
The data as entered by patient or pulled from the db. We do not need this in EFormFieldDef because that has no patient or db data. Used differently for different types:
            TextField: value in textBox. For allergiesOther, medsOther, and problemsOther, this is a comma-delimited list. Spaces by commas are ok. Like this: "Aspirin, Iodine, Latex"Label: Not used because no patient input.DateField: date in culture format, like 4/25/2024.Checkbox: "X" or blank "".RadioButton: String value chosen by patient. Pulled from PickListDb, not PickListVis. When importing, empty signifies that patient did not enter any choice, so do not import.ComboBox (not yet added): String value chosen by patient.SigBox: First char is 0 for our sigbox and 1 for Topaz. The remainder is the signature string. See OpenDentBusiness\UI\SignatureBoxWrapper.cs for details. Same format as used for all signatures in OD. Does not get imported.MedicationList: This holds a list of EFormMed objects, serialized as json.PatientList: This holds a PatNum representing the selected patient, if applicable. Example: "143".

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: 0 based.

column.name: PickListVis
column.summary: Pipe delimited list of strings, used for radioButtons, future comboBoxes, etc. This is the list of items that are visible to the patient. Setup enforces same number of items in PickListDb for 1:1 match. This list allows customization of what the patient sees vs what's in the db. Example: Vis=Hispanic, Db=2135-2. Example: Vis=Do Not Call, Db=DoNotCall. For radiobuttons, the number of items in the lists determines the number of radiobuttons to show to the patient. These editable lists also allow excluding some db options from being visible to patient. Example: Ins Relationship has 9 options, but only 4 of them are really used in dentistry. Just leave the other 5 off and force them to pick one of the 4. But it is also not required for them to pick one. Example: For Marital Status, you might only show Married and Child, excluding Divorced and Single from the pick list. The unselected state then represents no change, so an existing patient could leave both radio buttons unchecked and their status would remain Divorced or Single. However, we currently lack a feature to let them uncheck a radiobutton that is already checked. This is a rare edge case that nearly nobody will care about. You can also have a row with no db value. For example, a visible value of Separated might have no corresponding db value entered. In that case, an import would not cause any change to the existing db value. These lists also allow two radioButtons to represent one db item. Example: Gender Other in db can be expanded to show patient both Nonbinary and Other. When patient picks either of these, it goes into the db as Other. The lists also allow any or all items to be empty with no label. Example: Y/N radiobuttons for a series of allergies. Y/N label at top, but none of the radiobuttons need labels. When translation is added later, it will translate this list, not the PickListDb. PickListVis will, by default, simply be exactly the same as PickListDb. In this state, what the patient sees is the same as what's in the db. Must have at least two items for now.

column.name: PickListDb
column.summary: Pipe delimited list of strings, used for radioButtons, future comboBoxes, etc. This is the list of items as they would be stored in the database. See PickListVis above for examples of how to use. The value chosen from this list is what will be stored in the ValueString field. Never show this value to the patient.

column.name: IsHorizStacking
column.summary: Typically false. Set to true to cause this field to get stacked horizontally compared to its previous sibling. Example might be to set State and Zip fields to true. This request will be ignored if screen is too small, like on a phone. The following types are not allowed to stack: SigBox, PageBreak, MedicationList.

column.name: IsTextWrap
column.summary: Only applies when this is a TextField. Default is false, which creates a single row textbox that scrolls horizontally if text is too long. Set to true to cause text to wrap instead. This will cause the box to grow to fit the text.

column.name: Width
column.summary: This stores either pixel width or percentage width, depending on IsWidthPercentage. In either case, if this is blank/0, then width will be 100% of what's available. The discussion here is for fixed widths. See IsWidthPercentage for discussion of percentage widths. If fields are stacked horizontally, then they will wrap when they hit screen width. So horizontally stacked fields may end up vertically stacked on a small screen. But if a single field is still set to be wider than the current screen, it will shrink to fit the screen. This width uses WPF DIPs which are 1/96". Android phones define DIPs differently; they use 1/160" per DIP. But if you are using a language like Flutter, they are handling that conversion for you in the background. Regardless, we will be ignoring DIPs and scaling based solely on font size. The reason for this is to make fonts and boxes all look proportionally the same on both OD proper and in eClipboard. So assuming you use 14 flutter logical pixels for 100% font vs 11.5 in WPF, the conversion would look like this: Width/11.5*14. Notice that we are only converting based on font size. This makes our converted width a near perfect fit for the same text as the original. Width is only available on the field types that are h-stackable.

column.name: FontScale
column.summary: Applies to both the label on the field and the field itself. Never 0. Does not apply to Label types, though, since those are only handled by editing the rich text. Always has a valid value between 50 and 300. Default is 100, indicating normal size. WPF defines a DIP as 1/96". Open Dental uses 11.5 DIPs for nearly all fonts on desktop version. Old Microsoft font sizes were based on 1/72", so 11.5 converts to old 8.6. Android defines a DIP as 1/160". Typical recommended font size on Android seems to be about 16, which translates to 9.6 MS DIPs or 7.2 old Windows font. In other words, recommended phone fonts are physically slightly smaller than desktop fonts. EForms uses font sizes based on 100% being a standard normal size. 100% equates to 11.5 on desktop, probably about 16 on Android phones, and whatever our engineers come up with for tablets. By doing it this way, we do not have to explain anything complicated to users, and they also have very good control over font sizes.

column.name: IsRequired
column.summary: False by default. If this is set to true, the patient will be required to fill out the field. If conditional logic causes a required field to not show, it will not enforce the requirement. The only checkboxes that allow this field are AllergiesNone and ProblemsNone. For those fields, this makes sure that either an allergy/prob was checked or None was checked.

column.name: ConditionalParent
column.summary: This string is the label of the field that acts as the parent for conditional logic. Empty string by default indicates no parent. Truncated to the first 255 characters.

column.name: LabelAlign
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormLabelAlign 0-TopLeft, 1-LeftLeft, 2-Right. Only used in RadioButtons for now.

Enumeration.name: EnumEFormLabelAlign
Enumeration.summary: Describes where the label is in relation to the rest of the field.

EnumValue._: 0-Default. Above the radiobuttons, aligned left.
EnumValue.name: TopLeft

EnumValue._: 1-Left of the remainder of the field, and left aligned within that space.
EnumValue.name: LeftLeft

EnumValue._: 2-Right of the remainder of the field, and left aligned within that space.
EnumValue.name: Right

column.name: SpaceBelow
column.summary: The amount of space below each field. Overrides the form and global defaults. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space. If multiple fields are stacked horizontally, then only the right-most field can have this field set.

column.name: ReportableName
column.summary: Allows reporting on fields that don't have DbLink.

column.name: IsLocked
column.summary: If a field is locked, it stops a patient from editing the text when presented to them. Example is a consent form. Only available for TextField and CheckBox. This flag inherits from EFormFieldDef and there's no UI to change it once it's in EFormField. In FrmEFormFillEdit, this field has no effect because offices still need to be able to edit. So the field only has an effect when filling out by a patient on the web or in eClipboard. Patients can still use FrmEFormFillEdit, and in that case, it's assumed that office staff is watching to make sure they don't change the text.

column.name: Border
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormBorder 0-none, 1-3D. Shaded borders are optional on each field. They are on by default when most fields are added. But they don't make sense in some cases, like labels and stacks of Y/N radio buttons for allergies. When a border is present, any single row textbox inside it gets shown as a single underline instead of a rectangle. If the textbox has text wrapping turned on, it will always be a rectangle.

EnumValue._: 0-No border
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1-3D border with gradient shadows and rounded corners
EnumValue.name: ThreeD

column.name: IsWidthPercentage
column.summary: False=DIPs / pixels at 96 dpi, True=Percentage. There is no mechanisms for "fill remainder" or "auto size to contents". There is no allowed mixing of fixed and percentage on the same row. Wrap won't happen until all columns have hit their MinWidth. If someone specifies percentages that add up to more than 100, that's ok. We will proportionally adapt. So in addition to expected percentages like 30-30-40, the user would get the same behavior by using 150-150-200. Let's use the example of 150-150-200 and assume MinWidths were 110-100-100. If available width was 400, then the widths would be 120-120-160. If available width was 330, then the widths would be 110-94-126, or (minWidth)-3/7-4/7. Below 310, they would start wrapping. If percentages add up to less than 100, then they might stop short of 100%. For example, 25-25-25 would come up short. They would continue to occupy 75% of available space until the space got so small that they started to hit their MinWidths. Let's assume MinWidths in that example were 50-100-100. If available width was 600, then the widths would be 150-150-150 (still 75%). If available width was 300, then the widths would be 75-100-100 (only 92%). Below 250, they would start wrapping.

column.name: MinWidth
column.summary: Only used with IsWidthPercentage.  If left blank/0, then no minimum width. A number might be present here but will be ignored if IsWidthPercentage is false.

column.name: WidthLabel
column.summary: If the label is to the left of the field, this is the width of that label. Only used for RadioButtons right now because that's the only type that allows labels to the left. In RadioButtons, this is helpful to allow a stack of radioButtons to line up. Default is 0 to indicate automatic.

column.name: SpaceToRight
column.summary: The amount of space to the right of each field. Overrides the form and global defaults. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space. Not used for SigBox or MedicationList which use form level instead.

column.name: AutoImport
column.summary: The moment the patient completes the form, this field will import to the database. The field should also be required to prevent clearing it out. For example, even a field like last name needs to be able to change if patient got married or divorced, if office misspelled it, if child's last name is different, etc. Patient knows best. Also works great for address. This is important for multiple forms in a family to prevent duplicate entry of address, etc.

column.name: PrefillFromGuar
column.summary: If db value is empty for the patient, then prefill from guarantor. Used for addresses to avoid double entry for children.

table.name: eformfielddef
table.summary:
Individual fields for the EForm. Each field generally includes a label and a value. Links to a EFormDef by FKey to eformdef.EFormDefNum.
            NOTE: If any new fields get added to this class and EFormField, make sure to add them to the methods EFormFields.FromDef and EFormFields.ToDef

column.name: EFormFieldDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EFormDefNum
column.summary: FKey to eformdef.EFormDefNum

column.name: FieldType
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormFieldType 0-TextField, 1-Label, 2-CheckBox, etc.

EnumValue._: 0-A textbox that the user can type into. Frequently tied to a database field. Can frequently be prefilled from database if desired. In Sheets, this was two different field types: InputField and OutputField.
EnumValue.name: TextField

EnumValue._: 1-This can be used for a label, heading, title, paragraph, etc. These also support the exact same replacement fields as in sheets StaticText. See the extensive comments on the ValueLabel field.
EnumValue.name: Label

EnumValue._: 2-Some sort of textbox that's optimized for date input.
EnumValue.name: DateField

EnumValue._: 3-Simple checkbox that can be tied to a db field.
EnumValue.name: CheckBox

EnumValue._: 4-Not a single radiobutton, but a group of them.
EnumValue.name: RadioButtons

EnumValue._: 5-A signature box, directly on the screen with stylus/mouse. Just the drawing, no encryption to tie it to the data yet.
EnumValue.name: SigBox

EnumValue._: 7-A Medication List is a complex field. It consists of a list of medications with an optional second column for strength and frequency. Each medication has a Delete button to its right. There is also an Add button and a None checkbox at the bottom. The None checkbox only shows when the list is empty and allows satisfying a 'required' flag. There is no way to indicate 'no changes', but the office is free to add a separate No Changes checkbox below this list which doesn't actually do anything but which can serve as a visual indicator.
EnumValue.name: MedicationList

EnumValue._: 8-This is a complex field that includes a popup window. On the form, it shows as a read-only textbox with a button beside it that says 'Pick'. The popup window has a list of patients that are pulled from the live office db in real time. In that window, there's also an Add button which actually adds a new patient to the office database. For now, there's no mechanism to edit existing patients in that list. There's also a second popup window to enter the name and bd of a new patient. The only use so far is to pick insurance plan subscriber, but more uses are planned such as guarantor, guardian, adding children to family, etc.
EnumValue.name: PatientList

column.name: DbLink
column.summary: If this field is importable, then this links to a db field. The list of available fields for each type is in EFormFieldsAvailable. Users can pick from that list. Identical list as in Sheets. It's string-based instead of enum, just like Sheets, because it's too complex to use an enum, even for our reduced number of items. None is always represented in UI as "None" and in db as empty string. All DbLinks are available on all form types to give users more flexibility. Checkboxes can have DBLinks that look like "allergy:...", "med:...", or "problem:..."

column.name: ValueLabel
column.summary:
Used differently for different types:
            TextField, DateField, CheckBox: The label next to or above the textbox, or checkbox.RadioButtons: The label above the group of radiobuttons. Labels on each radiobutton are in PickListVis.Label: This label is the only thing that shows. A label is always a WPF FlowDocument, which is an XML format. This allows extensive rich text formatting, like bold, color, paragraph formatting, etc. This format can be used directly in OD proper, but it will need to be converted for some other programming languages using external tools. BUT, prior to that, it must be run through a method that adjusts all the font sizes. FlowDocuments only support absolute font sizes instead of relative font sizes. We use 11.5 as the base font size and all other fonts are considered to be relative to this base. So if a font size of 13.8 is present in the FlowDocument, that does not mean to use 13.8; it instead means to use 120%. If your chosen base font size on a mobile device is 16, then the conversion method needs to convert the 13.8 to 19.2 prior to using the FlowDocument.PageBreak: Not used.SigBox: Optional label above sig box.MedicationList: This holds an EFormMedListLayout object, serialized as json, including the Title, column headers, column widths, etc.PatientList: This holds an EFormPatListLayout object, serialized as json, including Label.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: 0 based.

column.name: PickListVis
column.summary: Pipe delimited list of strings, used for radioButtons, future comboBoxes, etc. This is the list of items that are visible to the patient. Setup enforces same number of items in PickListDb for 1:1 match. This list allows customization of what the patient sees vs what's in the db. Example: Vis=Hispanic, Db=2135-2. Example: Vis=Do Not Call, Db=DoNotCall. For radiobuttons, the number of items in the lists determines the number of radiobuttons to show to the patient. These editable lists also allow excluding some db options from being visible to patient. Example: Ins Relationship has 9 options, but only 4 of them are really used in dentistry. Just leave the other 5 off and force them to pick one of the 4. But it is also not required for them to pick one. Example: For Marital Status, you might only show Married and Child, excluding Divorced and Single from the pick list. The unselected state then represents no change, so an existing patient could leave both radio buttons unchecked and their status would remain Divorced or Single. However, we currently lack a feature to let them uncheck a radiobutton that is already checked. This is a rare edge case that nearly nobody will care about. You can also have a row with no db value. For example, a visible value of Separated might have no corresponding db value entered. In that case, an import would not cause any change to the existing db value. These lists also allow two radioButtons to represent one db item. Example: Gender Other in db can be expanded to show patient both Nonbinary and Other. When patient picks either of these, it goes into the db as Other. The lists also allow any or all items to be empty with no label. Example: Y/N radiobuttons for a series of allergies. Y/N label at top, but none of the radiobuttons need labels. When translation is added later, it will translate this list, not the PickListDb. PickListVis will, by default, simply be exactly the same as PickListDb. In this state, what the patient sees is the same as what's in the db. Must have at least two items for now.

column.name: PickListDb
column.summary: Pipe delimited list of strings, used for radioButtons, future comboBoxes, etc. This is the list of items as they would be stored in the database. See PickListVis above for examples of how to use. The value chosen from this list is what will be stored in the ValueString field. Never show this value to the patient.

column.name: IsHorizStacking
column.summary: Typically false. Set to true to cause this field to get stacked horizontally compared to its previous sibling. Example might be to set State and Zip fields to true. This request will be ignored if screen is too small, like on a phone. We don't allow this option for RadioButtons because they already stack horizontally, and that would be confusing. The following types are not allowed to stack: SigBox, PageBreak, MedicationList.

column.name: IsTextWrap
column.summary: Only applies when this is a TextField. Default is false, which creates a single row textbox that scrolls horizontally if text is too long. Set to true to cause text to wrap instead. This will cause the box to grow to fit the text.

column.name: Width
column.summary: This stores either pixel width or percentage width, depending on IsWidthPercentage. In either case, if this is blank/0, then width will be 100% of what's available. The discussion here is for fixed widths. See IsWidthPercentage for discussion of percentage widths. If fields are stacked horizontally, then they will wrap when they hit screen width. So horizontally stacked fields may end up vertically stacked on a small screen. But if a single field is still set to be wider than the current screen, it will shrink to fit the screen. This width uses WPF DIPs which are 1/96". Android phones define DIPs differently; they use 1/160" per DIP. But if you are using a language like Flutter, they are handling that conversion for you in the background. Regardless, we will be ignoring DIPs and scaling based solely on font size. The reason for this is to make fonts and boxes all look proportionally the same on both OD proper and in eClipboard. So assuming you use 14 flutter logical pixels for 100% font vs 11.5 in WPF, the conversion would look like this: Width/11.5*14. Notice that we are only converting based on font size. This makes our converted width a near perfect fit for the same text as the original. Width is only available on the field types that are h-stackable.

column.name: FontScale
column.summary: Applies to both the label on the field and the field itself. Never 0. Does not apply to Label types, though, since those are only handled by editing the rich text. Always has a valid value between 50 and 300. Default is 100, indicating normal size. WPF defines a DIP as 1/96". Open Dental uses 11.5 DIPs for nearly all fonts on desktop version. Old Microsoft font sizes were based on 1/72", so 11.5 converts to old 8.6. Android defines a DIP as 1/160". Typical recommended font size on Android seems to be about 16, which translates to 9.6 MS DIPs or 7.2 old Windows font. In other words, recommended phone fonts are physically slightly smaller than desktop fonts. EForms uses font sizes based on 100% being a standard normal size. 100% equates to 11.5 on desktop, probably about 16 on Android phones, and whatever our engineers come up with for tablets. By doing it this way, we do not have to explain anything complicated to users, and they also have very good control over font sizes.

column.name: IsRequired
column.summary: False by default. If this is set to true, the patient will be required to fill out the field. If conditional logic causes a required field to not show, it will not enforce the requirement. The only checkboxes that allow this field are AllergiesNone and ProblemsNone. For those fields, this makes sure that either an allergy/prob was checked or None was checked.

column.name: ConditionalParent
column.summary: This string is the label of the field that acts as the parent for conditional logic. Empty string by default indicates no parent. Truncated to the first 255 characters.

column.name: LabelAlign
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormLabelAlign 0-TopLeft, 1-LeftLeft, 2-Right. Only used in RadioButtons for now.

Enumeration.name: EnumEFormLabelAlign
Enumeration.summary: Describes where the label is in relation to the rest of the field.

EnumValue._: 0-Default. Above the radiobuttons, aligned left.
EnumValue.name: TopLeft

EnumValue._: 1-Left of the remainder of the field, and left aligned within that space.
EnumValue.name: LeftLeft

EnumValue._: 2-Right of the remainder of the field, and left aligned within that space.
EnumValue.name: Right

column.name: SpaceBelow
column.summary: The amount of space below each field. Overrides the form and global default. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space. If multiple fields are stacked horizontally, then only the right-most field can have this field set.

column.name: ReportableName
column.summary: Allows reporting on fields that don't have DbLink.

column.name: IsLocked
column.summary: If a field is locked, it stops a patient from editing the text when presented to them. Example is a consent form. Only available for TextField and CheckBox. This flag is set here in the EFormFieldDef and then EFormField inherits it with no UI to change it later. See additional notes in EFormField.

column.name: Border
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormBorder 0-none, 1-3D. Shaded borders are optional on each field. They are on by default when most fields are added. But they don't make sense in some cases, like labels and stacks of Y/N radio buttons for allergies. When a border is present, any single row textbox inside it gets shown as a single underline instead of a rectangle. If the textbox has text wrapping turned on, it will always be a rectangle.

EnumValue._: 0-No border
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1-3D border with gradient shadows and rounded corners
EnumValue.name: ThreeD

column.name: IsWidthPercentage
column.summary: False=DIPs / pixels at 96 dpi, True=Percentage. There is no mechanisms for "fill remainder" or "auto size to contents". There is no allowed mixing of fixed and percentage on the same row. Wrap won't happen until all columns have hit their MinWidth. If someone specifies percentages that add up to more than 100, that's ok. We will proportionally adapt. So in addition to expected percentages like 30-30-40, the user would get the same behavior by using 150-150-200. Let's use the example of 150-150-200 and assume MinWidths were 110-100-100. If available width was 400, then the widths would be 120-120-160. If available width was 330, then the widths would be 110-94-126, or (minWidth)-3/7-4/7. Below 310, they would start wrapping. If percentages add up to less than 100, then they might stop short of 100%. For example, 25-25-25 would come up short. They would continue to occupy 75% of available space until the space got so small that they started to hit their MinWidths. Let's assume MinWidths in that example were 50-100-100. If available width was 600, then the widths would be 150-150-150 (still 75%). If available width was 300, then the widths would be 75-100-100 (only 92%). Below 250, they would start wrapping.

column.name: MinWidth
column.summary: Only used with IsWidthPercentage.  If left blank/0, then no minimum width. A number might be present here but will be ignored if IsWidthPercentage is false.

column.name: WidthLabel
column.summary: If the label is to the left of the field, this is the width of that label. Only used for RadioButtons right now because that's the only type that allows labels to the left. In RadioButtons, this is helpful to allow a stack of radioButtons to line up. Default is 0 to indicate automatic.

column.name: SpaceToRight
column.summary: The amount of space to the right of each field. Overrides the form and global defaults. -1 indicates to use default. That way, 0 means 0 space. Not used for SigBox or MedicationList which use form level instead.

column.name: AutoImport
column.summary: The moment the patient completes the form, this field will import to the database. The field should also be required to prevent clearing it out. For example, even a field like last name needs to be able to change if patient got married or divorced, if office misspelled it, if child's last name is different, etc. Patient knows best. Also works great for address. This is important for multiple forms in a family to prevent duplicate entry of address, etc.

column.name: PrefillFromGuar
column.summary: If db value is empty for the patient, then prefill from guarantor. Used for addresses to avoid double entry for children.

table.name: eformimportrule
table.summary: This table is present, but it does absolutely nothing because we never finished implementing it. In the future: Each row is a rule that controls how form import logic works. It currently also applies to sheets. Clearly, if there's no row for a specific field, then a global rule handles it. Since there are 4 different situations, we might need 4 global rules just to start. But since users might forget to add them, if any of the 4 is missing, then that situation behaves the old way: action is Review.

column.name: EFormImportRuleNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FieldName
column.summary: If empty, this is a global rule. if the field name matches a DbLink value, then this applies to the Db field. Otherwise, it must be a Non-db field. It will first try to match a reportable name and then a label.

column.name: Situation
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormImportSituation

EnumValue._: 0-Original blank and entered new value
EnumValue.name: New

EnumValue._: 3-Setup UI explains the fields that this can apply to.
EnumValue.name: Invalid

column.name: Action
column.summary: Enum:EnumEFormImportAction

EnumValue._: 3-Setup UI explains the fixes that can be made.
EnumValue.name: Fix

table.name: ehramendment
table.summary: Used in EHR only.  Stores an entry indicating whether the office has accepted or denied the amendment.  Amendments can be verbal or written requests to add information to the patient's record.  The provider can either scan / import the document or create a detailed description that indicates what was verbally requested or where the document can be found.

column.name: EhrAmendmentNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: IsAccepted
column.summary: Enum:YN Y=accepted, N=denied, U=requested.

Enumeration.name: YN
Enumeration.summary: 0=Unknown,1=Yes, or 2=No. UI can be tricky. Some success with a 3x1 listbox, multicolumn, see FormPatientEdit. Radiobuttons are also an option.  You can also use another YN variable to store unknown, and then use a click event on the checkbox to change to Y or N.  You can also use a three state checkbox if you translate properly between the enums.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Description or user-defined location of the amendment.

column.name: Source
column.summary: Enum:AmendmentSource Patient, Provider, Organization, Other.  Required.

Enumeration.name: AmendmentSource
Enumeration.summary: Source Enumeration

column.name: SourceName
column.summary: User-defined name of the amendment source.  For example, a patient name or organization name.

column.name: FileName
column.summary: The file is stored in the A-Z folder in 'EhrAmendments' folder.  This field stores the name of the file.  The files are named automatically based on Date/time along with EhrAmendmentNum for uniqueness.  This meets the requirement of "appending" to the patient's record.

column.name: RawBase64
column.summary: The raw file data encoded as base64.  Only used if there is no AtoZ folder.  This meets the requirement of "appending" to the patient's record.

column.name: DateTRequest
column.summary: Date and time of the amendment request.

column.name: DateTAcceptDeny
column.summary: Date and time of the amendment acceptance or denial.  If there is a date here, then the IsAccepted will be set.

column.name: DateTAppend
column.summary: Date and time of the file being appended to the amendment or a link provided.

table.name: ehraptobs
table.summary: An EHR appointment observation.  Needed for syndromic surveillance messaging.  Each syndromic message requires at least one observation.

column.name: EhrAptObsNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: AptNum
column.summary: FK to appointment.AptNum.  There can be an unlimited number of observations per appointment.

column.name: IdentifyingCode
column.summary: Enum:EhrAptObsIdentifier - Used in HL7 OBX-3 for syndromic surveillance.

EnumValue._: 0 - Body temperature:Temp:Enctrfrst:Patient:Qn:	Loinc code 11289-6.
EnumValue.name: BodyTemp

EnumValue._: 1 - Illness or injury onset date and time:TmStp:Pt:Patient:Qn:	Loinc code 11368-8.
EnumValue.name: DateIllnessOrInjury

EnumValue._: 2 - Age Time Patient Reported	Loinc code 21612-7.
EnumValue.name: PatientAge

EnumValue._: 3 - Diagnosis.preliminary:Imp:Pt:Patient:Nom:	Loinc code 44833-2.
EnumValue.name: PrelimDiag

EnumValue._: 4 - Triage note:Find:Pt:Emergency department:Doc:	Loinc code 54094-8.
EnumValue.name: TriageNote

EnumValue._: 5 - Oxygen saturation:MFr:Pt:BldA:Qn:Pulse oximetry	Loinc code 59408-5.
EnumValue.name: OxygenSaturation

EnumValue._: 6 - Chief complaint:Find:Pt:Patient:Nom:Reported	Loinc code 8661-1.
EnumValue.name: CheifComplaint

EnumValue._: 7 - Treating Facility Identifier	PHINQUESTION code SS001.
EnumValue.name: TreatFacilityID

EnumValue._: 8 - Treating Facility Location	PHINQUESTION code SS002.
EnumValue.name: TreatFacilityLocation

EnumValue._: 9 - Facility / Visit Type	PHINQUESTION code SS003.
EnumValue.name: VisitType

column.name: ValType
column.summary: Enum:EhrAptObsType .  Used in HL7 OBX-2 for syndromic surveillance.  Identifies the data type for the observation value in ValReported.

EnumValue._: 0 - This should only be used with EhrAptObsIdentifier.TreatFacilityLocation.
EnumValue.name: Address

column.name: ValReported
column.summary: The value of the observation. The value format must match the ValType.  This field could be text, a datetime, a code, etc..  Used in HL7 OBX-5 for syndromic surveillance.

column.name: UcumCode
column.summary: Used in HL7 OBX-6 for syndromic surveillance when ValType is Numeric (otherwise left blank).

column.name: ValCodeSystem
column.summary:
When ValType is Coded, then this contains the code system corresponding to the code in ValReported.  When ValType is not Coded, then this field should be blank.
            Allowed values are LOINC,SNOMEDCT,ICD9,ICD10.

column.name: EhrCarePlanNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: SnomedEducation
column.summary: Snomed code describing the type of educational instruction provided.  Limited to terms descending from the Snomed 409073007 (Education Hierarchy).

column.name: Instructions
column.summary: Instructions provided to the patient.

column.name: DatePlanned
column.summary: This field does not help much with care plan instructions, but will be more helpful for other types of care plans if we expand in the future (for example, planned procedures).  We also saw examples where this date was included in the human readable part of a CCD, but not in the machine readable part.

table.name: ehrmeasure
table.summary: For EHR module, automate measure calculation.

column.name: EhrMeasureNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MeasureType
column.summary: Enum:EhrMeasureType

EnumValue._: 19- Summary of care record for transition or referral.
EnumValue.name: SummaryOfCare

column.name: Numerator
column.summary: 0-100, -1 indicates not entered yet.

column.name: Denominator
column.summary: 0-100, -1 indicates not entered yet.

table.name: ehrmeasureevent
table.summary: Stores events for EHR that are needed for reporting purposes.

column.name: EhrMeasureEventNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateTEvent
column.summary: Date and time of measure event.

column.name: EventType
column.summary: Enum:EhrMeasureEventType .

EnumValue._: 4, For one office visit.
EnumValue.name: ClinicalSummaryProvidedToPt

EnumValue._: 7 - When Summary of Care is provided in one of the following ways: Printed, exported, or sent to the patient portal (for referrals To doctors).
EnumValue.name: SummaryOfCareProvidedToDr

EnumValue._: 14 - When a Summary of Care is provided to a doctor electronically in one of the following ways: Exported (we assume they send another way), or a Direct message is sent with Summary of Care attached.
EnumValue.name: SummaryOfCareProvidedToDrElectronic

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: MoreInfo
column.summary: Used to provide extra information about a measure event.  Not typically used.

column.name: CodeValueEvent
column.summary: The code for this event.  Example: TobaccoUseAssessed can be one of three LOINC codes: 11366-2 History of tobacco use Narrative, 68535-4 Have you used tobacco in the last 30 days, and 68536-2 Have you used smokeless tobacco product in the last 30 days.

column.name: CodeSystemEvent
column.summary: The code system name for the event code.  Examples: LOINC, SNOMEDCT.

column.name: CodeValueResult
column.summary: The code for this event result.  Example: A TobaccoUseAssessed event type could result in a finding of SNOMED code 8517006 - Ex-smoker (finding).  There are 54 allowed tobacco user/non-user codes, and the user is allowed to select from any SNOMED code if they wish, for a TobaccoUseAssessed event.

column.name: CodeSystemResult
column.summary: The code system for this event result.  Example: SNOMEDCT,

column.name: FKey
column.summary: A foreign key to a table associated with the EventType.  0 indicates not in use.  Used to properly count denominators for specific measure types.

column.name: TobaccoCessationDesire
column.summary: How eager a tobacco user is to quit using tobacco.  Scale of 1-10.

column.name: DateStartTobacco
column.summary: The date the patient started using tobacco.

table.name: ehrnotperformed
table.summary: For EHR module, these are all the items 'not performed' on patients.  Each row will link to the ehrcode table to retrieve relevant data.  To join this table to the ehrcode table you must join on CodeValue and CodeSystem.  Some items will have associated reasons attached to specify why it was not performed.  Those reasons will also be defined in the ehrcode table, so it may be necessary to join with that table again for the data relevant to the reason.

column.name: EhrNotPerformedNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: CodeValue
column.summary: This will be a FK to a specific code system table identified by the CodeSystem column.  The code for this item from one of the code systems supported.  Examples: 90656 or 442333005.

column.name: CodeSystem
column.summary: FK to codesystem.CodeSystemName. The code system name for this code.  Possible values are: CPT, CVX, LOINC, SNOMEDCT.

column.name: CodeValueReason
column.summary: FK to ehrcode.CodeValue.  This code may not exist in the ehrcode table, it may have been chosen from a bigger list of available codes.  In that case, this will be a FK to a specific code system table identified by the CodeSystem column.  The code for the reason the item was not performed from one of the code systems supported.  Examples: 182856006 or 419808006.

column.name: CodeSystemReason
column.summary: FK to codesystem.CodeSystemName. The code system name for this code.  Possible value is: SNOMEDCT.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Relevant notes for this not performed item.  Just in case users want it, does not get reported in EHR quality measure reporting.

column.name: DateEntry
column.summary: The date and time this item was created.  Can be edited to the date and time the item actually occurred.

table.name: ehrpatient
table.summary: Patient information needed for EHR.  1:1 relation to patient table.  They are stored here because we want to try to keep the size of the patient table a bit smaller. Some non-EHR columns have also been added, which isn't a big deal.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Also the primary key for this table. Always one to one relationship with patient table.  A new patient might not have an entry here until needed.

column.name: MotherMaidenFname
column.summary: Mother's maiden first name.  Exported in HL7 PID-6 for immunization messages.

column.name: MotherMaidenLname
column.summary: Mother's maiden last name.  Exported in HL7 PID-6 for immunization messages.

column.name: VacShareOk
column.summary: Enum:YN  Indicates whether or not the patient wants to share their vaccination information with other EHRs.  Used in immunization export.

Enumeration.name: YN
Enumeration.summary: 0=Unknown,1=Yes, or 2=No. UI can be tricky. Some success with a 3x1 listbox, multicolumn, see FormPatientEdit. Radiobuttons are also an option.  You can also use another YN variable to store unknown, and then use a click event on the checkbox to change to Y or N.  You can also use a three state checkbox if you translate properly between the enums.

column.name: MedicaidState
column.summary:
The abbreviation for the state for the patient's MedicaidID.
            Displayed in patient information window, used to validate the length of the MedicaidID.

column.name: SexualOrientation
column.summary: The patient's sexual orientation. Stored as a SNOMED code or HL7 null flavor.

column.name: GenderIdentity
column.summary: The patient's gender identity. Stored as a SNOMED code or HL7 null flavor.

column.name: SexualOrientationNote
column.summary: Will be blank unless SexualOrientation is OTH, additional orientation.

column.name: GenderIdentityNote
column.summary: Will be blank unless GenderIdentity is OTH, additional gender identity.

column.name: DischargeDate
column.summary: Used in hospitals. Used to track patients discharge date.

table.name: ehrprovkey
table.summary: Used to store and track Ehr Provider Keys.  There can be multiple EhrProvKeys per provider.

column.name: EhrProvKeyNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Only used by HQ for generating keys for customers.  Will always be 0 for non-HQ users.

column.name: LName
column.summary: The provider LName.

column.name: FName
column.summary: The provider FName.

column.name: ProvKey
column.summary: The key assigned to the provider

column.name: FullTimeEquiv
column.summary: Usually 1.  Can be less, like .5 or .25 to indicate possible discount is justified.

column.name: Notes
column.summary: Any notes that the tech wishes to include regarding this situation.

column.name: YearValue
column.summary: Required when generating a new provider key.  It is used to determine annual EHR eligibility.  Format will always be YY.

table.name: ehrquarterlykey
table.summary: Also used by OD customer support to store and track Ehr Quarterly Keys for customers.

column.name: EhrQuarterlyKeyNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: YearValue
column.summary: Example 11

column.name: QuarterValue
column.summary: Example 2

column.name: PracticeName
column.summary: The customer must have this exact practice name entered in practice setup.

column.name: KeyValue
column.summary: The calculated key value, tied to year, quarter, and practice name.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Always zero for customer databases.  When used by OD customer support, this is the customer num.

column.name: Notes
column.summary: Any notes that the tech wishes to include regarding this situation.

table.name: ehrsummaryccd
table.summary: Can also be a CCR.  Received CCDs/CCRs are stored both here and in emailattach.  Sent CCDs are not saved here, but are only stored in emailattach.  To display a saved Ccd, it is combined with an internal stylesheet.

column.name: EhrSummaryCcdNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateSummary
column.summary: Date that this Ccd was received.

column.name: ContentSummary
column.summary: The xml content of the received text file.

column.name: EmailAttachNum
column.summary: FK to emailattach.EmailAttachNum.  The Direct email attachment where the CCD xml message came from.  Needed to sync PatNum with the email PatNum if the PatNum is changed on the email.

table.name: ehrtrigger
table.summary: CDS Triggers when referenced in UI. Used for CDS automation.  May later be expanded to replace "automation."

column.name: EhrTriggerNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Short description to describe the trigger.

column.name: DemographicsList
column.summary: Age, Gender.  Can be multiple age entries but only one gender entry as coma delimited values.  Example: " age,>18  age,<=55  gender,male"

column.name: LabLoincList
column.summary: List of loinc codes padded with spaces.

column.name: VitalLoincList
column.summary: Examples:  Height,>=72  Weight<,100  BMI=  (BP currently not implemented.)

column.name: Instructions
column.summary: The reccomended course of action for this intervention.

column.name: Bibliography
column.summary: Bibliographic information, not a URL.

column.name: Cardinality
column.summary: Enum:MatchCardinality Requires One, OneOfEachCategory, TwoOrMore, or All for trigger to match.

EnumValue._: 0 - If any one of the conditions are met from any of the categories.
EnumValue.name: One

EnumValue._: 1 - Must have one match from each of the categories with set values. Categories are :Medication, Allergy, Problem, Vitals, Age, Gender, and Lab Results.
EnumValue.name: OneOfEachCategory

EnumValue._: 2 - Must match any two conditions, may be from same category.
EnumValue.name: TwoOrMore

EnumValue._: 3 - Must match every code defined in the EhrTrigger.
EnumValue.name: All

table.name: electid
table.summary: Corresponds to the electid table in the database. Helps with entering elecronic/payor id's as well as keeping track of the specific carrier requirements. Only used by the X12 format.

column.name: ElectIDNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PayorID
column.summary: aka Electronic ID.  A simple string. This is not necessarily unique between different CarrierNames.  Also, different clearinghouses use different systems of PayorIDs.

column.name: CarrierName
column.summary: Used when doing a search.

column.name: IsMedicaid
column.summary: True if medicaid. Then, the billing and treating providers will have their Medicaid ID's attached.

column.name: ProviderTypes
column.summary: Integers separated by commas. Each long represents a ProviderSupplementalID type that is required by this insurance. Usually only used for BCBS or other carriers that require supplemental provider id's.  Even if we don't put the supplemental types in here, the user can still add them.  This just helps by doing an additional check for known required types.

column.name: Comments
column.summary: Any comments. Usually includes enrollment requirements and descriptions of how to use the provider id's supplied by the carrier because they might call them by different names.

column.name: CommBridge
column.summary: Enum:EclaimsCommBridge Where this Electronic ID came from. Will be 0 if created by the user. Currently, only ClaimConnect and EDS are supported.

Enumeration.name: EclaimsCommBridge
Enumeration.summary: Each clearinghouse can have a hard-coded comm bridge which handles all the communications of transfering the claim files to the clearinghouse/carrier.  Does not just include X12, but can include any format at all.

EnumValue._: 0-No comm bridge will be activated. The claim files will be created to the specified path, but they will not be uploaded.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 9 Canadian clearinghouse.
EnumValue.name: ITRANS

EnumValue._: 15 Canadian clearinghouse.
EnumValue.name: Claimstream

EnumValue._: 16 UK clearinghouse.
EnumValue.name: NHS

EnumValue._: 21 Canadian clearinghouse.  Similar to ITRANS except supports certificate and carrier list web fetching.
EnumValue.name: ITRANS2

column.name: Attributes
column.summary: Comma delimited list of which PayerAttributes of a CommBridge are supported by this Electronic ID. Example: "0,2,8". Enum values for either EnumClaimConnectPayerAttributes or EnumEDSPayerAttributes.

table.name: emailaddress
table.summary: Stores all the connection info for one email address.  Linked to clinic by clinic.EmailAddressNum.  Sends email based on patient's clinic.

column.name: EmailAddressNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SMTPserver
column.summary: For example smtp.gmail.com

column.name: EmailPassword
column.summary: Password associated with this email address.  Encrypted when stored in the database and decrypted before using.

column.name: ServerPort
column.summary: Usually 587, sometimes 25 or 465.

column.name: SenderAddress
column.summary: The email address of the sender as it should appear to the recipient.

column.name: Pop3ServerIncoming
column.summary: For example pop.gmail.com

column.name: ServerPortIncoming
column.summary: Usually 110, sometimes 995.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary:
FK to userod.UserNum.  Associates a user with this email address.  A user may only have one email address associated with them.
            Can be 0 if no user is associated with this email address.

column.name: AccessToken
column.summary: Needed for OAuth.

column.name: RefreshToken
column.summary: Needed for OAuth.

column.name: DownloadInbox
column.summary: When true, this will allow the user to download emails to their inbox.

column.name: QueryString
column.summary: Allows gmail users to specify search parameters

column.name: AuthenticationType
column.summary: Enum:OAuthType None=0,Google=1,Microsoft=2.  Indicates which OAuth type to use for the email address.

EnumValue._: 0 - Aka Using Password. Not using OAuth
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Using OAuth for Google
EnumValue.name: Google

EnumValue._: 2 - Using OAuth for Microsoft
EnumValue.name: Microsoft

table.name: emailattach
table.summary: Keeps track of one file attached to an email.  Multiple files can be attached to an email using this method.

column.name: EmailAttachNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EmailMessageNum
column.summary: FK to emailmessage.EmailMessageNum.  0 if EmailTemplateNum is set, otherwise must have a value.

column.name: DisplayedFileName
column.summary: The name of the file that shows on the email.  For example: tooth2.jpg.

column.name: ActualFileName
column.summary: The actual file is stored in the A-Z folder in EmailAttachments.  This field stores the sub directories and name of the file.  The files are named automatically based on Date/time along with a random number.  This ensures that they will be sequential as well as unique.

column.name: EmailTemplateNum
column.summary: FK to emailtemplate.EmailTemplateNum.  0 if EmailMessageNum is set, otherwise must have a value.

table.name: emailautograph
table.summary: A manually created autograph that can be inserted at the bottom of an outgoing email.

column.name: EmailAutographNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Description of the autograph.  This is what the user sees when picking an autograph.

column.name: EmailAddress
column.summary: Email address(es) that this autograph is associated with.  An autograph can be associated with multiple addresses.

column.name: AutographText
column.summary: The actual text of the autograph.

column.name: EmailHostingTemplateNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TemplateName
column.summary: Name of the template.

column.name: Subject
column.summary: Default subject line.

column.name: BodyPlainText
column.summary: Body of the email

column.name: BodyHTML
column.summary: Body of the email. When email is regular html this will only contain the body text. Will contain full html when email type is RawHtml

column.name: TemplateId
column.summary: The email hosting template's identifier

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum

column.name: EmailTemplateType
column.summary: Enum:EmailType The type of email template this is (Regular HTML or Full HTML)

EnumValue._: 0 - This is a regular email that may contain our special wiki markup. Not converted to html.
EnumValue.name: Regular

EnumValue._: 1 - Html. Basic html email which uses the master template supplied by OD. Template includes header, styles, and the opening body tag. The user only needs to provide the body itself, which can inclcude tags that get automatically replaced.
EnumValue.name: Html

EnumValue._: 2 - More advanced html that does not include the master template. User must provide everything.
EnumValue.name: RawHtml

column.name: TemplateType
column.summary: Enum:PromotionType the type of mass email this template is for

EnumValue._: 0 - Signifies Manually Sent Promotions like from Mass Emails
EnumValue.name: Manual

EnumValue._: 1 - Signifies Birthday Greetings
EnumValue.name: Birthday

EnumValue._: 2 - Promotional Treatment
EnumValue.name: Treatment

EnumValue._: 3 - Special Promotions
EnumValue.name: Special

table.name: emailmessage
table.summary: Stores both sent and received emails, as well as saved emails which are still in composition.

column.name: EmailMessageNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum. The patient whom is sending this message. May be sent by a guarantor on behalf of a dependent.

column.name: ToAddress
column.summary:
Either a single email address or a comma-delimited list of addresses.  
            For web mail messages, this will not be an email address.  Instead, it will be the name of the corresponding patient or provider.

column.name: FromAddress
column.summary: Valid email address.  For web mail messages, this will not be an email address.  Instead, it will be the name of the corresponding patient or provider.

column.name: Subject
column.summary: Subject line.

column.name: BodyText
column.summary: Body of the email

column.name: MsgDateTime
column.summary: Date and time the message was sent. Automated at the UI level.

column.name: SentOrReceived
column.summary: Enum:EmailSentOrReceived Neither, Received, Read, WebMailReceived, WebMailRecdRead, WebMailSent, WebMailSentRead, SentDirect, ReceivedEncrypted, ReceivedDirect, ReadDirect, AckDirectProcessed, AckDirectNotSent

Enumeration.name: EmailSentOrReceived
Enumeration.summary: 0=Neither, 1=Sent, 2=Received, 3=Read, 4=WebMailReceived, 5=WebMailRecdRead, 6=WebMailSent, 7=WebMailSentRead, 8=SentDirect, 9=ReceivedEncrypted, 10=ReceivedDirect, 11=ReadDirect, 12=AckDirectProcessed, 13=AckDirectNotSent

EnumValue._: 0 Unsent
EnumValue.name: Neither

EnumValue._: 1 For regular email only.
EnumValue.name: Sent

EnumValue._: 2 For regular email only.  Shows in Inbox.  Once it's attached to a patient it will also show in Chart module.
EnumValue.name: Received

EnumValue._: 3 For received regular email only.  Has been read.  Shows in Inbox.  Once it's attached to a patient it will also show in Chart module.
EnumValue.name: Read

EnumValue._: 4 WebMail received from patient portal.  Shows in OD Inbox and in pt Chart module.  Also shows in PP as a sent and unread WebMail msg.
EnumValue.name: WebMailReceived

EnumValue._: 5 WebMail received from patient portal that has been marked read.  Shows in the OD Inbox and in pt Chart module.  Also shows in PP as a sent and read WebMail.
EnumValue.name: WebMailRecdRead

EnumValue._: 6 Webmail sent from provider to patient.  Shows in Chart module and also shows in PP as a received and unread WebMail msg.
EnumValue.name: WebMailSent

EnumValue._: 7 Webmail sent from provider to patient and read by patient.  Shows in Chart module and also shows in PP as a received and read WebMail msg.
EnumValue.name: WebMailSentRead

EnumValue._: 8 Sent and encrypted using Direct. Required for counting messages in EHR modules g.1 and g.2, Automated Measure Calculation.
EnumValue.name: SentDirect

EnumValue._: 9 Received email matches application/pkcs7-mime mime type, but could not be decrypted.  Shows in Inbox.  The user can decrypt from FormEmailMessageEdit.  If the user has the correct private key, then the status will change to Read.
EnumValue.name: ReceivedEncrypted

EnumValue._: 10 Received email matches application/pkcs7-mime mime type and has been decrypted.  Shows in Inbox.  Once it's attached to a patient it will also show in Chart module.  When viewing inside of FormEmailMessageEdit, the XML body of the message shows as xhtml instead of raw.  Still need to work on supporting collapsing and expanding, as required for meaningful use in 2014.
EnumValue.name: ReceivedDirect

EnumValue._: 11 For received direct messages.  Has been read.  Shows in Inbox.  Once it's attached to a patient it will also show in Chart module.  When viewing inside of FormEmailMessageEdit, the XML body of the message shows as xhtml.
EnumValue.name: ReadDirect

EnumValue._: 12 Message Delivery Notification (MDN) processed.  Always outgoing.  Indicates to sender that a Direct message was received and decrypted, but not necessarily displayed for the user.  Does not show in patient Chart.  Attached to the same patient as the incoming email which caused the MDN to be sent.
EnumValue.name: AckDirectProcessed

EnumValue._:
13 Message Delivery Notification (MDN) created and saved to db, but not sent yet.  Does not show in patient Chart.  Attached to the same patient as the incoming email which caused the MDN to be created.
            This status is used to try resending MDNs if they fail to send.  The MDN is saved to the db so the unset MDNs can be found easily, and also because MDNs are hard to rebuild again later.
EnumValue.name: AckDirectNotSent

EnumValue._: 14 Email sent via EmailHostingAPI.
EnumValue.name: SecureEmailSent

EnumValue._: 15 Email received via EmailHostingAPI.  Has not been read.
EnumValue.name: SecureEmailReceivedUnread

EnumValue._: 16 Email sent via EmailHostingAPI.  has been read.
EnumValue.name: SecureEmailReceivedRead

EnumValue._: 17 Email failed to send.
EnumValue.name: SendFailed

column.name: RecipientAddress
column.summary:
Copied from the EmailAddress.EmailUsername field when a message is received into the inbox.
            Similar to the ToAddress, except the ToAddress could contain multiple recipient addresses
            or group email address instead. The recipient address helps match the an email to a particular EmailAddress.

column.name: RawEmailIn
column.summary:
For incomming email only.  The raw email contents for encrypted email or email which we had trouble parsing.
            For unencrypted (clear text) email, this will be similar to the raw email except the attachments will be dissolved to prevent db bloating. 
            Can be used for debugging if there are any issues parsing the content.
            This will bloat the database a little bit, but we need it for now to ensure our inbox is working in real world scenarios.
            Might be blank for a few emails downloaded immediately after the email inbox feature was created.

column.name: ProvNumWebMail
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  The provider to whom this message was sent or from whom this message was sent.  Only used when EmailSentOrReceived is WebMailReceived, WebMailRecdRead, WebMailSent, or WebMailSentRead.  Will be 0 if not a web mail message.

column.name: PatNumSubj
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum. Represents the patient to whom this email message is addressed, or from whom it is being sent on behalf of. If guarantor is sending on behalf of self then this field will match PatNum field.

column.name: CcAddress
column.summary: Single address or comma-delimited list of addresses.  User may enter multiple email addresses for visible carbon copies.

column.name: BccAddress
column.summary: Single email address or comma-delimited list of addresses.  User may enter multiple email addresses for blind carbon copies.

column.name: HideIn
column.summary: Enum:HideInFlags None=0,EmailInbox=1,ApptEdit=2,ContrChartProgNotes=4,ContrAccountGridProg.  Indicates which places in the program that should not show this email message, bitwise. 0 means don't hide anywhere.

EnumValue._: 0 - None

EnumValue._: 8 - No Longer Used - Was used to hide email from ContrAcount ProgNotes grid
EnumValue.name: AccountProgNotes

EnumValue._: 16 - Hide email from ContrAcount CommLog grid
EnumValue.name: AccountCommLog

table.name: encounter
table.summary: Mostly used for EHR.  This rigorously records encounters using rich automation, so that reporting can be easy and meaningful.  Encounters can also be tracked separately using billable procedures.  In contrast, encounters in this table are not billable.  There can be multiple encounters at one appointment because there can be different types.

In that case, this will be a FK to a specific code system table identified by the CodeSystem column.  The code for this item from one of the code systems supported.  Examples: 185349003 or 406547006.

column.name: CodeSystem
column.summary: FK to codesystem.CodeSystemName. This will determine which specific code system table the CodeValue is a FK to.  We only allow the following CodeSystems in this table: CDT, CPT, HCPCS, and SNOMEDCT.
