table.name: eservicelog
table.summary: Stores an ongoing record of EServices activity. User not allowed to edit.

column.name: EServiceLogNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: LogDateTime
column.summary: The date and time of the entry. It's value is set when inserting and can never change. Even if a user changes the date on their computer, this remains accurate because it uses server time.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum. Can be 0 if not applicable.

column.name: EServiceType
column.summary: Enum:eServiceType Indicates which eService feature this log entry is associated with, such as Web Sched, Patient Portal, or eClipboard.

Enumeration.name: eServiceType
Enumeration.summary: Specifies which eService feature is involved in the logged action. Some actions correspond to multiple eServices, so this clarifies which specific eService is logged. For example, eServiceAction.WSTimeSlotChoose could be used for WSRecall, WSNewPatient, WSExistingPatient, etc. A combination of eServiceAction.WSTimeSlotChoose and eServiceType.WSRecall would indicate that the patient chose a time slot using WebSched Recall.

EnumValue._: 0. This means the action done was Unknown.
EnumValue.name: Unknown

EnumValue._: 1. This means the action done was a Web Sched Recall.
EnumValue.name: WSRecall

EnumValue._: 2. This means the action done was for a New Patient.
EnumValue.name: WSNewPat

EnumValue._: 3. This means the action done was for an Existing Patient.
EnumValue.name: WSExistingPat

EnumValue._: 4. This means the action done was for a Web Sched ASAP.
EnumValue.name: WSAsap

EnumValue._: 5. This means the action done was for a Patient Portal.
EnumValue.name: PatientPortal

EnumValue._: 6. This means the action done was for a Mobile Checkin.
EnumValue.name: EClipboard

EnumValue._: 7. This means the action done was for a Appointment Confirmations.
EnumValue.name: ApptConfirmations

EnumValue._: 8. This means the action done was for a WebForm.
EnumValue.name: WebForms

EnumValue._: 9. This means the action done was for unspecified WebSched.
EnumValue.name: WSGeneral

EnumValue._: 10. This means the action done was for Arrivals.
EnumValue.name: Arrivals

EnumValue._: 11. This means the action done was for Integrated Texting.
EnumValue.name: IntegratedTexting

EnumValue._: 12. This means the action done was for the mobile app ODTouch.
EnumValue.name: ODTouch

EnumValue._: 13. This means the action done was for the Payment Portal.
EnumValue.name: PaymentPortal

EnumValue._: 14. This means the action done was for the Better Diagnostics AI bridge.
EnumValue.name: BetterDiag

EnumValue._: 15. This means the action was done for Mass Email.
EnumValue.name: MassEmail

EnumValue._: 16. This means the action was done for the Pearl AI bridge. Pearl is technically not an eService, but is logged using this table.
EnumValue.name: Pearl

column.name: EServiceAction
column.summary: Enum:eServiceAction Describes the specific action or event that occurred within the eService, such as scheduling an appointment, submitting a form, or logging in.

Enumeration.name: eServiceAction

Additional information for the log. This is intentionally limited to 255 characters to prevent bloat.
            Add any new uses of this field to the list below. Provide the eServiceAction types and what the Note field represents for those types.
            PPPaymentCreatedByXWeb:				The amount of the payment.

            PPOpenedHostedPaymentForm:			The name of the merchant service that the hosted payment form belongs to.

table.name: eservicesignal
table.summary: Communication item from workstation to OD HQ. Stores the statuses of the eConnector and other eServices.

column.name: EServiceSignalNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ServiceCode
column.summary: Enum eServiceCode. Service which this signal applies to.

column.name: ReasonCategory
column.summary: The enum is at HQ as OpenDentalWebCore.BroadcasterThreadDefs. Can be zero if no grouping is necessary per a given service.

column.name: ReasonCode
column.summary:
The enum is at HQ as OpenDentalWebCore.BroadcasterErrorCodes. This code is used to determine what actions to take and how to process this message. 
            It is a function of ReasonCategory. It will most likely be defined by an enum that lives on HQ-only closed source.

column.name: Severity
column.summary: Enum:eServiceSignalSeverity

Enumeration.name: eServiceSignalSeverity
Enumeration.summary: Used to determine that status of the entire service.  Order of enum is important, from lowest to highest importance.

EnumValue._: Service is not in use and is not supposed to be in use.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 0-Service is not in use and is not supposed to be in use.
EnumValue.name: NotEnabled

EnumValue._: 1-Used to convey information. Does not change the "working" status of the service. Will always be inserted with IsProcess=true.
EnumValue.name: Info

EnumValue._: 2-Service is operational and working as designed. Typcially used for heartbeat and initialization.
EnumValue.name: Working

EnumValue._: 3-Recoverable error has has occurred and no user intervention is required. Typically requires user acknowledgement only.
EnumValue.name: Warning

EnumValue._: 4-Recoverable error has has occurred and user intervention is probably required in addition to user acknowledgement only.
EnumValue.name: Error

EnumValue._: 5-Unrecoverable error and the service has shut itself off. Immediate user intervention is required.
EnumValue.name: Critical

column.name: Description
column.summary: Human readable description of what this signal means, or a message for the user.

column.name: SigDateTime
column.summary: Time signal was sent.

column.name: Tag
column.summary: Used to store serialized data that can be used for processing this signal.

column.name: IsProcessed
column.summary: After a message has been processed or acknowledged this is set true. Not currently used for heartbeat or service status signals.

table.name: etrans
table.summary: One electronic transaction.  Typically, one claim or response.  Or one benefit request or response.  Is constantly being expanded to include more types of transactions with clearinghouses.  Also stores printing of paper claims.  Sometimes stores a copy of what was sent.

column.name: EtransNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateTimeTrans
column.summary: The date and time of the transaction.

column.name: ClearingHouseNum
column.summary: FK to clearinghouse.ClearinghouseNum .  Can be 0 if no clearinghouse was involved.

column.name: Etype
column.summary: Enum:EtransType

EnumValue._: 7 Canada. Type 18. V02 type 10.

EnumValue._: 10 Canada. Type 04
EnumValue.name: RequestOutstand_CA

EnumValue._: 11 Canada. Type 05
EnumValue.name: RequestSumm_CA

EnumValue._: 12 Canada. Type 06
EnumValue.name: RequestPay_CA

EnumValue._: 13 Canada. Type 07
EnumValue.name: ClaimCOB_CA

EnumValue._: 14 Canada. Type 12
EnumValue.name: ReverseResponse_CA

EnumValue._: 15 Canada. Type 13
EnumValue.name: PredetermAck_CA

EnumValue._: 16 Canada. Type 23
EnumValue.name: PredetermEOB_CA

EnumValue._: 17 Canada. Type 14
EnumValue.name: OutstandingAck_CA

EnumValue._: 18 Canada. Type 24
EnumValue.name: EmailResponse_CA

EnumValue._: 19 Canada. Type 16
EnumValue.name: PaymentResponse_CA

EnumValue._: 20 Canada. Type 15
EnumValue.name: SummaryResponse_CA

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: FK to claim.ClaimNum if a claim. Otherwise 0.  Warning.  Original claim might have been deleted.  But if Canadian claim was successfully sent, then deletion will be blocked.

column.name: OfficeSequenceNumber
column.summary: For Canada. Unique for every transaction sent. Uncapped, but is modded by 1,000,000 when sent in a transaction as required by the standard.

column.name: CarrierTransCounter
column.summary: For Canada. Separate counter for each carrier. Uncapped, but is modded by 100,000 when sent in a transaction as required by the standard.

column.name: CarrierTransCounter2
column.summary: For Canada. Separate counter for each carrier. Uncapped, but is modded by 100,000 when sent in a transaction as required by the standard. If this claim includes secondary, then this is the counter for the secondary carrier.

column.name: CarrierNum
column.summary: FK to carrier.CarrierNum.

column.name: CarrierNum2
column.summary: FK to carrier.CarrierNum Only used if secondary insurance info is provided on a claim.  Necessary for Canada.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum This is useful in case the original claim has been deleted.  Now, we can still tell who the patient was.

column.name: BatchNumber
column.summary: Maxes out at 999, then loops back to 1.  This is not a good key, but is a restriction of (canadian?).  So dates must also be used to isolate the correct BatchNumber key.  Specific to one clearinghouse.  Only used with e-claims.  Claim will have BatchNumber, and 997 will have matching BatchNumber. (In X12 lingo, it's a functional group number)

column.name: AckCode
column.summary: A=Accepted, R=Rejected, blank if not able to parse, Recd=Received (835s only).  More options will be added later.  The incoming 997 or 999 sets this flag automatically.  To find the 997 or 999, look for a matching BatchNumber with a similar date, since both the claims and the 997 or 999 will both have the same batch number.  The 997 or 999 does not have this flag set on itself.

column.name: TransSetNum
column.summary: For sent e-claims, within each batch (functional group), each carrier gets it's own transaction set.  Since 997s and 999s acknowledge transaction sets rather than batches, we need to keep track of which transaction set each claim is part of as well as which batch it's part of.  This field can't be set as part of 997 or 999, because one 997 or 999 refers to multiple trans sets.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Typical uses include indicating that the report was printed, the claim was resent, reason for rejection, etc.  For a 270, this contains the automatically generated short summary of the response.  The response could include the reason for failure, or it could be a short summary of the 271.

column.name: EtransMessageTextNum
column.summary: FK to etransmessagetext.EtransMessageTextNum.  Can be 0 if there is no message text.  Multiple Etrans objects can refer to the same message text, very common in a batch.

column.name: AckEtransNum
column.summary:
FK to etrans.EtransNum.  Only has a non-zero value if there exists an ack etrans, like a 997, 999, 277ack, 271, 835, or ackError.
            There can be only one ack for any given etrans, but one ack can apply to multiple etran's that were sent as one batch.
            999 FK can be replaced by 277ack FK, and then by 835 FK.  This column does triple duty.
            The AckEtransNum can be used to chain together related etrans entries.  For example,
            if this is a 270 request, then AckEtransNum points to the 271 response.
            If this is a 271, then AckEtransNum points to the HTML (if any) for the response.

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.  Used if EtransType.BenefitInquiry270 and BenefitResponse271 and Eligibility_CA.

column.name: InsSubNum
column.summary: FK to inssub.InsSubNum.  Used if EtransType.BenefitInquiry270 and BenefitResponse271 and Eligibility_CA.

column.name: TranSetId835
column.summary: X12 ST02 Transaction Set Identifier for an 835.  Specifies the unique transaction id within the 835 that this etrans record corresponds to.  This column will always be set for 835s imported in version 14.3 or greater.  For 835s imported in version 14.2, this column will alway be blank.  If blank, and there is more than one transaction id within the 835, then FormEtrans835PickEob will show and allow the user to select the desired EOB from a list.  The X12 guide states that there is only one transaction (EOB) allowed per 835, but ClaimConnect returns multiple transactions (EOBs) within a single 835 and other clearinghouses probably do as well.  When an 835 is imported, it is examined to determine the number of transactions within it.  One etrans entry is created for each EOB within the 835.  We may have a similar issue with multiple transactions within 277s as well, but we have not seen any evidence yet.  Our current 277 implementation expects a single transaction, just as the X12 standard specifies.

column.name: CarrierNameRaw
column.summary:
Only used if the CarrierNum is 0.  If CarrierNum is not 0, the name associated to CarrierNum will override
            CarrierNameRaw in the FormClaimsSend history grid.  Added for 835s so that customer databases are not cluttered with dummy carriers and
            so there is no extra processing time when FormClaimsSend is loading.  Size is 60 bytes to match 835 carrier name length.

column.name: PatientNameRaw
column.summary:
Only used if the PatNum is 0.  If PatNum is not 0, the name associated to PatNum will override PatientNameRaw
            in the FormClaimsSend history grid.  Added for 835s so that there is no extra processing time when FormClaimsSend is loading,
            and so text representing the patient count can be used instead of an actual patient name.  Size is 133 bytes to match X12 specs for 
            last name (60), first name (35), middle name (25), suffix (10), and spaces in between (3).

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum

table.name: etrans835
table.summary: Corresponds to an etrans record containing a raw 835 X12 message attached in etransmessagetext table.  This is denoted by etrans.Etype=ERA_835

column.name: Etrans835Num
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EtransNum
column.summary: FK to etrans.EtransNum .

column.name: PayerName
column.summary: Up to 60 characters.  Corresponds to X835.PayerName, a read-only field.

column.name: TransRefNum
column.summary: Up to 50 characters.  Corresponds to X835.TransRefNum, a read-only field.

column.name: InsPaid
column.summary: Corresponds to X835.InsPaid, a read-only field.

column.name: ControlId
column.summary: Up to 9 characters.  Corresponds to X835.ControlId, a read-only field.

column.name: PaymentMethodCode
column.summary: Up to 3 characters.  Corresponds to X835._paymentMethodCode, a read-only field.

column.name: PatientName
column.summary:
Up to 100 characters (not based on actual patient name field sizes).
            Corresponds to Hx835_Claim.PatientName.ToString() if one patient, or says "(#)" if multiple patients to show count.

column.name: Status
column.summary: Enum:X835Status .  Calculated status.  Only changes when ERA changes.

EnumValue._: 1 - Just a place holder if there is an issue.  Should never show in UI.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 2 - There are no received claims attached to the ERA.  There can be one or more detached claims on the ERA.
EnumValue.name: Unprocessed

EnumValue._: 3 - Some claims for this ERA have had financial information entered, no finalaized claim payment.
EnumValue.name: Partial

EnumValue._: 4 - Ignores manually detached.  All claims for this ERA have had financial information entered, no finalaized claim payment.
EnumValue.name: NotFinalized

EnumValue._: 5 - Some claims have been manually detached but all other claims have had financial information entered and finalaized claim payment created.
EnumValue.name: FinalizedSomeDetached

EnumValue._: 6 - All claims have been manually detached.
EnumValue.name: FinalizedAllDetached

EnumValue._: 7 - All claims for this ERA have had financial information entered and a finalaized claim payment was created.
EnumValue.name: Finalized

column.name: AutoProcessed
column.summary: Enum:X835AutoProcessed .  The initial disposition of ERA's that have passed through our auto/semi-auto processing system.

Enumeration.name: X835AutoProcessed
Enumeration.summary: The initial disposition of ERA's that have passed through our auto/semi-auto processing system.

column.name: IsApproved
column.summary: True if a user has acknowledged the auto processed ERA.

table.name: etrans835attach
table.summary: Links a specific claim within an ERA 835 to an actual claim in the claims table.

column.name: Etrans835AttachNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EtransNum
column.summary: FK to etrans.EtransNum.

column.name: ClaimNum
column.summary: FK to claim.ClaimNum.  Can be 0, which indicates that the ERA claim does not have a match in OD.

column.name: ClpSegmentIndex
column.summary:
Segment index for the CLP/Claim segment within the X12 document containing the 835.
            This index is unique, even if there are multiple 835 transactions within the X12 document.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: DateTime that the row was inserted.

table.name: etransmessagetext
table.summary: Each row is big.  The entire X12 message text is stored here, since it can be the same for multiple etrans objects, and since the messages can be so big.

column.name: EtransMessageTextNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MessageText
column.summary: The entire message text, including carriage returns.

table.name: evaluation
table.summary: An evaluation is for one student and is copied from an EvaluationDef.

column.name: EvaluationNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: InstructNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: StudentNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: SchoolCourseNum
column.summary: FK to schoolcourse.SchoolCourseNum.  For example to PEDO 732.

column.name: EvalTitle
column.summary: Copied from evaluation def.  Not editable.

column.name: DateEval
column.summary: Date of the evaluation.

column.name: GradingScaleNum
column.summary: FK to gradingscale.GradingScaleNum.  The overall grading scale for this evaluation.  Copied from EvaluationDef.  Criteria will not all necessarily have the same scale.

column.name: OverallGradeShowing
column.summary: OverallGradeNumber is calculated as described below.  Once the nearest number on the scale is found, the corresponding gradescaleitem.GradeShowing is used here.

column.name: OverallGradeNumber
column.summary: Always recalculated as each individual criterion is changed, so no risk of getting out of synch.  Only considers criteria on the evaluation that use the same grading scale as the evaluation itself.  It's an average of all those criteria.  When averaging, the result will almost never exactly equal one of the numbers in the scale, so the nearest one must be found and used here.  For example, if the average is 3.6 on a 4 point scale, this will show 4.  Percentages will be rounded to the nearest whole number.  This is the value that will be returned in reports and also used in calculations of the student's grade for the term.

column.name: Notes
column.summary: Any note that the instructor wishes to place at the bottom of this evaluation.

table.name: evaluationcriterion
table.summary: One row on an evaluation.

column.name: EvaluationCriterionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EvaluationNum
column.summary: FK to evaluation.EvaluationNum

column.name: CriterionDescript
column.summary: Description that is displayed for the criterion.

column.name: IsCategoryName
column.summary: This row will show in bold and will not have a grade attached to it.

column.name: GradingScaleNum
column.summary: FK to gradingscale.GradingScaleNum.  The grading scale used for this criterion.  Having this here allows the instructor to edit saved grades and also allows the evaluation overall grade to consider whether to include this criterion in the calculation.

column.name: GradeShowing
column.summary: Copied from gradingscaleitem.GradeShowing.  Required.  For example A, B, C, D, F, or 1-10, pass, fail, 89, etc.  Except for percentages, must come from pick list.

column.name: GradeNumber
column.summary: Copied from gradingscaleitem.GradeNumber.  Required.  For example A=4, A-=3.8, pass=1, percentages stored as 89, etc.  Except for percentages, must come from pick list.

column.name: Notes
column.summary: A note about why this student received this particular grade on this criterion.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Copied from item order of def.  Defines the order that all the criteria show on the evaluation.  User not allowed to change here, only in the def.

column.name: MaxPointsPoss
column.summary: For ScaleType=Points, sets the maximum value of points for this criterion.

table.name: evaluationcriteriondef
table.summary: Rows on an evaluation def.  The individual items that will be graded. Criterion Defs

column.name: EvaluationCriterionDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EvaluationDefNum
column.summary: FK to evaluationdef.EvaluationDefNum.

column.name: CriterionDescript
column.summary: Description that is displayed for the criterion.

column.name: IsCategoryName
column.summary: This row will show in bold and will not have a grade attached to it.

column.name: GradingScaleNum
column.summary: FK to gradingscale.GradingScaleNum.  The grading scale used for this criterion.  As a user builds an evaluationDef, each criterion should default to the GradingScaleNum of the EvaluationDef, and then the user can change if needed.  The individual criteria do not have to be the same scale as the evaluation.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Defines the order that all the criteria show on the evaluation.  Copied to ItemOrder of actual criterion.

column.name: MaxPointsPoss
column.summary: For ScaleType=Points, sets the maximum value of points for this criterion.

table.name: evaluationdef
table.summary: An evaluation def is the entire form that the instructor sets up ahead of time.  Actual evaluations for students are copied from these 'templates', so an evaluation def can be altered or deleted without damaging any student record.  Evaluation defs are usually not specific to instructors, but if different instructors want different evaluation forms, they can use the description column to differentiate.  For example, the description can include the instructor's name or even the year.  But most commonly, the same evaluation will be used from year to year.  There should be a duplicate function to make a copy an entire evaluation def and then allow user to alter the SchoolCourseNum.

column.name: EvaluationDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SchoolCourseNum
column.summary: FK to schoolcourse.SchoolCourseNum.  For example to PEDO 732.

column.name: EvalTitle
column.summary: Description of this evaluation form.

column.name: GradingScaleNum
column.summary: FK to gradingscale.GradingScaleNum.  The default grading scale for this evaluation.  Each criterion will typically use the same scale, but that is not required.

table.name: famaging
table.summary:
This table stores intermediate family aged balances just prior to updating the patient table.  Once the aging calculations are finished
            and the patient table is updated, this table is truncated.  At the start of the aging calculations this table is checked and if there are existing
            rows, we will notify the user and force them to decide whether an aging calculation has already begun or an error happened that prevented the
            calculations from finishing and the rows are left over and can be deleted.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum.  Also the primary key for this table.  Always the PatNum for the Guarantor of a family.  A guarantor may not
            exist in this table if the family does not have a balance.  i.e. If a PatNum is not in this table, the aged balance columns on the patient table
            are set to 0, so either the patient is not the guarantor or the family has a zero balance.

column.name: Bal_0_30
column.summary: Aged balance from 0 to 30 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: Bal_31_60
column.summary: Aged balance from 31 to 60 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: Bal_61_90
column.summary: Aged balance from 61 to 90 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: BalOver90
column.summary: Aged balance over 90 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: InsEst
column.summary: Insurance Estimate for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: BalTotal
column.summary:
Total balance for entire family before insurance estimate.  Not the same as the sum of the 4 aging balances because this can be 
            negative.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: PayPlanDue
column.summary:
Amount "due now" for all payment plans such that someone in this family is the payment plan guarantor.  
            This is the total of all payment plan charges past due (taking into account the PayPlansBillInAdvanceDays setting) subtract the amount 
            already paid for the payment plans.  Only stored with family guarantor.

table.name: familyhealth
table.summary: For EHR, this lets us record medical problems for family members.  These family members will usually not be in our database, and they are just recorded by relationship.

column.name: FamilyHealthNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: Relationship
column.summary: Enum:FamilyRelationship

column.name: DiseaseDefNum
column.summary: FK to diseasedef.DiseaseDefNum, which will have a SnoMed associated with it.

column.name: PersonName
column.summary: Name of the family member.

table.name: fee
table.summary: There is one entry in this table for each fee for a single procedurecode.  So if there are 5 different fees stored for one procedurecode, then there will be five entries here.

column.name: FeeNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Amount
column.summary:
The amount usually charged.  If an amount is unknown, then the entire Fee entry is deleted from the database.  
            The absence of a fee is shown in the user interface as a blank entry.
            For clinic and/or provider fees, amount can be set to -1 which indicates that their fee should be blank and not use the default fee.

column.name: OldCode
column.summary: Do not use.

column.name: FeeSched
column.summary: FK to feesched.FeeSchedNum.

column.name: UseDefaultFee
column.summary: Not used.

column.name: UseDefaultCov
column.summary: Not used.

column.name: CodeNum
column.summary: FK to procedurecode.CodeNum.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  Must be 0 if feesched.IsGlobal=true.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  Must be 0 if feesched.IsGlobal=true.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Gets set automatically to the user logged in when the row is inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: DateEffective
column.summary: The date when the Fee is valid. Most users will have their fees with empty dates (0001-01-01) meaning their fees are always valid. This lets user enter a fee schedule ahead of an effective date. When we show fees to the user in areas where there is no date filter, we use today as the dateEffective. We don't show future fees in those areas.

table.name: feesched
table.summary: Fee schedule names used to be in the definition table, but now they have their own table.  We are about to have many many more fee schedules as we start automating allowed fees.

column.name: FeeSchedNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: The name of the fee schedule.

column.name: FeeSchedType
column.summary: Enum:FeeScheduleType

EnumValue._: 2, Formerly named "Allowed"
EnumValue.name: OutNetwork

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Unlike with the old definition table, this ItemOrder is not as critical in the caching of data.  The item order is only for fee schedules of the same type.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: True if the fee schedule is hidden.  Can't delete fee schedules or change their type once created.

column.name: IsGlobal
column.summary: True if the fee schedule is used globally and linked to the HQ. Localization of the fees is not allowed. ClinicNum and ProvNum must both be zero for all fees.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

table.name: feeschedgroup
table.summary: Can be used when using clinics, and a single fee schedule has fees that are region specific and need to be different for different clinics. A FeeSchedGroup stores a list of clinics that one fee schedule applies to, overriding the normal fee sched. This is designed so that you can have a few "groups" per fee sched instead of dozens or hundreds of clinics. Fees are still created on a per-clinic basis, and we attempt to manage them when we do things like change fees or edit groups.

column.name: FeeSchedGroupNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FeeSchedNum
column.summary: FK to FeeSched.FeeSchedNum.

column.name: ClinicNums
column.summary: Comma delimited list of Clinic.ClinicNums.

table.name: feeschednote
table.summary: A note that is attached to a fee schedule, and can additionally be associated with a clinic too.

column.name: FeeSchedNoteNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FeeSchedNum
column.summary: FK to feesched.FeeSchedNum.

column.name: ClinicNums
column.summary: A comma delimited list of clinic nums that the fee schedule note is for. Can include 0. For most offices this will be empty string because they do not use clinics.

column.name: Note
column.summary: A note for a particular fee schedule. No character lim.

column.name: DateEntry
column.summary: The date of this note. User is allowed to change this date.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change. The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program updates. Not user editable with the UI.

table.name: fhircontactpoint
table.summary:
Details of a Technology mediated contact point (phone, fax, email, etc.). https://www.hl7.org/fhir/datatypes.html#contactpoint 
            .

column.name: FHIRContactPointNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FHIRSubscriptionNum
column.summary: FK to fhirsubscription.FHIRSubscriptionNum.

column.name: ContactSystem
column.summary: Enum:ContactPointSystem

EnumValue._:
The value is a telephone number used for voice calls. Use of full international numbers starting with + is recommended to enable
            automatic dialing support but not required.
EnumValue.name: Phone

EnumValue._:
The value is a fax machine. Use of full international numbers starting with + is recommended to enable automatic dialing support 
            but not required.
EnumValue.name: Fax

EnumValue._: The value is an email address.
EnumValue.name: Email

EnumValue._: The value is a pager number. These may be local pager numbers that are only usable on a particular pager system.
EnumValue.name: Pager

EnumValue._:
A contact that is not a phone, fax, or email address. The format of the value SHOULD be a URL. This is intended for various personal 
            contacts including blogs, Twitter, Facebook, etc. Do not use for email addresses. If this is not a URL, then it will require human 
            interpretation.
EnumValue.name: Other

column.name: ContactValue
column.summary: The actual contact point details.

column.name: ContactUse
column.summary: Enum:ContactPointUse

EnumValue._:
A communication contact point at a home; attempted contacts for business purposes might intrude privacy and chances are one will 
            contact family or other household members instead of the person one wishes to call. Typically used with urgent cases, or if no other contacts 
            are available.
EnumValue.name: Home

EnumValue._: An office contact point. First choice for business related contacts during business hours.
EnumValue.name: Work

EnumValue._: A temporary contact point. The period can provide more detailed information.
EnumValue.name: Temp

EnumValue._: This contact point is no longer in use (or was never correct, but retained for records).
EnumValue.name: Old

EnumValue._:
A telecommunication device that moves and stays with its owner. May have characteristics of all other use codes, suitable for urgent 
            matters, not the first choice for routine business.
EnumValue.name: Mobile

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Specify preferred order of use (1 = highest)

column.name: DateStart
column.summary: Time when the contact point started to be in use.

column.name: DateEnd
column.summary: Timewhen the contact point stopped being used.

table.name: fhirsubscription
table.summary: A subscription by a client that requests an alert whenever a change is made to a FHIR resource.

column.name: FHIRSubscriptionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Criteria
column.summary: Rule for server push criteria.

column.name: Reason
column.summary: Description of why this subscription was created.

column.name: SubStatus
column.summary: Enum:SubscriptionStatus

Enumeration.name: SubscriptionStatus
Enumeration.summary: The status of a Subscription. https://www.hl7.org/fhir/valueset-subscription-status.html

EnumValue._: The client has requested the subscription, and the server has not yet set it up.
EnumValue.name: Requested

EnumValue._: The subscription is active.
EnumValue.name: Active

EnumValue._: The server has an error executing the notification.
EnumValue.name: Error

EnumValue._: Too many errors have occurred or the subscription has expired.
EnumValue.name: Off

column.name: ErrorNote
column.summary: Latest error note.

column.name: ChannelType
column.summary: Enum:SubscriptionChannelType

Enumeration.name: SubscriptionChannelType
Enumeration.summary: Only rest_hook is supported by Open Dental FHIR. https://www.hl7.org/fhir/valueset-subscription-channel-type.html

EnumValue._:
The channel is executed by making a post to the URI. If a payload is included, the URL is interpreted as the service base, and an 
            update (PUT) is made.
EnumValue.name: Rest_Hook

EnumValue._:
The channel is executed by sending a packet across a web socket connection maintained by the client. The URL identifies the 
            websocket, and the client binds to this URL.
EnumValue.name: Websocket

EnumValue._: The channel is executed by sending an email to the email addressed in the URI (which must be a mailto:).
EnumValue.name: Email

EnumValue._: The channel is executed by sending an SMS message to the phone number identified in the URL (tel:).
EnumValue.name: Sms

EnumValue._:
The channel is executed by sending a message (e.g. a Bundle with a MessageHeader resource etc.) to the application identified in 
            the URI.
EnumValue.name: Message

column.name: ChannelEndpoint
column.summary: Where the channel points to.

column.name: ChannelPayLoad
column.summary: Mimetype to send, or blank for no payload.

column.name: ChannelHeader
column.summary: Usage depends on the channel type.

column.name: DateEnd
column.summary: When to automatically delete the subscription.

column.name: APIKeyHash
column.summary: A hash of the API key that was used in the request to create this subscription.

table.name: fielddeflink
table.summary: A better name would be FieldHide. This specifies places where PatFields or ApptFields should be hidden. PatFieldDefs already have an IsHidden field, so this is redundant there. But it's powerful for letting PatFields show in some places but not other places.

column.name: FieldDefLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: FieldDefNum
column.summary: A generic FieldDefNum FK to any particular field def item that will be defined by the FieldDefType column.

column.name: FieldDefType
column.summary: Enum:FieldDefTypes Defines what FieldDefNum represents.

Enumeration.name: FieldDefTypes
Enumeration.summary: Enum representing different types of field defs.

column.name: FieldLocation
column.summary: Enum:FieldLocations Defines where this particular field def needs to be hidden.

Enumeration.name: FieldLocations
Enumeration.summary: Enum representing where the field def should be hidden.

table.name: formpat
table.summary: This is an old table that isn't really used anymore. We used to have a "questionnaire" that could be filled out by a patient, and this is it.  Each patient can have multiple questionnaires.

column.name: FormPatNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: FormDateTime
column.summary: The date and time that this questionnaire was filled out.

table.name: gradingscale
table.summary: Used in Evaluations.  Describes a scale to be used in grading.  Freeform scales are not allowed.  Percentage scales are handled a little differently than the other scales.

column.name: GradingScaleNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: For example, A-F or Pass/Fail.

column.name: ScaleType
column.summary: Enum:EnumScaleType Used to determine method of assigning grades.  PickList will be the only type that has GradingScaleItems.

Enumeration.name: EnumScaleType
Enumeration.summary: Used in GradingScale to determine how grades are assigned.

EnumValue._: 0- User-Defined list of possible grades.  Grade is calculated as an average.
EnumValue.name: PickList

EnumValue._: 1- Percentage Scale 0-100.  Grade is calculated as an average.
EnumValue.name: Percentage

EnumValue._: 2- Allows point values for grades.  Grade is calculated as a sum of all points out of points possible.
EnumValue.name: Weighted

table.name: gradingscaleitem
table.summary: Only used when GradingScale.ScaleType=PickList, not Percentage or Points.  The specific grades allowed on a scale.  Contains both the GradeShowing and the equivalent number.  There are no FKs to these items.  The values are all copied from here into student records as they are used.

column.name: GradingScaleItemNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: GradingScaleNum
column.summary: FK to gradingscale.GradingScaleNum

column.name: GradeShowing
column.summary: For example A, B, C, D, F.  Optional.  If not specified, it shows the number.

column.name: GradeNumber
column.summary: For example A=4, A-=3.8, pass=1, etc.  Required.  Enforced to be equal to or less than GradingScale.MaxPointsPoss.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Optional additional info about what this particular grade means.  Just used as guidance and does not get copied to the individual student record.

table.name: grouppermission
table.summary: Every user group has certain permissions.  This defines a permission for a group.  The absense of permission would cause that row to be deleted from this table.

column.name: GroupPermNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: NewerDate
column.summary: Only granted permission if newer than this date.  Can be Minimum (01-01-0001) to always grant permission.

column.name: NewerDays
column.summary: Can be 0 to always grant permission.  Otherwise, only granted permission if item is newer than the given number of days.  1 would mean only if entered today.

column.name: UserGroupNum
column.summary: FK to usergroup.UserGroupNum.  The user group for which this permission is granted.  If not authorized, then this groupPermission will have been deleted.

column.name: PermType
column.summary: Enum:EnumPermType Some permissions will treat a zero FKey differently. Some denote it as having access to everything for that PermType. I.e. Reports.

Enumeration.name: EnumPermType
Enumeration.summary: A hard-coded list of permissions which may be granted to usergroups.

column.name: FKey

table.name: guardian
table.summary:
Links patient to patient in a many to many database relationship.  The two PatNums need not be in the same family, but will usually be.
            The two PatNums could be in different families if the relationship was entered, then one of the patients in the relationship is moved to another family.
            This table can also be used for other relationship types besides guardians.  The table name is guardian because we only supported guardian relationships in the past,
            and we did not want to risk breaking queries by changing the table or column names. User can specify any relationship as a guardian or not a guardian.
            For example, a retired person might specify their brother or child as their guardian, or the user may want to record the brother of a patient as a non-guardian.

column.name: GuardianNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  If Relationship is "Mother", then this PatNum is the child of the mother.

column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  If Relationship is "Mother", then this is the PatNum of the mother.

column.name: IncomingFolder

Used for File mode and for SFTP mode.  For file mode, this is the folder for inbound HL7 messages.
            For SFTP mode, this is the relative path from the SFTP root directory to the directory where the result messages can be found.
            The root or home directory '.' can be included in the path but is not necessary.  Examples: /./results or /results or results.

column.name: IsInternal
column.summary: If this is set, then there will be no child tables. Internal types are fully defined within the C# code rather than in the database.

column.name: InternalType
column.summary: Enum:HL7InternalType Stored in db as string, but used in OD as enum HL7InternalType. Example: eCWTight.  This will always have a value because we always start with a copy of some internal type.

Enumeration.name: HL7InternalType
Enumeration.summary: Stored in the database as a string.  The items in this enumeration can be freely rearranged without damaging the database.  But can't change spelling or remove existing items.

EnumValue._: Message structure is identical to eCWTight, minor changes in the program like showing patient demographics and the account module.Like eCWTight, eCW dictates the patients' PatNums in PID.2, so we try to locate the patient with that PatNum.If not found we do not attempt to use PID.4 ChartNumber or name, we assume new patient and insert.
EnumValue.name: eCWFull

EnumValue._: Only Incoming ADT messages are processed, OD is responsible for adding patients so we assign PatNum.The incoming messages patient ID in PID.2 is stored as ChartNumber and PID.4 is not processed.The Account and Chart modules are visible and the users can change and add patients in OD.  No outgoing messages.
EnumValue.name: eCWStandalone

EnumValue._: Patient demographics are hidden as well as account and appt modules.We let eCW dictate the PatNum values in PID.2 and trust that they are unique and longs (no string characters).Unlike Standalone, if the pat isn't found by PID.2 PatNum we don't try to locate the pat by PID.4 ChartNumber or name, we assume it's a new pat.
EnumValue.name: eCWTight

EnumValue._: Account and Appointment modules are visible and users can change and add patients.Only outgoing DFT message defined, no incoming messages are processed.
EnumValue.name: Centricity

EnumValue._: Our default behavior for processing and sending HL7 messages.Send and receive ADT and SIU messages, receive DFT messages.The v2.6 documentation claims both PID.2 and PID.4 are only retained for backward compatibility and PID.3 is now required and used for a list of patient IDs.We will still put ChartNumber in PID.2 (used to be referred to as 'external ID' by HL7 doc) for outgoing msgs and look for our PatNum in PID.2 for incoming msgs.We will now also check PID.3 for a repitition that contains our PatNum as part of the CX data type.Account and Appointments module are visible and users can change and add patients.
EnumValue.name: HL7v2_6

EnumValue._:
This is currently used for LabCorp and is based on HL7 version 2.3 specifications.
            This interface has been built to the LabCorp standards and may not match the HL7 version 2.3 specs exactly.
EnumValue.name: MedLabv2_3

EnumValue.name: Change

The socket used to connect to the Sftp server for retrieving inbound HL7 messages.  Currently only used by MedLabv2_3 interfaces.
            This will be the address:port of the Sftp server to connect to for retrieving lab results.  Example: server.address.com:20020.

column.name: HasLongDCodes
column.summary:
For eCW HL7 interfaces only.  False by default.  When false, D codes sent in outbound DFT messages will be limited to 5 characters.
            Any additional characters will be stripped off when generating the HL7 message.  When true, D codes will not be truncated.

Enumeration.name: MessageTypeHL7
Enumeration.summary: The items in this enumeration can be freely rearranged without damaging the database.  But can't change spelling or remove existing item.

EnumValue._: Use this for unsupported message types
EnumValue.name: NotDefined

EnumValue._: Message Acknowledgment
EnumValue.name: ACK

EnumValue._: Demographics - A01,A04,A08,A28,A31
EnumValue.name: ADT

EnumValue._: Detailed Financial Transaction - P03
EnumValue.name: DFT

EnumValue._: Unsolicited Observation Message - R01
EnumValue.name: ORU

EnumValue._: Patient Problem - PC1,PC2,PC3
EnumValue.name: PPR

EnumValue._: Schedule Information Unsolicited - Event types S12 through S26.  Currently only S12, S14, S15, and S17 events are supported.  Inbound for eCW, outbound for other interfaces.
EnumValue.name: SIU

EnumValue._: Schedule Request Message - Event types S01 through S11.  Currently only S03 and S04 events are supported.  Not used for eCW, inbound for other interfaces.
EnumValue.name: SRM

EnumValue._: Schedule Request Response - Event types S01 through S11.  Currently only S03 and S04 events are supported.  Not used for eCW, inbound for other interfaces.
EnumValue.name: SRR

EnumValue._: Unsolicited Vaccination Record Update - V04
EnumValue.name: VXU

column.name: EventType
column.summary: Enum:EventTypeHL7 Stored in db as string, but used in OD as enum EventTypeHL7. Example: A04, which is only used with ADT/ACK.

Enumeration.name: EventTypeHL7
Enumeration.summary: The items in this enumeration can be freely rearranged without damaging the database.  But can't change spelling or remove existing item.

EnumValue._: Use this for unsupported event types
EnumValue.name: NotDefined

EnumValue._:
Only used with ADT/ACK.
            A04 - Register a PatientFor eCW, the A04 and A08 are inbound messages and are processed the same.  We attempt to locate the patient, if not found we insert one.For other interfaces, the same method of locating a patient and if not found inserting one will be used for inbound ADT^A04 and ADT^A08 messages.Outbound messages will have an A04 event type if a new patient is added
EnumValue.name: A04

EnumValue._:
Only used with ADT/ACK.
            A08 - Update Patient InfoA04 and A08 inbound are processed the same, but we will send the A08 event type if updating a patient in an outbound ADT.
EnumValue.name: A08

EnumValue._: Only used with DFT/ACK.
EnumValue.name: P03

EnumValue._:
Only used with PPR/ACK (Patient Problem messages).
            Not used for eCW.These are inbound messages for adding/updating patient problems.PC1 is the Add event.  Add and Update events will be handled the same for now.
EnumValue.name: PC1

EnumValue._:
Only used with PPR/ACK (Patient Problem messages).
            Not used for eCW.PC2 is the Update event.  Add and Update events will be handled the same for now.
EnumValue.name: PC2

EnumValue._:
Only used with SRM/SRR/ACK.  S03 - Request Appointment Modification.
            Not used for eCW.These will be inbound and are used for updating a limited amount of information for an existing appointment.S03 messages are used to update appointments.
EnumValue.name: S03

EnumValue._:
Only used with SRM/SRR/ACK.  S04 - Request Appointment Cancellation.
            Not used for eCW.S04 messages are used to set an appointment.AptStatus to ApptStatus.Broken.
EnumValue.name: S04

EnumValue._:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S12 - New ApptFor eCW, these are inbound, OD is considered an auxiliary application, and S12 and S14 messages are processed the same.For interfaces that require outbound SIU messages, OD is considered the filler application since OD has control over the operatories and schedules.As the filler application, events S12-S26 are the message events and they all have the same structure defined by HL7.Different actions in OD will cause a different outbound event type to be inserted, but the defined segments and fields will otherwise be the same.
EnumValue.name: S12

EnumValue._:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S13 - Appt Rescheduling
EnumValue.name: S13

EnumValue._:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S14 - Appt Modification
EnumValue.name: S14

EnumValue._:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S15 - Appt Cancellation
EnumValue.name: S15

EnumValue._:
Only used with SIU/ACK.
            S17 - Appt Deletion
EnumValue.name: S17

EnumValue.name: NotDefined

EnumValue._:
Used for ADT/ACK event types A01, A04, A08, and A13.
            We currently only support A04 and A08 event types, both will use this structure.
EnumValue.name: ADT_A01

EnumValue._:
Used for DFT/ACK event type P03.
            All outbound DFT's are this structure.
EnumValue.name: DFT_P03

EnumValue._:
Used for ORU/ACK event type R01.
            All inbound ORU - Unsolicited transmission of an observation message will use this structure.This is used for all inbound LabCorp messages.
EnumValue.name: ORU_R01

EnumValue._:
Used for PPR/ACK event types PC1, PC2, and PC3.
            We currently only support PC1 (add problem) and PC2 (update problem), both use this structure.
EnumValue.name: PPR_PC1

EnumValue._:
Used for SIU/ACK event types S12 through S24 and S26.
            We currently only support S12 through S17.Inbound SIU's are all treated the same, regardless of the event type.We send different event types in outbound SIU's depending on the action that causes the message.All SIU's, inbound or outbound, use this message structure.
EnumValue.name: SIU_S12

EnumValue._:
Used for SRM/ACK event types S01 through S11.
            We currently only support S03 (update appt request) and S04 (cancel appt request).SRM's are inbound and when the action of updating or cancelling the appt is completed, an SRR is sent.SRM's and SRR's still require ACK's.  i.e. SRM received, ACK sent, action completed leads to SRR sent, ACK received.SRM's and SRR's will all use this message structure.
EnumValue.name: SRM_S01

EnumValue._:
Used for SRR/ACK event types S01 through S11.
            We currently only support S03 and S04 (see SRM_S01).SRR's are outbound and sent when an SRM is processed correctly.
EnumValue.name: SRR_S01

column.name: CanRepeat
column.summary: For example, a DFT can have multiple FT1 segments.  This turns out to be a completely useless field, since we already know which ones can repeat.

column.name: IsOptional
column.summary: If this is false, and an incoming message is missing this segment, then it gets logged as an error/failure.  If this is true, then it will gracefully skip a missing incoming segment.  Not used for outgoing.

table.name: imagedraw
table.summary: Image text, lines. drawings, and scales. Attached to either a document or a mount. Drawings are in pixel coordinates of original image prior to any cropping or rotating. For a mount, coordinates are relative to the entire mount. Drawings do not get changed when cropping or rotating are changed. The result is that drawings always stay on the image in exactly the original location, and they move with the image.

column.name: ImageDrawNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: FK to document.DocNum

column.name: MountNum
column.summary: FK to mount.MountNum

column.name: ColorDraw
column.summary: For text, this is the foreground color. For lines, this is the color, and ColorBack is not used. For polygons, this is the fill color. No transparency component.

column.name: ColorBack
column.summary: Background color for text. Can be Transparent (0,255,255,255)=16777215.

column.name: DrawingSegment
column.summary: Point data for a drawing segment.  The format would look similar to this: 45.2,68.1;48,70;49,72;0,0;55,88;etc.  It's simply a sequence of points, separated by semicolons.  Only positive floats are used, rounded to one decimal place.  0,0 is the upper left of the image or mount.  Cropping is ignored.  If the pen is picked up, it becomes a new segment, so a new row in the database.  Or, if this is DrawType.ScaleValue, then this field stores scale, decimal places, and units, separated by spaces.  Example: "123.4 0 mm". The first two are required; units is optional.

column.name: DrawText
column.summary: The location of the text in pixels is incorporated into this string.  Example: 25,123;This shows.  Carriage returns etc are not supported.  ColorDraw and FontSize are also used.  Unlike tooth initial, this does not support floats.

column.name: FontSize
column.summary: This could vary significantly based on the size of the image.  It's always relative to orginal image or mount pixels. Always 0 for Pearl.

column.name: DrawType
column.summary: Enum:ImageDrawType

EnumValue._: 0 - Location and string, combined
EnumValue.name: Text

EnumValue._: 1 - A series of straight lines, stored the same as a pen drawing.
EnumValue.name: Line

EnumValue._: 2 - One continuous segment of a drawing.
EnumValue.name: Pen

EnumValue._: 3 - Stores a float, decimals, and units in the drawing segement. Only one of this type is allowed per image or mount.
EnumValue.name: ScaleValue

EnumValue._: 4 - A series of connected points forming the outline of a closed polygon. Stored same as pen drawing. Polygons only have a fill color, not any outline color.
EnumValue.name: Polygon

table.name: insbluebook
table.summary: Records insurance plan and insurance payment data of a procedure that has been paid on primary or secondary insurance claim made to out of network plans. This data is used to estimate allowed fees and make more accurate insurance estimates.

column.name: GroupNum

table.name: insbluebookrule
table.summary: The insbluebookrule table represents an ordered hierarchy of rules that the program will attempt to apply when determining insurance estimates for out of network plans. If the highest priority rule does not produce an estimate, the program attempts to apply the second rule, and so on, until an estimate is obtained.

column.name: InsBlueBookRuleNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: 0 based. This rule's priority in the hierarchy of all insbluebookrules. 0 is highest priority.

column.name: RuleType
column.summary: Enum:InsBlueBookRuleType Types 0 to 3 are for rules that determine estimates by looking at the payment history of an insurance plan, insurance plan group, carrier, or carrier group. Type 4 utilizes fee schedules that are attached to out of network plans that are manually maintained by the user. Type 5 bases estimates off of the UCR fee.

EnumValue._: 0 - Insurance Plan
EnumValue.name: InsurancePlan

EnumValue._: 1 - Group Number
EnumValue.name: GroupNumber

EnumValue._: 2 - Insurance Carrier
EnumValue.name: InsuranceCarrier

EnumValue._: 3 - Insurance Carrier Group
EnumValue.name: InsuranceCarrierGroup

EnumValue._: 4 - Manual Blue Book Schedule
EnumValue.name: ManualBlueBookSchedule

EnumValue._: 5 - UCR Fee
EnumValue.name: UcrFee

column.name: LimitValue
column.summary: The number of years, months, weeks, or days of insurance payment history that will be considered when generating a Blue Book estimate. Will be 0 if the RuleType is 4-ManualBlueBookSchedule or 5-UcrFee as limits do not apply to these rule types.

column.name: LimitType
column.summary: Enum:InsBlueBookRuleLimitType Determines the unit of time that InsBlueBookRule.LimitValue represents. Will be 0-None if the RuleType is 4-ManualBlueBookSchedule or 5-UcrFee as limits do not apply to these rule types.

EnumValue._: 0 - None
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Years
EnumValue.name: Years

EnumValue._: 2 - Months
EnumValue.name: Months

EnumValue._: 3 - Weeks
EnumValue.name: Weeks

EnumValue._: 4 - Days
EnumValue.name: Days

table.name: insplan

column.summary: FK to claimform.ClaimFormNum. eg. "1" for ADA2002.  For ADA2006, it varies by office.

column.name: IsMedical
column.summary: True if this is medical insurance rather than dental insurance.  When creating a claim, this, along with pref.

column.name: FilingCode
column.summary: FK to insfilingcode.InsFilingCodeNum.  Used for e-claims.  Also used for some complex reports in public health.  The e-claim usage might become obsolete when PlanID implemented by HIPAA.  Can be 0 to indicate none.  Then 'CI' will go out on claims.

column.name: DentaideCardSequence
column.summary: Canadian e-claim field. D11 and E07.  Zero indicates empty.  Mandatory value for Dentaide.  Not used for all others.  2 digit.  DEPRECATED - See CDAnet Message Formats+Standards 4.2_2021.

column.name: ShowBaseUnits
column.summary: If checked, the units Qty will show the base units assigned to a procedure on the claim form.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Set to true to hide it from the pick list and from the main list.

column.summary: The month, 1 through 12 when the insurance plan renews.  It will renew on the first of the month.  To indicate calendar year, set renew month to 0.

column.name: CanadianInstitutionCode
column.summary: Canadian C40. Required when CanadianPlanFlag is 'A'.

EnumValue._: 2=CarveOut

3=SecondaryMedicaid. The secondary insurance will reduce what it pays by what primary pays (like Basic). Then anything that would be the
            patient portion is a writeoff for the secondary insurance. Sometimes Medicaid is required to be the primary, so only use this if you are sure
            you are allowed to.

Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program

D8080 submitted on initial banding and OrthoAutoProc (usually D8670.auto) submitted at a set frequency regardless of visits.

column.name: ManualFeeSchedNum
column.fk: feesched that has a feeschedtype of 4-manualbluebook
column.summary: FK to feesched that has a FeeSchedType of 4-ManualBlueBook. Optional, can be 0.

column.name: IsBlueBookEnabled
column.summary: determines if the plan is going to have BlueBook Enabled or not

column.name: InsPlansZeroWriteOffsOnAnnualMaxOverride
column.summary: Overrides the InsPlansZeroWriteOffsOnAnnualMax preference. 0=use default, 1=true, 2=false.

column.name: InsPlansZeroWriteOffsOnFreqOrAgingOverride
column.summary: Overrides the InsPlansZeroWriteOffsOnFreqOrAging preference. 0=use default, 1=true, 2=false.

column.name: PerVisitPatAmount
column.summary: The per visit patient copay amount. 0 by default. When an appt is scheduled or set complete, a new proc gets created with code specified in pref.PerVisitPatAmountProcCode.

column.name: PerVisitInsAmount
column.summary: The per visit amount to bill insurance. 0 by default. When an appt is scheduled or set complete, a new proc gets created with code specified in pref.PerVisitInsAmountProcCode.

table.name: insplanpreference
table.summary: Used to create overrides at the insurance plan level.

column.name: InsPlanPrefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.

column.name: FKey
column.summary: FK to to a table associated with FKeyType

column.name: FKeyType
column.summary: Enum:InsPlanPrefFKeyType ProcCodeNoBillIns

Enumeration.name: InsPlanPrefFKeyType
Enumeration.summary: The different types of overrides that can be created for an insurance plan.

EnumValue._: 0 - Overrides the procedurecode.NoBillIns field at the insurance plan level. FKey stores the CodeNum. ValueString stores NoBillInsOverride enum value.
EnumValue.name: ProcCodeNoBillIns

column.name: ValueString
column.summary: Used to hold the override. NoBillIns stores 0, 1.

table.name: inssub
table.summary: Multiple subscribers can have the same insurance plan.  But the patplan table is still what determines coverage for individual patients.

column.name: InsSubNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.

column.name: Subscriber
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateEffective
column.summary: Date plan became effective. Is 0001-01-01 if not set.

column.name: DateTerm
column.summary: Date plan was terminated. Is 0001-01-01 if not set.

column.name: ReleaseInfo
column.summary: Release of information signature is on file.

column.name: AssignBen
column.summary: Assignment of benefits signature is on file.  For Canada, this handles Payee Code, F01.  Option to pay other third party is not included.

column.name: SubscriberID
column.summary: Number assigned by insurance company. No dashes. Not allowed to be blank.

column.name: BenefitNotes
column.summary: User doesn't usually put these in.  Only used when automatically requesting benefits, such as with Trojan.  All the benefits get stored here in text form for later reference.  Not at plan level because might be specific to subscriber.  If blank, we try to display a benefitNote for another subscriber to the plan.

column.name: SubscNote
column.summary: Use to store any other info that affects coverage.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

table.name: installmentplan
table.summary: Simpler than a payment plan.  Does not affect running account balances.  Allows override of finance charges.  Affects the "pay now" on statements.  Only one installmentplan is allowed for a family, attached to guarantor only.  This is loosely enforced.

column.name: InstallmentPlanNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateAgreement
column.summary: Date payment plan agreement was made.

column.name: DateFirstPayment
column.summary: Date of first payment.

column.name: MonthlyPayment
column.summary: Amount of monthly payment.

column.name: APR
column.summary: Annual Percentage Rate. e.g. 12.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Note

table.name: insverify
table.summary: A row for the most recent time an insplan benefit or patplan enrollment was verified. Also see insverifyhist, which keeps a historical record. When a new plan is created, a row gets created here with no date. There is never more than one row per plan because old ones get moved over to InsVerifyHist.

column.name: InsVerifyNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateLastVerified
column.summary: The date of the last successful verification. This date will be DateTime.MinVal upon insert and will not change until the user has verified insurance benefits or pat plan eligibility in FormInsVerificationList, or until the user has entered and saved a date in Eligibility Last Verified or Benefits Last Verified textboxes in FormInsPlan.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. Typically 0. There is an optional feature that lets an office "assign" users to a verification so that they can split the load of verifying between different users.

column.name: VerifyType
column.summary: Enum:VerifyTypes either InsuranceBenefits or PatientEnrollment

EnumValue._: 0.  This means FKey should be 0.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1.  This means FKey will link to insplan.PlanNum
EnumValue.name: InsuranceBenefit

EnumValue._: 2.  This means FKey will link to patplan.PatPlanNum
EnumValue.name: PatientEnrollment

column.name: FKey
column.summary: Foreign key either insplan.PlanNum or patplan.PatPlanNum.

column.name: DefNum
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. Links to the category InsVerifyStatus

column.name: Note
column.summary: Note for this insurance verification.

column.name: DateLastAssigned
column.summary: Optional feature that's part of "assigning" users to verification. Default is DateTime.MinVal. The DateTime of when a userod.UserNum was last assigned/unassigned a patient's insverify in FormInsVerificationList, the last time the status was changed on the insverify, when a patient's patplan has been dropped, or whenever an error occurs when trying to batch verify patient benefits.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: DateTime the row was added.

column.name: HoursAvailableForVerification
column.summary:
Number of hours that were available from the time the insurance needed verified to the date of the appointment.
            Includes minutes if applicable.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed.

column.name: SecurityHash
column.summary: Holds the salted hash of the following insverify fields: UserNum, DefNum, DateLastAssigned.

table.name: insverifyhist
table.summary: A historical copy of an insurance verification record.

column.name: InsVerifyHistNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: InsVerifyNum
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: DateLastVerified
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: VerifyType
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: FKey
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: DefNum
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: DateLastAssigned
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: HoursAvailableForVerification
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify.

column.name: VerifyUserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  User that was logged on when row was inserted.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Not copied from Task. Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed.

column.name: SecurityHash
column.summary: Copied from InsVerify. When an InsVerifyHist is created, the 3 hashed fields remain unchanged, so this hash remains valid.

table.name: intervention
table.summary: An intervention ordered or performed.  Examples: smoking cessation and weightloss counseling.  Links to a definition in the ehrcode table using the CodeValue and CodeSystem.

column.name: InterventionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: CodeValue
column.summary: FK to ehrcode.CodeValue.  This code may not exist in the ehrcode table, it may have been chosen from a bigger list of available codes.  In that case, this will be a FK to a specific code system table identified by the CodeSystem column.  The code for this item from one of the code systems supported.  Examples: V65.3 or 418995006.

column.name: CodeSystem
column.summary: FK to codesystem.CodeSystemName. The code system name for this code.  Possible values are: CPT, HCPCS, ICD9CM, ICD10CM, and SNOMEDCT.

column.name: Note
column.summary: User-entered details about the intervention for this patient.

column.name: DateEntry
column.summary: The date of the intervention.

column.name: CodeSet
column.summary: Enum:InterventionCodeSet AboveNormalWeight, BelowNormalWeight, TobaccoCessation, Nutrition, PhysicalActivity, Dialysis.

Enumeration.name: InterventionCodeSet
Enumeration.summary: Value sets for interventions.  This will limit the codes in FormInterventionEdit to a smaller list of codes and allow us to recommend codes to meet specific CQMs.

EnumValue._: 0 - Above Normal Weight Follow-up/Referrals where weight assessment may occur
EnumValue.name: AboveNormalWeight

EnumValue._: 1 - Below Normal Weight Follow-up/Referrals where weight assessment may occur
EnumValue.name: BelowNormalWeight

EnumValue._: 2 - Counseling for Nutrition
EnumValue.name: Nutrition

EnumValue._: 3 - Counseling for Physical Activity
EnumValue.name: PhysicalActivity

EnumValue._: 4 - Tobacco Use Cessation Counseling
EnumValue.name: TobaccoCessation

EnumValue._: 5 - Dialysis Education/Other Services Related to Dialysis
EnumValue.name: Dialysis

EnumValue._: 6 - None
EnumValue.name: None

column.name: IsPatDeclined
column.summary: Indicates whether the intervention was offered/recommended to the patient and the patient declined the treatment/referral.

table.name: journalentry
table.summary: Used in accounting to represent a single credit or debit entry.  There will always be at least 2 journal enties attached to every transaction.  All transactions balance to 0.

column.name: JournalEntryNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TransactionNum
column.summary: FK to transaction.TransactionNum

column.name: AccountNum
column.summary: FK to account.AccountNum

column.name: DateDisplayed
column.summary: Always the same for all journal entries within one transaction.

column.name: DebitAmt
column.summary: Negative numbers never allowed.

column.name: CreditAmt
column.summary: Negative numbers never allowed.

column.name: Splits
column.summary: A human-readable description of the splits.  Used only for display purposes.

column.name: CheckNumber
column.summary: Any user-defined string.  Usually a check number, but can also be D for deposit, Adj, etc.

column.name: ReconcileNum
column.summary: FK to reconcile.ReconcileNum. 0 if not attached to a reconcile. Not allowed to alter amounts if attached.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. The user who created this journal entry.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: The date and time that this journal entry was created.

column.name: SecUserNumEdit
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. The user who last edited this journal entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: The last time this journal entry was edited.

table.name: labcase
table.summary: A lab case.

column.name: LabCaseNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: LaboratoryNum
column.summary: FK to laboratory.LaboratoryNum. The lab that the case gets sent to.  Required.

column.name: AptNum
column.summary: FK to appointment.AptNum. This is how a lab case is attached to a scheduled appointment. Multiple labcases can be attached to any appointment. Labcase can exist without being attached to any appointments at all, making this zero.

column.name: PlannedAptNum
column.summary: FK to appointment.AptNum.  This is how a lab case is attached to a planned appointment in addition to the scheduled appointment.

column.name: DateTimeDue
column.summary: The due date that is put on the labslip.  NOT when you really need the labcase back, which is usually a day or two later and is the date of the appointment this case is attached to.

column.name: DateTimeCreated
column.summary: When this lab case was created. User can edit.

column.name: DateTimeSent
column.summary: Time that it actually went out to the lab.

column.name: DateTimeRecd
column.summary: Date/time received back from the lab.  If this is filled, then the case is considered received.

column.name: DateTimeChecked
column.summary: Date/time that quality was checked.  It is now completely ready for the patient.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: Instructions
column.summary: The text instructions for this labcase.

column.name: LabFee
column.summary: This is used for tracking and informational purposes only. The fee is not used in any calculation.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: Automatically updated whenever a row is added or changed.  Not user editable.

column.name: InvoiceNum
column.summary: Optional invoice number

table.name: laboratory
table.summary: A dental laboratory. Will be attached to lab cases.

column.name: LaboratoryNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Description of lab.

column.name: Phone
column.summary: Freeform text includes punctuation.

column.name: Notes
column.summary: Any notes.  No practical limit to amount of text.

column.name: Slip
column.summary: FK to sheetdef.SheetDefNum.  Lab slips can be set for individual laboratories.  If zero, then the default internal lab slip will be used instead of a custom lab slip.

table.name: labpanel
table.summary: One lab panel comes back from the lab with multiple lab results.  Multiple panels can come back in one HL7 message.  This table loosely corresponds to the OBR segment.

column.name: LabPanelNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: RawMessage
column.summary: The entire raw HL7 message.  Can contain other labpanels in addition to this one.

column.name: LabNameAddress
column.summary: Both name and address in a single field.  OBR-20.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: To be used for synch with web server.

column.name: SpecimenCondition
column.summary: OBR-13.  Usually blank.  Example: hemolyzed.

column.name: SpecimenSource
column.summary: OBR-15.  Usually blank.  Example: LNA&Arterial Catheter&HL70070.

column.name: ServiceId
column.summary: OBR-4-0, Service performed, id portion, LOINC.  For example, 24331-1.

column.name: ServiceName
column.summary: OBR-4-1, Service performed description.  Example, Lipid Panel.

column.name: MedicalOrderNum
column.summary: FK to medicalorder.MedicalOrderNum.  Used to attach in imported lab panel to a lab order.  Multiple panels may be attached to an order.

table.name: labresult
table.summary: Medical labs, not dental labs.  Multiple labresults are attached to a labpanel.  Loosely corresponds to the OBX segment in HL7.

column.name: LabResultNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: LabPanelNum
column.summary: FK to labpanel.LabPanelNum.

column.name: DateTimeTest
column.summary: OBX-14.

column.name: TestName
column.summary: OBX-3-1, text portion.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: To be used for synch with web server.

column.name: TestID
column.summary: OBX-3-0, id portion, LOINC.  For example, 10676-5.

column.name: ObsValue
column.summary: OBX-5. Value always stored as a string because the type might vary in the future.

column.name: ObsUnits
column.summary: OBX-6  For example, mL.  Was FK to drugunit.DrugUnitNum, but that would make reliable import problematic, so now it's just text.

column.name: ObsRange
column.summary: OBX-7  For example, <200 or >=40.

column.name: AbnormalFlag
column.summary: Enum:LabAbnormalFlag 0-None, 1-Below, 2-Normal, 3-Above.

EnumValue._: 0-No value.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1-Below normal.
EnumValue.name: Below

EnumValue._: 2-Normal.
EnumValue.name: Normal

EnumValue._: 3-Above high normal.
EnumValue.name: Above

table.name: labturnaround
table.summary: The amount of time it takes for a lab case to be processed at the lab.  Used to compute due dates.

column.name: LabTurnaroundNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: LaboratoryNum
column.summary: FK to laboratory.LaboratoryNum. The lab that this item is attached to.

column.name: Description
column.summary: The description of the service that the lab is performing.

column.name: DaysPublished
column.summary: The number of days that the lab publishes as the turnaround time for the service.

column.name: DaysActual
column.summary: The actual number of days.  Might be longer than DaysPublished due to travel time.  This is what the actual calculations will be done on.

table.name: language
table.summary: This is a list of phrases that need to be translated.  The primary key is a combination of the ClassType and the English phrase.  This table is currently filled dynmically at run time, but the plan is to fill it using a tool that parses the code.

column.name: LanguageNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EnglishComments
column.summary: No longer used.

column.name: ClassType
column.summary: A string representing the class where the translation is used.

column.name: English
column.summary: The English version of the phrase, case sensitive.

column.name: IsObsolete
column.summary: As this gets more sophisticated, we will use this field to mark some phrases obsolete instead of just deleting them outright.  That way, translators will still have access to them.  For now, this is not used at all.

table.name: languageforeign
table.summary: Will usually only contain translations for a single foreign language, although more are allowed.  The primary key is a combination of the ClassType and the English phrase and the culture.

column.name: LanguageForeignNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClassType
column.summary: A string representing the class where the translation is used.

column.name: English
column.summary: The English version of the phrase.  Case sensitive.

column.name: Culture
column.summary: The specific culture name.  Almost always in 5 digit format like this: en-US.

column.name: Translation
column.summary: The foreign translation.  Remember we use Unicode-8, so this translation can be in any language, including Russian, Hebrew, and Chinese.

column.name: Comments
column.summary: Comments for other translators for the foreign language.

table.name: languagepat
table.summary: Practice-defined translations for text shown to patients. Unlike Language and LanguageForeign, which translate Open Dental's interface to a practice's preferred language, LanguagePat allows practices to customize translations of messages and information displayed to patients in their preferred language. Used right now for about 30 prefs for things like email messages. Also used for EForms. So either PrefName will be empty or EFormFieldDefNum will be 0. Sheets are translated differently, by making a copy of each SheetFieldDef used for each language.

column.name: LanguagePatNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PrefName
column.summary: FK to pref.PrefName. There are about 30 of these in use. This allows us to translate the value stored for templates like email, postcard, text, etc. Will be empty string if this is an eForm translation.>

column.name: Language
column.summary:
