Three-letter language name or custom language name.  The custom language name is the full string name and is not necessarily supported by Microsoft.
            This will typically be matched to the patient's preferred language to select the appropriate translation.
            Three-letter language name examples: eng (English), spa (Spanish), fra (French).Custom language name examples: Tahitian, American Sign Language, Morse Code.
            The LanguagesUsedByPatients preference stores the three-letter names that the practice chooses to support.

column.name: Translation
column.summary: The translated text. Max 65,000 characters. Might store complex email templates.

column.name: EFormFieldDefNum
column.summary: FK to eformfielddef.EFormFieldDefNum. This is how eForms get translated. Once a def is converted to an eForm, this is not needed. The eForm fields have all the translated text. Will be 0 if this is a pref translation.

table.name: letter
table.summary: These are templates that are used to send simple letters to patients.

column.name: LetterNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Description of the Letter.

column.name: BodyText
column.summary: Text of the letter

table.name: lettermerge
table.summary: Describes the templates for letter merges to Word.

column.name: LetterMergeNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Description of this letter.

column.name: TemplateName
column.summary: The filename of the Word template. eg MyTemplate.doc.

column.name: DataFileName
column.summary: The name of the data file. eg MyTemplate.txt.

column.name: Category
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.

column.name: ImageFolder
column.summary:
FK to definition.DefNum. This determines the default Image Category that will be selected when printing or previewing the letter.
            Can be 0 which means 'None' will be selected.

table.name: lettermergefield
table.summary: When doing a lettermerge, a data file is created with certain fields.  This is a list of those fields for each lettermerge.

column.name: FieldNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: LetterMergeNum
column.summary: FK to lettermerge.LetterMergeNum.

column.name: FieldName
column.summary: One of the preset available field names.

table.name: limitedbetafeature
table.summary: Limited Beta features are specific parts of the OpenDental suite of services that are locked behind a registration process while the feature undergoes improvement or further testing. This registration process is done through HQ working with a customer to determine if they will be a good candidate to use and test this feature before it's full release. If they are a good candidate, a row is inserted into their database that will allow them access to the feature. LimitedBetaFeatures get inserted during the nightly synch with our eServiceSignups. This is done in our call to WebServiceMainHQProxy.GetEServiceSetupFull. The nightly synch is the only place these rows get altered.

column.name: LimitedBetaFeatureTypeNum
column.summary: Stores the integer value of the LimitedBetaFeatureEnum. This is done to prevent out of bounds exceptions due to versioning.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: ClinicNum that is signed up for the feature. Clinic independant features only have one row with a clinicNum of -1.

column.name: IsSignedUp
column.summary: An office is considered signed up if they have a valid version to be using this feature on, the feature is on limited beta, and they've signed up with HQ.

table.name: loginattempt
table.summary: Keeps track of failed login attempts.

column.name: LoginAttemptNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: UserName
column.summary: The username that was attempted. May not be a username that exists.

column.name: LoginType
column.summary:
Enum:UserWebFKeyType The part of the program where an attempt was made. If we want to use this for other parts of the program
            that are do not use the userweb table, we can change this enum to a different one.

Enumeration.name: UserWebFKeyType
Enumeration.summary: The type of row that identifies which table FKey links to.

EnumValue._: This is a default value that should never be saved into the table.
EnumValue.name: Undefined

EnumValue._: FK to patient.PatNum
EnumValue.name: PatientPortal

column.name: DateTFail
column.summary: When the failed attempt was attempted.

table.name: loinc
table.summary: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes (LOINC) used to identify both lab panels and lab results. Widths specified are from LOINC documentation and may not represent length of fields in the Open Dental Database.

column.name: LoincNum
column.summary: Primary key. Internal use only.

column.name: LoincCode
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Also called LOINC_NUM in the official LOINCDB. Width-10. LOINC244 column 1.

column.name: Component
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# First Major axis:component or analyte. Width-255. LOINC244 column 2.

column.name: PropertyObserved
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Second major axis:property observed (e.g., mass vs. substance). Width-30. LOINC244 column 3.

column.name: TimeAspct
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Third major axis:timing of the measurement (e.g., point in time vs 24 hours). Width-15. LOINC244 column 4.

column.name: SystemMeasured
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Fourth major axis:type of specimen or system (e.g., serum vs urine). Width-100 LOINC244. column 5.

column.name: ScaleType
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Fifth major axis:scale of measurement (e.g., qualitative vs. quantitative). Width-30. LOINC244 column 6.

column.name: MethodType
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Sixth major axis:method of measurement. Width-50. LOINC244 column 7.

column.name: StatusOfCode
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Width-10. LOINC244 column 13.ACTIVE = Concept is active. Use at will.TRIAL = Concept is experimental in nature. Use with caution as the concept and associated attributes may change. DISCOURAGED = Concept is not recommended for current use. New mappings to this concept are discouraged; although existing may mappings may continue to be valid in context. Wherever  possible, the superseding concept is indicated in the MAP_TO field in the MAP_TO table (see Table 28b) and should be used instead. DEPRECATED = Concept is deprecated. Concept should not be used, but it is retained in LOINC for historical purposes. Wherever possible, the superseding concept is indicated in the MAP_TO field (see Table 28b) and should be used both for new mappings and updating existing implementations..

column.name: NameShort
column.summary: #EULA REQUIRED# Introduced in version 2.07, this field is a concatenation of the fully specified LOINC name. The field width may change in a future release. Width 40. LOINC244 column 29.

column.name: ClassType
column.summary: 1=Laboratory class; 2=Clinical class; 3=Claims attachments; 4=Surveys. LOINC244 column 16.

column.name: UnitsRequired
column.summary: Y/N field that indicates that units are required when this LOINC is included as an OBX segment in a HIPAA attachment. LOINC244 column 26.

column.name: OrderObs
column.summary: Defines term as order only, observation only, or both. A fourth category, Subset, is used for terms that are subsets of a panel but do not represent a package that is known to be orderable we have defined them only to make it easier to maintain panels or other sets within the LOINC construct. LOINC244 column 30.

column.name: HL7FieldSubfieldID
column.summary: A value in this field means that the content should be delivered in the named field/subfield of the HL7 message. When NULL, the data for this data element should be sent in an OBX segment with this LOINC code stored in OBX-3 and with the value in the OBX-5. Width 50. LOINC244 column 32.

column.name: ExternalCopyrightNotice
column.summary: External copyright holders copyright notice for this LOINC code. LOINC244 column 33.

column.name: NameLongCommon
column.summary: This field contains the LOINC term in a more readable format than the fully specified name. The long common names have been created via a table driven algorithmic process. Most abbreviations and acronyms that are used in the LOINC database have been fully spelled out in English. Width 255. LOINC244 column 35.

column.name: UnitsUCUM
column.summary: The Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM) is a code system intended to include all units of measures being contemporarily used in international science, engineering, and business. (www.unitsofmeasure.org ) This field contains example units of measures for this term expressed as UCUM units. Width 255. LOINC244 column 1.

column.name: RankCommonTests
column.summary: Ranking of approximately 2000 common tests performed by laboratories in USA. LOINC244 column 45.

column.name: RankCommonOrders
column.summary: Ranking of approximately 300 common orders performed by laboratories in USA. LOINC244 column 46.

table.name: medicalorder
table.summary: Ehr. Lab and radiology orders.  Medication orders are simply fields in medicationPat.

column.name: MedicalOrderNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MedOrderType
column.summary: Enum:MedicalOrderType Laboratory=0,Radiology=1.

EnumValue._: 0- Laboratory
EnumValue.name: Laboratory

EnumValue._: 1- Radiology
EnumValue.name: Radiology

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: DateTimeOrder
column.summary: Date and time of order.

column.name: Description
column.summary: User will be required to type entire order out from scratch.

column.name: IsDiscontinued
column.summary: EHR requires Active/Discontinued status. 0=Active, 1=Discontinued.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

table.name: medication
table.summary: A list of medications, not attached to any particular patient.  Not allowed to delete if in use by a patient.  Not allowed to edit name once created due to possibility of damage to patient record.

column.name: MedicationNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MedName
column.summary: Name of the medication.  User can change this.  If an RxCui is present, the RxNorm string can be pulled from the in-memory table for UI display in addition to the MedName.

column.name: GenericNum
column.summary:
FK to medication.MedicationNum.  Cannot be zero.
            If this is a generic drug, then the GenericNum will be the same as the MedicationNum.
            Otherwise, if this is a brand drug, then the GenericNum will be a non-zero value corresponding to another medicaiton.

column.name: Notes
column.summary: Examples: interactions, drug class, contraindications, etc.  Not typically for dosage.  Mg, times per days, etc. typically entered into MedicationPat.PatNote.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: The last date and time this row was altered.  Not user editable.

column.name: RxCui
column.summary: RxNorm Code identifier.  We should have used a string type.  Used by EHR in CQM.  But the queries should use medicationpat.RxCui, NOT this RxCui, because all medicationpats (meds and orders) coming back from NewCrop will not have a FK to this medication table.  When this RxCui is modified by the user, then medicationpat.RxCui is automatically updated where medicationpat.MedicationNum matches this medication.

table.name: medicationpat
table.summary: Links medications to patients.  For ehr, some of these can be considered 'medication orders', but only if they contain a PatNote (instructions), a ProvNum, and a DateStart.

column.name: MedicationPatNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: MedicationNum
column.summary: FK to medication.MedicationNum.  If 0, implies that the medication order came from eRx.  This was done to allow MU2 measures to be set by either creating a medication from the medical window, or by creating an manual prescription.

column.name: PatNote
column.summary: Medication notes specific to this patient. Example: 10 mg tablets, two tabs 3 times per day

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: The last date and time this row was altered.  Not user editable.

column.name: DateStart
column.summary: Date that the medication was started.  Can be minval if unknown.

column.name: DateStop
column.summary: Date that the medication was stopped.  Can be minval if unknown.  If minval, then the medication is not "discontinued".  If prior to today, then the medication is "discontinued".  If today or a future date, then not discontinued yet.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum. Can be 0. Gets set to the patient's primary provider when adding a new med.  If adding the med from EHR, gets set to the ProvNum of the logged-in user.

column.name: MedDescript
column.summary: Used for eRx and eForm import. In these cases, MedicationNum=0.

column.name: RxCui
column.summary: For NewCrop medical orders, corresponds to the RxCui of the prescription (NewCrop only returns a value sometimes).  Otherwise, this field is synched with the medication.RxCui field based on medication.MedicationNum.  We should have used a string type.  The only purpose of this field is so that when CCDs are created, we have structured data to put in the XML, not just plain text.  Allergies exported in CCD do not look at this table, but only at the medication table.  Medications require MedicationPat.RxCui or Medication.RxCui to be exported on CCD.

column.name: ErxGuid
column.summary:
Uniquely identifies the prescription corresponding to the medical order.
            Allows us to update existing eRx medical orders when refreshing prescriptions in the Chart (similar to how prescriptions are updated).
            Also used in 2-way medication synching with eRx.

column.name: IsCpoe
column.summary: If eRx is used to prescribe a medication, a medication order is imported automatically into Open Dental.  If a provider is logged in, then this is CPOE (Computerized Provider Order Entry), and this will be true.   Or, if a provider is logged in and Rx entered through OD, it's also CPOE.  If a staff person is logged in, and enters an Rx through NewCrop or OD, then this is non-CPOE, so false.

table.name: medlab
table.summary:
The EHRLab table is structured to tightly with the HL7 standard and should have names that more reflect how the user will
            consume the data and for that reason for actual implementation we are using these medlab tables.
            Medical lab observation order.  This table is currently only used for LabCorp, but may be utilized by other third party lab
            services in the future.  These are the fields required for the LabCorp result report, used to link the order to the result(s),
            specimen(s), place(s) of service, or for linking parent and child results.
            This table contains data from the PID, ORC, OBR, and applicable NTE segments

column.name: MedLabNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SendingApp
column.summary:
MSH-2 - Sending Application.  Used to identify the LabCorp Lab System sending the results.
            Possible values for LabCorp (as of their v10.7 specs): '1100' - LabCorp Lab System, 'DIANON' - DIANON Systems,
            'ADL' - Acupath Diagnostic Laboratories, 'EGL' - Esoterix Genetic Laboratories.
            For backward compatibility only: 'CMBP', 'LITHOLINK', 'USLABS'

column.name: SendingFacility
column.summary:
MSH-3 - Sending Facility.  Identifies the LabCorp laboratory responsible for the client.
            It could be a LabCorp assigned 'Responsible Lab Code' representing the responsible laboratory or it could be a CLIA number.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  PID.2 - External Patient ID. LabCorp report field "Client Alt. Pat ID".

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  Can be 0. Attempt to match ordering prov external IDs to internal provnum.

column.name: PatIDLab
column.summary: PID.3 - Lab Assigned Patient Id.  LabCorp report field "Specimen Number".  LabCorp assigned, alpha numeric specimen number.

column.name: PatIDAlt
column.summary: PID.4 - Alternate Patient ID.  LabCorp report field "Patient ID".  Alternate patient ID.

column.name: PatAge
column.summary:
PID.7.2/7.3/7.4 - Patient Age Years/Months/Days.  LabCorp report field "Age (Y/M/D)".  YYY/MM/DD format.  Three chars for years,
            2 each for months and days.  Some tests require age for calculation of result.  This will be the age at the time of the test, so we will use
            the values in the message instead of re-calculating..

column.name: PatAccountNum
column.summary: PID.18.1 - Account Number.  LabCorp report field "Account Number".  LabCorp Client ID, 8 digit account number.

column.name: PatFasting
column.summary:
PID.18.7 - Fasting.  LabCorp report field "Fasting".  Y, N, or blank.
            A blank component will be stored as 0 - Unknown, the result report fasting field will be blank.

column.name: SpecimenID
column.summary:
ORC.2.1 and OBR.2.1 - Unique Foreign Accession or Specimen ID.  LabCorp report field "Client Accession (ACC)".
            ID sent on the specimen container.

column.name: SpecimenIDFiller
column.summary:
ORC.3.1 and OBR.3.1 - Internal (to LabCorp for example)/Filler Accession or Specimen ID.
            LabCorp assigned specimen number, reused on a yearly basis.

column.name: ObsTestID
column.summary: OBR.4.1 - Observation Battery Identifier.  Reflex result will have this value in OBR.29 to link the reflex to the parent.

column.name: ObsTestDescript
column.summary: OBR.4.2 - Observation Battery Text.  LabCorp report field "Tests Ordered".

column.name: ObsTestLoinc
column.summary:
OBR.4.4 - Alternate Battery Identifier (LOINC).  This is the LOINC code for the test performed.
            When displaying the results, LabCorp requires OBR.4.2, the text name of the test to be displayed, not the LOINC code.
            But we will store it so we can link to the LOINC code table for reporting purposes.

column.name: ObsTestLoincText
column.summary:
OBR.4.5 - Alternate Observation Battery Text (LOINC Description).  The LOINC code description for the test performed.
            We will display OBR.4.2 per LabCorp requirements, but we will store this description for reporting purposes.

column.name: DateTimeCollected
column.summary:
OBR.7 - Observation/Specimen Collection Date/Time.  LabCorp report field "Date & Time Collected".
            yyyyMMddHHmm format in the message, no seconds.  May be blank.

column.name: TotalVolume
column.summary:
OBR.9 - Collection/Urine Volume (Quantity/Field Value).  LabCorp report field "Total Volume".
            The LabCorp document says this field is "Numeric Characters", but the HL7 documentation data type as CQ, which is a number with units
            in the form of Quantity^Units.  The Units component has subcomponents: ID&Text&Name of Coding System&Alt ID&Alt Text&
            Name of Alt Coding System&Coding System Version ID&Alt Coding System Version ID&Original Text.
            We will make this a string column and store the Quantity with the Units ID subcomponent if present.
            The default unit of measurement is ML, so if the field is a number only we will add ML.

column.name: ActionCode
column.summary: Enum:ResultAction OBR.11 - Action Code.  Blank for normal result, "G" for reflex result.

Enumeration.name: ResultAction
Enumeration.summary: Order Result Action Code.  To identify the type of result being returned.

EnumValue._: 0 - None.  Standard results will be blank.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Add On.  Limited usage and not applicable for all add on tests.
EnumValue.name: A

EnumValue._: 2 - Reflex.  Lab generated result for test not on the original order.
EnumValue.name: G

column.name: ClinicalInfo
column.summary:
OBR.13.1 - Relevant Clinical Information.  LabCorp report field "Additional Information".  The report field will be filled with this
            value from the first OBR record in the message.  The message limits this field to 64 characters, the rest is truncated.

column.name: DateTimeEntered
column.summary:
OBR.14 - Date/Time of Specimen Receipt in Lab.  LabCorp report field "Date Entered".  yyyyMMddHHmm format in the message, no seconds.
            Date and time the order was entered in the Lab System.

column.name: OrderingProvNPI
column.summary:
ORC.12.1 and OBR.16.1 - Ordering Provider ID Number.  LabCorp report field "NPI".  ORC.12.* and OBR.16.* are repeatable, the eighth
            component identifies the source of the ID in the first component.  Component 8 possible values: "U"-UPIN,
            "P"-Provider Number (Medicaid or Commercial Ins Provider ID), "N"-NPI (Required for third party billing), "L"-Local (Physician ID).

column.name: OrderingProvLocalID
column.summary:
ORC.12.1 and OBR.16.1 - Ordering Provider ID Number.  LabCorp report field "Physician ID".  ORC.12.* and OBR.16.* are repeatable,
            the eighth component identifies the source of the ID in the first component.  Component 8 possible values: "U"-UPIN,
            "P"-Provider Number (Medicaid or Commercial Ins Provider ID), "N"-NPI (Required for third party billing), "L"-Local (Physician ID).

column.name: OrderingProvLName
column.summary: ORC.12.2 and OBR.16.2 - Ordering Provider Last Name.  LabCorp report field "Physician Name".  Last, First.

column.name: OrderingProvFName
column.summary: ORC.12.3 and OBR.16.3 - Ordering Provider First Initial.  LabCorp report field "Physician Name".  Last, First.

column.name: SpecimenIDAlt
column.summary: OBR.18 - Alternate Unique Foreign Accession / Specimen ID.  LabCorp report field "Control Number".

column.name: DateTimeReported
column.summary:
OBR.22 - Date/Time Observations Reported.  LabCorp report field "Date & Time Reported".  yyyyMMddHHmm format in the message, no secs.
            Date and time the results were released from the Lab System.

column.name: ResultStatus
column.summary: Enum:ResultStatus OBR.25 - Order Result Status.  LabCorp possible values: "F" - Final, "P" - Preliminary, "X" - Cancelled, "C" - Corrected.

Enumeration.name: ResultStatus
Enumeration.summary: Order Result Status.  Identification of status of results at the ordered item level.

EnumValue._: 0 - Corrected Result.
EnumValue.name: C

EnumValue._: 1 - Final.  Result complete and verified.
EnumValue.name: F

EnumValue._: 2 - Incomplete.  For Discrete Microbiology Testing.
EnumValue.name: I

EnumValue._: 3 - Preliminary.  Final not yet obtained.
EnumValue.name: P

EnumValue._: 4 - Canceled.  Procedure cannot be done.  Result canceled due to Non-Performance.
EnumValue.name: X

column.name: ParentObsID
column.summary:
OBR.26.1 - Link to Parent Result or Organism Link to Susceptibility.
            A reflex test will have the parent's OBX.3.1 value here for linking.

column.name: ParentObsTestID
column.summary: OBR.29 - Link to Parent Order.  A reflex test will have the value from OBR.4.1 of the original order in this field for linking.

column.name: NotePat
column.summary:
NTE.3 - Comment Text, PID Level.  The NTE segment is repeatable and the Comment Text component is limited to 78 characters.  Multiple
            NTE segments can be used for longer comments.  All NTE segments at the PID level will be concatenated and stored in this one field.

column.name: NoteLab
column.summary:
NTE.3 - Comment Text, OBR level.  The NTE segment is repeatable and the Comment Text component is limited to 78 characters.  Multiple
            NTE segments can be used for longer comments.  All NTE segments at the OBR level will be concatenated and stored in this one field.

column.name: FileName
column.summary:
Not unique. More than one MedLab object can point to the same FileName, so deleting the MedLab object does not necessarily mean the
            file can also be deleted.  This is the filename of the original archived message that was processed to create this medlab object as well as
            associated medlabresult, medlabspecimen, and medlabfacility obects.  The files will be stored in the OpenDentImages folder in a sub-folder
            called MedLabHL7.  If a message is processed correctly it will be moved into the sub-folder MedLabHL7/Processed.  Any message that remains in
            the MedLabHL7 folder and aren't moved into the Processed folder failed at some point during processing.  If the option to store images directly
            in the database is chosen, this will be an empty field and there will not be the option to display the original HL7 message.  
            This is a relative file path from the ImageStore.GetPreferredAtoZpath(), 
            Example: "MedLabHL7/FileName.txt" OR "MedLabHL7/Processed/FileName.txt" 
            Use: string pathToFile=ODFileUtils.CombinePaths(ImageStore.GetPreferredAtoZpath(),FileName)

column.name: OriginalPIDSegment
column.summary: The PID Segment from the HL7 message used to generate this MedLab object.

table.name: medlabfacattach
table.summary:
Links a MedLab or a MedLabResult to a place of service.  Either the MedLabNum OR the MedLabResultNum column will be populated, never
            both, so this will link the facility to EITHER a MedLab OR a MedLabResult object.
            Every MedLab and MedLabResult will have 1 to many laboratories attached.

column.name: MedLabFacAttachNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MedLabNum
column.summary: FK to medlab.MedLabNum.

column.name: MedLabResultNum
column.summary: FK to medlabresult.MedLabResultNum.

column.name: MedLabFacilityNum
column.summary: FK to medlabfacility.MedLabFacilityNum.

table.name: medlabfacility
table.summary:
Medical lab facility that performed the test procedure(s).  Contains data from the ZPS segment.  Each MedLab object can have one to
            many places of service, each in a repetition of the ZPS segment.  Each repetition will be its own row in this table.

column.name: MedLabFacilityNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FacilityName
column.summary: ZPS.3 - Facility Name.  Medical lab location name that performed the testing.

column.name: Address
column.summary: ZPS.4.1 - Facility Address.

column.name: City
column.summary: ZPS.4.3 - Facility City.

column.name: State
column.summary: ZPS.4.4 - Facility State or Province.  Upper case state abbreviation.

column.name: Zip
column.summary: ZPS.4.5 - Facility Zip or Postal Code.

column.name: Phone
column.summary: ZPS.5 - Facility Phone Number.

column.name: DirectorTitle
column.summary: ZPS.7.1 - Facility Director Title.

column.name: DirectorLName
column.summary: ZPS.7.2 - Facility Director Last Name.

column.name: DirectorFName
column.summary: ZPS.7.3 - Facility Director First Name.

table.name: medlabresult
table.summary:
Medical lab result.  The EHRLabResult table is structured too tightly with the HL7 standard and should have names that more reflect how 
            the user will consume the data and for that reason for actual implementation we are using these medlab tables.
            This table is currently only used for LabCorp, but may be utilized by other third party lab
            services in the future.  These fields are required for the LabCorp result report, used to link the result to an order,
            or for linking a parent and child result.  Contains data from the OBX, ZEF, and applicable NTE segments.

column.name: MedLabResultNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MedLabNum
column.summary: FK to medlab.medLabNum.  Each MedLab object can have one or more results pointing to it.

column.name: ObsID
column.summary: OBX.3.1 - Observation Identifier.  Reflex results will have the ObsID of the parent in OBR.26 for linking.

column.name: ObsText
column.summary: OBX.3.2 - Observation Text.  LabCorp report field "TESTS".  LabCorp test name.

column.name: ObsLoinc
column.summary:
OBX.3.4 - Alternate Identifier (LOINC).  This is the LOINC code for the observation.
            When displaying the results, LabCorp requires OBX.3.2, the text name of the test to be displayed, not the LOINC code.
            But we will store it so we can link to the LOINC code table for reporting purposes.

column.name: ObsLoincText
column.summary:
OBX.3.5 - Alternate Observation Text (LOINC Description).  The LOINC code description for the observation.
            We will display OBX.3.2 per LabCorp requirements, but we will store this description for reporting purposes.

column.name: ObsIDSub
column.summary:
OBX.4 - Observation Sub ID.  Used to aid in the identification of results with the same Observation ID (OBX.3) within a given OBR.
            This value is used to tie the results to the same organism.  The value in OBX.5.3 tells whether this OBX is the organism, observation, or
            antibiotic and then the value in OBX.4 links them together as to whether this is for organism #1, organism #2, etc.

column.name: ObsValue
column.summary:
OBX.5.1 - Observation Value.  LabCorp report field "RESULT".
            Can be null if coded entries, prelims, canceled, or >21 chars and being returned as an attached NTE.
            "TNP" will be reported for Test Not Performed.  For value >21 chars in length: OBX.2 will be 'TX' for text,
            OBX.5 will be NULL (empty field), and the value will be in attached NTEs.
            Examples: Value less than 21 chars:
            OBX|1|ST|001180^Potassium, Serum^L||K+ is >6.5 mEq/L.||3.5-5.5|A||N|F|19830527||200605040929|01|
            Value >21 chars:
            OBX|6|TX|001180^Potassium, Serum^L||||3.5-5.5|||N|C|19830527||200511071406|01|
            NTE|1|L|Red cells observed in serum. Glucose may be falsely decreased.
            NTE|2|L|Potassium may be falsely increased.

column.name: ObsSubType
column.summary:
Enum:DataSubtype OBX.5.3 - Data Subtype.  Used to identify the coding system. Required if Discrete Microbiology testing is ordered to identify
            Microbiology Result Type.  Example of use: If OBX.5.3 is ORM, then the observation sub ID in OBX.4 is used to associate the result with
            a specific organism.  OBX.4 might contain 1, 2, or 3 meaning the result is for organism #1, organism #2, or organism #3.

Enumeration.name: DataSubtype
Enumeration.summary:
Used to identify the coding system. Required if Discrete Microbiology testing is ordered to identify Microbiology Result Type.
            Example of use: If OBX.5.3 is ORM, then the observation sub ID in OBX.4 is used to associate the result with a specific organism.
            OBX.4 might contain 1, 2, or 3 meaning the result is for organism #1, organism #2, or organism #3.

EnumValue._: This idicates that we are unable to parse the value from the HL7 message into a data subtype.
EnumValue.name: Unknown

EnumValue._: Antibody (for Discrete Microbiology only)
EnumValue.name: ANT

EnumValue._: Organism identifier (for Discrete Microbiology only)
EnumValue.name: ORM

EnumValue._: Presumptive organism identifier (for Discrete Microbiology only)
EnumValue.name: ORP

EnumValue._: Observation (for Discrete Microbiology only)
EnumValue.name: OBS

EnumValue._: Modifier (for Discrete Microbiology only)
EnumValue.name: MOD

EnumValue._: Local Identifier (default when no Microbiology Result Text)
EnumValue.name: L

EnumValue._: Embedded PDF result type or separate PDF file
EnumValue.name: PDF

EnumValue._: Embedded TIF result type or a separate TIF file
EnumValue.name: TIF

column.name: ObsUnits
column.summary: OBX.6.1 - Identifier.  LabCorp report field "UNITS".  Units of measure, if too large it will be in the NTE segment.

column.name: ReferenceRange
column.summary: OBX.7 - Reference Ranges.  LabCorp report field "REFERENCE INTERVAL".  Only if applicable.

column.name: AbnormalFlag
column.summary:
Enum:AbnormalFlag OBX.8 - Abnormal Flags.  LabCorp report field "FLAG".  Blank or null is normal.  When this is displayed on the LabCorp report
            it must be the human readable display name, so for example _gt (>) is displayed as "Panic High" and _lt (<) is "Panic Low".

Enumeration.name: AbnormalFlag
Enumeration.summary: MedLab Abnormal Flags.  Similar to EhrLabResult.HL70078 abnormal flag enum.

EnumValue._: 0 - None.  Blank or null value indicates normal result, so no abnormal flag.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Panic High.  Actual value is ">" but symbol cannot be used as an enum value.
EnumValue.name: _gt

EnumValue._: 2 - Panic Low.  Actual value is "<" but symbol cannot be used as an enum value.
EnumValue.name: _lt

EnumValue._: 3 - Abnormal.  Applies to non-numeric results.
EnumValue.name: A

EnumValue._: 4 - Critical Abnormal.  Applies to non-numeric results.
EnumValue.name: AA

EnumValue._: 5 - Above High Normal.
EnumValue.name: H

EnumValue._: 6 - Alert High.
EnumValue.name: HH

EnumValue._: 7 - Intermediate.  For Discrete Microbiology susceptibilities only.
EnumValue.name: I

EnumValue._: 8 - Below Low Normal.
EnumValue.name: L

EnumValue._: 9 - Alert Low.
EnumValue.name: LL

EnumValue._: 10 - Negative for Drug Interpretation Codes and Discrete Microbiology.
EnumValue.name: NEG

EnumValue._: 11 - Positive for Drug Interpretation Codes and Discrete Microbiology.
EnumValue.name: POS

EnumValue._: 12 - Resistant.  For Discrete Microbiology susceptibilities only.
EnumValue.name: R

EnumValue._: 13 - Susceptible.  For Discrete Microbiology susceptibilities only.
EnumValue.name: S

column.name: ResultStatus
column.summary: Enum:ResultStatus OBX.11 - Observation Result Status.

Enumeration.name: ResultStatus
Enumeration.summary: Order Result Status.  Identification of status of results at the ordered item level.

EnumValue._: 0 - Corrected Result.
EnumValue.name: C

EnumValue._: 1 - Final.  Result complete and verified.
EnumValue.name: F

EnumValue._: 2 - Incomplete.  For Discrete Microbiology Testing.
EnumValue.name: I

EnumValue._: 3 - Preliminary.  Final not yet obtained.
EnumValue.name: P

EnumValue._: 4 - Canceled.  Procedure cannot be done.  Result canceled due to Non-Performance.
EnumValue.name: X

column.name: DateTimeObs
column.summary:
OBX.14 - Date/Time of Observation.  yyyyMMddHHmm format in the message, no seconds.
            Date and time tech entered result into the Lab System.

column.name: FacilityID
column.summary:
OBX.15 - Producer ID (Producer’s Reference).  LabCorp report field "LAB".  ID of LabCorp Facility responsible for performing the
            testing.  The Lab Name is supplied in the ZPS segment.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary:
FK to document.DocNum.  ZEF.2 - Embedded File.  Each result may have one or more ZEF segments for embedded files.
            The base-64 text version of the PDF is sent in ZEF.2.  If the file size exceeds 50k, then multiple segments will be sent with 50k blocks
            of the text.  When processing, we will concatenate all ZEF.2 fields, create the PDF document, store the file in the patient's image folder,
            and create an entry in the document table.  Then update this field with the pointer to the document table entry.

column.name: Note
column.summary:
NTE.3 at the OBX level.  The NTE segment is repeatable and the Comment Text component is limited to 78 characters.  Multiple NTE
            segments can be used for longer comments.  All NTE segments at the OBX level will be concatenated and stored in this one field.

table.name: medlabspecimen
table.summary:
The EHRLabSpecimen table is structured to tightly with the HL7 standard and should have names that more reflect how the user will
            consume the data and for that reason for actual implementation we are using these medlab tables.
            Medical lab specimen.  Contains data from the SPM segment.  Each MedLab object can have 0 to many specimen segments.
            Each segment will be its own row in this table.

column.name: MedLabSpecimenNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MedLabNum
column.summary: FK to medlab.MedLabNum.  Each MedLab object can have 0 to many specimens pointing to it.

column.name: SpecimenID
column.summary:
SPM.2 - Specimen ID.  Unique identifier for the specimen as referenced by the Placer application, the Filler application, or both.
            The value sent in this field should be the identification value sent on the specimen container.

column.name: SpecimenDescript
column.summary: SPM.14 - Specimen Description.  Additional information about the specimen.

column.name: DateTimeCollected
column.summary:
SPM.17 - Specimen Collection Date/Time.  yyyyMMddHHmm format in the message, no seconds.  The date and time when the specimen was
            acquired from the source.  This is a DR - Date/Time Range data type, so it may have more than one component if a specimen was collected over
            a period of time.  The first component is the start date/time so we will make sure to only store SPM.17.1 in this field.

table.name: mobileappdevice
table.summary: Stores information on mobile app devices. These are devices that utilize the Xamarin mobile application.

column.name: MobileAppDeviceNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.name: DeviceName
column.summary: The name of the device.

column.name: UniqueID
column.summary: The unique identifier of the device. Platform specific.

column.name: IsEclipboardEnabled
column.summary:
Indicates whether the device is allowed to operate the checkin app. 
            For BYOD sessions will always be true because BYOD is authenticated by a unique URL link in a text message.

column.name: EclipboardLastAttempt
column.summary: The date and time of the last attempted login for Eclipboard.

column.name: EclipboardLastLogin
column.summary: The date and time of the last successful login for Eclipboard.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum. Indicates which patient is currently using the device. 0 indicates the device is not in use. -1 indicates
            that the device is in use but we do not yet know which patient is using the device.

column.name: LastCheckInActivity
column.summary: The date and time when we last updated the PatNum field for this device (indication the current use-state of the device).

column.name: IsBYODDevice
column.summary: Indicates whether a device is a BYOD device, defaults to false.

column.name: DevicePage
column.summary: Current page of the device.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. Indicates which user is currently logged into the device. 0 indicates this device is not logged into.

column.name: IsODTouchEnabled
column.summary: Indicates whether this device is being used for ODTouch or not.

column.name: ODTouchLastLogin
column.summary: The date and time of the last successful login for ODTouch.

column.name: ODTouchLastAttempt
column.summary: The date and time of the last attempted login for ODTouch.

table.name: mobilebrandingprofile
table.summary: Branding Profile for eClipboard customization. One (or none) to One relationship with clinics. Allows customers to customize the look of their eClipboard with a Clinic name and Logo.

column.name: MobileBrandingProfileNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.name: OfficeDescription
column.summary: The Clinic Name that will be shown on eClipboard CheckIn

column.name: LogoFilePath
column.summary: eConnector will fetch this file. Same path for every computer, so maybe use a network shared file. Shows as 90x90 pixels.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: The time that this object was last modified or inserted. Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Used to determine if eClipboard needs to fetch an updated mobile branding profile.

table.name: mobiledatabyte
table.summary: Table used to send various types of objects as bytes to ODXam applications. Example is a PDF for TxPlan or PaymentPlan. Row gets added here, sent to mobile device, and then consumed. Old rows are ignored.

column.name: MobileDataByteNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: RawBase64Data
column.summary: The bytes in Base64.

column.name: RawBase64Code
column.summary: The unlock code in Base64. Blank if no unlock code required to retrieve.

column.name: RawBase64Tag
column.summary: Misc data in Base64

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: Can start out as 0.

column.name: ActionType
column.summary: Enum:eActionType Stores the intended action associated to this rows data.

Enumeration.name: eActionType
Enumeration.summary: Actions representing different endpoints in mobile app.

EnumValue._: 0 - Placeholder
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Row is associated to a TP pdf to be viewed in eClipboard.
EnumValue.name: TreatmentPlan

EnumValue._: 2 - Instructs eClipboard to present patient with payment window.
EnumValue.name: MakePayment

EnumValue._: 4 - Associated with a list of perio exams.
EnumValue.name: PerioExam

EnumValue._: 5 - Used by eClilpboard to fill out exam sheets.
EnumValue.name: ExamSheet

EnumValue._: 6 - Used by eClilpboard to checkin using QR code.
EnumValue.name: Checkin

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: The DateTime this row was entered.

column.name: DateTimeExpires
column.summary: The DateTime that this row should be removed.

table.name: mobilenotification
table.summary:
Mobile App devices periodically poll this table and retrieve any records that are relevant to the device itself, the user using the device, or the clinic
            the device belongs to. The mobile apps will then perform an action based on the mobile notification type.

column.name: MobileNotificationNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: NotificationType
column.summary: Enum:MobileNotificationType The type of notification. Example: TP. This will determine what actions the mobile app will perform upon retrieving this notification.

Enumeration.name: MobileNotificationType
Enumeration.summary: The different types of mobile notifications. Preserve order. CI = Checkin portion of app (eClipboard). ODM = ODMobile

EnumValue._: Default.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._:
Check-in a patient on a given device. For this type, the tag will have 3 items: the first name, last name, and birthdate of the patient in that order. 
            The birthdate will be in DateTime.Ticks.
EnumValue.name: CI_CheckinPatient

EnumValue._:
Tells the device that is currently filling out sheets to add a sheet to the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the patnum and
            the SheetNum in that order.
EnumValue.name: CI_AddSheet

EnumValue._:
Tells the device that is currently fillout out sheets to remove a sheet from the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the patnum
            and the SheetNum in that order.
EnumValue.name: CI_RemoveSheet

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification tells the device to stop whatever it is doing and go to a fresh checkin page. This may be a blank self-checkin or may be waiting for a mobile notification. This
            allows users from OD to "clear" the device of a stale patient. No primary keys or tags needed.
EnumValue.name: CI_GoToCheckin

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when the preferences for this device's clinic changes. The tags for this mobile notification will be the EClipboardAllowSelfCheckIn(bool), EClipboardMessageComplete(string), 
            EClipboardAllowSelfPortraitOnCheckIn(bool), and EClipboardPresentAvailableFormsOnCheckIn(bool) in that order.
EnumValue.name: CI_NewEClipboardPrefs

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when the MobileAppDevice.IsAllowed changed for this device. The tag for this mobile notification will be IsAllowed (bool).
            If true then device which is currently awaiting in 'Not Allowed' state will try another login, should work this time. If false then force signout. Used for eClipboard and ODTouch.
EnumValue.name: IsAllowedChanged

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when a permission has changed for a given OD user and they are no longer allowed to use OD Mobile. 
            The ListPrimaryKeys may contain the UserNum of the user who is no longer allowed. This session will then be logged out of versioned OD Mobile.
            If ListPrimaryKeys IsNullOrEmpty() then assume all users for the given ClinicNum should be logged out. No UserNum filter necessary in this case.
EnumValue.name: ODM_LogoutODUser

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when a OD proper user sends a patients treatment plan to a specific device to show the user.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [MobileDataByteNum, PatNum, TreatPlanNum].
            ListTags Keys => The treatPlan.Heading, hasPracticeSig(Obsolete; based on if TP sheet has SigBoxPractice) .
EnumValue.name: CI_TreatmentPlan

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when a TreatmentPlan is deleted in OD and we want to tell a specific device so that they can remove it when viewing TreatmentPlans.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [TreatPlan.PatNum,TreatPlan.TreatPlanNum]
EnumValue.name: CI_RemoveTreatmentPlan

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when a payment needs to be made on an eClip device. This either adds the Make Payment action item to the checkin checklist
            or it will open the QR code to scan from OD.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [TreatPlan.PatNum]
EnumValue.name: CI_SendPayment

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when a patient is currently on the device, when a payment is made, when a new card is added (XWeb only), and when a new 
            statement is created in OD.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [PatNum]
EnumValue.name: CI_RefreshPayment

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when an OD proper user sends a payment plan to a specific device.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [MobileDataByte.MobileDataByteNum,PayPlan.PatNum,PayPlan.PayPlanNum]
            ListTags => [PayPlan.PayPlanDate]
EnumValue.name: CI_PaymentPlan

EnumValue._:
This mobile notification occurs when a payment plan is removed from the associated eClip device or when a payment plan is removed from OD proper.
            This will remove a payment plan from user view on eClip.
            ListPrimaryKeys => [PayPlan.PatNum,PayPlan.PayPlanNum]
EnumValue.name: CI_RemovePaymentPlan

EnumValue._: Occurs when a new text message is received. This is a workaround due to android push notifications no longer being supported for xamarin.
EnumValue.name: ODM_NewTextMessage

EnumValue._: Tells the device that is currently filling out forms to add an eForm to the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the PatNum and the EFormNum in that order.
EnumValue.name: CI_AddEForm

EnumValue._: Tells the device that is currently filling out forms to remove an eForm from the list. For this type, the list of primary keys will have two items: the PatNum and the EFormNum in that order.
EnumValue.name: CI_RemoveEForm

column.name: DeviceId
column.summary: The device id for the mobile notification. Example is random string of 10-12 characters. Only the device with this DeviceId will retrieve this record.

column.name: PrimaryKeys
column.summary:
A comma-delimited list of primary keys associated with the mobile notification. See MobileNotificationType for what is included with each type. 
            Can include MobileDataByteNums, TreatPlanNum, SheetNums, and others.

column.name: Tags
column.summary: A comma-delimited list of tags for this mobile notification. Can be anything. Different for each MobileNotificationType. See MobileNotificationType for what is included with each type.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: DateTime notification was entered into Db. Should not be edited.

column.name: DateTimeExpires
column.summary: DateTime notification expires and becomes invalid.

column.name: AppTarget
column.summary:
Enum:EnumAppTarget Stores the mobile app that this notification is targeting. Prohibits a device running one app from consuming mobile 
            notifications intended for a different app.

Enumeration.name: EnumAppTarget
Enumeration.summary: The mobile apps that support mobile notifications. Must stay synched 1:1 with the ODXamBusiness.ApplicationTarget enum.

table.name: mount
table.summary: A mount shows in the images module just like other images in the tree.  But it is just a container for images within it rather than an actual image itself.  A mount layout cannot be edited once created for a patient (simply because we didn't add that functionality), but the individual images on it can be edited.

column.name: MountNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: DocCategory
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. Categories for documents.

column.name: DateCreated
column.summary: The date/time at which the mount itself was created. Usually, all the images on the mount are the same date, but not always.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Used to provide a document description in the image module tree-view.

column.name: Note
column.summary: To allow the user to enter specific information regarding the exam and tooth numbers, as well as points of interest in the xray images.

column.name: Width
column.summary: The width of the mount, in pixels.

column.name: Height
column.summary: The height of the mount, in pixels.

column.name: ColorBack
column.summary: Color of the mount background.  Typically white for photos and black for radiographs. Transparency not allowed.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum. Optional. Used for radiographs.

column.name: ColorFore
column.summary: Color of drawings and text.  Typically black for photos and white for radiographs.

column.name: ColorTextBack
column.summary: Color of drawing text background.  Typically white for photos and black for radiographs. Transparent is allowed.

column.name: FlipOnAcquire
column.summary: If true, each image will be flipped as it's acquired. Because ScanX images are backwards.

column.name: AdjModeAfterSeries
column.summary: If true, then it will switch to Adj mode instead of the usual Pan mode.

table.name: mountdef
table.summary: Template for each new mount.  But there is no linking of the mount back to this mountDef.  These can be freely deleted, renamed, moved, etc. without affecting any patient info.

column.name: MountDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The order that the mount defs will show in various lists.

column.name: Width
column.summary: The width of the mount, in pixels.

column.name: Height
column.summary: Height of the mount, in pixels.

column.name: ColorBack
column.summary: Color of the mount background.  Typically white for photos and black for radiographs.

column.name: ColorFore
column.summary: Color of drawings and text.  Typically black for photos and white for radiographs.

column.name: ColorTextBack
column.summary: Color of drawing text background.  Typically white for photos and black for radiographs. Transparent is allowed.

column.name: ScaleValue
column.summary: Scale, decimal places, and units, separated by spaces.  Example: "123.4 0 mm". The first two are required; units is optional.  When a mount is created, and if this isn't blank, then this is converted into an ImageDraw of type ScaleValue.

column.name: DefaultCat
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. If set, a new mount will go into this category, regardless of which category is currently selected.

column.name: FlipOnAcquire
column.summary: If true, each image will be flipped as it's acquired. Because ScanX images are backwards.

column.name: AdjModeAfterSeries
column.summary: If true, then it will switch to Adj mode instead of the usual Pan mode.

table.name: mountitem
table.summary: These are always attached to a mount. Like a mount, they cannot be edited.  Documents are attached to each MountItem using Document.MountItemNum field.  Image will always be cropped to make it look smaller or bigger if it doesn't exactly match the mount item rectangle ratio.

column.name: MountItemNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MountNum
column.summary: FK to mount.MountNum.

column.name: Xpos
column.summary: The x position, in pixels, of the item on the mount.

column.name: Ypos
column.summary: The y position, in pixels, of the item on the mount.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The ordinal position of the item on the mount. 1-indexed because users see it.  Any item with an ItemOrder of 0 is text, which cannot accept an image or be clicked on. Any item with an ItemOrder of -1 is unmounted and will show in the umounted area instead of on the mount.

column.name: Width
column.summary: The width, in pixels, of the mount item rectangle.

column.name: Height
column.summary: The height, in pixels, of the mount item rectangle.

column.name: ToothNumbers
column.summary: An optional list of tooth numbers. In Db, rigorously formatted as American numbers, and separated by commas.  For display, uses hyphens for sequences.  Very likely supports international tooth numbers, but not tested for that.  These tooth numbers are initially copied here from the MountItemDef. They are then copied to the document (image) that gets put in this mount item.  So mountitem.ToothNumbers is not actually used to indicate the final tooth numbers.  use document.ToothNumbers instead.

column.name: TextShowing
column.summary: Instead of an image, a mount item can show text. In this case, ItemOrder=0. Text color and background will be the mount default.

column.name: FontSize
column.summary: This could vary significantly based on the size of the mount.  It's always relative to mount pixels.

table.name: mountitemdef
table.summary: These are always attached to mountdefs.  Can be deleted without any problems.

column.name: MountItemDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: MountDefNum
column.summary: FK to mountdef.MountDefNum.

column.name: Xpos
column.summary: The x position, in pixels, of the item on the mount.

column.name: Ypos
column.summary: The y position, in pixels, of the item on the mount.

column.name: Width
column.summary: Width, in pixels, of the item rectangle on the mount.  Any cropping, rotating, etc, will all be defined in the original image itself.

column.name: Height
column.summary: Height, in pixels, of the item rectangle on the mount.  Any cropping, rotating, etc, will all be defined in the original image itself.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The ordinal position of the item on the mount. 1-indexed because users see it. 0 if TestShowing has a value.

column.name: ToothNumbers
column.summary: An optional list of tooth numbers. In Db, rigorously formatted as American numbers, and separated by commas.  For display, uses hyphens for sequences.  Very likely supports international tooth numbers, but not tested for that.

column.name: TextShowing
column.summary: Instead of an image, a mount item can show text. In this case, ItemOrder=0. Text color and background will be the mount default.

column.name: FontSize
column.summary: This could vary significantly based on the size of the mount.  It's always relative to mount pixels.

table.name: msgtopaysent
table.summary:
AutoComm object for MsgToPay messages that have been queued or sent by the eConnector. The HQ version of this object is MsgToPayActive where a record is kept for ShortGuid/redirect purposes.
            Inherits IAutoCommApptGuid since they will all be attached to appointments.

column.name: MsgToPaySentNum
column.summary: PK.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum for the corresponding patient.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to patient.ClinicNum for the corresponding patient.

column.name: SendStatus
column.summary: Indicates status of message.

column.name: Source
column.summary: Source of this object. Can be Manual (implemented) or EConnectorAutoComm (not yet implemented).

column.name: MessageFk
column.summary: FK to primary key of appropriate table.

column.name: Subject
column.summary: Subject of the message.

column.name: Message
column.summary: Content of the message.

column.name: EmailType
column.summary: Only used for manually sent emails.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Generated by OD. Timestamp when row is created.

column.name: DateTimeSent
column.summary: DateTime the message was sent.

column.name: ResponseDescript
column.summary: Generated by OD in some cases and HQ in others. Any human readable error message generated by either HQ or EConnector. Used for debugging.

column.name: ApptReminderRuleNum
column.summary: FK to apptreminderrule.ApptReminderRuleNum. Allows us to look up the rules to determine how to send this apptcomm out.

column.name: ShortGUID
column.summary: Generated by HQ. Identifies this AutoCommGuid in future transactions between HQ and OD.

column.name: ApptNum
column.summary: FK to Appointment.AptNum

column.name: StatementNum
column.summary: FK to Statement.StatementNum

column.name: OIDExternalNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: IDType
column.summary: Enum:IdentifierType Internal data type to be associated with.

Enumeration.name: IdentifierType
Enumeration.summary: Stored as string. Sorted and displayed in the order they are present in this enum.  Root should always be first.

EnumValue._: Will most likely be the root of all other OIDs.  Represents the organization.
EnumValue.name: Root

EnumValue._: FK to ehrlab.EhrLabNum.  root+".1"
EnumValue.name: LabOrder

EnumValue._: FK to patient.PatNum.  root+".2"
EnumValue.name: Patient

EnumValue._: FK to provider.ProvNum.  root+".3"
EnumValue.name: Provider

EnumValue._: This will be the root for all CQM reported items, like encounters, procedures, problems, etc.  root+".4"  The extension will be abbreviated name concatenated with the primary key of the object.  Examples: pat5231 or medpat197432 or proc231782 or notperf38291.  This is only used for generating QRDA documents and requires that the encounter, procedure, etc. is uniquely identified in the reports.  The root+".4" makes it unique to this office, the abbreviated name plus primary key makes it unique within the office.
EnumValue.name: CqmItem

EnumValue._: FK to disease.DiseaseNum.  root+".5"
EnumValue.name: Problem

EnumValue._: FK to appointment.AptNum.  root+".6"
EnumValue.name: Appointment

EnumValue._: FK to insplan.PlanNum.  root+".7"
EnumValue.name: InsPlan

EnumValue._: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum.  root+".8"
EnumValue.name: Procedure

column.name: IDInternal
column.summary: This should be a Primary Key to a Table Type defined by the IDType field. Example: If IDType==Patient, then this field should be a PatNum that is a FK to Patient.Patnum

column.name: IDExternal
column.summary: The OID extension, when combined with rootExternal it uniquely identifies an object.

column.name: rootExternal
column.summary: The OID root, when combined with IDExternal it uniquely identifies an object.

column.name: OIDInternalNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: IDType
column.summary: Enum:IdentifierType Internal data type to be associated with OIDRoot

Enumeration.name: IdentifierType
Enumeration.summary: Stored as string. Sorted and displayed in the order they are present in this enum.  Root should always be first.

EnumValue._: Will most likely be the root of all other OIDs.  Represents the organization.
EnumValue.name: Root

EnumValue._: FK to ehrlab.EhrLabNum.  root+".1"
EnumValue.name: LabOrder

EnumValue._: FK to patient.PatNum.  root+".2"
EnumValue.name: Patient

EnumValue._: FK to provider.ProvNum.  root+".3"
EnumValue.name: Provider

EnumValue._: This will be the root for all CQM reported items, like encounters, procedures, problems, etc.  root+".4"  The extension will be abbreviated name concatenated with the primary key of the object.  Examples: pat5231 or medpat197432 or proc231782 or notperf38291.  This is only used for generating QRDA documents and requires that the encounter, procedure, etc. is uniquely identified in the reports.  The root+".4" makes it unique to this office, the abbreviated name plus primary key makes it unique within the office.
EnumValue.name: CqmItem

EnumValue._: FK to disease.DiseaseNum.  root+".5"
EnumValue.name: Problem

EnumValue._: FK to appointment.AptNum.  root+".6"
EnumValue.name: Appointment

EnumValue._: FK to insplan.PlanNum.  root+".7"
EnumValue.name: InsPlan

EnumValue._: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum.  root+".8"
EnumValue.name: Procedure

column.name: IDRoot
column.summary: This is the root OID for this data type, when combined with extension, uniquely identifies a single object.

table.name: operatory
table.summary: Each row is a single operatory or column in the appts module.

column.name: OperatoryNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: OpName
column.summary: The full name to show in the column.

column.name: Abbrev
column.summary: 5 char or less. Not used much.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The order that this op column will show.  Changing views only hides some ops; it does not change their order.  Zero based.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Used instead of deleting to hide an op that is no longer used.

column.name: ProvDentist
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  The dentist assigned to this op.  If more than one dentist might be assigned to an op, then create a second op and use one for each dentist. If 0, then no dentist is assigned.

column.name: ProvHygienist
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  The hygienist assigned to this op.  If 0, then no hygienist is assigned.

column.name: IsHygiene
column.summary: Set true if this is a hygiene operatory.  The hygienist will then be considered the main provider for this op.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  0 if no clinic.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: Not user editable. The last time this row was edited.

column.name: SetProspective
column.summary: If true patients put into this operatory will have status set to prospective.

column.name: IsWebSched
column.summary: Operatories with IsWebSched set to true will be the ONLY operatories considered when searching for available time slots.

column.name: IsNewPatAppt
column.summary:
Deprecated as of 18.1.  Entries within the deflink table indicate if this operatory is in fact available for WebSched New Pat Appt.
            Old summary: Operatories with IsNewPatAppt set to true will be the ONLY operatories considered when searching for available time slots.
            This is in regards to the New Patient Appointment portion of the Web Sched web application.

column.name: OperatoryType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. The type of the Operatory. This value is not normally used, but rather to just mark which type the Operatory is.

table.name: orthocase
table.summary: Holds financial and timing information for a single ortho case. For procs linked to Orthocases, estimates are calculated based off of orthocase info, not insurance info. The orthocase numbers are automatically placed into InsPayEst and InsEstTotalOverride in Procedures.ComputeEstimates(), taking control from insurance. Procedure fees are calculated based on orthocase info, not fee schedules. All overrides (fees and insurance) are performed when the procedure is set complete.

column.name: OrthoCaseNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  The patient on this ortho case.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.name: Fee
column.summary: Total amount of procedure fees. Is editable by user.

column.name: FeeInsPrimary
column.summary: The amount that primary insurance will cover for the entire ortho case.

column.name: FeePat
column.summary: Calculated from Fee - FeeIns.

column.name: BandingDate
column.summary: Date of Banding.

column.name: DebondDate
column.summary: Date of Debond.

column.name: DebondDateExpected
column.summary: Date of expected Debond.

column.name: IsTransfer
column.summary: Used to denote that the banding date is used as the transfer date instead.

column.name: OrthoType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: DateTime ortho case was added. Not editable by user.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.usernum. The usernum that added the OrthoCase.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Timestamp of the last modification to the ortho case. Not editable by user.

column.name: IsActive
column.summary: Determines whether or not this is an active ortho case

column.name: FeeInsSecondary
column.summary:
The amount that secondary insurance will cover for the entire ortho case.
            Will be set to zero if patient doesn't have secondary insurance

table.name: orthochart
table.summary: For the orthochart feature, each row in this table is one cell in that grid.  An empty cell often corresponds to a missing db table row.

column.name: OrthoChartNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateService
column.summary: Deprecated, use orthochartrow table instead.  Date of service.

column.name: FieldName
column.summary: Keyed to displayfield.Description.

column.name: FieldValue
column.summary: Stores the text that the user entered or picked.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: Deprecated, use orthochartrow table instead. FK to userod.UserNum.  The user that created or last edited an ortho chart field.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: Deprecated, use orthochartrow table instead. FK to provider.ProvNum.  Can be 0.

column.name: OrthoChartRowNum
column.summary: FK to orthochartrow.OrthoChartRowNum.

table.name: orthochartlog
table.summary: This stores log entries for debugging the orthochart.  Logging gets turned on and off with the pref. This table will go away once the bug is found.

column.name: OrthoChartLogNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateTimeLog
column.summary: DateTime that this log entry was made

column.name: DateTimeService
column.summary: DateTime of the chart row.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  The user that created or last edited an ortho chart field.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: OrthoChartRowNum
column.summary: FK to orthochartrow.OrthoChartRowNum.

column.name: LogData
column.summary: This can be long and complex -- whatever you want. MediumText, so max length=16M.

table.name: orthochartrow
table.summary: Represent a row in the ortho chart UI grid.

column.name: OrthoChartRowNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateTimeService
column.summary: DateTime of service.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  The user that created or last edited an ortho chart field.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: Signature
column.summary: Examples: "0:ritwq/wV8vlrgUYahhK+RH5UeBFA6W4jCkZdo0cDWd63aZb1S/W3Z4eW5LmchqfgniG23" and "1:52222559445999975122111500485555". The 1st character is whether or not the signature is Topaz. The 2nd character is a separator. The rest of the string is the hashed signature data. Raw signature data is the concatenation of the FieldName and FieldValue of all cells (orthocharts), ordered by FieldName.

table.name: orthocharttab
table.summary: Links one orthocharttab to one displayfield.  Allows for displayfields to be part of multiple orthocharttabs.

column.name: OrthoChartTabNum
column.summary: FK to orthocharttab.OrthoChartTabNum.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Overrides the displayfield ItemOrder, so that each display field can have a different order in each ortho chart tab.

table.name: orthocharttablink
table.summary: Links one orthocharttab to one displayfield.  Allows for displayfields to be part of multiple orthocharttabs.

column.name: OrthoChartTabLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Overrides the displayfield ItemOrder, so that each display field can have a different order in each ortho chart tab.

column.name: OrthoChartTabNum
column.summary: FK to orthocharttab.OrthoChartTabNum.

column.name: DisplayFieldNum
column.summary: FK to displayfield.DisplayFieldNum.

column.name: ColumnWidthOverride
column.summary: Overrides the DisplayField.ColumnWidth for OrthChartTabLinks when not 0. Otherwise uses associated DisplayFieldFieldNums DisplayField.ColumnWidth value.

table.name: orthohardware
table.summary: Represents one bracket, wire, or elastic.

column.name: OrthoHardwareNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateExam
column.summary: Every hardware entry is tied to a single date. At each exam, a copy can be made of the hardware from the previous exam, and then it can be edited. It normally shows the most recent exam, and the hardware items showing in the ortho grid only include the most recent exam. Not sure yet how we will show hardware for previous exams/dates.

column.name: OrthoHardwareType
column.summary: Enum:EnumOrthoHardwareType Bracket, Wire, or Elastic.

column.name: OrthoHardwareSpecNum
column.summary: FK to orthohardwarespec.OrthoHardwareSpecNum. This is where the description and color come from.

column.name: ToothRange
column.summary: Tooth numbers stored here are always stored in Universal (1-32) notation. They are displayed to the user as Palmer notation. For brackets, always use single tooth numbers, like 8. For wires, must use a range like 2-15. For elastics, typically use 2 teeth separated with commas, but more are allowed.

table.name: orthohardwarespec
table.summary: Specification for ortho hardware. Linked to one type such as bracket, wire, or elastic. This is a pick list of description and color for the user. These remain linked to patient data, so changes here will affect historical chart entries.

column.name: OrthoHardwareSpecNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: OrthoHardwareType
column.summary: Enum:EnumOrthoHardwareType Bracket, Wire, or Elastic.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Example NITI 16x25

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: 0 indexed. User controls it with arrows.

table.name: orthoplanlink
table.summary: Used to attach payment plans and ortho schedules to an Ortho Case.

column.name: OrthoPlanLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: OrthoCaseNum
column.summary: FK to orthocase.OrthoCaseNum.

column.name: LinkType
column.summary: Enum:OrthoPlanLinkType  Holds the type of object that is being linked.

EnumValue._: 0 - OrthoSchedule
EnumValue.name: OrthoSchedule

EnumValue._: 1 - Insurance Payment Plan
EnumValue.name: InsPayPlan

EnumValue._: 2 - Patient Payment Plan
EnumValue.name: PatPayPlan

column.name: FKey
column.summary: Holds the FKey of the object from the LinkType.

column.name: IsActive
column.summary: Denotes if plan link is active or not.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: DateTime. Date plan link was added. Not editable by user.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UseNum. User that added the plan link.

table.name: orthoproclink
table.summary: Used to attach procedures to an OrthoCase. Multiple procs are typically attached to one OrthoCase.

column.name: OrthoProcLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: OrthoCaseNum
column.summary: FK to orthocase.OrthoCaseNum.

column.name: ProcNum
column.summary: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: DateTime proclink was added. Not editable by user.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. User that added the proc link.

column.name: ProcLinkType
column.summary: Enum:OrthoProcType Indicates what type of procedure is being associated to Ortho Case in link.

Enumeration.name: OrthoProcType
Enumeration.summary: A procedures type as it relates to an Ortho Case

EnumValue._: 0 - Procedure for putting appliance on.
EnumValue.name: Banding

EnumValue._: 1 - Procedure for removing appliance.
EnumValue.name: Debond

EnumValue._: 2 - All maintenance visits between Banding and Debond procedures.
EnumValue.name: Visit

table.name: orthorx
table.summary: A group of ortho hardware that allows for faster entry than one tooth at a time. Changes to this table do not affect any patient records.

column.name: OrthoRxNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: OrthoHardwareSpecNum
column.summary: FK to orthohardwarespec.OrthoHardwareSpecNum. Description comes from here.

column.name: Description
column.summary: The description used for picking the prescription from a list.

column.name: ToothRange
column.summary: Tooth numbers stored here are always stored in Universal (1-32) notation. They are displayed to the user as Palmer notation. For brackets and elastics, always use tooth numbers separated by commas, like 2,3,4,5,6. For wires, must use a range like 2-15.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: 0 indexed. User controls it with arrows.

table.name: orthoschedule
table.summary: Optional. Holds the Production Schedule for an OrthoCase.

column.name: OrthoScheduleNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: BandingDateOverride
column.summary: Override for banding date.

column.name: DebondDateOverride
column.summary: Override for debond date.

column.name: BandingAmount
column.summary: Amount to charge for banding procedure.

column.name: VisitAmount
column.summary: Used every visit until the total off all visits+BandingAmount+DebondAmount=Fee of linked OrthoCase.

column.name: DebondAmount
column.summary: Amount to charge for debond procedure.

column.name: IsActive
column.summary: Is true if the ortho schedule is active.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: DateTime the ortho schedule was last modified. Not editable by user.

table.name: patfield
table.summary: These are custom fields added and managed by the user. Each row here is a field value for one patient.

column.name: PatFieldNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: FieldName
column.summary: FK to patfielddef.FieldName.  The full name is shown here for ease of use when running queries.  But the user is only allowed to change fieldNames in the patFieldDef setup window.

column.name: FieldValue
column.summary: Any text that the user types in.  For picklists, this will contain the picked text.  For dates, this is stored as the user typed it, after validating that it could be parsed.  So queries that involve dates won't work very well.  If we want better handling of date fields, we should add a column to this table.  Checkbox will either have a value of 1, or else the row will be deleted from the db.  Currency is handled in a culture neutral way, just like other currency in the db.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

table.name: patfielddef
table.summary: These are the definitions for the custom patient fields added and managed by the user.

column.name: PatFieldDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FieldName
column.summary: This is treated as the key. The name of the field that the user will be allowed to fill in the patient info window.

column.name: FieldType
column.summary: Enum:PatFieldType Text=0,PickList=1,Date=2,Checkbox=3,Currency=4

EnumValue._: 2-Stored in db as entered, already localized.  For example, it could be 2/04/11, 2/4/11, 2/4/2011, or any other variant.  This makes it harder to create queries that filter by date, but easier to display dates as part of results.
EnumValue.name: Date

EnumValue._: 3-If checked, value stored as "1".  If unchecked, row deleted.
EnumValue.name: Checkbox

EnumValue._: 4-Numbers only.
EnumValue.name: Currency

EnumValue._: 5 - DEPRECATED. (Only used 16.3.1, deprecated by 16.3.4)
EnumValue.name: InCaseOfEmergency

EnumValue._: 6 - CareCredit pre-approval status. For example, FieldValue string="Pre-Approved", from CareCreditWebStatus enum.
EnumValue.name: CareCreditStatus

EnumValue._: 7 - CareCredit pre-approval amount.
EnumValue.name: CareCreditPreApprovalAmt

EnumValue._: 8 - CareCredit - Remaining available Credit for CareCredit cardholders.
EnumValue.name: CareCreditAvailableCredit

column.name: PickList
column.summary: Deprecated. Use patfieldpickitem.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Hides this PatField for any patient where it's currently blank. If already in use by a patient, then it still shows.

table.name: patfieldpickitem
table.summary: Each row is an item in a PatFieldDef picklist. Not used unless the PatFieldDef is a Picklist type. These objects are created and managed by user.

column.name: PatFieldPickItemNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatFieldDefNum
column.summary: FK to patfielddef.PatFieldDefNum

column.name: Name
column.summary: Full text of PickList item.

column.name: Abbreviation
column.summary: Abbr to show when PickList item is displayed in cramped spaces like columns. Only implemented in Superfamily grid so far.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: False for normal PickList items. Even if true/hidden, this item will still show in all the various windows where patient fields show. A hidden item will not normally show when picking from list for a patient unless the patient has already been assigned this item.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: 0-based.

table.name: patient
table.summary: One row for each patient.  Includes deleted patients.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: LName
column.summary: Last name.

column.name: FName
column.summary: First name.

column.name: MiddleI
column.summary: Middle initial or name.

column.name: Preferred
column.summary: Preferred name, aka nickname.

column.name: PatStatus
column.summary: Enum:PatientStatus

EnumValue._: 3 - This status is also used for a merged patient that you're not keeping.
EnumValue.name: Archived

EnumValue._: 6- Not an actual patient yet.
EnumValue.name: Prospective

column.name: Gender
column.summary: Enum:PatientGender

Enumeration.name: PatientGender
Enumeration.summary: Known as administrativeGender (HL7 OID of 2.16.840.1.113883.5.1) Male=M, Female=F, Unknown=Undifferentiated=UN.

EnumValue._: 2- Required by HIPAA for privacy.  Required by ehr to track missing entries. EHR/HL7 known as undifferentiated (UN).
EnumValue.name: Unknown

column.name: Position
column.summary: Enum:PatientPosition Marital status would probably be a better name for this column.

Enumeration.name: PatientPosition
Enumeration.summary: Single, Married, Child, Widowed, Divorced.

column.name: Birthdate
column.summary: Age is not stored in the database.  Age is always calculated as needed from birthdate.

column.name: SSN
column.summary: In the US, this is 9 digits, no dashes. For all other countries, any punctuation or format is allowed.

column.name: Address2
column.summary: Optional second address line.

column.name: State
column.summary: 2 Char in USA.  Used to store province for Canadian users.

column.name: Zip
