column.summary: Postal code.  For Canadian claims, it must be ANANAN.  No validation gets done except there.

column.name: HmPhone
column.summary: Home phone. Includes any punctuation

column.name: Guarantor
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Head of household.

column.name: CreditType
column.summary:
Single char. Shows at upper right corner of appointments.  Suggested use is A,B,or C to designate creditworthiness, 
            but it can actually be used for any purpose.

column.name: Salutation
column.summary:
Dear __.  This field does not include the "Dear" or a trailing comma.  If this field is blank, then the typical salutation is 
            FName.  Or, if a Preferred name is present, that is used instead of FName.

column.name: EstBalance
column.summary: Current patient balance.(not family). Never subtracts insurance estimates.

column.name: PriProv
column.summary:
FK to provider.ProvNum.  The patient's primary provider.  Required.  The database maintenance tool ensures that every patient 
            always has this number set, so the program no longer has to handle 0.

column.name: SecProv
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.  Secondary provider (hygienist). Optional.

column.name: FeeSched
column.summary:
FK to feesched.FeeSchedNum.  Fee schedule for this patient.  Usually not used.  If missing, the practice default fee schedule is 
            used. If patient has insurance, then the fee schedule for the insplan is used.

column.name: BillingType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  Must have a value, or the patient will not show on some reports.

column.name: ImageFolder
column.summary: Name of folder where images will be stored. Not editable for now.

column.name: AddrNote
column.summary: Address or phone note.  Unlimited length in order to handle data from other programs during a conversion.

column.name: FamFinUrgNote
column.summary: Family financial urgent note.  Only stored with guarantor, and shared for family.

column.name: MedUrgNote
column.summary: Individual patient note for Urgent medical.

column.name: ApptModNote
column.summary: Individual patient note for Appointment module note.

column.name: StudentStatus
column.summary: Single char.  Nonstudent='N' or blank, Parttime='P', Fulltime='F'.

column.name: SchoolName
column.summary:
College name.  If Canadian, then this is field C10 and must be filled if C9 (patient.CanadianEligibilityCode) is 1 and patient 
            is 18 or older.

column.name: ChartNumber
column.summary: Usually blank. Alternative and supplement to PatNum. Can take alphanumeric. Usually set during conversion or when bridging to imaging software. Historically, it typically showed as a sticker on the outside of a paper chart.  Max length 100 to support larger ids for bridging to imaging softwares.

column.name: MedicaidID
column.summary: Optional. The Medicaid ID for this patient.

column.name: Bal_0_30
column.summary: Aged balance from 0 to 30 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: Bal_31_60
column.summary: Aged balance from 31 to 60 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: Bal_61_90
column.summary: Aged balance from 61 to 90 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: BalOver90
column.summary: Aged balance over 90 days old. Aging numbers are for entire family.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: InsEst
column.summary: Insurance Estimate for entire family. Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: BalTotal
column.summary:
Total balance for entire family before insurance estimate.  Not the same as the sum of the 4 aging balances because this can be 
            negative.  Only stored with guarantor.

column.name: EmployerNum
column.summary: FK to employer.EmployerNum.

column.name: EmploymentNote
column.summary: Not used since version 2.8.

column.name: County
column.summary: FK to county.CountyName, although it will not crash if key absent.

column.name: GradeLevel
column.summary: Enum:PatientGrade Gradelevel.

Enumeration.name: PatientGrade
Enumeration.summary: Grade level used in public health.

column.name: Urgency
column.summary: Enum:TreatmentUrgency Used in public health screenings.

Enumeration.name: TreatmentUrgency
Enumeration.summary: For public health.  Unknown, NoProblems, NeedsCarE, or Urgent.

column.name: DateFirstVisit
column.summary: The date that the patient first visited the office.  Automated.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum. Can be zero if not attached to a clinic or no clinics set up.

column.name: HasIns
column.summary:
For now, an 'I' indicates that the patient has insurance.  This is only used when displaying appointments.  
            It will later be expanded.  User can't edit.

column.name: TrophyFolder
column.summary: The Trophy bridge is inadequate, this attempts to make it usable for offices that have invested in Trophy hardware.

column.name: PlannedIsDone
column.summary:
This simply indicates whether the 'done' box is checked in the chart module.  Used to be handled as a -1 in the NextAptNum field,
            but now that field is unsigned.

column.name: Premed
column.summary: Set to true if patient needs to be premedicated for appointments, includes PAC, halcion, etc.

column.name: Ward
column.summary: Only used in hospitals.

column.name: PreferConfirmMethod
column.summary: Enum:ContactMethod Used for eCR, which includes eReminders eConfirmations.

column.name: PreferContactMethod
column.summary: Enum:ContactMethod

column.name: PreferRecallMethod
column.summary: Enum:ContactMethod

column.name: SchedDayOfWeek
column.summary: We do not use this, but some users do, so here it is. 0=none. Otherwise, 1-7 for day.

column.name: Language
column.summary: The primary language of the patient.  Typically eng (English), fra (French), spa (Spanish), or similar.  If it's a custom language, then it might look like Tahitian. If none, then empty string.

column.name: AdmitDate
column.summary: Used in hospitals.  It can be before the first visit date.  It typically gets set automatically by the hospital system.

column.name: Title
column.summary:
Includes any punctuation.  For example, Mr., Mrs., Miss, Dr., etc.  
            There is no selection mechanism yet for user; they must simply type it in.

column.name: PayPlanDue
column.summary:
Amount "due now" for all payment plans such that someone in this family is the payment plan guarantor.  
            This is the total of all payment plan charges past due (taking into account the PayPlansBillInAdvanceDays setting) subtract the amount 
            already paid for the payment plans.  Only stored with family guarantor.

column.name: SiteNum
column.summary: FK to site.SiteNum. Can be zero. Replaces the old GradeSchool field with a proper foreign key.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or 
            program updates.  Not user editable.

column.name: ResponsParty
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum. Can be zero.  Person responsible for medical decisions rather than finances.  Guarantor is still responsible
            for finances.  This is useful for nursing home residents.  Part of public health.

column.name: CanadianEligibilityCode
column.summary:
C09.  Eligibility Exception Code.  A number between 1-4.  0 is not acceptable for e-claims.
            1=FT student, 2=disabled, 3=disabled student, 4=code not applicable.  Warning.  4 is a 0 if using CDAnet version 02. 
            This column should have been created as an int.

column.name: AskToArriveEarly
column.summary: Number of minutes patient is asked to come early to appointments.

column.name: PreferContactConfidential
column.summary: Enum:ContactMethod  Used for EHR.

column.name: SuperFamily
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum.  If this is the same as PatNum, then this is a SuperHead.  If zero, then not part of a superfamily.  
            Synched for entire family.  If family is part of a superfamily, then the guarantor for this family will show in the superfamily list in the
            Family module for anyone else who is in the superfamily.  Only a guarantor can be a superfamily head.

column.name: TxtMsgOk
column.summary: Enum:YN

Enumeration.name: YN
Enumeration.summary: 0=Unknown,1=Yes, or 2=No. UI can be tricky. Some success with a 3x1 listbox, multicolumn, see FormPatientEdit. Radiobuttons are also an option.  You can also use another YN variable to store unknown, and then use a click event on the checkbox to change to Y or N.  You can also use a three state checkbox if you translate properly between the enums.

column.name: SmokingSnoMed
column.summary: EHR smoking status as a SNOMED code.  Will always be the most recent smoking status for the patient.

column.name: Country
column.summary: Country name.  Only used by HQ to add country names to statements.

column.name: DateTimeDeceased
column.summary:
Needed for EHR syndromic surveillance messaging.  Used in HL7 PID-29.  Also for feature request #3040.  Date and time because we 
            need precision to the minute in syndromic surveillence messging.

column.name: BillingCycleDay
column.summary:
A number between 1 and 31 that is the day of month that repeat charges should be applied to this account. 
            Previously this was determined by the start date of the repeate charges.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Date automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  Date when patient was inserted.

column.name: HasSuperBilling
column.summary: 0 by default.  If true, this guarantor should be included in superbilling statements.

column.name: PatNumCloneFrom
column.summary:
Deprecated, use patientlink table instead.
            Indicates if this patient should act as a clone of another patient. Previously, ortho cloned patients were signified by capitalizing the name fields of the newly cloned patient.
            This field will allow for an explicit flag to be set to indicate cloned status.

column.name: DiscountPlanNum
column.summary:
Deprecated, use discountplansub table instead.
            FK to discountplan.DiscountPlanNum. Will be 0 if there is no DiscountPlan.

column.name: HasSignedTil
column.summary: Signed Truth in Lending, relates to client permission to be charged interest on a payment plan.

column.name: ShortCodeOptIn
column.summary: Indicates if the patient has opted in, out, or not yet to using Short Codes for Appointment Texts.

column.name: SecurityHash
column.summary: Holds the salted hash of the PatNum. This prevents 3rd parties from inserting patients without our Db Integrity system noticing.

table.name: patientlink
table.summary: Keeps track of patients who have been merged or cloned.

column.name: PatientLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNumFrom
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum. The patient that is linked from.
            For a Merge type, this is that patient that was merged from.
            For a Clone type, this is the original or master patient.

column.name: PatNumTo
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum, unless LinkType=PaySimple. The patient that is linked to.
            For a Merge type, this is that patient that was merged into.
            For a Clone type, this represents the clone that was made from the PatNumFrom patient.

column.name: LinkType
column.summary: Enum:PatientLinkType The type of link.

Enumeration.name: PatientLinkType
Enumeration.summary: The manner in which two patients are linked together.

EnumValue._: 1 - The two patients have been merged into each other.
EnumValue.name: Merge

EnumValue._: 2 - A clone has been made of the From patient.  PatNumFrom is the original or master and PatNumTo is the clone.
EnumValue.name: Clone

EnumValue._: 3 - The PatNumFrom column will hold the ID for PaySimple.  This should not be used in OpenDental to get a patient.
EnumValue.name: PaySimple

column.name: DateTimeLink
column.summary: The time the link was created.

table.name: patientnote
table.summary: Essentially more columns in the patient table.  They are stored here because these fields can contain a lot of information, and we want to try to keep the size of the patient table a bit smaller.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Also the primary key for this table. Always one to one relationship with patient table.  A new patient might not have an entry here until needed.

column.name: FamFinancial
column.summary: Only one note per family stored with guarantor.

column.name: ApptPhone
column.summary: No longer used.

column.name: Medical
column.summary: Medical Summary

column.name: Service
column.summary: Service notes. Shows in Medical information window and in the Pt Info section of the Chart module.

column.name: MedicalComp
column.summary: Complete current Medical History

column.name: Treatment
column.summary: Shows in the Chart module normally just below the graphical tooth chart. Also known as Odontogram Notes.

column.name: ICEName
column.summary: In Case of Emergency Name.

column.name: ICEPhone
column.summary: In Case of Emergency Phone.

column.name: OrthoMonthsTreatOverride
column.summary:
-1 by default. Overrides the default number of months for an ortho treatment for this patient.
            Gets automatically set to the current value found in the pref OrthoDefaultMonthsTreat when the first placement procedure has been completed and this value is -1.
            This column is an integer instead of a byte because it needs to store -1 so that users can override with the value of 0.
            When set to -1 the default practice value for the pref OrthoDefaultMonthsTreat is used.

column.name: DateOrthoPlacementOverride
column.summary:
Overrides the date of the first ortho procedure for this patient to use for ortho case patients. 
            If MinDate, then the date is derived by looking at the first ortho procedure for this patient.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed.

column.name: Consent
column.summary:
Enum:PatConsentFlags None=0,ShareMedicationHistoryErx=1, Indicates if the patient consents for DoseSpot to access their medication
            history, bitwise.

EnumValue._: 0 - None
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Patient consents for eRx to access their medication history
EnumValue.name: ShareMedicationHistoryErx

column.name: UserNumOrthoLocked
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. A real-time flag of which user is currently editing the Orth Chart.  Prevents concurrency issues. 0 indicates unlocked. -5 indicates that an instance of OD changed users locally in order to sign an Ortho Chart row. -1 indicates another user with the same username took control.

column.name: Pronoun
column.summary: Enum: PronounPreferred Patient Pronoun override. None indicates no override.

column.name: PatientPortalInviteNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum for the corresponding patient.

column.name: ApptNum
column.summary: Foreign key to the appointment represented by this AutoCommAppt.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum for the corresponding appointment.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Generated by OD. Timestamp when row is created.

column.name: TSPrior
column.summary: This was the TSPrior used to send this reminder.

column.name: SendStatus
column.summary: Indicates status of message.

column.name: MessageFk
column.summary: FK to primary key of appropriate table.

column.name: ResponseDescript
column.summary: Generated by OD in some cases and HQ in others. Any human readable error message generated by either HQ or EConnector. Used for debugging.

column.name: DateTimeSent
column.summary: DateTime the message was sent.

column.name: ApptReminderRuleNum
column.summary: FK to apptreminderrule.ApptReminderRuleNum. Allows us to look up the rules to determine how to send this apptcomm out.

column.name: ApptDateTime
column.summary: The Date and time of the original appointment. We need this in case the appointment was moved and needs another reminder sent out.

table.name: patientrace
table.summary: Each patient may have multiple races.  Used to represent a race or an ethnicity for a patient.

column.name: PatientRaceNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: Race
column.summary: Enum:PatRace Deprecated. CdcrecCode should be used exclusively.

Enumeration.name: PatRace
Enumeration.summary: Deprecated. Use CDCREC codes instead.

EnumValue._: -1 - The value for all PatientRace entries after the Race column was deprecated.
EnumValue.name: NotSet

EnumValue._: 0 - Hidden for EHR.
EnumValue.name: Aboriginal

EnumValue._: 1 - CDCREC:2054-5 Race
EnumValue.name: AfricanAmerican

EnumValue._: 2 - CDCREC:1002-5 Race
EnumValue.name: AmericanIndian

EnumValue._: 3 - CDCREC:2028-9 Race
EnumValue.name: Asian

EnumValue._: 4 - Our hard-coded option for EHR reporting.
EnumValue.name: DeclinedToSpecifyRace

EnumValue._: 5 - CDCREC:2076-8 Race
EnumValue.name: HawaiiOrPacIsland

EnumValue._: 6 - CDCREC:2135-2 Ethnicicty.  If EHR is turned on, our UI will force this to be supplemental to a base 'race'.
EnumValue.name: Hispanic

EnumValue._: 7 - We had to keep this for backward compatibility.  Hidden for EHR because it's explicitly not allowed.
EnumValue.name: Multiracial

EnumValue._: 8 - CDCREC:2131-1 Race.
EnumValue.name: Other

EnumValue._: 9 - CDCREC:2106-3 Race
EnumValue.name: White

EnumValue._: 10 - CDCREC:2186-5 Ethnicity.  We originally used the lack of Hispanic to indicate NonHispanic.  Now we are going to explicitly store NonHispanic to make queries for ClinicalQualityMeasures easier.
EnumValue.name: NotHispanic

EnumValue._: 11 - Our hard-coded option for EHR reporting.
EnumValue.name: DeclinedToSpecifyEthnicity

column.name: CdcrecCode
column.summary: FK to cdcrec.CdcrecCode. Example 2054-5. The value 'Declined to Specify' is stored as ASKU-ETHNICITY for ethnicity and ASKU-RACE as race.

table.name: patplan
table.summary: Each row represents the linking of one insplan to one patient for current coverage.  Dropping a plan will delete the entry in this table.  Deleting a plan will delete the actual insplan (if no dependencies).

column.name: PatPlanNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to  patient.PatNum.  The patient who currently has the insurance.  Not the same as the subscriber.

column.name: Ordinal
column.summary: Number like 1, 2, 3, etc.  Represents primary ins, secondary ins, tertiary ins, etc. 0 is not used

column.name: IsPending
column.summary: For informational purposes only. For now, we lose the previous feature which let us set isPending without entering a plan.  Now, you have to enter the plan in order to check this box.

column.name: Relationship
column.summary: Enum:Relat Remember that this may need to be changed in the Claim also, if already created.

Enumeration.name: Relat
Enumeration.summary: Relationship to subscriber for insurance.

column.name: PatID
column.summary: An optional patient ID which will override the insplan.SubscriberID on eclaims.  For Canada, this holds the Dependent Code, C17 and E17, and in that use it doesn't override subscriber id, but instead supplements it.

column.name: InsSubNum
column.summary: FK to inssub.InsSubNum.  Gives info about the subscriber.

column.name: OrthoAutoFeeBilledOverride
column.summary:
Only for Ortho practices. The fee that will be charged out by the auto procedureto insurance each period. 
            Overrides insplan.OrthoAutoFeeBilled. -1 to use insplan default. Instantiated to -1 in the program so that it defaults to the insplan default.

column.name: OrthoAutoNextClaimDate
column.summary:
Only for Ortho practices. The date before which the next automatic ortho procedure/claim cannot be automatically generated.
            If blank, this patient's ortho treatment has been stopped.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

table.name: patrestriction
table.summary: Currently only used to block scheduling of specific patients.

column.name: PatRestrictionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: PatRestrictType
column.summary: Enum:PatRestrict

EnumValue._:
1 - Patient cannot be scheduled nor have schedule edited. This PatRestrict should probably be checked every place the group 
            permissions AppointmentCreate, AppointmentMove, and AppointmentEdit are checked.
EnumValue.name: ApptSchedule

table.name: payconnectresponseweb
table.summary: This table will never delete records, only upsert.  PayConnectResponseWeb rows are records of all payments made from the Patient Portal via either PayConnect's Web Portal, or PayConnect's Merchant Services WebService if using a credit card token as a result of PayConnect's Web Portal.

column.name: PayConnectResponseWebNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: PayNum
column.summary: FK to payment.PayNum.

column.name: AccountToken
column.summary: The account token used to poll the processing status.

column.name: PaymentToken
column.summary: The payment token used for future payments.

column.name: ProcessingStatus
column.summary: Enum:PayConnectWebStatus Used to determine if the payment is pending, needs action, or is completed and attached to a payment.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual datetime of entry.

column.name: DateTimePending
column.summary: DateTime that the payment went to the pending status.

column.name: DateTimeCompleted
column.summary: DateTime that the payment went to the completed status and is attached to a payment.

column.name: DateTimeExpired
column.summary: DateTime that the payment opportunity time expired.

column.name: DateTimeLastError
column.summary: DateTime of the last time that the payment had an error.

column.name: LastResponseStr
column.summary: Raw JSON response (or error) from PayConnect.

column.name: CCSource

column.name: Amount
column.summary: The amount of the payment that is attempting to be made.

column.name: PayNote
column.summary: The note entered when making a payment.

column.name: IsTokenSaved
column.summary: Whether or not the credit card token can be saved for future uses.

column.name: PayToken
column.summary: The payment token used to poll the processing status.

column.name: ExpDateToken
column.summary: Provides the Expiration Date of the account being accessed. Format is yyMM from XWeb gateway. Will be converted to ExpirationDate.

column.name: RefNumber
column.summary: The RefNumber associated to this transaction.  Will only be set for Completed PayConnectWebStatuses.

column.name: TransType
column.summary: The Transaction Type associated to this transaction.  Will only be set for Completed PayConnectWebStatuses.

column.name: EmailResponse
column.summary: Email address used for a requested receipt provided by the user when making a payment via the patient portal.

column.name: LogGuid
column.summary: The GUID used in EserviceLogs related to this response. May be blank.

table.name: payment
table.summary: A patient payment.  Always has at least one split.

column.name: PayNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PayType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  This will be 0 if this is an income transfer to another provider. Examples: Cash, Check, CC, Refund.

column.name: PayDate
column.summary: The date that the payment displays on the patient account.

column.name: PayAmt
column.summary: Amount of the payment.  Must equal the sum of the splits.

column.name: CheckNum
column.summary: Check number is optional.

column.name: BankBranch
column.summary: Bank-branch code for checks. Example 19-7076.

column.name: PayNote
column.summary: Any admin note.  Not for patient to see.  Length 4000.

column.name: IsSplit
column.summary: No longer used.  Set to true to indicate that a payment has more than one paysplit.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  The patient where the payment entry will show.  But only the splits affect account balances.  This has a value even if the 'payment' is actually an income transfer to another provider.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  Can be 0 to indicate no clinic (unassigned). Copied from patient.ClinicNum when creating payment, but user can override.  Not used in provider income transfers.  Cannot be used in financial reporting when grouping by clinic, because payments may be split between clinics.

column.name: DateEntry
column.summary: The date that this payment was entered.  Not user editable.

column.name: DepositNum
column.summary: FK to deposit.DepositNum.  0 if not attached to any deposits.  Cash does not usually get attached to a deposit; only checks.

column.name: Receipt
column.summary: Text of printed receipt if the payment was done electronically. Allows reprinting if needed.

column.name: IsRecurringCC
column.summary: True if this was an automatically added recurring CC charge rather then one entered by the user.  This was set to true for all historical entries before version 11.1, but will be accurate after that.

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: RecurringChargeDate
column.summary: The date of the recurring charge that this payment applies to.

column.name: ExternalId
column.summary: External Id

EnumValue.name: PaySimpleAchPosted

EnumValue._: 2 - PaySimpleAchSettled
EnumValue.name: PaySimpleAchSettled

EnumValue._: 3 - PaySimpleAchFailed
EnumValue.name: PaySimpleAchFailed

column.name: IsCcCompleted
column.summary: A credit card transaction has been completed. This disables the CC buttons at the top of payment edit window to prevent duplicates.

column.name: MerchantFee
column.summary: Stores any additional fees charged to the customer during a transaction. For display purposes only.

table.name: payortype
table.summary: Used to identify the source of payment for a given patient at a given point in time.  As insurance is added and removed, rows should be either automatically inserted into this table, or the user should be prompted to specify what the new payor type is.  The DateStart of one payor type is interpreted as the end date of the previous payor type.  Example: Patient with no insurance may have payortype.SopCode=81 ("SelfPay").  Patient then adds Medicaid insurance and gets a second new PayorType entry with SopCode=2 (Medicaid).

column.name: PayorTypeNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateStart
column.summary: Date of the beginning of new payor type.  End date is the DateStart of the next payor type entry.

column.name: SopCode
column.summary: FK to sop.SopCode. Examples: 121, 3115, etc.

table.name: payperiod
table.summary: Used to view employee timecards.  Timecard entries are not linked to a pay period.  Instead, payperiods are set up, and the user can only view specific pay periods.  So it feels like they are linked, but it's date based.

column.name: PayPeriodNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateStart
column.summary: The first day of the payperiod

column.name: DateStop
column.summary: The last day of the payperiod.  Inclusive, ignoring time of day.

column.name: DatePaycheck
column.summary: The date that paychecks will be dated.  A few days after the dateStop.  Optional.

table.name: payplan
table.summary: Each row represents one signed agreement to make payments.

column.name: PayPlanNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  The patient who had the treatment done.

column.name: Guarantor
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum.  The person responsible for the payments.  Does not need to be in the same family as the patient.  
            Not necessarily the same as the guarantor on the PayPlanCharge.

column.name: PayPlanDate
column.summary: Date that the payment plan will display in the account.

column.name: APR
column.summary: Annual percentage rate.  eg 18.  This does not take into consideration any late payments, but only the percentage used to calculate the amortization schedule.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Generally used to archive the terms when the amortization schedule is created.

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.  Will be 0 if standard payment plan.  But if this is being used to track expected insurance payments, then this will be the foreign key to insplan.PlanNum, and Guarantor will be 0.

column.name: CompletedAmt
column.summary: The amount of the treatment that has already been completed.  This should match the sum of the principal amounts for most situations.  But if the procedures have not yet been completed, and the payment plan is to make any sense, then this number must be changed.

column.name: InsSubNum
column.summary: FK to inssub.InsSubNum.  Will be 0 if standard payment plan.  But if this is being used to track expected insurance payments, then this will be the foreign key to inssub.InsSubNum, and Guarantor will be 0.

column.name: PaySchedule
column.summary: Enum:PaymentSchedule How often payments are scheduled to be made.

EnumValue._: 0 - Pay 1 time every month.
EnumValue.name: Monthly

EnumValue._: 1 - Pay 1 time every month on a certain day of the week.
EnumValue.name: MonthlyDayOfWeek

EnumValue._: 2 - Pay every week per month.
EnumValue.name: Weekly

EnumValue._: 3 - Pay every other week per times per month.
EnumValue.name: BiWeekly

EnumValue._: 4 - Pay 4 times per year.
EnumValue.name: Quarterly

column.name: NumberOfPayments
column.summary: The number of payments that will be made to complete the payment plan.

column.name: PayAmt
column.summary: Payment amount due per payment plan charge.

column.name: DownPayment
column.summary: The amount paid toward the payment plan when it was first opened.

column.name: IsClosed
column.summary: True if this payment plan is closed.  Closed should not be edited.

column.name: Signature
column.summary: The encrypted and bound signature in base64 format.  The signature is bound to the concatenation of the Total Amount,APR,Number of Payments,Payment Amount

column.name: SigIsTopaz
column.summary: True if the signature is in Topaz format rather than OD format.

column.name: PlanCategory
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum

column.name: IsDynamic
column.summary: True if this payment plan is a dynamic payment plan, false if it is static.

column.name: ChargeFrequency
column.summary: Enum:PayPlanFrequency How often charges are created for the payment plan.

EnumValue._: 0 - Weekly
EnumValue.name: Weekly

EnumValue._: 1 - Every Other Week
EnumValue.name: EveryOtherWeek

EnumValue._: 2 - Specific day of month
EnumValue.name: OrdinalWeekday

EnumValue._: 3 - Monthly
EnumValue.name: Monthly

EnumValue._: 4 - Quarterly
EnumValue.name: Quarterly

column.name: DatePayPlanStart
column.summary: The date of the first payment plan charge. Does not include downpayment.

column.name: IsLocked
column.summary: True if the payment plan is locked. This is "Locked", not just disabling the terms. Locked payment plans cannot add production or modify terms. The checkbox 'Locked' can't be unchecked unless the user has the PayPlanUnlock permission. If the preference PayPlanRequireLockForAPR is enabled, 'Locked' must be checked before saving a plan with APR.

column.name: DateInterestStart
column.summary: The date on which the pay plan can begin posting interest charges.

column.name: DynamicPayPlanTPOption
column.summary: Enum:DynamicPayPlanTPOptions Indicates the selected mode for how treatment planned procedures are handled by a dynamic payment plan.

Enumeration.name: DynamicPayPlanTPOptions
Enumeration.summary: Enum that changes how treatment planned procedures are handled in dynamic payment plans.

column.name: MobileAppDeviceNum
column.summary: A FK to the mobile app device that the PayPlan is currently on.

column.name: SecurityHash
column.summary: Holds the salted hash of the following paysplit fields: Guarantor, PayAmt, IsClosed, IsLocked.

column.name: SheetDefNum
column.summary: FK to sheetdef.SheetDefNum. This is use when printing. Can only be payment plan type sheet. If 0, uses hard coded logic.

table.name: payplancharge
table.summary: One of the dated charges attached to a payment plan.  This has nothing to do with payments, but rather just causes the amount due to increase on the date of the charge.  The amount of the charge is the sum of the principal and the interest.

column.name: PayPlanChargeNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PayPlanNum
column.summary: FK to payplan.PayPlanNum.

column.name: Guarantor
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum.  The guarantor account that each charge will affect.  Does not have to match the guarantor of the payment plan.
            This column doesn't even have to point to a guarantor at all.  
            E.g. Credits and Closeout debits will be linked to the patient, not guarantor.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  The patient account that the principal gets removed from.

column.name: ChargeDate
column.summary: The date that the charge will show on the patient account.  Any charge with a future date will not show on the account yet and will not affect the balance.

column.name: Principal
column.summary: For Debits, this is the principal charge amount.  For Credits (version 2 only), then this is the credit amount.

column.name: Interest
column.summary: For Debits, this is the interest portion of this payment.  Always 0 for Credits.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Any note about this particular payment plan charge

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary:
FK to provider.ProvNum.  Since there is no ProvNum field at the payplan level, the provider must be the same for all payplancharges.  
            It's initially assigned as the patient priProv.  Payments applied should be to this provnum, 
            although the current user interface does not help with this.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  Since there is no ClincNum field at the payplan level, the clinic must be the same for all payplancharges.  It's initially assigned using the patient clinic.  Payments applied should be to this clinic, although the current user interface does not help with this.

column.name: ChargeType
column.summary: Enum:PayPlanChargeType The charge type of the payment plan. 0 - ChargeDue, 1 - Production.  Only relevant for those on Payment Plan Version 2, not Dynamic Payment Plans.

Enumeration.name: PayPlanChargeType
Enumeration.summary: Jordan-I would like to change this enum, but it would break the API.

EnumValue._: 1 -  The production can be either Procedure, Adjust, or Ortho. Only used for PPv2, not DPPs.
EnumValue.name: Credit

column.name: ProcNum
column.summary:
FK to procedurelog.ProcNum.  The procedure that this payplancharge is attached to.  Only applies to credits.
            Always 0 for ChargeDue.  Can be 0 for production not attached to a procedure.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: DateTime payplancharge was added to the payplan. Not editable by user.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: DateTime payplancharge was edited. Not editable by user.

column.name: StatementNum
column.summary: FK to statement.StatementNum.  Only used when the statement in an invoice.

column.name: FKey
column.summary: Only present for dynamic payment plans. Contains FKey of the link type. ProcNum, AdjNum, or OrthoCaseNum. Since one ChargeDue can be split to multiple procedures, multiple rows are created in that case. In UI, these would be grouped by due date unless user checked ungroup box.

column.name: LinkType
column.summary: Enum:PayPlanLinkType Only present for dynamic payment plans.

EnumValue._: 0 - None. Should only be this when charges/credits are for regular static payment plans.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Adjustment
EnumValue.name: Adjustment

EnumValue._: 2 - Procedure
EnumValue.name: Procedure

EnumValue._: 3 - OrthoCase
EnumValue.name: OrthoCase

column.name: IsOffset
column.summary: Set to true if this charge is created to offset an overcharge. Dynamic payment plans can get into this rare scenario where a charge has been inserted into the database for too much value. There is a 'fix' that users can apply from within the dynamic payment plan overcharge report which will create offsetting negative charges. The Income Transfer logic needs to know that this is an overcharge in order to remove value from corresponding charge that is linked to the same production entry (proc, adj, etc).

column.name: IsDownPayment
column.summary: Set to true if this charge is a down payment. There can be multiple charges marked as down payment on one pay plan because of how charges get split because they get attached to production.

table.name: payplanlink
table.summary: Each row represents Production for a Procedure, Adjustment, or OrthoCase on a (Dynamic) Payment Plan. The sum of these is the total of the payment plan.

column.name: PayPlanLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: PayPlanNum
column.summary: FK to payplan.PayPlanNum

column.name: LinkType
column.summary: Enum:PayPlanLinkType  The object type being linked to be credited.

EnumValue._: 0 - None. Should only be this when charges/credits are for regular static payment plans.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Adjustment
EnumValue.name: Adjustment

EnumValue._: 2 - Procedure
EnumValue.name: Procedure

EnumValue._: 3 - OrthoCase
EnumValue.name: OrthoCase

column.name: FKey
column.summary: Stores the FKey of object being linked, known from link type.

column.name: AmountOverride
column.summary: Optional override if full amount of object is not desired.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: DateTime. Date the link was created. If pref.PayPlanItemDateShowProc is false, then this is also the date that this entry shows in the main account module.

table.name: payplantemplate
table.summary: A template of payplan terms that can be copied to a payment plan.  Only used for dynamic payment plans, not patient payment plans.

column.name: PayPlanTemplateNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: PayPlanTemplateName
column.summary: The name of the Pay Plan Template.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum. Can be 0.

column.name: APR
column.summary: Annual percentage rate.  eg 18.

column.name: InterestDelay
column.summary: The number of payments before interest is applied.

column.name: PayAmt
column.summary: The total payment amount due for each period.

column.name: NumberOfPayments
column.summary: The total number of periods for the payment plan. If the Pay Plan is dynamic and NumberOfPayments is not 0 then this is only used to calculate the PayAmt. After the PayAmt is calculated, NumberOfPayments is set to 0.

column.name: ChargeFrequency
column.summary: Enum:PayPlanFrequency How often charges are created for the payment plan. Monthly, weekly, etc.  Only for Dynamic Payment Plans.

EnumValue._: 0 - Weekly
EnumValue.name: Weekly

EnumValue._: 1 - Every Other Week
EnumValue.name: EveryOtherWeek

EnumValue._: 2 - Specific day of month
EnumValue.name: OrdinalWeekday

EnumValue._: 3 - Monthly
EnumValue.name: Monthly

EnumValue._: 4 - Quarterly
EnumValue.name: Quarterly

column.name: DownPayment
column.summary: The amount paid toward the payment plan when it was first opened.

column.name: DynamicPayPlanTPOption
column.summary: Enum:DynamicPayPlanTPOptions Indicates the selected mode for how treatment planned procedures are handled by a dynamic payment plan.  None, AwaitComplete, or TreatAsComplete.

Enumeration.name: DynamicPayPlanTPOptions
Enumeration.summary: Enum that changes how treatment planned procedures are handled in dynamic payment plans.

column.name: Note
column.summary: A detailed note of the terms shows for future reference. Any changes made to the terms will be added to the note. Other notes can be added as needed.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Templates can not be deleted, but can be hidden if not needed any more.

column.name: SheetDefNum
column.summary: FK to sheetdef.SheetDefNum. This is use when printing. Can only be payment plan type sheet. If 0, uses hard coded logic.

table.name: paysplit
table.summary: Always attached to a payment.  Always affects exactly one patient account and one provider.

column.name: SplitNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SplitAmt

table.name: pearlrequest
table.summary: Stores the necessary information for polling Pearl’s API to check if AI annotations have been generated for an image sent from the Imaging module. It's also used to prevent duplicate image submissions to the API, which would return an error response. To poll Pearl for an image, all that's required is a request_id and an organization_id. Organization_id is stored in the Pearl program link.

column.name: PearlRequestNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: RequestId
column.summary: Request ID given to Pearl to uniquely identify this request. Generated as a GUID before uploading an image to Pearl.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: FK to document. Links this request to the image that was sent. This is sufficient for mounts because mount images are sent individually to Pearl.

column.name: RequestStatus
column.summary: Enum:EnumPearlStatus Keeps track of the request's status. Can be Polling, Received, or Error.

EnumValue._: 0 - An individual machine is actively polling Pearl.
EnumValue.name: Polling

EnumValue._: 1 - The image was successfully processed and AI annotations were returned from Pearl.
EnumValue.name: Received

EnumValue._: 2 - An error occurred on Pearl’s side. Only set for errors that prevent this request from ever being fulfilled, such as the image being rejected.
EnumValue.name: Error

EnumValue._: 3 - Pearl did not give results within the timeout period of 10 minutes. Polling for this request can be retried.
EnumValue.name: TimedOut

EnumValue._: 4 - The image is being uploaded to Pearl.
EnumValue.name: Uploading

EnumValue._: 5 - An error occurred while uploading. The image should be reuploaded..
EnumValue.name: ErrorUploading

EnumValue._: 6 - An error occurred while processing. The image should not be reuploaded, but we can restart polling.
EnumValue.name: ErrorProcessing

EnumValue._: 7 - Pearl returned no results for this image. The image show not be reuploaded.
EnumValue.name: NoResults

column.name: DateTSent
column.summary: The time the image was originally sent to Pearl.

column.name: DateTChecked
column.summary: The most recent time an API call was made to Pearl to check the status of this request.

table.name: popup
table.summary: If an existing popup message gets changed, then an archive first gets created that's a copy of the original.  This is so that we can track historical changes.  When a new one gets created, all the archived popups will get automatically repointed to the new one.  If you "delete" a popup, it actually archives that popup.  All the other archives of that popup still point to the newly archived popup, but now there is no popup in that group with the IsArchived flag not set.

column.name: PopupNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: Description
column.summary: The text of the popup.

column.name: IsDisabled
column.summary: Deprecated. Use DateTimeDisabled instead.

column.name: PopupLevel
column.summary: Enum:EnumPopupLevel 0=Patient, 1=Family, 2=Superfamily. If Family, then this Popup will apply to the entire family.  If Superfamily, then this popup will apply to the entire superfamily.

EnumValue._: 0=Patient
EnumValue.name: Patient

EnumValue._: 1=Family
EnumValue.name: Family

EnumValue._: 2=SuperFamily
EnumValue.name: SuperFamily

EnumValue._: 3=Automation. Not in db. This is only used in FormPopupsForFam as a dummy status for temporary display objects that will not be in db.
EnumValue.name: Automation

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: The server time that this note was entered.  Cannot be changed by user.  Does not get changed automatically when level or isDisabled gets changed.  If note itself changes, then a new popup is created along with a new DateTimeEntry. Current popup's edit date gets set to the previous entry's DateTimeEntry

column.name: IsArchived
column.summary: Indicates that this is not the most current popup and that it is an archive.  True for any archived or "deleted" popups.

column.name: PopupNumArchive
column.summary: This will be zero for current popups that show when a patient is selected.  Archived popups will have a value which is the FK to its parent Popup.  The parent popup could be the most recent popup or another archived popup.  Will be zero for current and "deleted" popups.

column.name: DateTimeDisabled
column.summary: The DateTime at which this popup will be disabled. If this is DateTime.MinValue, then it will never be disabled.

Enum:BypassLockStatus Specifies whether a proceduce with this code can be created before the global lock date. The only values that

Enumeration.name: BypassLockStatus
Enumeration.summary: The conditions when the global lock date can be bypassed.

EnumValue._: 0 - Never bypass the lock date.
EnumValue.name: NeverBypass

EnumValue._: 1 - Bypass the lock date if the fee is zero.
EnumValue.name: BypassIfZero

EnumValue._: 2 - Always bypass the global lock date.
EnumValue.name: BypassAlways

column.name: TaxCode

column.name: DiagnosticCodes
column.summary: Text to store up to 4 ICD-10 codes. Codes are comma-separated with no whitespace. Used to set the default DiagnosticCode, DiagnosticCode2, DiagnosticCode3, and DiagnosticCode4 fields for new procedures linked to the procedure code. When this field is not empty or null, it will override the ICD9DefaultForNewProcs preference. Example: M26.31,K08.401,K02.51

table.name: procedurelog
table.summary:
Database table is procedurelog.  A procedure for a patient.  Can be treatment planned or completed.  Once it's completed, it gets tracked more closely by the security portion of the program.  A procedure can NEVER be deleted.  Status can just be changed to "deleted".
            A "Group Note" is a special kind of procedure. Its status is always EC. It always uses DCode "~GRP~". Just like all other procs, it can have exactly one ProcNote. To attach other procs to a Group Note, we use the ProcGroupItem table. It does not have to have any attachd procs, and their ProcStatuses do not matter (except maybe D). The ProvNum of a GroupNote can be set independently of the ProvNums on the attached procs.

column.name: ProcNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: AptNum
column.summary: FK to appointment.AptNum.  Only allowed to attach proc to one appt(not counting planned appt)

column.name: OldCode
column.summary: No longer used.

column.name: ProcDate
column.summary: Procedure date that will show in the account as the date performed.  If just treatment planned, the date can be the date it was tp'd, or the date can be min val if we don't care. This has no time component. Also see ProcTime column.

column.name: ProcFee
column.summary: Procedure fee.

column.name: Surf
column.summary: Surfaces, or use "UL" etc for quadrant, "2" etc for sextant, "U","L" for arches.  Sextants in the United States are: 1 (Upper Right), 2 (Upper Anterior), 3 (Upper Left), 4 (Lower Left), 5 (Lower Anterior), 6 (Lower Right).  In Canada, Sextants are 03 through 08 (add 2 to the US sextant and prepend a zero).

column.name: ToothNum
column.summary: May be blank, otherwise 1-32 or A-T, 1 or 2 char. For supernumerary, add 50 to use 51-82. For supernumerary primary, use AS-TS.  For Canadian users, using FDI nomenclature, we use 51 as a placeholder for supernumerary teeth, which is tooth number 99 according to CDHA standards (2/17/2014).  Logic for this is handled in the tooth logic class.

column.name: ToothRange
column.summary: May be blank, otherwise is series of toothnumbers separated by commas. No dashes. Tooth numbers include 1-32 or A-T.  Supernumeraries not supported here yet.

column.name: Priority
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum, which contains the text of the priority.

Enumeration.name: ProcStat
Enumeration.summary: Procedure Status.  These statuses are transalted via class type "enumProcStat" (ex Lan.g("enumProcStat","..."))

EnumValue._: 1- Treatment Plan.
EnumValue.name: TP

EnumValue._: 2- Complete.
EnumValue.name: C

EnumValue._: 3- Existing Current Provider.
EnumValue.name: EC

EnumValue._: 4- Existing Other Provider.
EnumValue.name: EO

EnumValue._: 5- Referred Out.
EnumValue.name: R

EnumValue._: 6- Deleted.
EnumValue.name: D

EnumValue._: 7- Condition.
EnumValue.name: Cn

EnumValue._: 8- Treatment Plan inactive.

column.summary: If this flag is set, then the proc is locked down tight.  No changes at all can be made except to append, sign, or invalidate. Invalidate really just sets the proc to status 'deleted'.  An invalidated proc retains its IsLocked status.  All locked procs will be status of C or D.  Locked group notes will be status of EC or D.

column.summary: Stores the dollar amount of the discount, not full price.  E.g.  for a 10% discount, Fee = $160 Discount = $16.  This column is used by treatment planned procedures to create an adjustment when set complete.  It should not be used as an accurate monetary discount value for completed procedures.

column.summary:
The ICD code version for all diagnosis codes on this procedure, including DiagnosisCode, DiagnosisCode2, DiagnosisCode3,
            and DiagnosisCode4.  Value of 9 for ICD-9, 10 for ICD-10, etc.  Default value is 9.  This value is copied from the DxIcdVersion preference

column.name: IsCpoe
column.summary:
Procedures will be flagged as CPOE (Computerized Provider Order Entry) if this procedure was created by a provider.
            If a provider views, edits, or has any interaction with this procedure after its creation, it will be flagged as IsCPOE.
            Also, there will be a helpful window where providers can go to to "approve" non-CPOE procedures and mark them as CPOE to help meet EHR measures.
            If a staff person is logged in and enters this procedure then this is non-CPOE, so false.

Enumeration.name: ProcUrgency
Enumeration.summary: Used for marking procedures on medical claims as 'Emergency' on the 1500 claim form.

EnumValue._:
0 - Standard procedure urgency.  Most procedures will have this ProcUrgency.  This will result in the 1500 Medical Claim Form box 24c
            being blank.  (Normal=blank,Emergency='Y')
EnumValue.name: Normal

EnumValue._: 1 - Emergency ProcUrgency is used to populate the 1500 Medical Claim Form box 24c with a 'Y'. (Emergency='Y',Normal=blank)
EnumValue.name: Emergency

column.name: DiscountPlanAmt

table.name: procnote
table.summary: A procedure note for one procedure.  User does not have any direct control over this table at all.  It's handled automatically.  When user "edits" a procedure note, the program actually just adds another note.  No note can EVER be edited or deleted.

column.name: ProcNoteNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.summary: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum

column.summary: The server time that this note was entered. Essentially a timestamp.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.

column.name: Note
column.summary: The actual note.

column.name: SigIsTopaz
column.summary: There are two kinds of signatures.  Topaz signatures use hardware manufactured by that company, and the signature is created by their library.  OD signatures work exactly the same way, but are only for on-screen signing.

column.name: Signature
column.summary: The encrypted signature.  A signature starts as a collection of vectors.  The Topaz .sig file format is proprietary.  The OD signature format looks like this: 45,68;48,70;49,72;0,0;55,88;etc. Tenths are allowed, so it's common to see decimals.  It's simply a sequence of points, separated by semicolons.  0,0 represents pen up.  Then, a hash is created from the Note, concatenated directly with the userNum.  For example, "This is a note3" gets turned into a hash of 2849283940385391 (16 bytes).  The hash is used to encrypt the signature data string using symmetric encryption.  Therefore, the actual signature cannot be retrieved from the database by ordinary means.  Also, the signature info cannot even be retrieved by Open Dental at all unless it supplies the same hash as before, proving that the data has not changed since signed.  If OD supplies the correct hash, then it will be able to extract the sequence of vectors which it will then use to display the signature.  The OD sigs are not compressed, and the Topaz sigs are.  But there is very little difference in their sizes.  It would be very rare for a signature to be larger than 1000 bytes.

table.name: proctp
table.summary: These are copies of procedures that are attached to saved treatment plans.  The ProcNumOrig points to the actual procedurelog row.

column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TreatPlanNum
column.summary: FK to treatplan.TreatPlanNum.  The treatment plan to which this proc is attached.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: ProcNumOrig
column.summary: FK to procedurelog.ProcNum. This procNum is only here to compare and test the existence of the referenced procedure.  If present, it will check to see whether the procedure is still status TP.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The order of this proc within its tp.  This is set when the tp is first created and can't be changed.  Drastically simplifies loading the tp.

column.name: Priority

column.summary: A simple string displaying the tooth number.  If international tooth numbers are used, then this will be in international format already.  For Canadian users, using FDI nomenclature, we use 51 as a placeholder for supernumerary teeth, which is tooth number 99 according to CDHA standards (2/17/2014).  Logic for this is handled in the tooth logic class.

column.name: Surf
column.summary: Tooth surfaces or area.  This is already converted for international use.  If arch or quad, then it will have U,LR, etc.

column.name: ProcCode

column.name: Descript
column.summary: Description is originally copied from procedurecode.Descript, but user can change it.

column.name: FeeAmt

column.name: PriInsAmt

column.name: SecInsAmt

column.name: PatAmt
column.summary: The amount the patient is expected to pay. Never gets automatically updated.

column.name: Discount

column.name: Prognosis

column.name: Dx
column.summary: Text from diagnosis definition.  Can be changed by user at any time.

column.name: ProcAbbr

table.name: program
table.summary: Each row is a bridge to an outside program, frequently an imaging program.  Most of the bridges are hard coded, and simply need to be enabled.  But user can also add their own custom bridge.

column.name: ProgramNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ProgName
column.summary: Unique name for built-in program bridges. Not user-editable. enum ProgramName

column.name: ProgDesc
column.summary: Description that shows.

column.name: Enabled
column.summary: True if enabled.

column.name: Path
column.summary: The path of the executable to run or file to open. Text since 255 is the largest VARCHAR supported and some softwares have long paths that can easily exceed 255 characters.

column.name: CommandLine
column.summary: Some programs will accept command line arguments. Text since 255 is the largest VARCHAR supported and some softwares have long command line arguments that can easily exceed 255 characters.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Notes about this program link. Peculiarities, etc.

column.name: PluginDllName
column.summary: If this is a Plugin, then this is the filename of the dll, including the extension.  The dll must be located in the application directory.

column.name: ButtonImage
column.summary:
If no image, then will be an empty string.  In this case, the bitmap will be null when loaded from the database.
            Must be a 22 x 22 image, and thus needs (width) x (height) x (depth) = 22 x 22 x 4 = 1936 bytes.

column.name: FileTemplate
column.summary: For custom program links only.  Stores the template of a file to be generated when launching the program link.

column.name: FilePath
column.summary: For custom program links only.  Stores the path of a file to be generated when launching the program link.

column.name: IsDisabledByHq
column.summary: Do not use directly.  Call Programs.IsEnabledByHq() instead.  Has HQ disabled this program for all customers via WebServiceHq.EnableAdditionalFeatures().  Using 'Disabled' because the web method will only send Programs HQ cares about.  Any user defined Programs should not be marked as 'Disabled' by default.

column.name: CustErr
column.summary: Typically blank. A value is added to this if we have disabled this program at HQ's side and will be updated during HqProgram.Download()

table.name: programproperty
table.summary: Some program links (bridges), have properties that need to be set.  The property names are always hard coded.  User can change the value.  The property is usually retrieved based on its name.

column.name: ProgramPropertyNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ProgramNum
column.summary: FK to program.ProgramNum

column.name: PropertyDesc
column.summary:
The description or prompt for this property.  Blank for workstation overrides of program path.
            Many bridges use this description as an "internal description". This way it can act like a FK in order to look up this particular property.  Users cannot edit.

column.name: PropertyValue
column.summary: The value. Could contain FK to other tables.

column.name: ComputerName
column.summary: The human-readable name of the computer on the network (not the IP address).  Only used when overriding program path.  Blank for typical Program Properties.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  This is only used by a few bridges.  Set to 0 for most bridges.

column.name: IsMasked
column.summary: Is true if the program property is sensitive information that would need to be masked in the UI. False by default.

column.name: IsHighSecurity
column.summary: Is true if the program property is a high security property. False by default.

table.name: reactivation
table.summary:
Track patient contact via a commlog type ("Reactivation").  
            Any commlogs of this type that occur after the last completed procedure will be considered a contact attempt.
            Patients should show in this list if they have previously completed procedures (excluding broken/canceled), 
            and the most recent was completed before the time span specified by the "Days Past" preference.  
            Include Patients with the following PatStatus: Patient, Inactive, Prospective
            Patients should not show in this list if they have been marked "Do not contact".
            Patients should not show in this list if a future appointment is scheduled.
            Once contacted, Patient should not show in this list.  Patient will later reappear in this list if the "Reactivation contact interval" time 
            period passes since the last contact and an appointment has not yet been scheduled.  
            If the patient is contacted the maximum number of times specified by the "Count Contact Max" preference, mark the patient as "Do Not Contact".  
            Example:
            Johnny Patient had his last procedure completed on 1/1/2018.  There is a "Reactivation" type commlog on his chart from 6/1/2018.  
            He does not have any future scheduled appointments.  Johnny would be included in the list of "Reactivation" patients, with a single contact 
            attempt having been made already.

column.name: ReactivationNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: ReactivationStatus
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. Uses the existing RecallUnschedStatus DefCat.

column.name: ReactivationNote
column.summary: An administrative note for staff use.

column.name: DoNotContact
column.summary: The patient can set this property if they don't want to be contacted so that it won't interfere with the max attempts to contact option.

table.name: recall
table.summary:
A patient can only have one recall object per type.  
            The recall table stores a few dates that must be kept synchronized with other information in the database.  This is difficult.  
            Anytime one of the following items changes, things need to be synchronized: 
            procedurecode.SetRecall, any procedurelog change for a patient (procs added, deleted, completed, status changed, date changed, etc), patient status changed.  
            There are expected to be a few bugs in the synchronization logic, so anytime a patient's recall is opened, it will also update.
            
            During synchronization, the program will frequently alter DateDueCalc, DateDue, and DatePrevious based on trigger procs.  
            The system will also add and delete recalls as necessary. 
            But it will not delete a recall unless all values are default and there is no useful information.  
            When a user tries to delete a recall, they will only be successful if the trigger conditions do not apply.  
            Otherwise, they will have to disable the recall instead.

column.name: RecallNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateDueCalc
column.summary:
Not editable.  The calculated date due. Generated by the program and subject to change anytime the conditions change. 
            It can be blank (0001-01-01) if no appropriate triggers.

column.name: DateDue
column.summary:
This is the date that is actually used when doing reports for recall. 
            It will usually be the same as DateDueCalc unless user has changed it. System will only update this field if it is the same as DateDueCalc.  
            Otherwise, it will be left alone.  Gets cleared along with DateDueCalc when resetting recall.  
            When setting disabled, this field will also be cleared.  This is the field to use if converting from another software.

column.name: DatePrevious
column.summary:
Not editable. Previous date that procedures were done to trigger this recall. It is calculated and enforced automatically.  
            If you want to affect this date, add a procedure to the chart with a status of C, EC, or EO.

column.name: RecallInterval
column.summary: The interval between recalls.  The Interval struct combines years, months, weeks, and days into a single integer value.

column.name: RecallStatus
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum, or 0 for none.

column.name: Note
column.summary: An administrative note for staff use.

column.name: IsDisabled
column.summary:
If true, this recall type will be disabled (there's only one type right now). 
            This is usually used rather than deleting the recall type from the patient because trigger conditions must be enforced for all patients.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: Last datetime that this row was inserted or updated.

column.name: RecallTypeNum
column.summary: FK to recalltype.RecallTypeNum.

column.name: DisableUntilBalance
column.summary: Default is 0.  If a positive number is entered, then the family balance must be less in order for this recall to show in the recall list.

column.name: DisableUntilDate
column.summary: If a date is entered, then this recall will be disabled until that date.

column.name: DateScheduled
column.summary: This will only have a value if a recall is scheduled.

column.name: Priority
column.summary: Enum:RecallPriority Indicates if the appointment has any special priority.

Enumeration.name: RecallPriority
Enumeration.summary: Scheduling priority used by Recalls.

EnumValue._: 0 - Default priority
EnumValue.name: Normal

EnumValue._: 1 - Used to identify items for the ASAP list
EnumValue.name: ASAP

column.name: TimePatternOverride
column.summary: Default is an empty string. Used to override a RecallTypes time pattern.

column.name: TimePattern

Stores the length of the appointment in /'s and X's.  Used when scheduling the appointment.

Set to true if this recall type should be automatically appended to the appointment when scheduling a special recall type.

table.name: registrationkey
table.summary:
Keeps track of which product keys have been assigned to which customers.
            This datatype is only used if the program is being run from a distributor installation.
            A single customer is allowed to have more than one key, to accommodate for various circumstances, including having multiple physical business locations.

column.name: RegistrationKeyNum
column.summary: Primary Key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum. The customer to which this registration key applies.

column.name: RegKey
column.summary: The registration key as stored in the customer database.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Db note about the registration key. Specifically, the note must include information about the location to which this key pertains, since once at least one key must be assigned to each location to be legal.

column.name: DateStarted
column.summary: This will help later with tracking for licensing.

column.name: DateDisabled
column.summary: This is used to completely disable a key.  Might possibly even cripple the user's program.  Usually only used if reassigning another key due to abuse or error.  If no date specified, then this key is still valid.

column.name: DateEnded
column.summary: This is used when the customer cancels monthly support.  This still allows the customer to get downloads for bug fixes, but only up through a certain version.  Our web server program will use this date to deduce which version they are allowed to have.  Any version that was released as a beta before this date is allowed to be downloaded.

column.name: IsForeign
column.summary: This is assigned automatically based on whether the registration key is a US version vs. a foreign version.  The foreign version is not able to unlock the procedure codes.  There are muliple layers of safeguards in place.

column.name: UsesServerVersion
column.summary: Deprecated.

column.name: IsFreeVersion
column.summary: We have given this customer a free version.  Typically in India.

column.name: IsOnlyForTesting
column.summary: This customer is not using the software with live patient data, but only for testing and development purposes.

column.name: VotesAllotted
column.summary: Typically 100, although it can be more for multilocation offices.

column.name: IsResellerCustomer
column.summary: This is a customer of a reseller, so this customer will not have full access to all our services.

column.name: HasEarlyAccess
column.summary: This is a customer that is allowed early access to certain features.  E.g. downloading the Alpha version of the software.

column.name: DateTBackupScheduled
column.summary: Deprecated. Moved to the supplementalbackups database. Next date and time of supplemental backup for the customer who owns this registration key.

column.name: BackupPassCode
column.summary: Deprecated. Moved to the supplementalbackups database. Pass code for next supplemental backup expected from this customer.

column.name: CriterionFK
column.summary: Foreign key to disease.DiseaseDefNum, medicationpat.MedicationNum, or allergy.AllergyDefNum. Will be 0 if Age, Gender, or LabResult are the trigger.

column.name: CriterionValue
column.summary: Only used if Age, Gender, or LabResult are the trigger. Examples: "<25"(must include < or >), "Male"/"Female", "INR" (the simple description of the lab test)

column.name: Message
column.summary: Text that will show as the reminder.

table.name: repeatcharge
table.summary: Each row represents one charge that will be added. Usually monthly, but quarterly and annually are allowed.

column.name: RepeatChargeNum
column.summary: Primary key

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: ProcCode
column.summary: FK to procedurecode.ProcCode.  The code that will be added to the account as a completed procedure.

column.name: ChargeAmt
column.summary: The amount that will be charged.  The amount from the procedurecode will not be used.  This way, a repeating charge cannot be accidentally altered.

column.name: DateStart
column.summary: The date of the first charge if UseBillingCycleDays is not enabled.  Charges will always be added on the same day of the frequency start date. If UseBillingCycleDays is enabled, repeat charges will be applied on billing cycle day instead. If more than one frequency cycle goes by without applying repeating charges, then multiple procedures will be added.

column.name: DateStop
column.summary: The last date on which a charge is allowed. Can be blank (0001-01-01) to represent a perpetual repeating charge.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Any note for internal use.

column.name: CopyNoteToProc
column.summary: Indicates that the note should be copied to the corresponding procedure billing note.

column.name: CreatesClaim
column.summary: Set to true to have a claim automatically created for the patient with the procedure that is attached to this repeating charge.

column.name: IsEnabled
column.summary: Set to false to disable the repeating charge.  This allows patients to have repeating charges in their history that are not active.  Used mainly for repeating charges with notes that should not be deleted.

column.name: UsePrepay
column.summary: Set to true to use prepayments for repeating charges.

column.name: Npi
column.summary: Stores the NPI of the provider on this repeating charge for Erx.  This used to be stored in the Note field but got moved over to its own column in 17.2.

column.name: ErxAccountId
column.summary: Stores the Erx Account ID on this repeating charge for Erx.  This used to be stored in the Note field but got moved over to its own column in 17.2.

column.name: ProviderName
column.summary: Stores the name of the provider on this repeating charge for Erx.  Value is received directly from NewCrop.

column.name: ChargeAmtAlt
column.summary: HQ Only. An alternate amount to be charged for this RepeatCharge in some cases. Should always default to -1 as -1 will be used as a flag to indicate it has not been set. A value of 0 means ChargeAmtAlt has been intentionally set to 0.

column.name: UnearnedTypes
column.summary: If UsePrepay is true, when the procedure is created from this repeat charge, it will allocate payments from these unearned types. Stored as a comma separated list of DefNums of Category PaySplitUnearnedType. If empty, then all unearned types will be considered.

column.name: Frequency
column.summary: Enum:EnumRepeatChargeFrequency 0-Monthly, 1-Quarterly, 2-Annually.

EnumValue._: 0 - Monthly
EnumValue.name: Monthly

EnumValue._: 1 - Quarterly
EnumValue.name: Quarterly

EnumValue._: 2 - Annually
EnumValue.name: Annually

table.name: replicationserver
table.summary: Replication server information. Used for server specific replication settings, manually entered by the user.  Each row is one server.

column.name: ReplicationServerNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Descript
column.summary: The description or name of the server.  Optional.

column.name: ServerId
column.summary: Db admin sets this server_id server variable on each replication server.  Allows us to know what server each workstation is connected to.  In display, it's ordered by this value.  Users are always forced to enter a value here.

column.name: RangeStart
column.summary: Deprecated. Only used for Random Primary Keys. The start of the key range for this server.  0 if no value entered yet.

column.name: RangeEnd
column.summary: Deprecated. Only used for Random Primary Keys. The end of the key range for this server.  0 if no value entered yet.

column.name: AtoZpath
column.summary: The AtoZpath for this server. Optional.

column.name: UpdateBlocked
column.summary: If true, then this server cannot initiate an update.  Typical for satellite servers.

column.name: SlaveMonitor
column.summary: Deprecated. Monitoring the status of replication is now monitored by a separate service. See online manual for information on installing the new service. The description or name of the comptuer that will monitor replication for this server.

table.name: rxalert
table.summary: Many-to-many relationship connecting Rx with DiseaseDef, AllergyDef, or Medication.  Only one of those links may be specified in a single row; the other two will be 0.

column.name: RxAlertNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: RxDefNum
column.summary: FK to rxdef.RxDefNum.  This alert is to be shown when user attempts to write an Rx for this RxDef.

column.name: DiseaseDefNum
column.summary: FK to diseasedef.DiseaseDefNum.  Only if DrugProblem interaction.  This is compared against disease.DiseaseDefNum using PatNum.  Drug-Problem (they call it Drug-Diagnosis) checking is also performed in NewCrop.

column.name: AllergyDefNum
column.summary: FK to allergydef.AllergyDefNum.  Only if DrugAllergy interaction.  Compared against allergy.AllergyDefNum using PatNum.  Drug-Allergy checking is also perfomed in NewCrop.

column.name: MedicationNum
column.summary: FK to medication.MedicationNum.  Only if DrugDrug interaction.  This will be compared against medicationpat.MedicationNum using PatNum.  Drug-Drug checking is also performed in NewCrop.

column.name: NotificationMsg
column.summary: This is typically blank, so a default message will be displayed by OD.  But if this contains a message, then this message will be used instead.

column.name: IsHighSignificance
column.summary: False by default.  Set to true to flag the drug-drug or drug-allergy intervention as high significance.

table.name: rxdef
table.summary: Rx definitions.  Can safely delete or alter, because they get copied to the rxPat table, not referenced.

column.name: RxDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Drug

column.name: Sig
column.summary: Directions intended for the pharmacist.

column.name: Disp
column.summary: Amount to dispense.

column.name: Refills
column.summary: Number of refills.

column.name: Notes
column.summary: Notes about this drug. Will not be copied to the rxpat.

column.name: IsControlled
column.summary: Is a controlled substance.  This will affect the way it prints.

column.name: RxCui
column.summary: RxNorm Code identifier.  Copied down into medicationpat.RxCui (medical order) when a prescription is written.

column.name: IsProcRequired
column.summary: If true will require procedure be attached to this prescription when printed.  Usually true if IsControlled is true.

column.name: PatientInstruction
column.summary: Directions intended for the patient.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Only used for RxNorms, not Multums.

table.name: rxpat

column.name: IsControlled

table.name: schedule
table.summary: One block of time.  Either for practice, provider, employee, or blockout.

column.name: ScheduleNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SchedDate
column.summary: Date for this timeblock.

column.name: StartTime
column.summary: Start time for this timeblock.

column.name: StopTime
column.summary: Stop time for this timeblock.

column.name: SchedType
column.summary:
Enum:ScheduleType 0=Practice,1=Provider,2=Blockout,3=Employee.  Practice is used as a way to indicate holidays and as a way to put a
            note in for the entire practice for one day.  But whenever type is Practice, times will be ignored.

Enumeration.name: ScheduleType
Enumeration.summary: For schedule timeblocks.

EnumValue._: 4 - A slot of time that an ASAP appointment can be moved up to.
EnumValue.name: WebSchedASAP

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum if a provider type.

column.name: BlockoutType
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum if blockout.  eg. HighProduction, RCT Only, Emerg.

column.name: Note
column.summary: This contains various types of text entered by the user.

column.name: Status
column.summary:
Enum:SchedStatus enumeration 0=Open,1=Closed,2=Holiday.  All blocks have a status of Open, but user doesn't see the status.
            The "closed" status was previously used to override the defaults when the last timeblock was deleted.  But it's nearly phased out now.
            Still used by blockouts.  Holidays are a special type of practice schedule item which do not have providers attached.
            Used to differentiate between Practice SchedType Holidays and Notes.

Enumeration.name: SchedStatus
Enumeration.summary: Schedule status.  Open=0,Closed=1,Holiday=2.

column.name: EmployeeNum
column.summary: FK to employee.EmployeeNum.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: Last datetime that this row was inserted or updated.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary:
FK to clinic.ClinicNum if SchedType.Practice (holidays and practice notes) and applies to one clinic (operatories for one clinic). If
            SchedType.Practice and this applies to all clinics, or if any other SchedType, ClinicNum will be 0.  There won't be any scheduleop rows linking
            this schedule to operatories when the type is SchedType.Practice.  Instead, the linkage is implied based on the operatory.ClinicNum and applies
            to all operatories for the clinic.

Action to be selected by user when scheduling. When adding a new value to this Enum add a case for it in the ScheduledProcessThread in

column.name: TimeToRun

Frequency with which an action will be run. When adding a new value to this Enum add a case for it in the ScheduledProcessThread in

column.name: LastRanDateTime
