column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum

column.name: LogDateTime
column.summary: The date and time of the entry.  It's value is set when inserting and can never change.  Even if a user changes the date on their computer, this remains accurate because it uses server time.

column.name: LogText
column.summary: The description of exactly what was done. Varies by permission type.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  Can be 0 if not applicable.

column.name: FKey
column.summary:
A foreign key to a table associated with the PermType.  0 indicates not in use.  
            This is typically used for objects that have specific audit trails so that users can see all audit entries related to a particular object.  
            Every permission using FKey should be included and implmented in the CrudAuditPerms enum so that securitylog FKeys are note orphaned.
            Additonaly, the tabletype will to have the [CrudTable(CrudAuditPerms=CrudAuditPerm._____] added with the new CrudAuditPerm you created.
            For the patient portal, it is used to indicate logs created on behalf of other patients.  
            It's uses include:  AptNum with PermType AppointmentCreate, AppointmentEdit, or AppointmentMove tracks all appointment logs for a particular 
            appointment.
            CodeNum with PermType ProcFeeEdit currently only tracks fee changes.  
            PatNum with PermType PatientPortal represents an entry that a patient made on behalf of another patient.
            	The PatNum column will represent the patient who is taking the action.  
            PlanNum with PermType InsPlanChangeCarrierName tracks carrier name changes.

column.name: ShowInTerminal
column.summary: Examples: 1, 2, etc. The order that this sheet will show in the Kiosk queue, or zero if not set. Also determines if it will show in eClipboard in addition to any eClipboardSheetDef. For eClipboard, this is just treated like a boolean and that actual order is ignored.

column.name: IsMultiPage

column.name: IsDeleted
column.summary: Indicates whether or not this sheet has been marked deleted.

column.name: SheetDefNum
column.summary: FK to sheetdef.SheetDefNum. The SheetDef that was used to create this sheet. Will be 0 if an internal sheet or if the sheet was created before 17.2. Can be 0 for sheets that were created from web forms that were associated to web form sheet defs missing this value at HQ. The original purpose of this column was to use it in connection with RefID of the Sheet and SheetDef to automate the updating of forms such as office policies when they change significantly. It is now also used when making a copy of a sheet. Also used alongside EClipboardSheetDef to determine whether patient has already filled out a form.

column.name: DocNum

column.name: ClinicNum

column.name: DateTSheetEdited
column.summary:
The date and time the sheet was inserted or last time someone opened the sheet and clicked OK on FormSheetFillEdit.
            Gets updated even if no changes were made to the sheet or sheetfields, because we don't want to do the lengthy work of comparing all fields.
            Used when editing a sheet to warn user if the sheet has been edited by someone else.

column.name: HasMobileLayout
column.summary: If true then this Sheet has been designed for mobile and will be displayed as a mobile-friendly WebForm.

column.name: RevID
column.summary: Revision ID. Used to determine in conjunction with PrefillMode for eClipboard to determine whether to show a patient a new form or have them update their last filled out form. Must match up with SheetDef RevID to show a previously filled out form.

column.name: WebFormSheetID
column.summary: Only set when this sheet was created from a Web Form. FK to webforms_sheet.SheetID within the Web Forms server. Used to determine if this particular Web Form has been retrieved before in order to avoid creating duplicate sheet entries for a single Web Form.

column.summary: Enum:BypassLockStatus Specifies whether a sheet can be created before the global lock date.

Enumeration.summary: The conditions when the global lock date can be bypassed.

EnumValue._: 0 - Never bypass the lock date.
EnumValue.name: NeverBypass

EnumValue._: 1 - Bypass the lock date if the fee is zero.

column.name: HasMobileLayout
column.summary: If true then this Sheet has been designed for mobile and will be displayed as a mobile-friendly WebForm.

column.name: DateTCreated
column.summary:
The Date and time that SheetDef was created. Defaults to 0001-01-01 00:00:00 for existing sheets. When duplicating a custom sheet,
            if the original custom sheet's DateTCreated is 0001-01-01 00:00:00, the duplicate's DateTCreated will also be 0001-01-01 00:00:00. This is
            because this column is used for altering text fields' positions in PDFs.

column.name: RevID
column.summary: Revision ID. Gets updated any time a sheet field is added or deleted from a sheetdef (this includes any time a new language is added) or a static text field is changed. Used to determine in conjunction with PrefillMode for eClipboard to determine whether to show a patient a new form or have them update their last filled out form. Must match up with Sheet RevID to show a filled out form.

column.name: AutoCheckSaveImage
column.summary: Indicates whether sheets created with this sheet def get copied to the imaging module when changes are made and saved. It's badly named since we don't use a checkbox anymore.

column.name: AutoCheckSaveImageDocCategory
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. Used to override the category that is selected when auto saving the sheet to the imaging module.  This allows users to choose the category that a sheet is saved to on a per sheet basis.

table.name: sheetfield
table.summary: One field on a sheet. Any language translations have already happened. See SheetFieldDef. How Exam Sheet replacement fields work: Fields can be placed in a static text field on a PatientLetter, ReferralSlip, or ReferralLetter. 1. For fields with FieldName (not misc), format is [ExamSheet:ExamSheetName;FieldName]. 2. For misc radiobuttons, format is [ExamSheet:ExamSheetName;RadioButtonGroupName]. 3. For other fields, format is [ExamSheet:ExamSheetName;ReportableName].

column.name: SheetFieldNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SheetNum
column.summary: FK to sheet.SheetNum.

column.name: FieldType
column.summary: Enum:SheetFieldType  OutputText, InputField, StaticText,Parameter(only used for SheetField, not SheetFieldDef),Image,Drawing,Line,Rectangle,CheckBox,SigBox,PatImage,Grid, etc.

EnumValue._: 0-Pulled from the database to be printed on the sheet.  Or also possibly just generated at runtime even though not pulled from the database.   User still allowed to change the output text as they are filling out the sheet so that it can different from what was initially generated.
EnumValue.name: OutputText

EnumValue._: 1-A blank box that the user is supposed to fill in.
EnumValue.name: InputField

EnumValue._: 2-This is text that is defined as part of the sheet and will never change from sheet to sheet.
EnumValue.name: StaticText

EnumValue._: 3-Stores a parameter other than the PatNum.  Not meant to be seen on the sheet.  Only used for SheetField, not SheetFieldDef.
EnumValue.name: Parameter

EnumValue._: 4-Any image of any size, typically a background image for a form.
EnumValue.name: Image

EnumValue._: 5-One sequence of dots that makes a line.  Continuous without any breaks.  Each time the pen is picked up, it creates a new field row in the database.
EnumValue.name: Drawing

EnumValue._: 6-A simple line drawn from x,y to x+width,y+height.  So for these types, we must allow width and height to be negative or zero.
EnumValue.name: Line

EnumValue._: 7-A simple rectangle outline.
EnumValue.name: Rectangle

EnumValue._: 8-A clickable area on the screen.  It's a form of input, so treated similarly to an InputField.  The X will go from corner to corner of the rectangle specified.  It can also behave like a radio button
EnumValue.name: CheckBox

EnumValue._:
9-A signature box, either Topaz pad or directly on the screen with stylus/mouse.  The signature is encrypted based an a hash of all
            other field values in the entire sheet, excluding other SigBoxes.  The order is critical.
EnumValue.name: SigBox

EnumValue._: 10-An image specific to one patient.
EnumValue.name: PatImage

EnumValue._: 11-Special: Used for ToothChart, ToothChartLegend, and 3 more chart module items.
EnumValue.name: Special

EnumValue._: 12-Grid: Placeable grids similar to ODGrids. Used primarily in statements.
EnumValue.name: Grid

EnumValue._: 13-ComboBox: Placeable combo box for selecting filled options.
EnumValue.name: ComboBox

EnumValue._: 14-ScreenChart: A tooth chart that is desiged for screenings.
EnumValue.name: ScreenChart

EnumValue._:
15-MobileHeader: The parent field of a group of fields. All fields in between this field and the next MobileHeader will be grouped toghether in the mobile view.
            EG... "Personal", "Address and Home Phone", "Insurance".
EnumValue.name: MobileHeader

EnumValue._:
16-A signature box, either Topaz pad or directly on the screen with stylus/mouse.  The signature is encrypted based an a hash of all 
            other field values in the entire sheet, excluding other SigBoxes.  The order is critical.
EnumValue.name: SigBoxPractice

column.name: FieldValue
column.summary:
For OutputText, this value is set before printing.  This is the data obtained from the database and ready to print.  For StaticText, this is copied from the sheetFieldDef, but in-line fields like [this] will have been filled.  For an archived sheet retrieved from the database (all SheetField rows), this value will have been saved and will not be filled again automatically.For InputField, this is the filled value. If an an AutoNote attached, then this field will start out like AutoNoteNum:### until they click to fill out the autonote.Parameter fieldtype: this will store the value of the parameter. FKs are numbers. Two of the parameters allow multiple ProcNums, which are stored here as comma list.Drawing fieldtype: this will be the point data for the lines.  The format would look similar to this: 45,68;48,70;49,72;0,0;55,88;etc.  It's simply a sequence of points, separated by semicolons.CheckBox: it will either be an X or empty.SigBox: the first char will be 0 or 1 to indicate SigIsTopaz, and all subsequent chars will be the Signature itself.PatImage: FK to document.DocNum, or blank, or "MountNum:####" to indicate mount instead of document.ComboBox: The chosen option, semicolon, then a pipe delimited list of options such as: March;January|February|March|AprilScreenChart: Contains a semicolon delimited list of a single number followed by groups of comma separated surfaces.
            The first digit represents what type of ScreenChart it is.  0 = Permanent, 1 = Primary
            It may look like 0;S,P,N;S,S,S;... etc.Grid: Not used. All grid content is generated on the fly rather than preserved in the database. In the future, we should serialize grid content into here. But we probably have to overhaul the language translation for grids first.

column.name: FontSize
column.summary: The fontSize for this field regardless of the default for the sheet.  The actual font must be saved with each sheetField.

column.name: FontName
column.summary: The fontName for this field regardless of the default for the sheet.  The actual font must be saved with each sheetField.

column.name: XPos
column.summary: In pixels.

column.name: YPos
column.summary: In pixels.

column.name: Width
column.summary: The field will be constrained horizontally to this size.  Not allowed to be zero.

column.name: Height
column.summary: The field will be constrained vertically to this size.  Not allowed to be stored as 0.  It's not allowed to be zero so that it will be visible on the designer. Set to 0 in memory by SheetUtil.CalculateHeights if image is innacessible for printing.

column.name: GrowthBehavior
column.summary: Enum:GrowthBehaviorEnum

Enumeration.name: GrowthBehaviorEnum
Enumeration.summary: For sheetFields

EnumValue._: Not allowed to grow.  Max size would be Height and Width.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: Can grow down if needed, and will push nearby objects out of the way so that there is no overlap.
EnumValue.name: DownLocal

EnumValue._: Can grow down, and will push down all objects on the sheet that are below it.  Mostly used when drawing grids.
EnumValue.name: DownGlobal

EnumValue._: Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill to the right and bottom of the parent control, does not check for overlap.
EnumValue.name: FillRightDown

EnumValue._: Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill to the bottom of the parent control, does not check for overlap.
EnumValue.name: FillDown

EnumValue._: Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill to the right of the parent control, does not check for overlap.
EnumValue.name: FillRight

EnumValue._:
Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill vertical space in parent control and fit grid width to include all columns.
            Primarily for ProgressNotes grid in Chart Module.
EnumValue.name: FillDownFitColumns

column.name: RadioButtonValue
column.summary: This is only used for checkboxes that you want to behave like radiobuttons.  Set the FieldName the same for each Checkbox in the group.  The FieldValue will likely be X for one of them and empty string for the others.  Each of them will have a different RadioButtonValue.  Whichever box has X, the RadioButtonValue for that box will be used when importing.  This field is not used for "misc" radiobutton groups.

column.name: RadioButtonGroup
column.summary: Only used for radiobuttons with FieldName set to "misc". Name which identifies the group within which the radio button belongs.

column.name: IsRequired
column.summary: Set to true if this field is required to have a value before the sheet is closed.

column.name: TabOrder
column.summary: Tab stop order for all fields. Only checkboxes and input fields can have values other than 0.

column.name: ReportableName
column.summary: Allows reporting on misc fields using queries. See summary of this table for explanation of how they can be used in Exam Sheet replacement fields.

column.name: TextAlign
column.summary: Text Alignment for text fields.

column.name: ItemColor
column.summary: Text color, line color, rectangle color. -16777216 is Black. 0 means Empty, which we attempt to treat as black if it happens.

column.name: DateTimeSig
column.summary: DateTime that a sheet was signed.

column.name: IsLocked
column.summary: Only used in Output and Static fields. Gets locked in the setup and cannot be edited at all once it is in the SheetFillEdit window. Example might be for text of a consent form.

column.name: TabOrderMobile
column.summary:
Tab stop order for all fields of a mobile sheet. One-based.  Only mobile fields can have values other than 0.
            If all SheetFieldDefs for a given SheetField are 0 then assume that this sheet has no mobile-specific view.

column.name: UiLabelMobile
column.summary: Each input field for a mobile will need a corresponding UI label. This is what the user sees as the label describing what this input is for. EG "First Name:, Last Name:, Address, etc." For check boxes, this field should be blank otherwise a group box will be displayed to the user with this text. For radio buttons, this field should be set to the text of the group caption.

column.name: UiLabelMobileRadioButton
column.summary: Human-readable label that will be displayed for radio button or checkbox item.  Cannot use UiLabelMobile for this purpose as it is already dedicated to the radio group header that groups radio button items together. This is also used in Exam Sheet replacement fields as the value to show for checkboxes and radiobuttons.

column.name: SheetFieldDefNum
column.summary: FK to sheetfielddef.SheetFieldDefNum. Only used in the Patient Forms window when the Prefill button is clicked.

column.name: CanElectronicallySign
column.summary: When true, allows a user to sign a signature box electronically.  ESign/DigitallySign means clicking instead of using a stylus to write out the sig. If IsSigProvRestricted, then the E button for digitally signing won't be visible unless a provider is signed in. There is a completely separate Pref.SignatureAllowDigital which only applies to signing procedures etc., not sheets.

column.name: IsSigProvRestricted
column.summary: When true, only allows the signature box to be signed by a provider. There is a completely separate Pref.NotesProviderSignatureOnly for doing the same thing in procedures etc., not sheets.

table.name: sheetfielddef
table.summary: One field on a sheetDef. Language translations are handled by the LanguagePat table. See SheetField. How Exam Sheet replacement fields work: Fields can be placed in a static text field on a PatientLetter, ReferralSlip, or ReferralLetter. 1. For fields with FieldName (not misc), format is [ExamSheet:ExamSheetName;FieldName]. 2. For misc radiobuttons, format is [ExamSheet:ExamSheetName;RadioButtonGroupName]. 3. For other fields, format is [ExamSheet:ExamSheetName;ReportableName].

column.name: SheetFieldDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SheetDefNum
column.summary: FK to sheetdef.SheetDefNum.

column.name: FieldType
column.summary: Enum:SheetFieldType  OutputText, InputField, StaticText,Parameter(only used for SheetField, not SheetFieldDef),Image,Drawing,Line,Rectangle,CheckBox,SigBox,PatImage,Grid, etc.

EnumValue._: 0-Pulled from the database to be printed on the sheet.  Or also possibly just generated at runtime even though not pulled from the database.   User still allowed to change the output text as they are filling out the sheet so that it can different from what was initially generated.
EnumValue.name: OutputText

EnumValue._: 1-A blank box that the user is supposed to fill in.
EnumValue.name: InputField

EnumValue._: 2-This is text that is defined as part of the sheet and will never change from sheet to sheet.
EnumValue.name: StaticText

EnumValue._: 3-Stores a parameter other than the PatNum.  Not meant to be seen on the sheet.  Only used for SheetField, not SheetFieldDef.
EnumValue.name: Parameter

EnumValue._: 4-Any image of any size, typically a background image for a form.
EnumValue.name: Image

EnumValue._: 5-One sequence of dots that makes a line.  Continuous without any breaks.  Each time the pen is picked up, it creates a new field row in the database.
EnumValue.name: Drawing

EnumValue._: 6-A simple line drawn from x,y to x+width,y+height.  So for these types, we must allow width and height to be negative or zero.
EnumValue.name: Line

EnumValue._: 7-A simple rectangle outline.
EnumValue.name: Rectangle

EnumValue._: 8-A clickable area on the screen.  It's a form of input, so treated similarly to an InputField.  The X will go from corner to corner of the rectangle specified.  It can also behave like a radio button
EnumValue.name: CheckBox

EnumValue._:
9-A signature box, either Topaz pad or directly on the screen with stylus/mouse.  The signature is encrypted based an a hash of all
            other field values in the entire sheet, excluding other SigBoxes.  The order is critical.
EnumValue.name: SigBox

EnumValue._: 10-An image specific to one patient.
EnumValue.name: PatImage

EnumValue._: 11-Special: Used for ToothChart, ToothChartLegend, and 3 more chart module items.
EnumValue.name: Special

EnumValue._: 12-Grid: Placeable grids similar to ODGrids. Used primarily in statements.
EnumValue.name: Grid

EnumValue._: 13-ComboBox: Placeable combo box for selecting filled options.
EnumValue.name: ComboBox

EnumValue._: 14-ScreenChart: A tooth chart that is desiged for screenings.
EnumValue.name: ScreenChart

EnumValue._:
15-MobileHeader: The parent field of a group of fields. All fields in between this field and the next MobileHeader will be grouped toghether in the mobile view.
            EG... "Personal", "Address and Home Phone", "Insurance".
EnumValue.name: MobileHeader

EnumValue._:
16-A signature box, either Topaz pad or directly on the screen with stylus/mouse.  The signature is encrypted based an a hash of all 
            other field values in the entire sheet, excluding other SigBoxes.  The order is critical.
EnumValue.name: SigBoxPractice

column.name: FieldName
column.summary:
FieldName is used differently for different FieldTypes. 
            For OutputText, each sheet typically has a main datatable type. For example statements correspond to the statment table. See SheetFieldsAvailable.GetList() for available values.     If the output field exactly matches a column from the main table this will be the <ColumnName>. For example, "FName" on patient Forms.     If the output field exactly matches a column from a different table this will be the <tablename>.<ColumnName>. For example, appt.Note on Routing Slips.     If the output field is not a database column it must start with a lowercase letter. For example, "statementReceiptInvoice" on Statements.For InputField, these are hardcoded to correspond to DB fields, for example "FName" corresponsds to patient.FName. See SheetFieldsAvailable.GetList() for available values.For Image, this file name with extention, for example "image1.jpg". Some image names are handled specially, for example "Patient Info.gif". Images are stored in <imagefolder>\SheetImages\image1.jpg.For CheckBox, this groups checkboxes together so that only one per group can be checked.For PatImage, this is the name of the DocCategory.For Special, identifies the type of special field. Currently only ToothChart and ToothChartLegend.For Grid, this is the specific type of grid. See SheetUtil.GetDataTableForGridType() for values. For example "StatementPayPlan". Column names use hard coded display fields that can be found in SheetUtil.GetGridColumnAvailable().For all other fieldtypes, FieldName is blank or irrelevant.

column.name: FieldValue
column.summary:
For StaticText, this text can include bracketed fields, like [nameLF].
            For OutputText and InputField, this will be blank.  If an InputField has an AutoNote attached, then this field will be AutoNoteNum:'value'. Example AutoNoteNum:268.For CheckBoxes, either X or blank.  Even if the checkbox is set to behave like a radio button.  For Pat Images, this is blank.  The filename of a PatImage will later be stored in SheetField.FieldValue.For ComboBoxes, the chosen option, semicolon, then a pipe delimited list of options such as: March;January|February|March|AprilFor ScreenCharts, a semicolon delimited list of comma separated surfaces.  It may look like S,P,N;S,S,S;... etc.For Grid, not used. All grid content is generated on the fly rather than preserved in the database. In the future, we should serialize grid content into here. But we probably have to overhaul the language translation for grids first.

column.name: FontSize
column.summary: The fontSize for this field regardless of the default for the sheet.  The actual font must be saved with each sheetField.

column.name: FontName
column.summary: The fontName for this field regardless of the default for the sheet.  The actual font must be saved with each sheetField.

column.name: XPos
column.summary: In pixels.

column.name: YPos
column.summary: In pixels.

column.name: Width
column.summary:
The field will be constrained horizontally to this size.  Not allowed to be zero.
            When SheetType is associated to a dynamic layout def and GrowthBehavior is set to a dynamic value this value represents the corresponding controls minimum width.

column.name: Height
column.summary:
The field will be constrained vertically to this size.  Not allowed to be 0.  It's not allowed to be zero so that it will be visible on the designer.
            When SheetType is associated to a dynamic layout def and GrowthBehavior is set to a dynamic value this value represents the corresponding controls minimum height.

column.name: GrowthBehavior
column.summary: Enum:GrowthBehaviorEnum

Enumeration.name: GrowthBehaviorEnum
Enumeration.summary: For sheetFields

EnumValue._: Not allowed to grow.  Max size would be Height and Width.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: Can grow down if needed, and will push nearby objects out of the way so that there is no overlap.
EnumValue.name: DownLocal

EnumValue._: Can grow down, and will push down all objects on the sheet that are below it.  Mostly used when drawing grids.
EnumValue.name: DownGlobal

EnumValue._: Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill to the right and bottom of the parent control, does not check for overlap.
EnumValue.name: FillRightDown

EnumValue._: Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill to the bottom of the parent control, does not check for overlap.
EnumValue.name: FillDown

EnumValue._: Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill to the right of the parent control, does not check for overlap.
EnumValue.name: FillRight

EnumValue._:
Used with dynamic grids to grow the grid to fill vertical space in parent control and fit grid width to include all columns.
            Primarily for ProgressNotes grid in Chart Module.
EnumValue.name: FillDownFitColumns

column.name: RadioButtonValue
column.summary: This is only used for checkboxes that you want to behave like radiobuttons.  Set the FieldName the same for each Checkbox in the group.  The FieldValue will likely be X for one of them and empty string for the others.  Each of them will have a different RadioButtonValue.  Whichever box has X, the RadioButtonValue for that box will be used when importing.  This field is not used for "misc" radiobutton groups.

column.name: RadioButtonGroup
column.summary: Only used for radiobuttons with FieldName set to "misc". Name which identifies the group within which the radio button belongs.

column.name: IsRequired
column.summary: Set to true if this field is required to have a value before the sheet is closed.

column.name: TabOrder
column.summary: Tab stop order for all fields. One-based.  Only checkboxes and input fields can have values other than 0.

column.name: ReportableName
column.summary: Allows reporting on misc fields using queries. See summary of this table for explanation of how they can be used in Exam Sheet replacement fields.

column.name: TextAlign
column.summary: Text Alignment for text fields.

column.name: IsPaymentOption
column.summary: Used to determine if the field should be hidden when printing statements.

column.name: ItemColor
column.summary: Text color, line color, rectangle color.

column.name: IsLocked
column.summary: Only used in Output and Static fields. Gets locked in the setup and cannot be edited at all once it is in the SheetFillEdit window. Example might be for text of a consent form.

column.name: TabOrderMobile
column.summary: Tab stop order for all fields of a mobile sheet. One-based.  Only mobile fields can have values other than 0. If all SheetFieldDefs for a given SheetField are 0 then assume that this sheet has no mobile-specific view.

column.name: UiLabelMobile
column.summary: Each input field for a mobile will need a corresponding UI label. This is what the user sees as the label describing what this input is for. EG "First Name:, Last Name:, Address, etc." For check boxes, this field should be blank otherwise a group box will be displayed to the user with this text. For radio buttons, this field should be set to the text of the group caption.

column.name: UiLabelMobileRadioButton
column.summary: Human-readable label that will be displayed for radio button or checkbox item.  Cannot use UiLabelMobile for this purpose as it is already dedicated to the radio group header that groups radio button items together. This is also used in Exam Sheet replacement fields as the value to show for checkboxes and radiobuttons.

column.name: LayoutMode

Enumeration.name: SheetFieldLayoutMode

EnumValue._: Valid for every SheetTypeEnum. When SheetTypeEnum is associated to a dynamic layout this is the way the layout will show by default.

EnumValue._: Chart module dynamic layout when ECW is enabled.

EnumValue.name: EcwTreatPlan

EnumValue._: Deprecated. Chart module dynamic layout when Orion is enabled.
EnumValue.name: Orion

column.name: Language
column.summary: Blank by default. When set, patient.Language will attempt to match to SheetFieldDefs with a matching Language value.

column.name: CanElectronicallySign
column.summary: When true, allows a user to sign a signature box electronically which means clicking instead of using a stylus to write out the sig. If IsSigProvRestricted, then the E button for digitally signing won't be visible unless a provider is signed in. There is a completely separate Pref.SignatureAllowDigital which only applies to signing procedures etc., not sheets.

column.name: IsSigProvRestricted
column.summary: When true, only allows the signature box to be signed by a provider. There is a completely separate Pref.NotesProviderSignatureOnly for doing the same thing in procedures etc., not sheets.

table.name: sigelementdef
table.summary:
This defines the items that will be available for clicking when composing a manual message.
            Also, these are referred to in the button definitions as a sequence of elements.

column.name: SigElementDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: LightRow
column.summary: If this element should cause a button to light up, this would be the row.  0 means none.

column.name: LightColor
column.summary:
If a light row is set, this is the color it will turn when triggered.  Ack sets it back to white.
            Note that color and row can be in two separate elements of the same signal.

column.name: SigElementType

Enumeration.summary: 0=User,1=Extra,2=Message.

EnumValue._: 0-To and From lists.  Not tied in any way to the users that are part of security.

EnumValue._: Typically used to insert "family" before "phone" signals.
EnumValue.name: Extra

EnumValue._: Elements of this type show in the last column and trigger the message to be sent.

column.name: SigText
column.summary: The text that shows for the element, like the user name or the two word message.  No long text is stored here.

column.name: Sound
column.summary: The sound to play for this element.  Wav file stored in the database in string format until "played".  If empty string, then no sound.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The order of this element within the list of the same type.

column.name: ToUser

table.name: signalod
table.summary: Open Dental uses a memory cache for many common small tables that don't change very often. If one computer makes a change to a cache table, then they insert a row in this table to indicate to all other computers that they need to update their cache for the given table. Certain entries in this table can also trigger the Appointments module to refresh if that date is currently showing for any computer. To add a row here, use DataValid.SetInvalid. See the discussion in Cache.cs.

column.name: SignalNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateViewing
column.summary: If IType=Date, then this is the affected date in the Appointments module.

column.name: SigDateTime
column.summary:
The exact server time when this signal was entered into db.
            This does not need to be set by sender since it's handled automatically.

column.name: FKey
column.summary:
Usually identifies the object that was edited to cause the signal to be created.
            Can be used for special scenarios based on the FKeyType.  E.g. for SmsMsgUnreadCount, this represents a count, not an FK.

column.name: FKeyType
column.summary: Enum:KeyType Describes the type of object referenced by the FKey.

Enumeration.name: KeyType
Enumeration.summary: Do not combine with SignalType, they must be seperate. Stored as string, safe to reorder enum values.

EnumValue._: Probably represented by empty string, but possibly by "Undefined".
EnumValue.name: Undefined

EnumValue._:
HQ only.  FKey will be the extension of the corresponding phone that is invalid.
            Specifically used to talk to the PhoneTrackingServer in order to let it know that an extension has changed (e.g. queue change).
EnumValue.name: PhoneExtension

EnumValue._:
Special KeyType that does not use a FK but instead will set FKey to a count of unread messages.
            Used along side the SmsTextMsgReceivedUnreadCount InvalidType.
EnumValue.name: SmsMsgUnreadCount

EnumValue._:
Used to identify which signals a form can ignore.  If the FKey==Process.GetCurrentProcess().Id then this process sent it so ignore
            it.  Used in FormTerminal, FormTerminalManager, and FormSheetFillEdit (for forms being filled at a kiosk).
EnumValue.name: ProcessId

table.name: smsblockphone
table.summary: If a number is entered in this table, then any incoming text message will not be entered into the database.

column.name: SmsBlockPhoneNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: BlockWirelessNumber
column.summary: The phone number to be blocked.

table.name: smsfrommobile
table.summary: A Mobile Originating SMS bound for the office. Will usually be a re-constructed message.

column.name: SmsFromMobileNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum. Not sent from HQ.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.name: CommlogNum
column.summary: FK to commlog.CommlogNum. Not sent from HQ.

column.name: MsgText
column.summary: Contents of the message.

column.name: DateTimeReceived
column.summary: Date and time message was inserted into the DB. Not sent from HQ.

column.name: SmsPhoneNumber
column.summary: This is the Phone Number of the office that the mobile device sent a message to.

column.name: MobilePhoneNumber
column.summary: This is the PhoneNumber that this message was sent from.

column.name: MsgPart
column.summary:
Message part sequence number. For single part messages this should always be 1. 
            For messages that exist as multiple parts, due to staggered delivery of the parts, this will be a number between 1 and MsgTotal.

column.name: MsgTotal
column.summary:
Total count of message parts for this single message identified by MsgRefID.
            For single part messages this should always be 1.

column.name: MsgRefID
column.summary: Each part of a multipart message will have the same MsgRefID.

column.name: SmsStatus
column.summary: Enum:SmsFromStatus .

Enumeration.name: SmsFromStatus
Enumeration.summary: Status of an incoming message.

column.name: Flags
column.summary:
Words surrounded by spaces, flags should be all lower case. This allows simple querrying. Example: " junk  recall " allows you to 
            write "WHERE Flags like "% junk %" without having to worry about commas. Also, adding and removing tags is easier. Example: Flags=Flags.Replace(" junk ","");

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Messages are not deleted, they can only be hidden.

column.name: GuidMessage
column.summary: FK to confirmationrequest.GuidMessageFromMobile. Generated at HQ when the confirmation pending is terminated with confirmation text message.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

table.name: smsphone
table.summary:
A phone number used to send and receive SMS.
            When clinics is enabled all SmsPhones with clinic num 0 should be updated to have clinic num of the lowest numbered clinic.
            When clinics are disabled, all SmsPhones with the lowest numbered clinic num should be re-associated to clinic number 0.

column.name: SmsPhoneNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.name: PhoneNumber
column.summary: String representation of the phone number in international format. Ex: 15035551234 This field should not contain any formatting characters.

column.name: DateTimeActive
column.summary: Date and time this phone number became active.

column.name: DateTimeInactive
column.summary: Date and time this phone number became inactive. Once inactive, the phone is dead and cannot be reactivated. A new number will have to be purchased.

column.name: InactiveCode
column.summary: Used to indicate why this phone number was made inactive.

column.name: CountryCode
column.summary: Country linked to this phone's clinic at the instant that this phone is created. Based on ISO31661.

table.name: smstomobile
table.summary: Messages are only inserted into this table after they are accepted by ODHQ.

column.name: SmsToMobileNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: GuidMessage
column.summary: GUID. Uniquely identifies this message and is used for tracking message status.

column.name: GuidBatch
column.summary: GUID. When sending batch messages, all messages will have the same batch GUID that should be the GUID of the first message within the batch.

column.name: SmsPhoneNumber
column.summary: This is the sending phone number in international format. Each office may have several different numbers that they use.

column.name: MobilePhoneNumber
column.summary: The phone number that this message was sent to. Must be kept in addition to the PatNum.

column.name: IsTimeSensitive
column.summary: Set to true if this message should "jump the queue" and be sent asap.

column.name: MsgType

Enumeration.name: SmsMessageSource

EnumValue._:
5. Used when sending confirmations.
            Short Code Supported: YES

EnumValue._:
6. Used when sending confirmation requests. Will be the subject of automated response processing.
            Short Code Supported: YES

EnumValue._:
7. Used when sending batch recall SMS from the eConnector.
            Short Code Supported: YES
EnumValue.name: RecallAuto

EnumValue._:
8. Used when sending single or batch SMS from the clicking the Text button on the ASAP window.
            Short Code Supported: YES

EnumValue._:
9. Sending an SMS for the Web Sched ASAP feature.
            Short Code Supported: YES
EnumValue.name: WebSchedASAP

EnumValue._:
10. Sending an SMS for the Web Sched verify feature.
            Short Code Supported: YES
EnumValue.name: Verify

EnumValue._:
11. Sending an SMS to let the patient know that a statement is available.
            Short Code Supported: YES
EnumValue.name: Statements

EnumValue._:
12. Sending an SMS to let the patient know that a statement is available.
            Short Code Supported: YES
EnumValue.name: VerifyWSNP

EnumValue._:
13. Sent from the Headmaster app.
            Short Code Supported: NO
EnumValue.name: Headmaster

EnumValue._:
14. Used with Short Codes to send an AutoReply message (not a monitored line).
            Short Code Supported: N/A
EnumValue.name: NoReply

EnumValue._:
15. Send from ODMobile app.
            Short Code Supported: NO

EnumValue._:
16. Used when sending appointment/schedule Thank You's.
            Short Code Supported: YES

17. Used when the patient responds positively to an eConfirmation.

            Short Code Supported: YES

20. Used with Short Codes to send a message detailing help options for the patient.
            Short Code Supported: YES
EnumValue.name: HelpReply

21. Used with Short Codes to send a message confirming the patient has opted in to receiving Short Code sms.
            Short Code Supported: YES
EnumValue.name: OptInReply

EnumValue.name: GeneralMessage

26. Used when sending verification codes for the Payment Portal web app.

27. Used for Payment Portal Msg-To-Pay messages.
            Short Code Supported: NO
EnumValue.name: MsgToPay

28. Used when the office checks NO for the patient to receive texts in the patient edit form.

EnumValue.name: OptOutReply

column.name: MsgText
column.summary: The contents of the message.

column.name: SmsStatus
column.summary: Enum:SmsDeliveryStatus  Set by the Listener, tracks status of SMS.

Enumeration.name: SmsDeliveryStatus
Enumeration.summary: None should never be used, the code should be re-written to not use it.

EnumValue._: 0. Should not be used.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1. After a message has been accepted at ODHQ. Before any feedback.
EnumValue.name: Pending

EnumValue._: 2. Delivered to customer, carrier replied with confirmation.
EnumValue.name: DeliveryConf

EnumValue._: 3. Delivered to customer, no confirmation of failure or delivery sent back from carrier.
EnumValue.name: DeliveryUnconf

EnumValue._: 4. Attempted delivery, failure message return after arriving at handset.
EnumValue.name: FailWithCharge

EnumValue._: 5. Attempted delivery, immediate failure confirmation received from carrier.
EnumValue.name: FailNoCharge

column.name: MsgParts
column.summary:
The count of parts that this message will be broken into when sent.
            A single long message will be broken into several smaller 153 utf8 or 70 unicode character messages.

column.name: MsgChargeUSD
column.summary: The amount charged to the customer. Total cost for this message always stored in US Dollars.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  0 when not using clinics.

column.name: CustErrorText
column.summary: Only used when SmsDeliveryStatus==Failed.

column.name: DateTimeSent
column.summary: Time message was accepted at ODHQ.

column.name: DateTimeTerminated
column.summary: Date time that the message was either successfully delivered or failed.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Messages are hidden, not deleted.

column.name: MsgDiscountUSD
column.summary:
Any discount applied to this message. 
            If a particular messages has a MsgDiscountUSD > 0  then the MsgChargeUSD will reflect the charge to the customer after the discount has already been applied. 
            Multi-part messages will still be charged the wholesale rate for all parts after the first part.
            To calculate the typical charge that this customer would pay without the discount use MsgChargeUSD + MsgDiscountUSD.
            To calculate the percentage discounted off standard charges use (MsgDiscountUSD / (MsgChargeUSD + MsgDiscountUSD)).

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

table.name: snomed
table.summary: We do not import synonyms, only "Fully Specified Name records". Snomed for holding a large list of codes. Codes in use are copied into the DiseaseDef table.  SNOMED CT maintained, owned and copyright International Health Terminology Standards Development Organisation (IHTSDO).

column.name: SnomedNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SnomedCode
column.summary: Used as FK by other tables.  Also called the Concept ID.  Not allowed to edit this column once saved in the database.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Also called "Term", "Name", or "Fully Specified Name".  Not editable and doesn't change.

table.name: stateabbr
table.summary:
State abbreviations are always copied to patient records rather than linked.  
            Items in this list can be freely altered or deleted without harming patient data.

column.name: StateAbbrNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Description
column.summary: Full state name

column.name: Abbr
column.summary: Short state abbreviation (usually 2 digit)

column.name: MedicaidIDLength
column.summary: The length that the Medicaid ID should be for this state. If 0, then the Medicaid length is not enforced for this state

table.name: statement
table.summary: Represents one statement for one family.  Usually already sent, but could still be waiting to send.

column.name: StatementNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum. Typically the guarantor.  Can also be the patient for walkout statements.

column.name: DateSent
column.summary: This will always be a valid and reasonable date regardless of whether it's actually been sent yet.

column.name: DateRangeFrom
column.summary: Typically 45 days before dateSent

column.name: DateRangeTo
column.summary: Any date >= year 2200 is considered max val.  We generally try to automate this value to be the same date as the statement rather than the max val.  This is so that when payment plans are displayed, we can add approximately 10 days to effectively show the charge that will soon be due.  Adding the 10 days is not done until display time.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Can include line breaks.  This ordinary note will be in the standard font.

column.name: NoteBold
column.summary: More important notes may go here.  Font will be bold.  Color and size of text will be customizable in setup.

column.name: Mode_
column.summary: Enum:StatementMode Mail, InPerson, Email, Electronic.

Enumeration.name: StatementMode
Enumeration.summary: 0-Mail, 1-InPerson, 2-Email, 3-Electronic

EnumValue._: 3-Send the statement over the internet to an API. That company then sends the actual statement, usually by paper.
EnumValue.name: Electronic

column.name: HidePayment
column.summary: Set true to hide the credit card section, and the please pay box.

column.name: SinglePatient
column.summary: One patient on statement instead of entire family.

column.name: Intermingled
column.summary: If entire family, then this determines whether they are all intermingled into one big grid, or whether they are all listed in separate grids.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: FK to document.DocNum when a pdf has been archived.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: Date/time last altered.

column.name: IsReceipt
column.summary: The only effect of this flag is to change the text at the top of a statement from "statement" to "receipt".  It might later do more.

column.name: IsInvoice
column.summary: This flag is for marking a statement as Invoice.  In this case, it must have procedures and/or adjustments attached.

column.name: IsInvoiceCopy
column.summary: Only used if IsInvoice=true.  The first printout will not be a copy.  Subsequent printouts will show "copy" on them.

column.name: EmailSubject
column.summary: Empty string by default.  Only used to override BillingEmailSubject pref when emailing statements.  Only set when statements are created from the Billing Options window.  No UI for editing.

column.name: EmailBody
column.summary: Empty string by default.  Only used to override BillingEmailBodyText pref when emailing statements.  Only set when statements are created from the Billing Options window.  No UI for editing.  Limit in db: 16M char.

column.name: SuperFamily
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum.  Typically zero unless a super family statement is desired.
            Will be non-zero if the patient is associated with a super family and a super family statement is desired.

column.name: IsBalValid
column.summary: True for statements generated in version 16.1 or greater. Older statements did not store InsEst or BalTotal.

column.name: InsEst
column.summary: Insurance Estimate for entire family, taken from guarantor at time of statement being sent/saved. For invoices, this field contains the total adjustment amount instead.

column.name: BalTotal
column.summary:
Total balance for entire family before insurance estimate.  
            Not the same as the sum of the 4 aging balances because this can be negative.

column.name: StatementType
column.summary: Enum:StmtType Statement, Receipt, Invoice, LimitedStatement.

Enumeration.name: StmtType
Enumeration.summary: The type of this statement.  This will eventually replace IsReceipt and IsInvoice. Stored as EnumAsString.

EnumValue._: Regular statement.
EnumValue.name: NotSet

EnumValue._: Contains information about specific procedures.
EnumValue.name: LimitedStatement

column.name: ShortGUID
column.summary: A short alphanumeric string used to uniquely identify this statement.

column.name: StatementShortURL
column.summary: A short URL that can be visited to view this statement. Useful to include in text messages.

column.name: StatementURL
column.summary: A URL that can be visited to view this statement.

column.name: SmsSendStatus
column.summary: Enum:AutoCommStatus Stores what should be done or was done in regards to SMS messaging for this statement.

Enumeration.name: AutoCommStatus
Enumeration.summary: Used by both Statement and WebSchedRecall (and probably other places).

EnumValue._: 0 - Should not be in the database but can be used in the program.
EnumValue.name: Undefined

EnumValue._: 1 - Do not send a reminder.
EnumValue.name: DoNotSend

EnumValue._: 2 - We will send, but send has not been attempted yet.
EnumValue.name: SendNotAttempted

EnumValue._: 3 - Has been sent successfully.
EnumValue.name: SendSuccessful

EnumValue._: 4 - Attempted to send but not successful.
EnumValue.name: SendFailed

EnumValue._: 5 - Has been sent successfully, awaiting receipt.
EnumValue.name: SentAwaitingReceipt

column.name: LimitedCustomFamily
column.summary: Enum:EnumLimitedCustomFamily Indicates the scope of a custom limited statement. Special behavior in getSuperFamAccount for Family and SuperFamily.

Enumeration.name: EnumLimitedCustomFamily
Enumeration.summary: Indicates the scope of a limited custom statement. Special logic in AccountModules.GetSuperFamAccount when set

EnumValue._: None=0
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: Patient=1
EnumValue.name: Patient

EnumValue._: Family=2
EnumValue.name: Family

EnumValue._: SuperFamily=3
EnumValue.name: SuperFamily

table.name: statementprod
table.summary: Links production items to a statement. Also tracks whether or not a late charge adjustment has been created for the production item.

column.name: StatementProdNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: StatementNum
column.summary: FK to statement.StatementNum. The statement that the production item is on.

column.name: FKey
column.summary: Foreign key to linked production item.

column.name: ProdType
column.summary: Enum:ProductionType Type of production item.

EnumValue._: 0 - Procedure
EnumValue.name: Procedure

EnumValue._: 1 - Adjustment
EnumValue.name: Adjustment

EnumValue._: 2 - PayPlanCharge
EnumValue.name: PayPlanCharge

column.name: LateChargeAdjNum
column.summary: FK to adjustment.AdjNum. The late charge adjustment made for this production item. 0 if no late charge has been made.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: FK to document.DocNum. The pdf document last associated to the statement. Will be 0 for statements that are sent electronically when pdfs are not saved for electronic statements.

table.name: stmtlink
table.summary: Attaches individual rows of Procs, Adjustments, Payments, etc to a Statement object so that we can recreate the statement again later.

column.name: StmtLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: StatementNum
column.summary: FK to statement.StatementNum.

column.name: StmtLinkType
column.summary: Enum:StmtLinkTypes Represents what object FKey corresponds to.

EnumValue._: 0 - Procedure
EnumValue.name: Proc

EnumValue._: 1 - Pay split
EnumValue.name: PaySplit

EnumValue._: 2 - Adjustment
EnumValue.name: Adj

EnumValue._: 3 - ClaimPay
EnumValue.name: ClaimPay

EnumValue._: 4 - Pay plan charge
EnumValue.name: PayPlanCharge

EnumValue._: 5 - Patient
EnumValue.name: PatNum

EnumValue._: 6 - MsgToPaySent
EnumValue.name: MsgToPaySent

column.name: FKey
column.fk: type of pk of another object depending on stmtlinktype value
column.summary: FK to type of PK of another object depending on StmtLinkType value. E.g. procedurelog.ProcNum, paysplit.PaySplitNum, adjustment.AdjNum, etc.

table.name: substitutionlink
table.summary: Entries in this table will represent procedurecodes that the insurance plan wants to SKIP when considering substitution codes.

column.name: SubstitutionLinkNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PlanNum
column.summary: FK to insplan.PlanNum.

column.name: CodeNum
column.summary: FK to procedurecode.CodeNum.

column.name: SubstitutionCode
column.summary: FK to procedurecode.ProcCode.

column.name: SubstOnlyIf
column.summary: Enum:SubstitutionCondition

Enumeration.name: SubstitutionCondition
Enumeration.summary: Used for insurance substitutions conditions of procedurecodes.  Mostly for posterior composites.

table.name: supplier
table.summary: A company that provides supplies for the office, typically dental supplies.

column.name: SupplierNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: CustomerId
column.summary: The customer ID that this office uses for transactions with the supplier

column.name: Website
column.summary: Full address to website.  We might make it clickable.

column.name: UserName
column.summary: The username used to log in to the supplier website.

column.name: Password
column.summary: The password to log in to the supplier website.  Not encrypted or hidden in any way.

column.name: Note
column.summary: Any note regarding supplier.  Could hold address, CC info, etc.

table.name: supply
table.summary: A dental supply or office supply item.

column.name: SupplyNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SupplierNum
column.summary: FK to supplier.SupplierNum

column.name: CatalogNumber
column.summary: The catalog item number that the supplier uses to identify the supply.

column.name: Descript
column.summary: The description can be similar to the catalog, but not required.  Typically includes qty per box/case, etc.

column.name: Category
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  User can define their own categories for supplies.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: The zero-based order of this supply within it's category.  Hidden supplies can be included in this order.

column.name: LevelDesired
column.summary: Aka Stock Level.  The level that a fresh order should bring item back up to.  Can include fractions.  If this is 0, then it will be displayed as having this field blank rather than showing 0.  This simply gives a cleaner look.

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: If hidden, then this supply item won't normally show in the main list.

column.name: Price
column.summary: The price per unit that the supplier charges for this supply.  If this is 0.00, then no price will be displayed.

column.name: BarCodeOrID
column.summary: Scanned code from a reader.

column.name: DispDefaultQuant
column.summary: Only used for dental schools.  This is the typical quantity dispensed at the window.

column.name: DispUnitsCount
column.summary: Only used in dental schools.  For example, 20 capsules composite per container.

column.name: DispUnitDesc
column.summary: Only used in dental schools.  Description of the units when dispensing for use.  For example: Capsule, cartridge, carpule, glove, or needle.

column.name: LevelOnHand
column.summary: Deprecated.

column.name: OrderQty
column.summary: The amount to order when the next SupplyOrder is created.  Creating a SupplyOrder then zeroes this out, so it's just a temporary value.

table.name: supplyneeded
table.summary: A supply freeform typed in by a user.

column.name: SupplyNeededNum
column.summary: Primary key.

table.name: supplyorder
table.summary: One supply order to one supplier.  Contains SupplyOrderItems.

column.name: SupplyOrderNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SupplierNum
column.summary: FK to supplier.SupplierNum.

column.name: DatePlaced
column.summary: A date greater than 2200 (eg 2500), is considered a max date.  A max date is used for an order that was started but has not yet been placed.  This puts it at the end of the list where it belongs, but it will display as blank.  Only one unplaced order is allowed per supplier.

column.name: AmountTotal
column.summary: The sum of all the amounts of each item on the order.  If any of the item prices are zero, then it won't auto calculate this total.  This will allow the user to manually put in the total without having it get deleted.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. User that placed the order, is editable.

column.name: ShippingCharge
column.summary: The order's shipping charge.

column.name: DateReceived
column.summary:
The date the order was received. If the SupplyOrder existed before updating to version 19.4, then this value will be set to the date
            the office updated to 19.4.

table.name: supplyorderitem
table.summary: One item on one supply order.  This table links supplies to orders as well as storing a small amount of additional info.

column.name: SupplyOrderItemNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SupplyOrderNum
column.summary: FK to supplyorder.supplyOrderNum.

column.name: SupplyNum
column.summary: FK to supply.SupplyNum.

column.name: Qty
column.summary: How many were ordered.

column.name: Price
column.summary: Price per unit on this order.

column.name: DateReceived
column.summary: Optional. The order itself already has this field. But if a partial order comes in, and if the user wants to track item dates separately, then they can do it here.

table.name: task
table.summary: A task is a single todo item.  Also see taskhist, which keeps a historical record.

column.name: TaskNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TaskListNum
column.summary: FK to tasklist.TaskListNum.  If 0, then it will show in the trunk of a section.  This is temporarily -1 to indicate that a task is not assigned to a tasklist yet, but -1 never gets saved to the db.

column.name: DateTask
column.summary: Only used if this task is assigned to a dated category.  Children are NOT dated.  Only dated if they should show in the trunk for a date category.  They can also have a parent if they are in the main list as well.

column.name: KeyNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum or appointment.AptNum. Only used when ObjectType is not 0.

column.name: Descript
column.summary: The description of this task.  Might be very long.

column.name: TaskStatus
column.summary: Enum:TaskStatusEnum New,Viewed,Done.  We may want to put an index on this column someday.

column.name: IsRepeating
column.summary: True if it is to show in the repeating section.  There should be no date.  All children and parents should also be set to IsRepeating=true.

column.name: DateType
column.summary: Enum:TaskDateType  None, Day, Week, Month.  If IsRepeating, then setting to None effectively disables the repeating feature.

Enumeration.name: TaskDateType
Enumeration.summary: For use by the older Repeating Tasks feature.

column.name: FromNum
column.summary: FK to task.TaskNum  If this is derived from a repeating task, then this will hold the TaskNum of that task.  It helps automate the adding and deleting of tasks.  It might be deleted automatically if not are marked complete.

column.name: ObjectType
column.summary: Enum:TaskObjectType  0=none,1=Patient,2=Appointment.  More will be added later. If a type is selected, then the KeyNum will contain the primary key of the corresponding Patient or Appointment.  Does not really have anything to do with the ObjectType of the parent tasklist, although they tend to match.

Enumeration.name: TaskObjectType
Enumeration.summary: Used when attaching objects to tasks.  These are the choices.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary:
The date and time that this task was added.  User editable.
            For reminder tasks, this field is used to indicate the date and time the reminder will take effect.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  The person who created the task.

column.name: DateTimeFinished
column.summary: The date and time that this task was marked "done".

column.name: PriorityDefNum
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum.  The priority for this task which is used when filling task lists.  The placement of the task in the list is dependent on the item order of the definitions.

column.name: ReminderGroupId
column.summary:
Optional.  Set to null or empty if not a reminder task.
            For repeating reminders, the ReminderGroupId will be the same for each task spawned from any task in the group.

column.name: ReminderType
column.summary: Bit field.

column.name: DateTimeOriginal
column.summary:
The original datetime that the row was inserted.  Used to sort the list by the order entered.
            Using taskhist.DateTimeOriginal will get the datetime that the task row was inserted, not the taskhist.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed.

column.name: DescriptOverride
column.summary: Limited to 256 char. If present, shows only this text in task list grids instead of prepending date, aggregating notes, etc. Shows as Short Descript in the UI for space reasons.

column.name: IsReadOnly
column.summary: Determines if this task can be edited by most users. Currently only used at HQ.

column.name: TriageCategory
column.summary: FK to definition.DefNum. The category for this task. Currently only used at HQ.

table.name: taskancestor
table.summary:
Represents one ancestor of one task.  Each task will have at least one ancestor unless it is directly on a main trunk.  
            An ancestor is defined as a tasklist that is higher in the heirarchy for the task, regardless of how many levels up it is.  
            This allows us to mark task lists as having "new" tasks, and it allows us to quickly check for new tasks for a user on startup.

column.name: TaskAncestorNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TaskNum
column.summary: FK to task.TaskNum

column.name: TaskListNum
column.summary: FK to tasklist.TaskListNum

table.name: taskattachment
table.summary: An attachment to a task. Attachment can be a document or a string.

column.name: TaskAttachmentNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TaskNum
column.summary: FK to task.TaskNum.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: FK to document.DocNum. If no document is attached, then this field will be 0.

column.name: TextValue
column.summary: Used to store text that doesn't need to be visible from the main task edit window at all times.

column.name: Description
column.summary: A brief description of this attachment. If document is linked, used for document description as well.

table.name: taskhist
table.summary: A historical copy of a task.  These are generated as a result of a task being edited, so there can be multiple entries here per task.  When creating for insertion it needs a passed-in Task object.

column.name: TaskHistNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: UserNumHist
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum  Identifies the user that changed this task from this state, not the person who originally wrote it.

column.name: DateTStamp
column.summary: The date and time that this task was edited and added to the Hist table. This value will not be updated by MySQL whenever the row changes.

column.name: IsNoteChange
column.summary: True if the note was changed when this historical copy was created.

column.name: TaskNum
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: TaskListNum
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: DateTask
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: KeyNum
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: Descript
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: TaskStatus
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: IsRepeating
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: DateType
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: FromNum
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: ObjectType
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: DateTimeFinished
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: PriorityDefNum
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: ReminderGroupId
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: ReminderType
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: ReminderFrequency
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: DateTimeOriginal
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: Not copied from Task. Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed.

column.name: DescriptOverride
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: IsReadOnly
column.summary: Copied from Task.

column.name: TriageCategory
column.summary: Copied from Task.

table.name: tasklist
table.summary: A tasklist is like a folder system, where it can have child tasklists as well as tasks.

column.name: TaskListNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Descript
column.summary: The description of this tasklist.  Might be very long, but not usually.

column.name: Parent
column.summary: FK to tasklist.TaskListNum  The parent task list to which this task list is assigned.  If zero, then this task list is on the main trunk of one of the sections.

column.name: DateTL
column.summary: Optional. Set to 0001-01-01 for no date.  If a date is assigned, then this list will also be available from the date section.

column.name: IsRepeating
column.summary: True if it is to show in the repeating section.  There should be no date.  All children should also be set to IsRepeating=true.

column.name: DateType
column.summary: Enum:TaskDateType  None, Day, Week, Month.  If IsRepeating, then setting to None effectively disables the repeating feature.

Enumeration.name: TaskDateType
Enumeration.summary: For use by the older Repeating Tasks feature.

column.name: FromNum
column.summary: FK to tasklist.TaskListNum  If this is derived from a repeating list, then this will hold the TaskListNum of that list.  It helps automate the adding and deleting of lists.  It might be deleted automatically if no tasks are marked complete.

column.name: ObjectType
column.summary: Enum:TaskObjectType  0=none, 1=Patient, 2=Appointment.  More will be added later. If a type is selected, then this list will be visible in the appropriate places for attaching the correct type of object.  The type is not copied to a task when created.  Tasks in this list do not have to be of the same type.  You can only attach an object to a task, not a tasklist.

Enumeration.name: TaskObjectType
Enumeration.summary: Used when attaching objects to tasks.  These are the choices.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: The date and time that this list was added.  Used to sort the list by the order entered.

column.name: GlobalTaskFilterType
column.summary:
Enum:GlobalTaskFilterType 0=Disabled, 1=Default, 2=None, 3=Clinic, 4=Region.  If a type is selected, then tasks in this tasklist will be filtered by default 
            such that only tasks that match the tasklist's GlobalFilterType will show in the view. Disabled is not valid for tasklists and will be treated as Default if applied to a tasklist; it is only valid on the TasksGlobalFilterType preference.

column.name: TaskListStatus
column.summary: Enum:TaskListStatusEnum 0=Active, 1=Archived. Archived task lists are hidden from the Task Window's User, Main, and Reminder tabs by default.

Enumeration.name: TaskListStatusEnum
Enumeration.summary: Determines if a task list is Active or Archived.

EnumValue._: 0 - Active.
EnumValue.name: Active

EnumValue._: 1 - Archived.
EnumValue.name: Archived

table.name: tasknote
table.summary: A tasknote is a note that may be added to a task. Many notes may be attached to a task. A user may only edit their own tasknotes within a task.

column.name: TaskNoteNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TaskNum
column.summary: FK to task.TaskNum. The task this tasknote is attached to.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. The user who created this tasknote.

column.name: DateTimeNote
column.summary: Date and time the note was created or last modified (editable).

column.name: Note
column.summary: Note. Text that the user wishes to show on the task.

table.name: tasksubscription
table.summary: A subscription of one user to either a tasklist or to a task.

column.name: TaskSubscriptionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum

column.name: TaskListNum
column.summary: FK to tasklist.TaskListNum  When this is not 0 then TaskNum will be 0.

column.name: TaskNum
column.summary: FK to task.TaskNum.  When this is not 0 then TaskListNum will be 0.

table.name: taskunread
table.summary: When a task is created or a comment made, a series of these taskunread objects are created, one for each user who is subscribed to the tasklist.  Duplicates are intelligently avoided.  Rows are deleted once user reads the task.

column.name: TaskUnreadNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: TaskNum
column.summary: FK to task.TaskNum.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.

table.name: terminalactive
table.summary: Each row is 1 computer, or if in RDP session, 1 connection from 1 computer, currently acting as a terminal for patient input.

column.name: TerminalActiveNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ComputerName
column.summary: The name of the computer where the terminal is active. On RDP, this is client name.

column.name: TerminalStatus
column.summary:
Enum:TerminalStatusEnum  No longer used.  Instead, the PatNum field is used.  Used to indicates at what point the patient was in the
            sequence. 0=standby, 1=PatientInfo, 2=Medical, 3=UpdateOnly.  If status is 1, then nobody else on the network could open the patient edit
            window for that patient.

Enumeration.name: TerminalStatusEnum
Enumeration.summary: Indicates at what point the patient is in the sequence. 0=standby, 1=PatientInfo, 2=Medical, 3=UpdateOnly.

EnumValue._: 3. Only the patient info tab will be visible.  This is just to let patient up date their address and phone number.
EnumValue.name: UpdateOnly

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.  The patient currently showing in the terminal.  If 0, then terminal is in standby mode.

column.name: SessionId
column.summary:
The ID of the session from which this terminal instance was started.  The session ID is unique per computer login, so if this is a
            terminal server every remote connection will have a unique session ID.  A kiosk is identified by ComputerName+SessionId+ProcessId.

column.name: ProcessId
column.summary:
The ID of the process that initiated this kiosk instance.  This is unique per active computer process, so if a row exists with a
            ProcessId that matches the instance we're about to start, we know it is safe to delete it, it must be left over and needs cleaned up.

column.name: SessionName
column.summary:
The name of the computer used to make the remote connection to the app server when enabling kiosk mode. Could also be a name manually
            entered by the user if there's already a connection to the app server from the same computer session. This serves as a human-readable name for
            the ComputerName+SessionId+ProcessId to uniquely identify a kiosk. We will display the ComputerName and SessionName to the user in the kiosk
            manager, but we will use the ComputerName+SessionId+ProcessId when the kiosk checks for available forms to display.

table.name: timeadjust
table.summary: Used on employee timecards to make adjustments.  Used to make the end-of-the week OT entries.  Can be used instead of a clock event by admin so that a clock event doesn't have to be created.

column.name: TimeAdjustNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EmployeeNum
column.summary: FK to employee.EmployeeNum

column.name: TimeEntry
column.summary: The date and time that this entry will show on timecard.

column.name: RegHours
column.summary: The number of regular hours to adjust timecard by.  Can be + or -.

column.name: OTimeHours
column.summary: Overtime hours. Usually +.  Automatically combined with a - adj to RegHours.  Another option is clockevent.OTimeHours.

column.name: IsAuto
column.summary: Set to true if this adjustment was automatically made by the system.  When the calc weekly OT tool is run, these types of adjustments are fair game for deletion.  Other adjustments are preserved.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  The clinic the TimeAdjust was entered at.

column.name: PtoDefNum
column.summary:
FK to definition.DefNum.  Defaults to 0.  Is set to 0 for general adjustments.
            When not 0, points to a definition in the TimeCardAdjTypes category.

column.name: PtoHours
column.summary: PTO Hours.  The number of PTO hours applied to a specific day.  Ignored if PtoDefNum is 0.

column.name: IsUnpaidProtectedLeave
column.summary: Defaults to false. True when this TimeAdjust is for unpaid protected leave. Hours from unpaid protected leave adjustments contribute to hours worked, but not to payable hours.

column.name: SecuUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. The user that created this TimeAdjust.

table.name: timecardrule
table.summary: A rule for automation of timecard overtime.  Can apply to one employee or all.

column.name: TimeCardRuleNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: EmployeeNum
column.summary: FK to employee.EmployeeNum. If zero, then this rule applies to all employees.

column.name: OverHoursPerDay
column.summary: Typical example is 8:00.  In California, any work after the first 8 hours is overtime.

column.name: AfterTimeOfDay
column.summary: Typical example is 16:00 to indicate that all time worked after 4pm for specific employees is at Rate2 rate.

column.name: BeforeTimeOfDay
column.summary: Typical example is 6:00 to indicate that all time worked before 6am for specific employees is at Rate2 rate.

column.name: IsOvertimeExempt
column.summary: Indicates if the employee should have overtime calculated for their hours worked in a pay period.

column.name: MinClockInTime
column.summary: When set this is the earliest an employee can clock in. Otherwise minimum dateTime represents not set.

column.name: HasWeekendRate3
column.summary: Indicates if the employee is eligible to earn Rate3 rate for weekend hours.

table.name: toolbutitem
table.summary: Each row represents one toolbar button to be placed on a toolbar and linked to a program.

column.name: ToolButItemNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ProgramNum
column.summary: FK to program.ProgramNum.

column.name: ToolBar
column.summary: Enum:EnumToolBar The toolbar to show the button on.

EnumValue._: 7 Shows in the toolbar at the top that is common to all modules.
EnumValue.name: MainToolbar

EnumValue._: 8 Shows in the main menu Reports submenu.
EnumValue.name: ReportsMenu

column.name: ButtonText
column.summary: The text to show on the toolbar button.

table.name: toothgridcell
table.summary: Holds one recorded cell value for a tooth grid, which is a special kind of sheet field type that shows a grid with 32 rows and configurable columns.  The entire grid is a single large sheet field.

column.name: ToothGridCellNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SheetFieldNum
column.summary: FK to sheetfield.SheetFieldNum.  Required.

column.name: ToothGridColNum
column.summary: FK to toothgridcol.ToothGridColNum.  This tells which column it belongs in.  Can't use the column name here because multiple columns could have the same name.

column.name: ValueEntered
column.summary: Cannot be empty.  For a tooth-level cell, the only allowed value is X.  If the cell is unchecked, then it won't even have a row in this table.  For a surface level column, only valid surfaces can be entered:MOIDBFLV  Enforced.  FreeText columns can have any text up to 255 char.

column.name: ToothNum
column.summary: Corresponds exactly to procedurelog.ToothNum.  May be blank, otherwise 1-32, 51-82, A-T, or AS-TS, 1 or 2 char.  Gets internationalized as being displayed.

table.name: toothgridcol
table.summary: Defines the columns present in a single completed tooth grid, which is a special kind of sheet field that shows a grid with 32 rows and configurable columns.  The entire grid is a single large sheet field.  This table defines how the grid is layed out on an actual sheet, pulled initially from a ToothGridDef.  The data itself is recorded in ToothGridCell.

column.name: ToothGridColNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: SheetFieldNum
column.summary: FK to sheet.SheetFieldNum.  Required.

column.name: NameItem
column.summary: Pulled from the ToothGridDef.  This can be a NameInternal , or it can be a NameShowing if it's a user-defined column.

column.name: CellType
column.summary: Enum:ToothGridCellType  0=HardCoded, 1=Tooth, 2=Surface, 3=FreeText.

Enumeration.name: ToothGridCellType
Enumeration.summary: 0=HardCoded, 1=Tooth, 2=Surface, 3=FreeText.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Order of the column to display.  Every entry must have a unique itemorder.

column.name: CodeNum
column.summary: FK to procedurecode.CodeNum.  This allows data entered to flow into main program as actual completed or tp procedures.

column.name: ProcStatus
column.summary: Enum:ProcStat  If these flow into main program, then this is the status that the new procs will have.

Enumeration.name: ProcStat
Enumeration.summary: Procedure Status.  These statuses are transalted via class type "enumProcStat" (ex Lan.g("enumProcStat","..."))

EnumValue._: 1- Treatment Plan.
EnumValue.name: TP

EnumValue._: 2- Complete.
EnumValue.name: C

EnumValue._: 3- Existing Current Provider.
EnumValue.name: EC

EnumValue._: 4- Existing Other Provider.
EnumValue.name: EO

EnumValue._: 5- Referred Out.
EnumValue.name: R

EnumValue._: 6- Deleted.
EnumValue.name: D

EnumValue._: 7- Condition.
EnumValue.name: Cn

EnumValue._: 8- Treatment Plan inactive.
EnumValue.name: TPi

table.name: toothgriddef
table.summary: Defines the columns present in a tooth grid, which is a special kind of sheet field def that shows a grid with 32 rows and configurable columns.  Can be edited without damaging any completed sheets.

column.name: ToothGridDefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: NameInternal
column.summary: This is the internal name that OD uses to identify the column.  Blank if this is a user-defined column.  We will keep a hard-coded list of available NameInternals in the code to pick from.

column.name: NameShowing
column.summary: The user may override the internal name for display purposes.  If this is a user-defined column, this is the only name, since there is no NameInternal.

column.name: CellType
column.summary: Enum:ToothGridCellType  0=HardCoded, 1=Tooth, 2=Surface, 3=FreeText.

Enumeration.name: ToothGridCellType
Enumeration.summary: 0=HardCoded, 1=Tooth, 2=Surface, 3=FreeText.

column.name: ItemOrder
column.summary: Order of the column to display.  Every entry must have a unique itemorder.

column.name: CodeNum
column.summary: FK to procedurecode.CodeNum.  This allows data entered to flow into main program as actual completed or tp procedures.

column.name: ProcStatus
column.summary: Enum:ProcStat  If these flow into main program, then this is the status that the new procs will have.

Enumeration.name: ProcStat
Enumeration.summary: Procedure Status.  These statuses are transalted via class type "enumProcStat" (ex Lan.g("enumProcStat","..."))

EnumValue._: 1- Treatment Plan.
EnumValue.name: TP

EnumValue._: 2- Complete.
EnumValue.name: C

EnumValue._: 3- Existing Current Provider.

EnumValue._: 4- Existing Other Provider.

EnumValue._: 5- Referred Out.
EnumValue.name: R
