column.name: SheetFieldDefNum
column.summary: FK to sheetfielddef.SheetFieldDefNum

table.name: toothinitial
table.summary: Used to track missing teeth, primary teeth, movements, and drawings.

column.name: ToothInitialNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum

column.name: ToothNum
column.summary: 1-32 or A-Z. Supernumeraries not supported here yet.

column.name: InitialType
column.summary: Enum:ToothInitialType

EnumValue._: 1 - Also hides the number.  Number can be primary or permanent.
EnumValue.name: Hidden

EnumValue._: 2 - Only used with 1-32.  "sets" this tooth as a primary tooth.  The result is that the primary tooth shows in addition to the perm, and that the letter shows in addition to the number.  It also does a Shift0 -12 and some other handy movements.  Even if this is set to true, there can be a separate entry for a missing primary tooth; this would be almost equivalent to not even setting the tooth as primary, but would also allow user to select the letter.
EnumValue.name: Primary

EnumValue._: 3 - Mesial mm
EnumValue.name: ShiftM

EnumValue._: 4 - Occlusal/incisal mm
EnumValue.name: ShiftO

EnumValue._: 5 - Buccal aka Labial mm
EnumValue.name: ShiftB

EnumValue._: 6 - Clockwise as viewed from occlusal/incisal.
EnumValue.name: Rotate

EnumValue._: 7 - Mesial degrees
EnumValue.name: TipM

EnumValue._: 8 - Buccal degrees
EnumValue.name: TipB

EnumValue._: 9 - One segment of a drawing.
EnumValue.name: Drawing

EnumValue._: 10 - Location and string, combined
EnumValue.name: Text

column.name: Movement
column.summary: Shift in mm, or rotation / tipping in degrees.

column.name: DrawingSegment
column.summary: Point data for a drawing segment.  The format would look similar to this: 45,68;48,70;49,72;0,0;55,88;etc.  It's simply a sequence of points, separated by semicolons.  Only positive numbers are used.  0,0 is the upper left of the tooth chart, and the lower right is at 410,307.  This scale of 410,307 is always used, regardless of how the tooth chart control is scaled for viewing.  Floats with tenths can be included.  If the pen is picked up, it becomes a new segment, so a new row in the database.

column.name: SecDateTEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: DrawText
column.summary: For text not associated with any tooth. The location of the text within 410,307 is incorporated into this string.  Example: 25.3,123.8;This shows.  Carriage returns etc are not supported.  ColorDraw is used.

table.name: transaction
table.summary: Used in the accounting section of the program.  Each row is one transaction in the ledger, and must always have at least two JournalEntries (splits).  All JournalEntries must always add up to zero.

column.name: TransactionNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateTimeEntry
column.summary: Not user editable.  Server time.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. The user that entered this transaction.

column.name: DepositNum
column.summary: FK to deposit.DepositNum.  Will eventually be replaced by a source document table, and deposits will just be one of many types.

column.name: PayNum
column.summary: FK to payment.PayNum.  Like DepositNum, it will eventually be replaced by a source document table, and payments will just be one of many types.

column.name: SecUserNumEdit
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum. The user who last edited this transaction.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary: The last time this transaction was edited.

column.name: TransactionInvoiceNum
column.summary: FK to transactioninvoice.TransactionInvoiceNum.  A document that can be attached to the transaction.

table.name: transactioninvoice
table.summary: Used in the accounting section of the program.  Each row contains a document that is attached to a transaction.

column.name: TransactionInvoiceNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: FileName
column.summary: File name including the extension.

column.name: InvoiceData
column.summary: The raw file data converted to base64. Will be blank when using FilePath.

column.name: FilePath
column.summary: Full file path. Will be blank when using InvoiceData.

table.name: treatplan
table.summary:
Stores all treatment plans, including Active, Inactive, and Saved treatment plans. 
             Active and Inactive treatment plans use treatplanattaches to reference attached procedures. As procedures are set complete, they get
             removed from active and inactive treatment plans. Saved treatment plans use proctps, which are copies of the procedure, 
             and will not change after being saved.

column.name: TreatPlanNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: DateTP
column.summary: The date of the treatment plan

column.name: Heading
column.summary: The heading that shows at the top of the treatment plan.  Usually 'Proposed Treatment Plan'

column.name: Note
column.summary: A note specific to this treatment plan that shows at the bottom.

column.name: Signature
column.summary: The encrypted and bound signature in base64 format.  The signature is bound to the concatenation of the tp Note, DateTP, and to each proctp Descript and PatAmt.

column.name: SigIsTopaz
column.summary: True if the signature is in Topaz format rather than OD format.

column.name: ResponsParty
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum. Can be 0.  The patient responsible for approving the treatment.  Public health field not visible to everyone else.

column.name: DocNum
column.summary: FK to document.DocNum. Can be 0.  If signed, this is the pdf document of the TP at time of signing. See PrefName.TreatPlanSaveSignedToPdf

column.name: TPStatus
column.summary: Enum:TreatPlanStatus Determines the type of treatment plan this is. 0 - Saved, 1 - Active, 2 - Inactive.

Enumeration.name: TreatPlanStatus
Enumeration.summary: 0 - Saved, 1 - Active, 2 - Inactive

EnumValue._: 0 - Saved treatment plans. Prior to version 15.4.1 all treatment plans were considered archived. Archived TPs are linked to ProcTPs.
EnumValue.name: Saved

EnumValue._: 1 - Current active TP. There should be only one Active TP per patient. This is a TP linked directly to procedures via the TreatPlanAttach table.
EnumValue.name: Active

EnumValue._: 2 - Current inactive TP. This is a TP linked directly to procedures via the TreatPlanAttach table.
EnumValue.name: Inactive

column.name: SecUserNumEntry
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  Set to the user logged in when the row was inserted at SecDateEntry date and time.

column.name: SecDateEntry
column.summary: Timestamp automatically generated and user not allowed to change.  The actual date of entry.

column.name: SecDateTEdit
column.summary:
Automatically updated by MySQL every time a row is added or changed. Could be changed due to user editing, custom queries or program
            updates.  Not user editable with the UI.

column.name: UserNumPresenter
column.summary:
FK to userod.UserNum. The user that will present the treatment plan. 
            Defaults to the user that entered the treatment plan, but can be changed with the TreatPlanPresenterEdit permission.

column.name: TPType
column.summary: Enum:TreatPlanType Determines the type of insurance this treatment plan was saved with.  Used for displaying proper information when loading.

Enumeration.name: TreatPlanType
Enumeration.summary: 0 - Insurance, 1 - Discount Plan

EnumValue._: 0 - Treatment plan saved for regular insurance.
EnumValue.name: Insurance

EnumValue._: 1 - Treatment plan saved for discount plan.
EnumValue.name: Discount

column.name: SignaturePractice
column.summary: The encrypted and bound signature in base64 format.  The signature is bound to the concatenation of the tp Note, DateTP, and to each proctp Descript and PatAmt.

column.name: DateTSigned

table.name: tsitranslog
table.summary:
Transworld Systems Inc (TSI) transaction log.  Logs communication between the Open Dental program and TSI.  Entries contain information
            about accounts placed with TSI, payments or adjustments to accounts placed, or transactions to Suspend, Reinstate or Cancel accounts.

column.name: TsiTransLogNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary:
FK to patient.PatNum for the guarantor of the account sent to TSI for collection services.  TSI refers to this as the Debtor Reference
            or Responsible Party Account Number.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary:
FK to userod.UserNum.  The user who sent the account for placement with TSI or who suspended, reinstated or cancelled collection
            services for an account placed with TSI or who created the payment/adjustment for an account placed with TSI.

column.name: TransType
column.summary:
Enum:TsiTransType - Identifies the transaction message sent to TSI.  Can be a message for placing/cancelling/suspending/reinstating
            collection services for an account or to notify TSI of a payment/writeoff/adjustment entered into OD.

Enumeration.name: TsiTransType
Enumeration.summary:
Identifies the transaction type represented by this log entry.  Could be a for placing/cancelling/suspending/reinstating an account or
            for a payment/writeoff/adjustment to an account balance.  Don't remove items or alter order of this enum, FKey requires this to be static.

EnumValue._:
-1 - None: Used for marking trans that represent account items NOT sent to Transworld.  The TsiTransLogs with this trans type are used
            to prevent the ODService from sending the trans to Transworld.  Example: Transworld collects a payment from a patient and deduct their fee and
            send the practice the remaining amt.  Office staff will enter the payment in the patient's account and then enter an adjustment to account for
            Transworld's fee.  TsiTransLogs will be inserted for the paysplits and adjustment so that we don't send those trans to Transworld and cause an
            infinite loop of sorts.  Logs with this type will be update by the ODService if the ledger trans amt is updated.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 0 - Cancel: cancel collection services for an account. Collection services can be restarted but will incur another TSI fee.
EnumValue.name: CN

EnumValue._: 1 - Credit Adjustment: negative adjustment to reduce balance. Example: a discount given or portion of the debt written off.
EnumValue.name: CR

EnumValue._:
2 - Debit Adjustment: positive adjustment to increase balance.  Offices are supposed to stop all finance charges once placed with TSI,
            but there may be other transactions that require increasing the amount owed.
EnumValue.name: DB

EnumValue._: 3 - Paid in Full: payment entered that pays off account balance.  Closes account with TSI and stops collection activity.
EnumValue.name: PF

EnumValue._: 4 - Placement: account sent to TSI for Accelerator/Profit Recovery/Collection services.
EnumValue.name: PL

EnumValue._: 5 - Partial Payment: payment by either patient or ins payment/writeoff that pays a portion of the balance.
EnumValue.name: PP

EnumValue._:
6 - Paid in Full, Thank You: payment entered that pays off account balance.  Closes account with TSI and stops collection activity.
            TSI will send a Thank You letter to the patient free of charge.
EnumValue.name: PT

EnumValue._:
7 - Reinstate: an account that has been suspended can be reinstated within 50 days and the collection services will resume where it
            left off.  After 50 days the account is automatically cancelled and in order to restart collection services the office would have to initiate a
            new placement, which will incur another TSI fee.
EnumValue.name: RI

EnumValue._:
8 - Suspend: places collection services for the account on hold for up to 50 days.  Example: After an account is placed with TSI, the
            customer comes into the office and agrees to a payment plan.  The account can be suspended and if the patient fails to make a payment within 50
            days the account can be reinstated and the collection process will resume where it left off and TSI will not charge an additional fee.  After
            50 days the account is automatically cancelled by TSI and in order to restart the collection process, the office would have to initiate a new
            placement which starts the collection process over and will result in an additional TSI fee.
EnumValue.name: SS

EnumValue._: 9 - To differentiate aggregate rows from rows linked to transactions in the OD db.
EnumValue.name: Agg

EnumValue._:
10 - Used for adjustments entered with the SyncExcludePosAdjType or SyncExcludeNegAdjType set in the Transworld program link.
            Excluded from syncing with TSI and from the amount due calculation used in future msgs or to determine if the acct is paid in full.
EnumValue.name: Excluded

column.name: TransDateTime
column.summary:
Timestamp at which this row was created. Auto generated on insert.  Identifies exactly when the action happened in OD to cause the
            message to be sent to TSI.

column.name: ServiceType
column.summary: Enum:TsiServiceType - for placements, this is the type of collection activity that will start on the account being placed.

Enumeration.name: TsiServiceType
Enumeration.summary: Don't remove items or alter order of this enum, FKey requires this to be static.

EnumValue._: 0 - Accelerator
EnumValue.name: Accelerator

EnumValue._: 1 - Profit Recovery
EnumValue.name: ProfitRecovery

EnumValue._: 2 - Professional Collections
EnumValue.name: ProfessionalCollections

column.name: ServiceCode
column.summary: Enum:TsiServiceCode - for placements, intensity of first letter sent to guarantor.  Will usually be 0 - Diplomatic.

Enumeration.name: TsiServiceCode
Enumeration.summary:
The service code determines the intensity of the first letter.  According to a TSI rep during a conference call, Diplomatic will be what
            most offices will want to use.  Bad check is rarely used.  Don't remove items or alter order of this enum, FKey requires this to be static.

EnumValue._: 0 - Diplomatic: most commonly used service code.
EnumValue.name: Diplomatic

EnumValue._: 1 - Intensive:  More intense first letter.
EnumValue.name: Intensive

EnumValue._: 3 - Bad Check: in a conference call with TSI one of the reps said this is rarely used.
EnumValue.name: BadCheck

column.name: TransAmt
column.summary: Used for payments/writeoffs/adjustments, amount applied to the debt.

column.name: AccountBalance
column.summary:
Total balance due on the account by the patient, i.e. BalTotal-InsPayEst-WoEst.  If this is a placement, this is the debt amount TSI
            is going to attempt to collect.  If this is a payment/writeoff/adjustment, this is the new balance after the transaction amount is applied to
            the debt.

column.name: FKeyType
column.summary: Enum:TsiFKeyType - Used in conjunction with FKey to point to the item that this log row represents.

Enumeration.name: TsiFKeyType
Enumeration.summary: Identifies the table to which FKey points.  Don't remove items or alter order of this enum, FKey requires this to be static.

EnumValue._: -1 - None.  For place, suspend, cancel, agg.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._:
0 - adjustment.AdjNum.  Can be a positive (Debit) or negative (Credit) adjustment to the amount owed.  The resulting message
            TsiTransType is DB (Debit) for positive adjustments or CR (Credit) for negative adjustments.
EnumValue.name: Adjustment

EnumValue._:
1 - claimproc.ClaimProcNum. For ins payments and/or writeoffs entered after the account has been placed with TSI.  The resulting
            message TsiTransType is PP (Partial Payment), PF (Paid in Full), or PT (Paid in Full, Thank You).
EnumValue.name: Claimproc

EnumValue._:
2 - payplan.PayPlanNum.  In payplan version 1 the entire CompletedAmt is aged, so it will be negative (credit) and decrease the amount
            owed.  The resulting message TsiTransType is CR (Credit).
EnumValue.name: PayPlan

EnumValue._:
3 - payplancharge.PayPlanChargeNum.  Depends on payplan version, could be negative (credit - decrease amount owed) or
            positive (debit - increase amount owed).  The resulting message TsiTransType is DB (Debit) if positive or CR (Credit) if negative.
EnumValue.name: PayPlanCharge

EnumValue._:
4 - paysplit.SplitNum.  Patient payment on an account placed with TSI.  The resulting message TsiTransType is PP (Partial Payment),
            PF (Paid in Full), or PT (Paid in Full, Thank You).
EnumValue.name: PaySplit

EnumValue._: 5 - procedurelog.ProcNum.  Positive (debit, increases the amount owed).  The resulting message TsiTransType is DB (Debit).
EnumValue.name: Procedure

column.name: FKey
column.summary:
Foreign key to the table defined by the corresponding FKeyType.  Currently supports paysplit.SplitNum, claimproc.ClaimProcNum,
            adjustment.AdjNum, procedurelog.ProcNum, payplan.PayPlanNum, payplancharge.PayPlanChargeNum.

column.name: RawMsgText
column.summary: Raw pipe-delimited message sent to TSI.

column.name: ClientId
column.summary:
If ServiceType for the placement is TsiDemandType.AcceleratorPr, this will be the Accelerator/Profit Recovery client ID.
            If TsiServiceType.Collection it will be the Collection client ID. Will always match the first field, Client Number, in the RawMsgText.

column.name: TransJson
column.summary:
Json serialized string representation of the TsiTrans list used to calculate the account balance for this guarantor at the time of
            placement with Transworld.  Used to update Transworld if any of the transactions are modified after placement.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  This will be 0 if clinics are not enabled.  This will be 0 for logs prior to version 18.4.

column.name: AggTransLogNum
column.summary: FK to tsitranslog.TransLogNum.  Will be 0 if not part of an aggregate group.  Will be 0 for logs prior to version 18.4.

table.name: ucum
table.summary: Unified Code for Units of Measure.  UCUM is not a stricly defined list of codes but is instead a language definition that allows for all units and derived units to be named.  Examples: g (grams), g/L (grams per liter), g/L/s (grams per liter per second), g/L/s/s (grams per liter per second per second), etc... are all allowed units meaning there is an infinite number of units that can be defined using UCUM conventions.  The codes stored in this table are merely a common subset that was readily available and premade.

column.name: UcumNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: UcumCode
column.summary: Indexed.  Also called concept code. Example: mol/mL

column.name: Description
column.summary: Also called Concept Name.  Human readable form of the UCUM code. Example: Moles Per MilliLiter [Substance Concentration Units]

column.name: IsInUse
column.summary: True if this unit of measure is or has ever been in use.  Useful for assisting users to select common units.

table.summary:
Makes an entry every time Open Dental has successfully updated to a newer version.

            This will also help EHR customers when attesting or when they get audited.

column.name: UpdateHistoryNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: DateTimeUpdated
column.summary: DateTime that OD was updated to the Version.

column.name: ProgramVersion
column.summary: The version that OD was updated to.

column.name: Signature
column.summary: Obfuscated string containing when and who accepted the license agreement.

table.name: userod
table.summary: (User OD since "user" is a reserved word) Users are a completely separate entity from Providers and Employees even though they can be linked.  A usernumber can never be changed, ensuring a permanent way to record database entries and leave an audit trail.  A user can be a provider, employee, or neither.

column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: Password

The password details in a "HashType$Salt$Hash" format, separating the different fields by '$'.
            This is NOT the actual password but the encoded password hash.
            If the contents of this variable are not in the aforementioned format, it is assumed to be a legacy password hash (MD5).

column.name: EmployeeNum

FK to clinic.ClinicNum.  Default clinic for this user.  It causes new patients to default to this clinic when entered by this user.  
            If 0, then user has no default clinic or default clinic is HQ if clinics are enabled.

column.name: ProvNum

column.name: IsHidden
column.summary: Set true to hide user from login list.

column.name: TaskListInBox

column.name: AnesthProvType

column.name: DefaultHidePopups

column.name: ClinicIsRestricted

When true, prevents user from having access to clinics that are not in the corresponding userclinic table.
            Many places throughout the program will optionally remove the 'All' option from this user when true.

column.name: InboxHidePopups
column.summary: If set to true, the BlockInbox button will start out pressed for this user.

column.name: UserNumCEMT
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.  The user num within the Central Manager database.  Only editable via CEMT.  Can change when CEMT syncs.

column.name: DateTFail
column.summary: The date and time of the most recent log in failure for this user.  Set to MinValue after user logs in successfully.

column.name: FailedAttempts
column.summary: The number of times this user has failed to log into their account.  Set to 0 after user logs in successfully.

column.name: DomainUser
column.summary: The username for the ActiveDirectory user to link the account to.

column.name: IsPasswordResetRequired
column.summary: Boolean.  If true, the user's password needs to be reset on next login.

column.name: MobileWebPin
column.summary: A hashed pin that is used for mobile web validation on eClipboard. Not used in OD proper.

column.name: MobileWebPinFailedAttempts
column.summary: The number of attempts the mobile web pin has failed. Reset on successful attempt.

column.name: DateTLastLogin
column.summary:
Minimum date if last login date and time is unknown.
            Otherwise contians the last date and time this user successfully logged in.

column.name: EClipboardClinicalPin
column.summary: Pin for ODT. This is the hashed value of the pin. Not used in OD proper.

column.name: BadgeId
column.summary: A unique number that corresponds to the number on an employee badge. The last numbers on an employee badge. Will be 1 to 4 digits. These numbers are assigned to the badges by the factory. We order a specific range of badges, such as 1801-2000, which are assigned in order and not reused to avoid duplicates. The first four digits on the badges are not used by the Lenel OnGuard software, so we do not use them here either.

column.name: UserOdPrefNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: UserNum
column.summary: FK to userod.UserNum.

column.name: Fkey
column.summary: Foreign key to a table associated with FkeyType.  Can be 0 if the user preference does not need a foreign key.

column.name: FkeyType
column.summary: Enum:UserOdFkeyType Specifies which flag is overridden for the specified definition, since an individual definition can have multiple flags.

Enumeration.name: UserOdFkeyType
Enumeration.summary:
These FKey Types are to be used as an identifier for what table the Fkey column is associated to, or what type of override it is.
            Not always associated to a database table.  Example:  TaskCollapse,SuppressLogOffMessage,AcctProcBreakdown.
            Do not reorder items, since they are numbers in the db.

EnumValue._: 0 - Imaging module expanded categories.  Presence of a row means expanded.  Absence means collapsed.  No valuestring needed.
EnumValue.name: ImageCategoryExpanded

EnumValue._: 2 - Wiki home pages use ValueString to store the name of the wiki page instead of Fkey due to how FormWiki loads pages.
EnumValue.name: WikiHomePage

EnumValue._:
3 - ValueString will be a comma delimited list of DefNums for the last expanded categories for the user.  When FormAutoNotes loads,
            these categories will be expanded again.
EnumValue.name: AutoNoteExpandedCats

EnumValue._: 4 - Controls whether tasks will be collapsed or not by default
EnumValue.name: TaskCollapse

EnumValue._: 5 - When FormCommItem is in Persistent mode, clear the note text box after the user creates a commlog.
EnumValue.name: CommlogPersistClearNote

EnumValue._: 6 - When FormCommItem is in Persistent mode, clear the End text box after the user creates a commlog.
EnumValue.name: CommlogPersistClearEndDate

EnumValue._: 7 - When FormCommItem is in Persistent mode, update the Date / Time text box with NOW() whenver the patient changes.
EnumValue.name: CommlogPersistUpdateDateTimeWithNewPatient

EnumValue._: 8 - Whether or not to display just the currently selected exam in the Perio Chart.
EnumValue.name: PerioCurrentExamOnly

EnumValue._: 9 - Text message grouping preference. 0 - None; 1 - By Patient;
EnumValue.name: SmsGroupBy

EnumValue._: 10 - Stores a TaskListNum that the corresponding user wants to block all pop ups from.
EnumValue.name: TaskListBlock

EnumValue._: 11 - Stores user specific values for programs.  Currently only used in DoseSpot for the DoseSpot User ID.
EnumValue.name: Program

EnumValue._: 13 - Sets the default state of the Account Module "Show Proc Breakdowns" checkbox.
EnumValue.name: AcctProcBreakdown

EnumValue._: 14 - Stores user specific username for programs.
EnumValue.name: ProgramUserName

EnumValue._: 15 - Stores user specific password for programs.
EnumValue.name: ProgramPassword

EnumValue._: 16 - Stores user specific dashboard to open on load. FKey points to the SheetDef.SheetDefNum that the user last had open.
EnumValue.name: Dashboard

EnumValue._: 17 - Deprecated.
EnumValue.name: UserTheme

EnumValue._: 18 - Stores the Dynamic Chart Layout SheetDef.SheetDefNum selected by a user.
EnumValue.name: DynamicChartLayout

EnumValue._: 19 - Whether or not to set the perio auto advance to custom.
EnumValue.name: PerioAutoAdvanceFacialsFirst

EnumValue._: 20 - Stores the value (in minutes) of when the user should be auto logged off.
EnumValue.name: LogOffTimerOverride

EnumValue._: 21 - Whether or not we check the "Show received" checkbox when loading the supply order history.
EnumValue.name: ReceivedSupplyOrders

EnumValue._: 22 - Color defs can be used for different proc statuses when the user's provider doesn't match the procedure's provider.
EnumValue.name: ToothChartUsesDiffColorByProv

EnumValue._: 23 - Whether to show automated commlogs in the account module, chart module, and appointment edit window.
EnumValue.name: ShowAutomatedCommlog

EnumValue._: 24 - Preference that indicates whether stack traces are to be logged by the query monitor or not. If exists 0 or 1 is stored in ValueString
EnumValue.name: QueryMonitorHasStackTraces

EnumValue._: 25 - Preference only applies to the wiki search. Set and unset only in the wiki search window. Box defaults to checked if this preference is not set.
EnumValue.name: WikiSearchIncludeContent

EnumValue._: 26 - Sets whether tasks popups that are blocked still play the sound notification. If true, sound will play. If false, sound will be blocked as well.
EnumValue.name: TaskBlockedMakeSound

EnumValue._: 27 - Stores the width of the left Image Selector (tree) of the Imaging module in the ValueString. Does not store expanded/collapsed, so this width would be the expanded width. There is no limit. Does not use FKey.
EnumValue.name: ImageSelectorWidth

column.summary: FK to vaccinepat.VaccinePatNum.

column.name: ValType
column.summary: Enum:VaccineObsType Coded, Dated, Numeric, Text, DateAndTime.  Used in HL7 OBX-2.

Enumeration.name: VaccineObsType
Enumeration.summary: Corresponds to HL7 table 0125.

EnumValue._: 0 - Code CE.  Coded entry. (default)
EnumValue.name: Coded

EnumValue._: 1 - Code DT.  Date (no time).
EnumValue.name: Dated

EnumValue._: 2 - Code NM.  Numeric.
EnumValue.name: Numeric

EnumValue._: 3 - Code ST.  String.
EnumValue.name: Text

EnumValue._: 4 - Code TS.  Date and time.
EnumValue.name: DateAndTime

column.name: IdentifyingCode
column.summary: Enum:VaccineObsIdentifier  Identifies the observation question.  Used in HL7 OBX-3.

Enumeration.name: VaccineObsIdentifier
Enumeration.summary:
Corresponds to HL7 value set NIP003 (http://hl7v2-iz-testing.nist.gov/mu-immunization/).
            This code set is a subset of LOINC codes.  Used in HL7 OBX-3.

EnumValue._: 0 - LOINC code 29768-9.  Date vaccine information statement published:TmStp:Pt:Patient:Qn: (default)
EnumValue.name: DatePublished

table.name: vitalsign
table.summary: For EHR module, one dated vital sign entry.  BMI is calulated on demand based on height and weight and may be one of 4 ALOINC codes. 39156-5 "Body mass index (BMI) [Ratio]" is most applicable.

column.name: VitalsignNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: Height
column.summary: Height of patient in inches. Fractions might be needed some day.  Allowed to be 0.  Six possible LOINC codes, most applicable is 8302-2, "Body height".

column.name: Weight
column.summary: Lbs.  Allowed to be 0. Six possible LOINC codes, most applicable is 29463-7, "Body weight".

column.name: BpSystolic
column.summary: Units are mmHg (millimeters of mercury). Allowed to be 0. LOINC code 8480-6.

column.name: BpDiastolic
column.summary: Units are mmHg (millimeters of mercury). Allowed to be 0. LOINC code 8462-4.

column.name: DateTaken
column.summary: The date that the vitalsigns were taken.

column.name: HasFollowupPlan
column.summary: For an abnormal BMI measurement this must be true in order to meet quality measurement.

column.name: IsIneligible
column.summary: If a BMI was not recorded, this must be true in order to meet quality measurement.  For children, this is used as an IsPregnant flag, the only valid reason for not taking BMI on children.

column.name: Documentation
column.summary: For HasFollowupPlan or IsIneligible, this documents the specifics.

column.name: WeightCode
column.summary: Used for CQMs.  SNOMED CT code either Normal="", Overweight="238131007", or Underweight="248342006".  Set when BMI is found to be "out of range", based on age groups.  Should be calculated when vital sign is saved.  Calculate based on age as of Jan 1 of the year vitals were taken.  Not currently displayed to user.

column.name: HeightExamCode
column.summary: FK to ehrcode.CodeValue.  Also FK to LOINC.LoincCode.  Used for CQMs.  LOINC code used to describe the height exam performed.  Examples: Body Height Measured=3137-7, Body Height Stated=3138-5, Body Height --pre surgery=8307-1.  We will default to Body Height=8302-2, but user can choose another from the list of 6 allowed.  Can be blank if BP only.

column.name: WeightExamCode
column.summary: FK to ehrcode.CodeValue.  Also FK to LOINC.LoincCode.  Used for CQMs.  LOINC code used to describe the weight exam performed.  Examples: Body Weight Measured=3141-9, Body Weight Stated=3142-7, Body Weight --with clothes=8350-1.  We will default to Body Weight=29463-7, but user can choose another from the list of 6 allowed.  Can be blank if BP only.

column.name: BMIExamCode
column.summary: FK to ehrcode.CodeValue.  Also FK to LOINC.LoincCode.  Used for CQMs.  LOINC code used to describe the BMI percentile calculated.  We will use LOINC 59576-9 - BMI Percentile Per age and gender.  Can be blank if BP only.

column.name: EhrNotPerformedNum
column.summary: FK to ehrnotperformed.EhrNotPerformedNum.  This will link a vitalsign to the EhrNotPerformed object where the reason not performed will be stored.  The linking will allow us to display the not performed reason directly in the vital sign window and will make CQM queries easier.  Will be 0 if not linked to an EhrNotPerformed object.

column.name: PregDiseaseNum
column.summary: FK to disease.DiseaseNum.  This will link this vitalsign object to a pregnancy diagnosis for this patient.  It will be 0 for non pregnant patients.  The disease it is linked to will be inserted automatically based on the default value set.  In order to change this code for this specific exam it will have to be changed in the problems list.

column.name: BMIPercentile
column.summary: BMI percentile of patient, based on gender and age and the calculated BMI.  We will use the CDC numbers to calculate percentile found here: (http://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data/series/sr_11/sr11_246.pdf).

column.name: Pulse
column.summary: Recorded pulse of the patient. Stored in beats per minute.

column.name: WebSchedCarrierRuleNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.summary: Name of the carrier.

column.summary: Set by the user. This is what is shown as a selection for the patient in the WebSched UI.

column.name: Message
column.summary: Return message sent back to patients through WebSched. Set by the office after a patient has made a carrier selection.

column.name: Rule
column.summary: Enum:RuleType Allow, AllowWithInput, AllowWithMessage, Block.

column.summary: Generated by OD. Timestamp when row is created.

column.summary: The date that the recall is due.

column.summary: The number of reminders that have been sent for this recall.

column.summary: DateTime the message was sent.

column.name: DateTimeSendFailed
column.summary: The most recent time that sending a reminder failed. Will be 01/01/0001 if a reminder has never been attempted.

column.name: SendStatus
column.summary: Enum:AutoCommStatus The status of the email or text being sent for this recall.

Enumeration.name: AutoCommStatus
Enumeration.summary: Used by both Statement and WebSchedRecall (and probably other places).

EnumValue._: 0 - Should not be in the database but can be used in the program.
EnumValue.name: Undefined

EnumValue._: 1 - Do not send a reminder.
EnumValue.name: DoNotSend

EnumValue._: 2 - We will send, but send has not been attempted yet.
EnumValue.name: SendNotAttempted

EnumValue._: 3 - Has been sent successfully.
EnumValue.name: SendSuccessful

EnumValue._: 4 - Attempted to send but not successful.
EnumValue.name: SendFailed

EnumValue._: 5 - Has been sent successfully, awaiting receipt.
EnumValue.name: SentAwaitingReceipt

column.name: ShortGUID
column.summary: Generated by HQ. Identifies this AutoCommGuid in future transactions between HQ and OD.

column.name: ResponseDescript
column.summary: Generated by OD in some cases and HQ in others. Any human readable error message generated by either HQ or EConnector. Used for debugging.

column.name: Source
column.summary: Enum:WebSchedRecallSource Where this row came from.

EnumValue._: 0 - Should not be in the database.
EnumValue.name: Undefined

EnumValue._: 1 - Originated from a user clicking the Web Sched button in the Recall List.
EnumValue.name: FormRecallList

EnumValue._: 2 - The eConnector created this row in the Auto Comm Web Sched thread.
EnumValue.name: EConnectorAutoComm

column.name: CommlogNum
column.summary: FK to commlog associated to this WebSchedRecall.

column.name: MessageType
column.summary: Enum:CommType The type of message being sent for this recall.

EnumValue._: -1 - Do not use.
EnumValue.name: Invalid

EnumValue._: 0 - Use text OR email based on patient preference.
EnumValue.name: Preferred

EnumValue._: 1 - Attempt to send text message, if successful do not send via email. (Unless, a SendAll bool is used, which usually negates the need for this enumeration.)
EnumValue.name: Text

EnumValue._: 2 - Attempt to send email message, if successful do not send via text. (Unless, a SendAll bool is used, which usually negates the need for this enumeration.)
EnumValue.name: Email

EnumValue._: 3 - Attempt to send secure email message.
EnumValue.name: SecureEmail

table.name: xwebresponse
table.summary:
Received as XML output from XWeb gateway. Not all fields are available for all method calls. This is a combination of all possible output fields. 
            The fields that are available are dependent on which method was called and the given result.
            HPF (XWeb Hosted Payment Form) Payments and HPF CC Alias creations will each enter a row in this table. That row will be monitored by the eConnector and updated when the XWebResponseCode changes from Pending.
             -- 1) Create the row and indicate the HPF/OTK.
             -- 2) Poll the OTK (one-time key) until an XWebResponseCode is available. Update the row with information about the transaction.
            DTG (XWeb Direct To Gateway) Will enter 1 row in this table. 
             -- 1) Make the DTG payment using a pre-authorized CC alias. Create row with information about the transaction.
            Any fields prefixed with 'Gateaway output' come directly as XML output from the XWeb Gateway. All other fields are derived by OD.
            The class instance will created by eConnector by deserializing an XML string as received from XWeb Gateway. 
            The fields names MUST NOT CHANGE for this reason. XML will not deserialize if the names do not match EXACTLY.

column.name: XWebResponseNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: PatNum
column.summary: FK to patient.PatNum.

column.name: ProvNum
column.summary: FK to provider.ProvNum.

column.name: ClinicNum
column.summary: FK to clinic.ClinicNum.

column.name: PaymentNum
column.summary: FK to payment.PayNum.

column.name: DateTEntry
column.summary: Timestamp at which this row was created. Auto generated on insert.

column.name: DateTUpdate
column.summary: Timestamp at which this row was last updated. Will be updated each time the OTK status is polled and one final time when XWebResponseCode changes from Pending.

column.name: TransactionStatus
column.summary: Inidicates which phase of the XWeb process this transaction is in. See class summary for details.

column.name: ResponseCode
column.summary: Gateaway output. Pre-defined responses generated by XWeb. Will be converted to strongly typed enum XWebResponseCode.

column.name: XWebResponseCode
column.summary: Enum:XWebResponseCodes Strongly typed representation of ResponseCode. Initialized by XWebInputAbs.CreateGatewayResponse().

Enumeration.name: XWebResponseCodes
Enumeration.summary: Various statuses from XWeb. These states are returned directly from the XWeb gateway. A more complete list can be found at \\opendental.od\serverfiles\Storage\OPEN DENTAL\Programmers Documents\Bridge Info\X-Charge\EdgeExpress\EdgeExpress Card Not Present API 1.0.0.pdf.

EnumValue._: 032 - Used when creating a card alias.
EnumValue.name: ZeroDollarAuthApproval

EnumValue._: 101 - Expired Without Approval.  Hosted Form timed out without Approval, OTK was never launched, or Invalid OTK
EnumValue.name: ExpiredWithoutApproval

EnumValue._: 102 - Pending (neither of the above events has occurred yet)
EnumValue.name: Pending

EnumValue._: 800 Parsing Error Unable to parse the XML request sent.
EnumValue.name: ParsingError

EnumValue._: 801 Maximum Request Data Exceeded Error - The XML request exceeds the 2048-byte maximum size.
EnumValue.name: MaxRequestDataExceededError

EnumValue._: 802 Duplicate Field Error - The XML request had more than one copy of a particular field. The field causing the error may be specified.
EnumValue.name: DuplicateFieldError

EnumValue._: 803 Improper DLL Error - Unrecognized DLL name. This can be caused by a wrong URL entered into the "Server Location" setting under XCharge Server Setup, Credit Cards, Connection.
EnumValue.name: ImproperDLLError

EnumValue._: 804 Specification Version Error - XML error, the Specification Version field is set incorrectly.
EnumValue.name: SpecificationVersionError

EnumValue._: 805 Authentication Error -  The XWeb ID, Auth Key or Terminal ID fields are incorrect (check for leading and trailing spaces if they appear to match those on file). The field causing the error may be specified.
EnumValue.name: AuthenticationError

EnumValue._: 806 Production Merchant Set Up Error - The Mode was incorrectly sent. This can happen when trying to process on theProduction server with a Development or Test Mode Processing Account.
EnumValue.name: ProductionMerchantSetUpError

EnumValue._: 807 Test Merchant Set Up Error - The Mode was incorrectly sent. This can happen when trying to process on the Testserver with a Development or Production Mode Processing Account.
EnumValue.name: TestMerchantSetUpError

EnumValue._: 808 Development Merchant Set Up Error - The Mode was incorrectly sent. This can happen when trying to process on theDevelopment server with a Production or Test Mode Processing Account.
EnumValue.name: DevelopmentMerchantSetUpError

EnumValue._: 809 Required Field Not Sent Error - A field that is required for this transaction type was not sent. The field causing theerror may be specified.
EnumValue.name: RequiredFieldNotSentError

EnumValue._: 810 Inconsistent Conditional Field Error - A field that does not have to be sent was sent in the wrong context. The field causing the error may be specified.
EnumValue.name: InconsistentConditionalFieldError

EnumValue._: 811 Improper Field Data Error - A field sent to the EdgeExpress Gateway was not formatted correctly. This could pertain to Processing Account Information configured in XCharge or BMS, or card information. The field causing the error may be specified.
EnumValue.name: ImproperFieldDataError

EnumValue._: 812 Unrecognized Name / Tag Error - The XML tag sent is not in the API
EnumValue.name: UnrecognizedNameTagError

EnumValue._: 813 Duplicate Transaction Error - A transaction was run for the same amount on the same card within a certain time limit. The duplicate checking time is set on the EdgeExpress Gateway, usuallybetween 1 and 60 minutes.
EnumValue.name: DuplicateTransactionError

EnumValue._: 814 Invalid Reference Error - The Transaction ID used for a Void, Return, etc. is invalid.
EnumValue.name: InvalidReferenceError

EnumValue._: 815 Transaction Already Voided - The Transaction ID used for a Void was already voided.
EnumValue.name: TransactionAlreadyVoided

EnumValue._: 816 Transaction Already Captured - The Transaction ID used for a Capture of an Authorized charge was already used and the transaction has been Captured.
EnumValue.name: TransactionAlreadyCaptured

EnumValue._: 817 Empty Batch - The batch is empty and cannot settle. You cannot settle an empty batch.
EnumValue.name: EmptyBatch

EnumValue._: 818 Merchant Locked For Settlement - The Processing Account is in the process of being settled/batched. Wait a moment and try again.
EnumValue.name: MerchantLockedForSettlement

EnumValue._: 819 Merchant Locked for Maintenance - The Processing Account is locked for database or server maintenance. Wait amoment and try again.
EnumValue.name: MerchantLockedForMaintenance

EnumValue._: 820 Temporary Service Outage - Retry Transaction - The EdgeExpress Gateway itself may be down. Wait a moment and try again.
EnumValue.name: TemporaryServiceOutage

EnumValue._: 821 Processing Host Unavailable - Certain back end account parameters may not be set correctly. Have the account settings checked. Specifically, check the TSYS Vital Hierarchy Values.
EnumValue.name: ProcessingHostUnavailable

EnumValue._: 823 Invalid Account Data - A field sent to the EdgeExpress Gateway appears invalid (correctly formatted but not on file). This could pertain to Processing Account Information configured in XCharge or BMS, or card information. The field causing the response may be specified.
EnumValue.name: InvalidAccountData

EnumValue._: 824 Industry Mismatch Error - The Processing Account is configured with the incorrect Market Type. This can occur if XCharge or BMS is not configured with the same Market Type as the EdgeExpress Gateway.
EnumValue.name: IndustryMismatchError

EnumValue._: 825 Rejected Internal support only -  Reserved for Fraud, Not currently implemented.
EnumValue.name: RejectedInternalSupportOnly

EnumValue._: 827 Invalid Card Type -  The account number entered is not valid for the card type entered. If Card Type is Visa, then the account number must be for a Visa account.
EnumValue.name: InvalidCardType

EnumValue._: 828 Card Type Not Supported - The card type (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, etc.) for the attempted transaction is not enabled at the EdgeExpress Gateway.
EnumValue.name: CardTypeNotSupported

EnumValue._: 829 Card Code Required - The Card Security Code (CSC, also known as the CVV, CVC or CID) is set to "required" for keyed transactions at the EdgeExpress Gateway, but was not sent.
EnumValue.name: CardCodeRequired

EnumValue._: 830 Address Required - The address (house number, part of the Address Verification System) is set to "required" for keyed transactions at the EdgeExpress Gateway, but was not sent.
EnumValue.name: AddressRequired

EnumValue._: 831 ZIP Code Required - The ZIP code (part of the Address Verification System) is set to "required" for keyed transactions at the EdgeExpress Gateway, but was not sent.
EnumValue.name: ZipCodeRequired

EnumValue._: 832 Encoded Data Format Error - Encoded format of check image file could not be read or was not submitted when expected.
EnumValue.name: EncodedDataFormatError

EnumValue._: 833 Check Services Image Error - MICR and Amount cannot be read The attempted paper check scan failed. When this occurs you should have the option to manually enter the check information or rescan the check.
EnumValue.name: CheckServicesImageErrorMICRAndAmout

EnumValue._: 834 Check Services Image Error - MICR cannot be read The attempted paper check scan failed. When this occurs you should have the option to manually enter the check information or rescan the check.
EnumValue.name: CheckServicesImageErrorMICROnly

EnumValue._: 835 Check Services Image Error - Amount cannot be read The attempted paper check scan failed. When this occurs you should have the option to manually enter the check information or rescan the check.
EnumValue.name: CheckServicesImageErrorAmountOnly

EnumValue._: 838 Email Service Error - The EdgeExpress Gateway attempted to send an email, possibly for a password reset request, but the attempt failed.
EnumValue.name: EmailServiceError

EnumValue._: 842 Invalid Reference Error - Response code returned when the referenced transaction type does not match.
EnumValue.name: InvalidReferenceErrorResponseCodeMisMatch

EnumValue._: 900 TSYS Error - Error thrown by Processor TSYS: the EdgeExpress Gateway is setup correctly, but the card or other value submitted is incorrect. SERV NOT ALLOWED usually indicates a Decline, Failure CV indicates the Card Type is not supported (e.g. AMEX needs to be enabled), and Failure HV indicates an error on the account setup in the EdgeExpress Gateway.
EnumValue.name: TSYSError

EnumValue._: 901 Processor Error Can be Global or TSYS errors (900 Global Payments Error: or 900 TSYS Error:) are returned on transactions if a processor setting is not configured correctly. This can occur if the card or transaction type is not enabled on the Processing Account at the processor end.
EnumValue.name: ProcessorError

column.name: ResponseDescription
column.summary: Gateaway output. Gives a more detailed description on the ResponseCode.

column.name: OTK
column.summary:
Gateaway output. This is the One Time Key that is used to launch the Hosted Payment Form. 
            The status of the OTK can be polled to determine if the end user has completed the HPF or if it has expired.

column.name: HpfUrl
column.summary:
This URL will be generated as a result of the OTK. The URL can be browsed in an IFRAME to create a secure portal between a browser 
            and the XWeb server. It is used with EdgeExpress now that HPF is deprecated.

column.name: HpfExpiration
column.summary: Timestamp at which this HPF will expire. The end user will only be able to access the HPF before it has expired. This expiration is set explicitly when creating the HPF.

column.name: TransactionID
column.summary: Gateaway output. Each transaction is given a reference for future use. This can be used to void the transaction.

column.name: TransactionType
column.summary: Gateaway output. The type of transaction that was processed. Must be a string data type because it comes from the Gateway as a string.

column.name: Alias
column.summary:
Gateaway output. A credit transaction will return an alias which is now linked to the credit card which was used. 
            This alias can be used in the future to make DTG payments and circumvent the need for the secure HPF. Only applies when Credit (not Debit) data is submitted on the HPF.
            CreditCard.XChargeToken is often set to this value.

column.name: CardType
column.summary:
Gateaway output. The card type used for this transaction. 
            "Credit" - for cards that support signature only.
            "Debit/Credit" - for cards that support either PIN-entry or signature.
            "Debit/ATM" - for cards that support PIN-entry only.
            "FSA" - for Flexible Spending Accounts

column.name: CardBrand
column.summary: Gateaway output. The card brand used for this transaction. Possible values include "Visa", "MasterCard", "Discover", "American Express", "Diners Club", "JCB", "PayPal".

column.name: CardBrandShort
column.summary:
Gateaway output. The industry standard abbreviation of the card brand. 
            Possible values include "VS", "MC", "DS", "AX", "DCIDISC", "JCB-DISC", "PP" (order respective to CardBrand above).

column.name: MaskedAcctNum
column.summary:
Gateaway output. Provides a masked format of the account number. The format will show the last 4 digits, the remainder will masked out with an asterisk character.
            End user will be presented with a list of previously generated MaskedAcctNum(s) when making a payment. These are linked to an alias which can be used to make a DTG payment.
            This ciccumvents the need to use the HPF.

column.name: Amount
column.summary: Gateaway output. Amount of credit card and check transactions.

column.name: ApprovalCode
column.summary: Gateaway output. A 6 digit authorization approval code.

column.name: CardCodeResponse
column.summary: Gateaway output. Response from the Card Security Code lookup. Only applies when Credit(not Debit) data is submitted on the HPF.

column.name: ReceiptID
column.summary: Gateaway output. An identification number assigned by the OpenEdge Gateway to the receipt.

column.name: ExpDate
column.summary: Gateaway output. Provides the Expiration Date of the account being accessed. Format is yyMM from XWeb gateway. Will be converted to ExpirationDate.

column.name: EntryMethod
column.summary: Gateaway output. Indicates how the account number was entered by the end user. Always 'KEYED' in our case.

column.name: ProcessorResponse
column.summary: Gateaway output. The response from the processor. It is only returned on transactions that are processed by the processor (Auth, Sales and Settlements).

column.name: BatchNum
column.summary: Gateaway output. This indicates the current open batch number.

column.name: BatchAmount
column.summary: Gateaway output. Net amount of Credit and Debit Card transactions in batch.

column.name: AccountExpirationDate
column.summary: The expiration date of the credit card that was referenced in this transaction. DateTime representation of ExpDate. Initialized by XWebInputAbs.CreateGatewayResponse().

column.name: DebugError
column.summary: Debug information regarding this response. Can only be set by XWebResponses.ProcessOutstandingTransactions().

column.name: PayNote
column.summary: Will be entered as Payment.PayNote once payment transaction has completed.

column.name: CCSource
column.summary: Enum:CreditCardSource The source of where this transaction originated from.

EnumValue._: 0 - This is used when the payment is not a Credit Card. If CC, then this means we are storing the actual credit card number. Not recommended.
EnumValue.name: None

EnumValue._: 1 - Local installation of X-Charge
EnumValue.name: XServer

EnumValue._: 2 - Credit card created via X-Web (an eService)
EnumValue.name: XWeb

EnumValue._: 3 - PayConnect web service (from within OD).
EnumValue.name: PayConnect

EnumValue._: 4 - Credit card has been added through the local installation of X-Charge and the PayConnect web service.
EnumValue.name: XServerPayConnect

EnumValue._: 5 - Made from the login screen of the Patient Portal.
EnumValue.name: XWebPortalLogin

EnumValue._: 6 - PaySimple web service (from within OD).
EnumValue.name: PaySimple

EnumValue._: 7 - PaySimple ACH web service (from within OD).
EnumValue.name: PaySimpleACH

EnumValue._: 8 - PayConnect credit card (made from Patient Portal)
EnumValue.name: PayConnectPortal

EnumValue._: 9 - PayConnect credit card (made from Patient Portal Login screen).
EnumValue.name: PayConnectPortalLogin

EnumValue._: 10 - CareCredit.
EnumValue.name: CareCredit

EnumValue._: 11 - EdgeExpress Cloud when calling the RCM program.
EnumValue.name: EdgeExpressRCM

EnumValue._: 12 - EdgeExpress Card Not Present API.
EnumValue.name: EdgeExpressCNP

EnumValue._: 13 - Payment taken through Open Dental API.
EnumValue.name: API

EnumValue._: 14 - EdgeExpress payment taken through the Payment Portal.
EnumValue.name: EdgeExpressPaymentPortal

EnumValue._: 15 - EdgeExpress payment taken through the Payment Portal as a guest.
EnumValue.name: EdgeExpressPaymentPortalGuest

EnumValue._: 16 - PayConnect payment taken through the Payment Portal.
EnumValue.name: PayConnectPaymentPortal

EnumValue._: 17 - PayConnect payment taken through the Payment Portal as a guest.
EnumValue.name: PayConnectPaymentPortalGuest

EnumValue._: 18 - PaySimple payment taken through the Payment Portal.
EnumValue.name: PaySimplePaymentPortal

EnumValue._: 19 - PaySimple payment taken through the Payment Portal as a guest.
EnumValue.name: PaySimplePaymentPortalGuest

EnumValue._: 20 - PaySimple ACH Payment taken through the Payment Portal.
EnumValue.name: PaySimplePaymentPortalACH

EnumValue._: 21 - XWeb payment taken through the Payment Portal.
EnumValue.name: XWebPaymentPortal

EnumValue._: 22 - XWeb payment taken through the Payment Portal as a guest.
EnumValue.name: XWebPaymentPortalGuest

column.name: OrderId
column.summary: Generated by us but necessary for Card Not Present API calls. Used to link transactions together (e.g. for returns).

column.name: EmailResponse
column.summary: Email address used for a requested receipt provided by the user when making a payment via the patient portal.

column.name: LogGuid
column.summary: The GUID used in EserviceLogs related to this response. May be blank.

table.name: zipcode
table.summary: Zipcodes are also known as postal codes.  Zipcodes are always copied to patient records rather than linked.  So items in this list can be freely altered or deleted without harming patient data.

column.name: ZipCodeNum
column.summary: Primary key.

column.name: ZipCodeDigits
column.summary: The actual zipcode.

column.name: IsFrequent
column.summary: If true, then it will show in the dropdown list in the patient edit window.
